1.intent: Synthesize Zenoh-compatible networking stack from SCXML + SCE Forge as single source of truth; two interoperable backends.
outputs_bullets: AP backend: Rust, std, tokio runtime, Linux x86_64 / aarch64
MCU backend: C11, no_std-equivalent, no heap; SoC TBD until deploy authoring
Wire-interoperability with upstream zenohd for the sec 2 documented subset
caveats_bullets: Not a rewrite of zenoh/zenoh-pico; demonstration that SCE synthesizes industrial wire protocols.

10.intent: Per-binding QoS configuration sourced from deploy.yaml and emitted as runtime structs (AP) or static const tables (MCU).

10/10.1.intent: deploy.yaml QoS вҖ” per-binding priority/reliability/express/congestion_control declared in machine binding block; typed config consumed by codegen.

10/10.2.intent: QoS config codegen вҖ” AP emits Rust const QosConfig (data section); MCU emits C const struct in .rodata (Flash, no RAM cost).

11.intent: Multi-layer testing strategy: byte-equivalence parity, wire replay, upstream interop, no-alloc guard, drift detection, fuzz.

11/11.1.intent: Cross-backend parity gate вҖ” every algorithm/codec with sce:test-vector runs on both backends and is byte-diffed; gate on merge.

11/11.2.intent: Wire replay tests вҖ” pcap captures from upstream zenoh peers replayed into generated stack with declared expected event sequence.

11/11.3.intent: Upstream interop CI вҖ” dockerized zenohd + AP node + MCU host-mode build (lwIP shim, renode/QEMU later) asserting full handshake + pub/sub roundtrip.

11/11.4.intent: MCU heap prohibition enforced as five layers вҖ” direct trap, linker wrap, libc pin, call-graph reachability, exception ban вҖ” not runtime trap alone.
rationale_bullets: Layer 1 direct trap: malloc/calloc/realloc/free stubs emit __builtin_trap on call.
Layer 2 linker --wrap: -Wl,--wrap on alloc symbols routes libc-internal indirect calls to traps.
Layer 3 libc pin: newlib-nano or picolibc tiny stdio prevents allocating printf path.
Layer 4 call-graph: post-link analyzer rejects any reach from out/mcu to deny-listed allocator.
Layer 5 exception ban: -fno-exceptions/-fno-rtti/-fno-unwind-tables blocks libstdc++ machinery.

11/11.5.intent: Drift gate: every emitted file carries source-hash + template-hash; sce-build verify recomputes and fails on mismatch; manual edits to out/ forbidden.
rationale_bullets: Per-file header carries source-hash + template-hash (sha256 over inputs and sce-build binary).
sce-build verify recomputes both hashes and fails the gate on mismatch (CI + pre-commit).
Fixed artifact set: out/{ap,mcu}, Cargo.toml, CMakeLists.txt, linker fragments, sce_sourcemap.json.
Manual edits to out/ forbidden: path is RFC or sce:extern; exceptions tracked with expiry date.
sce_sourcemap.json hash mismatch surfaces as traceability/sourcemap-source-hash-mismatch.

11/11.6.intent: Adversarial input fuzz testing on generated codec/parsers; deferred Phase D+ work; complements В§11.1 cross-backend parity.

12.intent: Generated AP/MCU code dependency surface: SCE crates, runtime crates, intrinsics, and user-provided platform pieces.

12/12.1.intent: Generated code dependency surface вҖ” AP Rust pulls sce_core/forge_runtime/link_runtime_tokio/intrinsics + bytes/tokio/heapless; MCU C pulls forge_runtime_c/link_runtime_lwip/intrinsics + lwIP/HAL.

12/12.2.intent: Runtime crate versioning вҖ” SemVer; generated code pins exact minor at build time via Cargo.toml/CMakeLists constraint to avoid SSoT drift between codegen and runtime.

12/12.3.intent: External user-provided surface вҖ” MCU consumer provides SoC HAL/lwIP config/master linker script; AP binary ships self-contained.

13.intent: Pre-SCE prose and tooling work: wire spec, pcap corpus, deploy skeletons, FSM sketches, meta-source generator (RFC 5.G workaround).

14.intent: Living-doc maintenance notes: when Phase A lands, what sections to revise and how to track architecture-vs-code drift.

15.intent: Placeholder for observability stack (logging, metrics, tracing); decisions deferred per section 14 living-doc notes.

16.intent: Placeholder for performance targets (OPEN handshake latency on MCU, AP TX throughput); awaits first benchmark numbers.

2.intent: Scope partition: MVP (zenoh-pico parity), Deferred (with migration path), Permanent constraints, Extensibility invariants, Protocol pinning.

2/2.0.intent: Long-term migration vision (every Zenoh feature eventually lands) + MVP criterion (full zenoh-pico parity, peer+client both backends).

2/2.1.intent: MVP scope table вҖ” peer+client modes, full message set, 5 transports, QoS dims, bounded KeyExpr matching, fragmentation, both targets, zero-copy posture.

2/2.2.intent: Phase D+ deferred items (router mode, BLE/Raweth, API shim, shared-mem, admin/scripting) each carries an explicit migration path; not permanently excluded.

2/2.3.intent: MCU-class permanent constraints (no router, no aggregation, no heap, no scripting, no unbounded KeyExpr, no dynamic loading); AP unaffected.

2/2.4.intent: Six extensibility invariants: static-first, link-extensible, kinds-additive, library-not-monolith, platform gating, out/ SSoT.
rationale_bullets: Static-first, dynamic-opt-in: every MCU-static feature should admit an AP-dynamic variant.
Link drivers extensible: open set via target plugin, not closed enum.
Kinds additive: ForgeKind set grows; existing kinds remain stable across versions.
Generated code exports as library, not monolith: out/ap and out/mcu are library targets.
Platform gating only when physically necessary; keep pool/link/worker kinds platform-neutral.
out/ is SSoT-downstream: manual edits forbidden; new features land via SCE extensions.

2/2.5.intent: Zenoh 1.x wire format pinned via deploy.yaml protocol block; additive extensions allowed within 1.x, 2.x is a separate scope decision.

3.intent: Four design invariants: zero-runtime abstraction, location transparency, strict SSoT, hardware-aware codegen.

3/3.1.intent: Zero-Runtime Abstraction вҖ” no vtables, no dynamic dispatch in the hot path; all bindings resolve at build time to direct calls (SCE AOT philosophy carried through).

3/3.2.intent: Location Transparency вҖ” SCXML uses logical identities; physical transport realization and addressing live in deploy.yaml; same SCXML deploys to AP or MCU.

3/3.3.intent: Strict SSoT вҖ” wire format / algorithms / FSMs defined once in SCXML+Forge; AP and MCU are descendants; cross-backend drift is a generator bug, not expected.

3/3.4.intent: Hardware-Aware Codegen вҖ” MCU code integrates with target SoC capabilities (SRAM placement, DMA pairing, alignment) declared via deploy.yaml; AP/MCU share no memory.

4.intent: Mapping of SCE existing capabilities, RFC-requested extensions, and watching-zenoh authoring scope.

4/4.1.intent: SCE existing surface вҖ” SCXML parser/IR/W3C algos, expression transpiler, 11 Forge kinds, multi-language codegen, no_std Rust runtime, diagnostics + composition.

4/4.2.intent: SCE extensions requested via RFC вҖ” new kinds (algorithm/link/buffer-pool/worker/bounded-collection), codec DSL, fragment pattern, C11 backend, no_std statechart, sce:extern, deploy fields.

4/4.3.intent: Project authoring scope вҖ” SCXML statecharts, Forge codec/algorithm/bounded-collection/link/pool/worker sources, deploy.yaml variants, runtime crates, optional API shim.

5.intent: Cargo workspace structure: out/ap library, crates/watching_zenoh_{api,bin}, plugin extension path.

6.intent: Build stages from SCXML+SCE through codegen, out/, cargo, to AP/MCU artifacts; per-target artifact list.

6/6.1.intent: Build pipeline stages вҖ” sce-codegen single-invocation parse/validate/IR/typecheck/const-fold/emit/sidecar driving Cargo + CMake builds.

6/6.2.intent: Per-target artifacts вҖ” AP gets src/Cargo.toml/build.rs; MCU gets headers/sources/CMake/linker fragment with linker-flavor matrix (GNU LD default, scatter/ICF/os-managed via target plugin).

7.intent: Runtime layout: AP backend (Rust+tokio), MCU backend (C11+lwIP), shared runtime crates/headers.

7/7.1.intent: AP backend stack вҖ” generated code on top of sce_core + sce_link_runtime_tokio + Rust std/bytes/tokio; tokio::spawn workers, mpsc channels, BytesMut pools.

7/7.2.intent: MCU stack вҖ” generated code over sce_forge_runtime_c + sce_link_runtime_lwip; cooperative tick scheduler with WCET budget; static pools + DMA descriptors.

7/7.3.intent: Shared runtime crates/headers вҖ” sce_core (AP only), sce_forge_runtime (Rust+C variants), sce_link_runtime_{tokio,lwip}, sce_intrinsics_runtime (whitelisted).

8.intent: Wire format layering: scouting/transport/network/zenoh/extensions; FSM, codec, link layers.

8/8.1.intent: Wire format stack layer diagram вҖ” Application -> Session FSM -> Network FSM -> Codec -> Link -> OS/HAL; bottom-up data flow on RX, top-down on TX.

8/8.2.intent: Session FSM sketch вҖ” Init/Scouting/Opening/Established/Closing path authored as session_unicast/multicast.scxml; full model lives in docs/session-fsm.md.

8/8.3.intent: Codec layer вҖ” one SCXML file per message type using RFC В§5.B extensions (flags/vle/present-if/len-prefix/TLV); test vectors drive cross-backend parity.

8/8.4.intent: Link layer вҖ” kind=link SCXML files (UDP unicast/multicast/TCP/Serial/WS) with framer + RX-pool + backpressure declared; driver and address bound via deploy.yaml.

9.intent: Zero-copy: TX always zero-copy; RX happy path zero-copy; oversized/fragment stage-copy; pool placement; platform-aware link.

9/9.1.intent: TX path zero-copy вҖ” Codec encodes directly into pool slot, link send-from-slot, DMA reads via prebuilt descriptor; pool slot in DMA-coherent SRAM.

9/9.2.intent: RX happy path zero-copy вҖ” DMA writes pre-programmed RX slot; codec parses in place; event emitted with slot borrow; slot returned to freelist.

9/9.3.intent: Stage-copy RX path for oversized/multi-DMA вҖ” one copy DMA-slot to stage; build-time fragmentation analysis; stage_copy_policy warn/error/forbid per machine.

9/9.4.intent: Pool placement across .dtcm/.sram1/.flash regions; per-pool placement declared in kind source; memory regions in deploy.yaml; linker fragment ties them.

9/9.5.intent: Platform-aware link substrate: 5-row matrix (MCU bare_metal, linux+epoll, linux+io_uring, QNX, etc) sharing pool FSM.

architecture--watching-zenoh.intent: Pre-implementation design document for watching-zenoh (Zenoh stack synthesized from SCE), blocked on SCE Phase A.
caveats_bullets: Status: Pre-implementation (2026-04-24); blocked on SCE Phase A landing.
Becomes living reference updated in lockstep with code once SCE Phase A lands.

intrinsics-runtime--symbol-surface.intent: Defines sce_intrinsics_runtime symbol surface (C11/Rust variants) for atomics/fences/cache/IRQ/RNG/HMAC/HW-sem.
inputs_bullets: RFC sec 5.I sce:extern whitelist вҖ” atomics, fences, cache maintenance, IRQ control
RFC sec 5.J.2 вҖ” statechart no_std HAL trait shape (ticks/wake/irq-save)
RFC sec 5.K target_plugin extension вҖ” per-SoC HMAC + HW semaphores out of core whitelist
session-fsm sec 2.7 G-SFM-5 вҖ” HMAC + RNG primitive ownership question (OQ-W15)
reassembly-fsm sec 6 вҖ” cache maintenance pinning (sec 5.E pool FSM call sites)
ARCHITECTURE sec 9.5 5-row matrix вҖ” same symbol set, different bodies per row
outputs_bullets: Categorized symbol table marking core whitelist vs target plugin per category
sec 5.J.2 statechart no_std HAL trait mapped onto these symbols
Cross-reference into RFC sec 5.I diagnostic list for authoring violation traceability
caveats_bullets: OQ-W14 (HW-sem standardization) and OQ-W15 (HMAC+RNG ownership) gate final shape.
Initial proposal: option 2 for HMAC (target plugin), option 1 for RNG (core whitelist).
Non-outputs: symbol body implementations, per-SoC HMAC accelerator binding.

intrinsics-runtime--symbol-surface/2-1-a.intent: Atomics core whitelist вҖ” per-width (u8-u64+usize) load/store/cas/fetch_* with explicit memory ordering encoded in the symbol name; no integer ordering parameter.

intrinsics-runtime--symbol-surface/2-2-f.intent: Fences core whitelist вҖ” atomic_fence_{acquire/release/acq_rel/seq_cst}, compiler_barrier (no runtime cost), dma_fence (DSB on ARMv7-M+, no-op on linux).

intrinsics-runtime--symbol-surface/2-3-c.intent: Cache maintenance core whitelist on MCU, no-op on AP linux (kernel-coherent); emitted only on В§5.E pool lifecycle FSM edges; direct <sce:call> rejected.

intrinsics-runtime--symbol-surface/2-4-i.intent: IRQ control core whitelist (optional) вҖ” irq_save/irq_restore; only emitted for workers with sce:critical-section (RFC В§5.D, Phase C+); MVP deploys do not use these.

intrinsics-runtime--symbol-surface/2-5-r.intent: RNG target plugin row (OQ-W15 (a) Q1 ratified Round 11: core whitelist rejected; sce_random_fill consumed by stateless_accept cookie HMAC key gen).
caveats_bullets: Q1 = No ratified Round 11: SCE BASELINE_SYMBOLS boundary is architecture-fixed primitives only.
Impl multiplicity (HW TRNG / ADC+Yarrow / getrandom / arc4random) places RNG in plugin tier.
Fallback validated by SCE c13_beta_antiflood tests (baseline вҲӘ plugin lookup, 4570a389).
Counter-offer tracked as OQ-W24 (RFC В§5.I tier-explicit separation, boundary hardening).
R62 cookie key currently caller-supplied static bytes (no RNG fill yet)
R70b carry: AP-side getrandom integration for SigningKey::new entropy

intrinsics-runtime--symbol-surface/2-6-h.intent: HMAC target plugin (OQ-W15 (a) Q2 ratified Round 11: per-SoC accelerator selection; sce_hmac_sha256 consumed by listener links with untrusted_source).
caveats_bullets: Q2 = Yes ratified Round 11: proposal target_plugin path confirmed by SCE maintainer.
Per-SoC accelerator surface (STM32H7 CRYP / ESP32 SHA / Cortex-M0+ SW) keeps baseline crypto-free.
SW fallback authorable as algorithm kind; wire output identical across backings.
R62 AP path uses RustCrypto hmac+sha2 (std-coupled, vendored)
R70 SigningKey newtype + zeroize-on-drop wraps the secret key bytes
RFC 4231 TC1+TC2 byte-equiv pinned in wz-runtime-tokio unit tests (R70)
Phase W (MCU) swaps to sce_intrinsics_runtime::hmac_sha256 per RFC В§5.I

intrinsics-runtime--symbol-surface/2-7-h.intent: HW semaphores/mailboxes target plugin (OQ-W14) вҖ” sce_hw_sem_*/sce_hw_mbox_*; required at multi-core MCU bring-up (Phase C cross-core); single-core MVP omits.

intrinsics-runtime--symbol-surface/2-sym.intent: Symbol surface partition into 7 categories (atomics/fences/cache/IRQ/RNG/HMAC/HW-sem) marked core or target-plugin; OQ-W15/W14 gate unresolved cells.

intrinsics-runtime--symbol-surface/3-whi.intent: Whitelist policy 2x2 matrix вҖ” core if every platform has body AND choice is not deploy-meaningful; target plugin if author/vendor needs to pick between non-interchangeable bodies.

intrinsics-runtime--symbol-surface/4-dep.intent: deploy.yaml extern_symbols block (RFC В§5.K) names runtime crate variant + target plugin; activates 3-tuple per RFC В§5.J.3; three concrete deploy states (mcu_target/ap_standalone/ap_mcu_pair).

intrinsics-runtime--symbol-surface/5-1-t.intent: StatechartHal trait (Rust pseudocode) вҖ” now_us monotonic tick microseconds, wake cooperative-scheduler hint, irq_save/restore (Phase C+ critical section).

intrinsics-runtime--symbol-surface/5-2-m.intent: HAL method body mapping per platform вҖ” now_us via DWT/SysTick on MCU vs Instant::now on AP linux; wake via worker_wake or tokio reactor; irq_save/restore via В§2.4 or empty.

intrinsics-runtime--symbol-surface/5-3-t.intent: HAL trait belongs in sce_intrinsics_runtime (statechart concern), not sce_link_runtime_* (I/O concern); both consumers share one symbol surface as single dependency.

intrinsics-runtime--symbol-surface/5-no-.intent: RFC В§5.J.2 statechart no_std HAL trait вҖ” provided by sce_intrinsics_runtime, bottoms out on В§2 symbols; trait shape MCU-class per RFC В§5.J.4.

intrinsics-runtime--symbol-surface/6-sel.intent: Self-review against ARCHITECTURE В§2.4 six invariants вҖ” every row PASS; symbol surface identical across platforms with body-elided per platform-class (same shape, different body).

intrinsics-runtime--symbol-surface/7-nex.intent: Next-step scaffolding when Phase A SCE codegen lands вҖ” sce_intrinsics_runtime_c skeleton, _rust skeleton, target_extensions_stm32h747.yaml; OQ-W23 deferral noted.

intrinsics-runtime--symbol-surface/8-cha.intent: Change log вҖ” 2026-05-01 initial draft (third of three runtime-crate sibling docs; symbol surface partitioned into 7 categories; OQ-W15 (a) initial proposal locked in).

oq-w15-a--ratification-summary-for-sce-m.intent: Two binary ratification decisions on sce_intrinsics_runtime_* whitelist membership for RNG and HMAC primitives, individually decidable but coupled by shared consumer.
inputs_bullets: Shared consumer: stateless_accept: cookie_hmac_sha256 (RFC В§5.K hard error if absent)
Decisions are individually decidable вҖ” RNG location does not constrain HMAC location
outputs_bullets: Decision 1: sce_random_fill(buf, len) -> int вҶ’ core whitelist (yes/no)
Decision 2: sce_hmac_sha256(key, key_len, msg, msg_len, out) вҶ’ target plugin (yes/no)

oq-w15-a--ratification-summary-for-sce-m.intent: Phase A entry of public-Internet-facing MCU listeners is blocked until both stateless_accept primitives have ratified placement.
rationale_bullets: RFC В§5.K stateless_accept required when domain_attrs.untrusted_source: true (build refusal)
stateless_accept body uses sce_hmac_sha256 (cookie HMAC) and sce_random_fill (key generation)
Both symbols must be placed (whitelist or plugin) before SCXML can author stateless_accept
inputs_bullets: Exposure class: domain_attrs.untrusted_source flag on listener link
deploy/stateless-accept-required-on-untrusted-source вҖ” hard error if missing
outputs_bullets: Public-Internet-facing MCU listener Phase A blocked pending placement decision
Private-LAN MCU listener Phase A not gated (caps alone suffice)

oq-w15-a--ratification-summary-for-sce-m.intent: Watching-zenoh proposes RNG to core whitelist (universal entropy primitive) and HMAC to target plugin (per-SoC accelerator selection).
rationale_bullets: RNG universal вҖ” every platform has some entropy source (HW RNG / ADC noise / OS getrandom)
RNG no SoC-specific accelerator selection вҖ” bundled with HAL, single canonical symbol
RNG WCET cleanly handled by canonical symbol with measured bound, no per-deploy redeclaration
HMAC HW-accelerator selection genuinely per-SoC (CRYP/SHA/SW fallback)
HMAC SW fallback authorable as algorithm kind (option 3 fallback path)
HMAC bounded blast radius вҖ” keeps whitelist small, contains crypto precedent
inputs_bullets: Decision questions from В§1 of this doc (RNG location / HMAC location)

alternatives_rejected.reason: loses universality, every public-Internet deploy ships plugin
not viable, entropy generation is platform-coupled
adds 32-byte crypto, sets precedent for more crypto primitives
loses HW accelerator, ~10x CPU on STM32H7, tight WCET headroom

oq-w15-a--ratification-summary-for-sce-m.intent: Detailed counter-option matrix for the two decisions, expanding the trade-offs that В§3 cites in summary form.

alternatives_rejected.reason: every deploy ships entropy plugin even when platform is canonical
not viable; entropy is irreducibly platform-coupled
HW dispatch survives but precedent for more crypto additions set
zero plugin dep but HW unused, ~10x CPU, tighter burst headroom

oq-w15-a--ratification-summary-for-sce-m.intent: Forward-compatibility implications across the 2x2 matrix of RNG and HMAC placement decisions.
rationale_bullets: RNG core (proposal): RNG features land free; missing entropy вҶ’ build-time refusal
HMAC plugin (proposal): future crypto land by precedent; whitelist stays small
HMAC plugin (proposal): SoC-vendor crypto IP changes absorbed without RFC churn
RNG plugin alt: duplicated authoring per canonical deploy; each new RNG re-states it
HMAC core alt: faster Phase A on listener MCUs; precedent for crypto whitelist adds
caveats_bullets: R62-R70 AP-side HMAC land (RustCrypto std-coupled) ratifies OQ-W15(a) Q2 plugin path
SigningKey newtype + zeroize: Phase W MCU swap MUST preserve drop-time wipe semantics

oq-w15-a--ratification-summary-for-sce-m.intent: Ratification ask: yes/no on each of the two proposal decisions, with optional counter-direction and audit artifact in the OQ-W15 entry.
inputs_bullets: Initial proposal (this doc) as ratification subject
Counter-option matrix as alternatives if proposal rejected
outputs_bullets: Ratify or reject proposal (yes/no on each of two decisions)
If reject: counter-direction from matrix or proposed independently
Decision artifact: short note in rfc-open-questions-log.md OQ-W15 entry
caveats_bullets: OQ-W15 (b) MCU/AP defaults are out of scope here; settle post-Phase-A via HIL measurement
Decision Outcome (Round 11, 2026-05-14): Q1 = No, Q2 = Yes ratified by SCE maintainer.
Q1 rationale: BASELINE_SYMBOLS = architecture-fixed primitives only; RNG вҶ’ plugin tier.
Q2 rationale: per-SoC accelerator selection genuinely plugin-meaningful (proposal aligned).
Counter-offer (tier separation) tracked as OQ-W24; spec restructure deferred to next round.
Fallback path validated (SCE C13-ОІ tests, 4570a389); deploys unblock via 4-line plugin row.

oq-w15-a--ratification-summary-for-sce-m.intent: Citation index linking this artifact to its source material in the watching-zenoh internal docs.

reassembly-fsm--prose-sketch/1-framing-o.intent: Reassembly FSM between codec (Fragment) and network FSMs; chain key=(peer_zid, reliability) вҖ” two buffers per peer per upstream wire shape.
inputs_bullets: Chain key (peer_zid 16B, reliability 1bit); no priority, no sn_base in key
Wire: Fragment {sn:u64 VLE, payload:ZSlice, more bit, reliability bit}
FRAGMENT_FIRST/DROP exts patch-1+; legacy patch-0 infers first via empty+consecutive SN
Fragment classification: First / Continue / Final / Drop / unmatched-continue dropped at Router

reassembly-fsm--prose-sketch/10-next-ste.intent: Reassembly FSM unblocks Phase A SCXML authoring of sources/network/reassembly.scxml; deploy.yaml reassembly_pool block fully named; OQ-W22 closed.

reassembly-fsm--prose-sketch/2-1-hierarc.intent: ReassemblyDispatcher = Router region + N parallel Slot regions (N=slot_count); one-active-per-key invariant; codegen N is build-time const.
inputs_bullets: Router: chain-keyвҶ’slot-index map (bounded-collection<SlotEntry, N>), per-peer quota check
Slot[0..N-1]: parallel sub-region, four-state machine each
Upstream divergence: shared bounded pool decoupled from peer count (vs upstream inline 2Г—peers)
Parity invariant: slot_count вүҘ 2 Г— peer_table.capacity reproduces upstream no-exhaustion
MCU slot_count=4, AP=32 (OQ-W8); over-commits trade strict parity for memory cost

reassembly-fsm--prose-sketch/2-2-slot-st.intent: Slot four-state FSM: Empty вҶ’ Receiving вҶ’ Complete / TimedOut / Aborted (three terminals funnel back to Empty via slot release).
inputs_bullets: Empty: slot free, awaiting allocation from Router
Receiving: chain in progress; consecutive Continue allowed
Complete: Final received with consecutive SN
TimedOut: reassembly_timeout_ms expired
Aborted: 7 reasons (OOO/Drop/duplicate-First/oldest-wins/codec-error/unmatched/peer-drop)
Slot release transitions buffer-pool slot cpu-mut/cpu-ref вҶ’ free (lifecycle FSM funnel)

reassembly-fsm--prose-sketch/2-3-router-.intent: Router region routes Fragment.* events to Slot regions by chain key; enforces per-peer quota; refuses second First on already-open key (duplicate-first abort).

reassembly-fsm--prose-sketch/2-4-transit.intent: Eight detailed transitions across Slot states: EmptyвҶ’Receiving (First), ReceivingвҶ’Receiving (Continue), FinalвҶ’Complete, plus 5 abort variants and TerminalвҶ’Empty release funnel.
outputs_bullets: В§2.4.1 EmptyвҶ’Receiving: Fragment.First admitted by Router (key not-active, quota OK, slot OK)
В§2.4.2 ReceivingвҶ’Receiving: Fragment.Continue with consecutive SN appends payload
В§2.4.3 ReceivingвҶ’Complete: Fragment.Final consecutive SN, emit message-complete event
В§2.4.4 ReceivingвҶ’Aborted: out-of-order Continue (non-consecutive SN, strict in-order)
В§2.4.5 ReceivingвҶ’TimedOut: reassembly_timeout_ms expired
В§2.4.6 ReceivingвҶ’Aborted: oldest-wins eviction by Router under pool pressure
В§2.4.7 ReceivingвҶ’Aborted: codec error on payload decode (В§2.4.7a peer-drop via DROP ext)
В§2.4.8 TerminalвҶ’Empty: slot release funnel; lifecycle FSM cpu-mutвҶ’free

reassembly-fsm--prose-sketch/2-5-out-of-.intent: Strict in-order policy (OQ-W21 closed): non-consecutive Continue aborts the chain (option 2 ratified); upstream consecutive-SN parity preserved.

reassembly-fsm--prose-sketch/2-6-mapping.intent: Mapping prose four-state Slot FSM (Empty/Receiving/Complete/TimedOut/Aborted) to RFC В§5.M three-state high-level sketch (Idle/Active/Done) with terminal split.

reassembly-fsm--prose-sketch/3-tx-fragme.intent: TX fragmentation sibling FSM вҖ” chunks outbound NetworkMessage exceeding link MTU into Fragment.{First, Continue, Final} with consecutive SN.

reassembly-fsm--prose-sketch/4-timer-quo.intent: Reassembly timer / per-peer quota / pool config from deploy.yaml вҖ” reassembly_timeout_ms, per_peer_quota, slot_count, slot_size live under buffer_pools.reassembly_pool.

reassembly-fsm--prose-sketch/5-trust-cla.intent: Trust-class interaction вҖ” reassembly forbidden on untrusted/session_arming links; established_session sibling instance receives reassembly bindings (OQ-W22 close).

reassembly-fsm--prose-sketch/6-buffer-po.intent: Buffer-pool lifecycle FSM (В§5.E) interaction вҖ” reassembly slot acquire = pool slot cpu-mut; terminal release = cpu-mut/cpu-ref вҶ’ free.
caveats_bullets: R51-R52 RxFrame { bytes: Vec u8 } owned вҖ” pool-slot zero-copy not yet wired
Pool-slot Rx borrows carry: wz-runtime-tokio В§4.1 io_uring fixed-buffer opt-in

reassembly-fsm--prose-sketch/7-1-reassem.intent: Build-time reassembly capacity check вҖ” max_fragments Г— per_peer_quota Г— peer_table.capacity sufficiency for slot_count; hard error if violated.

C11 header shape for sce_link_runtime_lwip вҖ” opaque sce_link_t, sce_link_event_t enum, RX dispatch entry, sce_pool_slot_handle_t for В§5.E ownership edge.
deploy.yaml links.<name>.driver mapping вҖ” four values (lwip_udp/lwip_tcp/serial_uart/raw_eth) for bare_metal; raw_eth via target plugin (vendor MAC HAL).
Trust-class compile-time elision вҖ” three classes (untrusted/session_arming/established_session); absent methods not declared (compile fail, not runtime); listener two-instance emission per OQ-W22.
Self-review against ARCHITECTURE В§2.4 six invariants вҖ” every row PASS; static handles, bounded queues, header platform-gated to (c11, bare_metal).
R63 reverted R53 NOP-stub skeleton (doc-around-hack). Phase W lands wz-runtime-lwip with real lwIP API + MCU cross-compile; emitter audit kept as link_emitter_b6 Layer 3 test.
Change log вҖ” 2026-05-01 initial draft (KICKOFF #2); header shape locked four outbound + 6 events + poll + ISR; trust-class compile-time elision; typestate via Clang -Wconsumed.

scouting-fsm--prose-sketch/3-2-oq-w3-ans.intent: OQ-W3 closed: Interest is not router-only in 1.x вҖ” three transport-class mechanisms (unicast acceptor push / multicast Interest pull / client-router push).
outputs_bullets: Mechanism 1 (unicast peer-peer): acceptor pushes ALL local declares + DeclareFinal at handshake
Mechanism 2 (multicast peer mesh): joining peer Interest{CURRENT,KEYEXPRS} вҶ’ each remote replies
Mechanism 1 (client unicast to router): router-side same as peer-peer
Client unicast no router: not supported (Mech1 absent, Mech2 absent, inbound Interest = no-op)

scouting-fsm--prose-sketch/3-3-implicati.intent: Scouting FSM doesn't participate in Interest itself, but mode choice determines which Interest mechanism downstream session-establishment fires.

mode=passive вҶ’ multicast peer join вҶ’ Mechanism 2 (Interest pull + multicast reply)
mode=static + connect[] вҶ’ Mechanism 1 (same as active for resulting unicast handshake)
Scouting is the trigger chain; declare_fsm authors consult here for Interest-trigger boundary

scouting-fsm--prose-sketch/6-1-per-link-.intent: Trust-class table extension: untrusted required on scouting links / forbidden elsewhere; three-layer mechanical defense (wire / session-fsm / reassembly).

session_arming: forbidden on scouting / required on listener / forbidden on reassembly
established_session: forbidden on scouting / required on data-plane / required on reassembly

Layer 2 (session-fsm): no Accepting.* on untrusted (session-fsm В§2.6 row 1)

session-fsm--prose-level-sketch/10-next-.intent: R54-R55b: R29 script-action placeholders wired to wz-runtime-tokio LinkDriver via sce-rust-lua + Engine::process_event drives InitвҶ’LinkOpening verified.

session-fsm--prose-level-sketch/2-1-unic.intent: Unicast session FSM has eight states plus Closed terminal; splits Opening (initiator) and Accepting (acceptor) explicitly.
inputs_bullets: Init: created, link not yet open; entry from outbound or inbound
LinkOpening: outbound link driver attempting open (TCP connect / WS upgrade)
Scouting: active scout loop pre-target (mode=active deploys only)
Opening: initiator driving 4-way Init/Open handshake
Accepting: acceptor responding to remote-driven handshake
Established: handshake done, RX/TX regions enabled (sub-region container)
Closing: graceful close in progress; sends Close with reason
Closed: terminal, link released, pool slots freed

session-fsm--prose-level-sketch/2-2-unic.intent: Unicast transitions вҖ” outbound (LinkOpening вҶ’ Opening.SentInitSyn вҶ’ GotInitAck вҶ’ Established) + inbound (Accepting.AwaitingInitSyn вҶ’ SentInitAck вҶ’ SentOpenAck вҶ’ Established).
outputs_bullets: Outbound: Init вҶ’ LinkOpening вҶ’ Opening.SentInitSyn вҶ’ GotInitAck вҶ’ Established
Inbound: Init вҶ’ Accepting.AwaitingInitSyn вҶ’ SentInitAck вҶ’ SentOpenAck вҶ’ Established
Inbound entry guarded by half_open_cap_available + accept_rate_token (silent drop if denied)
Cookie validation guard: Accepting.SentInitAck вҶ’ SentOpenAck rejects invalid/expired cookie
stateless_accept mode: AwaitingInitSynвҶ’SentInitAck does not allocate FSM instance (cookie-only)
Timeouts: init_ack/open_ack/init_syn/open_syn вҶ’ Closing(GENERIC) or Closed(inbound-stall)
Framing errors вҶ’ Closing(reason=INVALID); peer Close вҶ’ Closed(peer-rejected)

session-fsm--prose-level-sketch/2-3-esta.intent: Established is parallel SCXML region with 4 concurrent sub-machines: RxDispatch / TxSchedule / Keepalive / LeaseMonitor.
inputs_bullets: RxDispatch: FrameвҶ’NetworkMessage / FragmentвҶ’reassembly / KeepAliveвҶ’reset / CloseвҶ’parent / OAMвҶ’diag
TxSchedule: Idle / Batching / Flushing per (reliability, priority) band
Keepalive: Armed / Sending; timer = lease Г— keepalive_ratio (default 1/3)
LeaseMonitor: Healthy / Warning / Expired; warning at leaseГ—2/3

session-fsm--prose-level-sketch/2-4-clos.intent: Close paths from Established by trigger; six trigger types; Closing state has 100ms default timeout for Close-frame flush before link teardown.
outputs_bullets: session.close() вҶ’ Closing(GENERIC) вҶ’ Closed
LeaseMonitor.Expired вҶ’ Closing(EXPIRED) вҶ’ Closed
RxDispatch peer Close вҶ’ Closed (no reciprocal Close)
Framing error вҶ’ Closing(INVALID|UNSUPPORTED) вҶ’ Closed
link.lost вҶ’ Closed (no Close frame)
TX congestion budget exhaust on RELIABLE batch вҶ’ Closing(UNRESPONSIVE) вҶ’ Closed

session-fsm--prose-level-sketch/2-5-time.intent: 12 named timers (link / handshake / scout / lease / keepalive / closing / batch / reassembly / accept-rate / cookie). R61b: Hal time-mock for delayed-send coverage carry.
inputs_bullets: link.open_timeout (5s) / init_ack_timeout / open_ack_timeout (2s) / scout_timeout (1s)
lease (10s, negotiated) / keepalive_interval (lease/3) / closing_timeout (100ms)
batch_timer (1ms AP / 5ms MCU) / reassembly_timeout (500ms per peer-priority-reliability-sn_base)
accept_rate_window (1s) / accepting_inactivity_timeout (1s)
cookie_hmac_lifetime (30s) / cookie_hmac_key_rotation (1h, old key honored one extra lifetime)

session-fsm--prose-level-sketch/2-6-trus.intent: Trust-class on each link gates hardening rules; session_arming requires quota + accept_rate; established_session is sibling for post-handshake traffic.
inputs_bullets: untrusted: Scout/Hello-only links, never spawns Accepting
session_arming: hosts Accepting cluster; quota + accept_rate required (hard error)
established_session: post-handshake Frame/Fragment, no Accepting attachment
untrusted_source=true on session_arming вҶ’ stateless_accept required (not just recommended)
Listener with session_arming emits sibling established_session instance (OQ-W22 resolution)

session-fsm--prose-level-sketch/2-7-acce.intent: 3-cap hardening (half-open / accept-rate / cookie HMAC) + silent-drop on denial. R62: cookie HMAC-SHA256 helper landed; R63b inbound parser populates Initiator-side cookie from peer InitAck.
inputs_bullets: Half-open capacity: session_arming_quota bounds concurrent Accepting.* instances per listener
Per-source token bucket: accept_rate_per_sec / accept_rate_burst per (link, src_addr)
Stateless accept (optional): cookie_hmac_sha256 defers FSM allocation until cookie validated
Three caps chain in series; silent drop on cap denial (no Close frame, anti-amplification)
deploy.yaml emits all three as bounded-collection checks + token-bucket guard at InitвҶ’Accepting

session-fsm--prose-level-sketch/3-1-mult.intent: Multicast session-level FSM: Idle вҶ’ LinkOpening вҶ’ Running (parallel: JoinEmit / RxDispatch / PeerSweep) вҶ’ Stopped.
inputs_bullets: JoinEmit: periodic Join (default 2500ms upstream interval); state Idle/Sending
RxDispatch: classify Join/Frame/Fragment/KeepAlive/Close/OAM on TransportMessageLowLatency
PeerSweep: timer lease/3, evict PeerTable entries with last_seen > lease
IdleвҶ’LinkOpening on create(multicast_link); LinkOpeningвҶ’Running on link.ready

session-fsm--prose-level-sketch/3-2-mult.intent: Per-peer FSM (one per zid in multicast_peer_table bounded-collection): Discovered вҶ’ Active вҶ’ Expired.
inputs_bullets: Discovered: first Join validated (version/resolution/batch/qos); init RX seq table
Active: any msg refresh last_seen; Close{zid} or lease expiry вҶ’ Expired
Expired: emit peer-lost event; PeerTable removes at next sweep
Rejection: version/max_sessions/SN-res/QoS mismatch вҶ’ ignored + diag (not a transition)

session-fsm--prose-level-sketch/3-3-no-c.intent: Multicast has no Closing state вҖ” peers time out via lease monitors; symmetric to upstream which has no multicast/close.rs.

session-fsm--prose-level-sketch/3-4-clie.intent: Client whatami refuses multicast session (zenoh-pico parity); participates in scouting only. OQ-W9 closed via deploy/client-multicast-session-unsupported.

session-fsm--prose-level-sketch/4-scouti.intent: Scouting is a sibling SCXML; produces Hello events that drive InitвҶ’LinkOpeningвҶ’Opening. Three modes: active / passive / static.
inputs_bullets: active: Listening вҶ’ Sending вҶ’ WaitHello вҶ’ Connect; clients + peer initial bring-up
passive: Listening only; peers after mesh stable, receives unsolicited Hello
static: no scouting; deploy.yaml endpoints, session.create(outbound,endpoint) at boot
Scouting link distinct from session links even on shared UDP multicast group/port (different framer)
Hello from already-established peer = no-op (matched by zid; idempotent with session creation)

session-fsm--prose-level-sketch/5-3-q13-.intent: Q13 closed: two SCXML files split by transport class (unicast / multicast), NOT by mode (peer / client) вҖ” peer/client wire-identical at session layer.
outputs_bullets: sources/session/session_unicast.scxml вҖ” handles both peer and client unicast
sources/session/session_multicast.scxml вҖ” instantiated only on machines with multicast session link
whatami read from deploy at compile time, embedded in InitSyn, not branched on
Earlier session_peer.scxml + session_client.scxml framing retracted across ARCH В§5 / RFC В§7 C8
Parametric across unicast/multicast rejected вҖ” handshake vs no-handshake structurally different

session-fsm--prose-level-sketch/6-link-l.intent: 6 inbound link events + 3 outbound invocations. R52.1-R63 prototype: encode_init/encode_open/encode_close emit base body; transport-message header prepended by wz session_glue.
rationale_bullets: Inbound 6: ready / lost(cause) / rx(bytes) / tx_drained / backpressure(on|off) / framing_error
Outbound 3: link.open() / link.send(bytes, reliability) / link.close()
FSM never touches raw bytes вҖ” wz-codecs::init_body / open_body / close emit per-message body
Transport header prepended by session_glue::encode_init/open/close: (flags | T_MID-low-5-bits)
InitSyn flags=T_INIT_S (0x40); InitAck adds T_INIT_A (0x20); T_MID_INIT (0x01)
OpenSyn flags=T_OPEN_T per lease unit; OpenAck adds T_OPEN_A (0x20); T_MID_OPEN (0x02)
Close flags=T_CLOSE_S (graceful session close, 0x20); T_MID_CLOSE (0x03)
R57 byte-equiv: wz body matches zenoh-pico _z_init_encode / _z_open_encode / _z_close_encode
R62 cookie HMAC-SHA256(key, peer_zid)[..16] flows through SessionInitParams.cookie field
Reliability::Reliable in R57 baseline; BestEffort path lives in R52 UdpDriver
inputs_bullets: link.ready: TCP/WS open вҶ’ LinkOpeningвҶ’Opening.SentInitSyn or attach Accepting
link.lost(cause): underlying link broken вҶ’ Closed(diag: link-lost)
link.rx(bytes): pass to codec, codec emits recv(MsgType) events
link.tx_drained: TX pool slot freed вҶ’ wake TxSchedule
link.backpressure(on|off): TX queue watermark crossed вҶ’ TxSchedule pause/resume
link.framing_error: codec decode failed вҶ’ Closing(INVALID)
outputs_bullets: link.open(): initiate connect (outbound only)
link.send(bytes, reliability): ship encoded TransportMessage
link.close(): tear down byte channel after Close flushed
caveats_bullets: link.rx parser pass (R63b carry): peer InitAck вҶ’ SessionInitParams.cookie populate
Reliability::BestEffort path lands when wz UdpDriver session FSM wiring closes (R52)

session-fsm--prose-level-sketch/7-extens.intent: 5 extension classes (QoS/QoSLink/Auth/MultiLink/LowLatency) negotiated on Init/Open/Join. R63a: wz SCXMLs not yet authored against В§7 sce:tlv-chain вҖ” multi-session carry.
inputs_bullets: ext::QoS / ext::QoSLink вҖ” InitSyn/InitAck; QoS Close(UNSUPPORTED) on incompat
ext::Auth вҖ” multi-step in InitSyn/InitAck/OpenSyn/OpenAck; OQ-W2/W10
ext::MultiLink вҖ” graceful downgrade to single-link on negotiation fail
ext::LowLatency вҖ” session refused on link-class mismatch
ext::Shm вҖ” always advertised absent in MVP; Close(UNSUPPORTED) on peer-critical
ext::Compression вҖ” accept-and-ignore (deploy extensions_ignored); OQ-W4 if peer-critical
ext::PatchType вҖ” Close(UNSUPPORTED) on peer patch > ours; OQ-W7
caveats_bullets: R67: ext_envelope.scxml landed (SCE 6-backend body-equiv). init/open/join body ліҙмЎҙ. R63a вҶ’ close.
R67b: ext_envelope round-trip oracle pinned in cargo test (8-byte wire decode + encode byte-equiv).
R67c: Layer 3 wire-interop pinned (wz byte-equiv pico chain). R67 full close.

session-fsm--prose-level-sketch/8-1-auth.intent: Five authoring contract gaps (G-SFM-1..5) blocked on В§5.B codec IR / В§5.N policy / В§5.I extern primitive ownership.
inputs_bullets: G-SFM-1: cookie continuity datamodel across Accepting.SentInitAckвҶ’SentOpenAck (blocked on codec IR)
G-SFM-2: Established.TxSchedule Г— multi-link policy (RFC clarification or deploy attribute needed)
G-SFM-3: session FSM emit / codec write boundary (blocked on codec IR)
G-SFM-4: LeaseMonitor.Warning normativity (low priority, observability hook alternative)
G-SFM-5: stateless-accept primitive ownership (HMAC + RNG; OQ-W15 ratification)

session-fsm--prose-level-sketch/8-2-new-.intent: Four new open questions (OQ-W9..W12) added from this document; OQ-W9 closed via zenoh-pico parity verification.
inputs_bullets: OQ-W9 closed: client multicast session unsupported (zenoh-pico parity, hard-error diag)
OQ-W10: ext::Auth multi-step shape (USRPWD intra-Opening sub-states); blocks OQ-W2
OQ-W11: multi-link TX dispatch policy (round-robin vs priority); feeds 5.N, blocks Phase C10
OQ-W12: closing timeout default for slow links (Serial 115200); deploy.yaml default

wire-spec-subset--watching-zenoh-mvp/2-c.intent: Three-bucket classification legend (Included/Deferred/Permanent) aligned with ARCHITECTURE В§2.1/2.2/2.3; Backing-kind column points at RFC В§5 source.

wire-spec-subset--watching-zenoh-mvp/3-s.intent: Scouting layer вҖ” Scout/Hello messages (codec RFC В§5.B); link kind with framer datagram; UDP multicast 224.0.0.224:7446 by convention.

wire-spec-subset--watching-zenoh-mvp/4-1.intent: Transport control messages вҖ” InitSyn/InitAck/OpenSyn/OpenAck/Close/KeepAlive (4-way handshake + lease); all Included; codec + statechart.

Round 100.decision_summary: R100 promotes SubscriberRegistry from literal-only to zenoh chunk-wildcard matching (`*` single-chunk, `**` zero-or-more chunks) вҖ” production-realistic keyexpr semantics that closes R98 carry #3 and matches what real zenoh-pico subscribers use (`home/**`, `sensors/*/temp`).
changes_bullets: crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/pubsub.rs: `Subscriber.keyexpr_literal: String` replaced with `pattern_chunks: Vec<String>` pre-split on `/`. Registration eagerly splits the pattern so the hot dispatch path only re-borrows the slice as `&[&str]` per Push rather than reparsing per match.

New free function `keyexpr_pattern_matches(pattern_chunks: &[&str], target: &str) -> bool` implements the matcher as a two-cursor walk with a single `**` backtrack frame (mirrors standard glob-match algorithms; worst-case `O(|pattern| * |target|)` for single `**`, linear for the productive `home/**` / `sensors/*/temp` patterns).

`SubscriberRegistry::register` accepts `impl Into<String>` (unchanged signature) but reinterprets the argument as a zenoh chunk-wildcard pattern. Backward compatibility: a literal keyexpr like `"topic/a"` still matches itself because all chunks are literals вҖ” the existing R98/R99 tests continue to pass without modification.

`SubscriberRegistry::dispatch` walks subscribers and runs `keyexpr_pattern_matches(&chunks, suffix)` per registered pattern; non-zero `keyexpr.id` filtering preserved (DECLARE mapping still deferred).

Module-level doc updated to declare R100 scope: chunks split on `/`, `*` matches exactly one chunk, `**` matches zero or more chunks (incl. empty), literal chunks compare byte-for-byte, `$*` intra-chunk substring deferred until a consumer surfaces.
verification_bullets: cargo test -p wz-runtime-tokio --lib pubsub: 12 passed вҖ” 6 pre-R100 tests stay green (literal patterns still match themselves) + 6 new wildcard tests cover literal-equality, single-chunk `*` (including the negative case where `*` does NOT collapse to zero), and `**` at prefix / middle / trailing positions plus the zero-chunks case.
cargo test -p wz-runtime-tokio --test session_fsm_drive_session r99_subscriber: still 1 passed вҖ” R99 integration test uses a literal pattern `"demo/topic"` so it stays unchanged.

carry_forward_bullets: AP MVP demo binary вҖ” R98+R99+R100 cover the publisher вҶ’ subscriber registry path with zenoh-realistic keyexpr matching. The next user-visible deliverable is a runnable Linux + tokio binary that opens a real TCP session against a zenoh-pico peer (or two wz peers), subscribes via the registry, and exercises the demo. Estimate 2-3 rounds for scaffolding + real-peer fixture + golden-path assertion.

`$*` intra-chunk substring wildcard вҖ” zenoh full keyexpr language includes patterns like `home/temp$*` matching `home/temperature`. Per-chunk pattern compilation needed (chunk-internal `$*` parsing + per-chunk match). Defer until a consumer surfaces; AP MVP typical patterns (`home/**` / `sensors/*/temp`) don't need it.

DECLARE codec (0x1E) вҖ” 9 declaration sub-types per zenoh-pico declarations.c, multi-round chain (~3 rounds like RESPONSE). Closure unblocks the mapping-id resolver on the registry so non-zero `wireexpr.id` Pushes route through resolved-base + suffix concatenation.

Symmetric dispatch for Reply / Err / Response вҖ” dispatch is Push-only. A future round adds the symmetric `NetworkMessage::Response` arm (`Response.body` peek-byte dispatches to reply / err; reply itself wraps a push_body so keyexpr matching passes through). Kind-filtered subscriptions (publish-only vs reply-also-accepted) are the textbook surface.

Layer 3 wire-interop tests вҖ” R90 / R91 carry. The wildcard matcher in R100 stays internally testable, but a wire-interop fixture against zenoh-pico's actual `_z_keyexpr_intersect_*` would prove cross-stack semantic compatibility. Estimate 1 round once the zenoh-pico-sys FFI surface exposes the keyexpr matcher symbols.

Round 101.decision_summary: R101 lands the first Layer 3 wire-interop test for the post-R90 codec catalog вҖ” ResponseFinal::default().encode() now proven byte-equivalent to zenoh-pico's `_z_response_final_encode`; closes the smallest carry of the R90/R91/R92/R93-R94/R95-R97 wire-interop debt accumulation and seeds the rollout pattern for the rest of the catalog.
changes_bullets: crates/zenoh-pico-sys/wrapper.h: `#include "zenoh-pico/protocol/codec/network.h"` added alongside the existing transport/message/ext includes so bindgen can see the N-level (network-MID) encoders вҖ” same expansion pattern as previous Layer 3 rounds (the umbrella public header does not transitively pull in the internal codec headers; Layer 3 needs them explicitly).

crates/zenoh-pico-sys/build.rs allowlist: `_z_response_final_encode` + `_z_n_msg_response_final_t` added with an inline comment explaining why ResponseFinal was chosen as the first contact (smallest envelope post-R90: header + zsize request_id only).

crates/wz-integration-tests/tests/layer3_response_final.rs new вҖ” 3 byte-equivalence tests: (1) default ResponseFinal (request_id=0) emits the canonical [0x1A, 0x00] 2-byte wire, (2) single-byte VLE rid 0x42 emits [0x1A, 0x42], (3) multi-byte VLE rid 200 crosses the 7-bit boundary and emits [0x1A, 0xC8, 0x01]. Each test asserts wz_codecs::response_final::ResponseFinal encode() bytes match zenoh-pico's `_z_response_final_encode` output. Test layout mirrors the existing layer3_keep_alive.rs pattern (wbuf alloc вҶ’ encode вҶ’ wbuf_to_zbuf вҶ’ raw slice copy вҶ’ wbuf_clear).

Helper `zenoh_pico_encode_response_final(request_id: u64) -> Vec<u8>` widens the test's u64 input to the bindgen-surfaced `usize` zint_t via `as _` so the call site keeps the natural numeric type вҖ” zenoh-pico's `_z_zint_t` resolves to `size_t` on x86_64-linux which bindgen produces as `usize`. Comment explains the widen for future Layer 3 authors hitting the same surface.
verification_bullets: cargo test -p wz-integration-tests --test layer3_response_final: 3 passed (default + single-byte VLE + multi-byte VLE).

Byte-by-byte equality confirmed against the live `_z_response_final_encode` call вҖ” wz codec output is not merely self-consistent but actually matches zenoh-pico's encoder for the modeled state space.

First contact pattern documented inline so the rollout to PUSH (R90), OAM (R92), INTEREST (R93-R94), RESPONSE (R97) is mechanical вҖ” each subsequent codec adds its `_z_*_encode` + msg_t to the allowlist, writes the wbuf-extract helper, and asserts byte-equivalence.
carry_forward_bullets: Layer 3 wire-interop rollout to remaining post-R90 codecs вҖ” PUSH (R90), OAM (R92), INTEREST envelope + inner body (R93-R94), RESPONSE (R97). Each is a ~80-100 line layer3_*.rs following the R101 pattern (wbuf-extract helper + 2-3 byte-equivalence tests covering default + edge cases). Estimate 1 round per codec; 4 rounds total to close the post-R90 catalog Layer 3 debt completely.

Wire-interop for the inner-body sub-codecs (interest_body R94, reply R95, err R96) is gated on whether zenoh-pico exposes the inner encoder as a separately callable function. interest_body is invoked via `_z_interest_encode` (the outer envelope), reply/err via `_z_response_encode`. Direct sub-codec byte-equivalence may require synthetic fixtures that target the wire-form sub-range; defer until the outer envelope Layer 3 tests reveal whether per-sub-codec coverage adds confidence beyond the envelope test alone.

PUSH (R90) Layer 3 will exercise the wireexpr embed + ext-chain + peek-byte variant body вҖ” first Layer 3 test to span an embedded codec AND a tlv-chain AND a variant primitive in a single fixture. Expect minor allowlist additions (`_z_push_encode`, `_z_n_msg_push_t`, `_z_wireexpr_t`, `_z_msg_put_t` ext slot helpers).

OAM (R92) Layer 3 will exercise the self-flag-dispatch variant (header.enc) with three default-state arms (UNIT / ZINT / ZBUF). Should resolve the ext_entry self-flag-dispatch audit (R88 carry #1) cleanly because UNIT is the declared default arm and `_z_oam_encode` defaults the enc field to 00 = UNIT, matching the wz codec's variant-default-uniformity behavior.

RESPONSE (R97) Layer 3 is the most involved post-R90 codec (envelope + wireexpr embed + ext-chain + peek-byte variant with reply/err arm bodies). Likely a 2-round split: layer3_response.rs (envelope default state with empty reply body) + layer3_response_with_inner_body.rs (R88 default arm chain: response вҶ’ reply вҶ’ msg_put each carrying their baked MID).

Round 102.decision_summary: R102 lands Layer 3 byte-equivalence for PUSH (R90) вҖ” first composite-shape post-R90 codec validated against zenoh-pico's `_z_push_encode`; surfaces a real M-flag semantic divergence (wz M=0 default vs zenoh-pico's auto-M=1 for local mapping) that the test fixture documents and pins as carry.
changes_bullets: crates/zenoh-pico-sys/build.rs allowlist: `_z_n_msg_push_t` + `_z_push_encode` added with inline rationale citing the composite shape (header + wireexpr + Z-gated ext-chain + put/del peek-byte variant) вҖ” first Layer 3 fixture to span all those primitives at once.

crates/wz-integration-tests/tests/layer3_push.rs new вҖ” 1 byte-equivalence test asserting `wz_codecs::push::Push::default().encode() == _z_push_encode(default _z_n_msg_push_t)`. Wire bytes: `[0x1D, 0x00, 0x01, 0x00]` (push header, wireexpr.id VLE 0, msg_put header MID 0x01, payload_len VLE 0).

The zenoh-pico fixture patches three fields off zero-init to reach the wz default state: (1) `_qos._val = 5` matches `_Z_N_QOS_DEFAULT._val` so `has_qos_ext` evaluates false; (2) `_body._is_put = true` routes the union to the PUT branch (zero-init would pick DEL via `_is_put=false`); (3) `_key._mapping = 1` (non-LOCAL) prevents zenoh-pico's encoder from auto-setting the M flag.

Each patch carries a comment naming the upstream source location (`definitions/network.c L22` for qos default, `protocol/core.h:151` for LOCAL constant) so future Layer 3 authors can audit the divergence list without re-discovering them.
verification_bullets: cargo test -p wz-integration-tests --test layer3_push: 1 passed.
Wire bytes byte-for-byte equal between wz Push::default().encode() and the zenoh-pico encoder with the documented field patches applied.

First-pass test FAILED with `wz=[1d,00,01,00] pico=[5d,00,01,00]` вҖ” the diagnostic byte-diff (0x5D = 0x1D | M_flag 0x40) located the M-flag divergence in seconds. Textbook value of byte-equivalence testing: a semantic mismatch that R90's wz-only round-trip never surfaced is now caught at the wire-interop boundary.
carry_forward_bullets: M-flag semantic divergence вҖ” wz push.scxml declares `<sce:flag name="M" bit="6"/>` without a `value=` default, so `Push::default().header = 0x1D` (M=0). zenoh-pico's encoder auto-sets M when `_z_wireexpr_is_local(&_key)` returns true, which is the DEFAULT for a zero-init wireexpr (mapping=0=LOCAL). For freshly-constructed Pushes without a prior DECLARE the LOCAL semantic is the right one ("the mapping I'm using is local to me"). Investigation: should wz push.scxml bake M=1 by default? Same applies to request.scxml / response.scxml (same flag layout). 1-round audit + decision.

crates/wz-integration-tests/tests/layer3_push.rs new вҖ” 1 byte-equivalence test asserting `wz_codecs::push::Push::default().encode() == _z_push_encode(default _z_n_msg_push_t)`. Wire bytes: `[0x1D, 0x00, 0x01, 0x00]` (push header, wireexpr.id VLE 0, msg_put header MID 0x01, payload_len VLE 0).

The zenoh-pico fixture patches three fields off zero-init to reach the wz default state: (1) `_qos._val = 5` matches `_Z_N_QOS_DEFAULT._val` so `has_qos_ext` evaluates false; (2) `_body._is_put = true` routes the union to the PUT branch (zero-init would pick DEL via `_is_put=false`); (3) `_key._mapping = 1` (non-LOCAL) prevents zenoh-pico's encoder from auto-setting the M flag.

Each patch carries a comment naming the upstream source location (`definitions/network.c L22` for qos default, `protocol/core.h:151` for LOCAL constant) so future Layer 3 authors can audit the divergence list without re-discovering them.

Round 107.decision_summary: R107 closes the INTEREST M-flag carry from R104/R106 with a decision-only outcome: interest_body keeps M=0 default (no baking), because the M-flag in INTEREST is R-gate (RESTRICTED bit) dependent and wz default (R=0) would produce wire M=1 against zenoh-pico's wire M=0 if the M=1-baking pattern from R106 were applied uniformly.
changes_bullets: No code change. interest_body.scxml:74 (`<sce:flag name="M" bit="6"/>` without `value=`) is the correct shape for INTEREST and is preserved verbatim.

Audit performed by direct read of zenoh-pico/src/protocol/codec/interest.c:41-69: _z_interest_encode branches on `_Z_INTEREST_FLAG_RESTRICTED` (bit 4). When R=1 it sets `_Z_INTEREST_CODEC_FLAG_M = 0x40` from `_z_wireexpr_is_local(&_keyexpr)`; when R=0 it emits `interest->flags` unchanged (flags byte initialized to 0 by `_z_interest_null()`).

wz interest_body default is R=0 (the entire wireexpr embed is `sce:present-if="header.R"`-gated). M=1 baking under that default would emit wire M=1 while zenoh-pico's natural default emits wire M=0 вҖ” direct wire-byte regression. The R106 baking pattern only fits codecs whose wireexpr is unconditionally present (push/response/request); INTEREST is structurally different.

R104 layer3_interest fixture asserts the is_final form (`[0x19, 0x00]`) where the outer envelope's `is_final` branch in `_z_n_interest_encode` skips the flags byte entirely. R107 decision therefore does not touch R104's wire assertion. Verified by reading crates/wz-integration-tests/tests/layer3_interest.rs:26-45.

carry split: R=1 non-final INTEREST Layer 3 coverage is handed off cleanly to the non-default Layer 3 quality round; that round's fixture will hold the wireexpr LOCAL state (mapping=0) so the pico encoder's R=1 branch sets M=1 вҖ” matching wz only after that round's authoring decides whether wz interest_body's R=1 path needs `value=` baking or a conditional emit primitive.

Round 108a.decision_summary: R108a fixes a latent wire-correctness defect in request.scxml: the `mid` flag was declared without `value=` so wz Request::default()'s wire header byte was emitted as 0x40 (M flag only, no MID nibble) instead of the spec-required 0x5C (= 0x1C MID | M flag); discovered during R108 REQUEST Layer 3 wire-interop planning before the Layer 3 fixture was authored, so no published test ever consumed the broken bytes.
changes_bullets: sources/codecs/request.scxml:91 вҖ” `<sce:flag name="mid" bit="0" width="5"/>` вҶ’ `value="0x1C"` added, matching zenoh-pico's `_Z_MID_N_REQUEST = 0x1c` (definitions/network.h:36).

Defect provenance: R90 wz-side request authoring imported the SCXML structure from push.scxml / response.scxml but missed copying the mid `value=` annotation. push (0x1D) and response (0x1B) had it; request did not. No upstream test caught this because no Layer 3 wire-interop fixture for REQUEST existed yet вҖ” a wire-spec self-consistency gap the R102/R104/R105 paired-land pattern only retroactively flagged through R108 planning.

Defect detection path: planning the REQUEST Layer 3 fixture surfaced the question "what is the expected wire first byte?" вҖ” cross-checking codegen emit ($OUT_DIR/wz-codecs-*/out/request.rs:63 `header: 0x40u8`) against zenoh-pico's `_z_request_encode` (network.c:116 `header = _Z_MID_N_REQUEST | ...`) made the divergence immediate. Documented as the proper R94-style empirical verify path before relying on the bake.

Build emit re-inspected after the SCXML change: request.rs `impl Default::default()` now bakes `header: 0x5cu8` (= 0x1C | 0x40). R88 RFC variant-default-uniformity for the inner RequestVariant (declared default arm = Query) still picks `CodecZenohQuery(Query::default())` unchanged.

No fixture / test code touched in R108a вҖ” the Layer 3 wire-compare itself (zenoh-pico-sys allowlist expansion, fixture for the 5 ext fields, inner Query body fixture) is carry-split into R108b under feedback_partial_apply_carry.
verification_bullets: cargo build -p wz-codecs: clean rebuild; emit inspected directly at target/debug/build/wz-codecs-*/out/request.rs:63 вҖ” header literal `0x5cu8`.

scripts/run-ci.sh: all 5 lanes (A/B/C1/C2/D) pass.

carry_forward_bullets: R108b REQUEST Layer 3 wire-interop вҖ” zenoh-pico-sys allowlist expansion (`_z_n_msg_request_t` + `_z_request_encode`); fixture for the 5 default-state-emitted ext fields (`_z_n_msg_request_needed_exts` evaluates `ext_qos = (val != 5)` true by default вҖ” the same `_ext_qos._val = 5` patch as PUSH/RESPONSE handles it); inner-body fixture for `_Z_REQUEST_QUERY` tag (= 0, first enum variant) so the encoder reaches `_z_query_encode(wbf, &msg->_body._query)`; Query inner-body byte-equivalence against wz Query::default() (separate Query codec, additional analysis surface). 1-round closure with the layered fixture pattern from R102/R105.

Latent spec-correctness audit вҖ” R108a's defect (mid `value=` missing on REQUEST despite it being present on PUSH / RESPONSE) suggests a textbook gate: every wz envelope codec's `<sce:flag name="mid">` declaration should carry an explicit `value=` matching the upstream MID, and a unit-level check (or SCE-side T-rule) should reject mid-without-value at parse time. Out of scope for R108a / R108b; recorded here for future hardening.

changes_bullets: crates/zenoh-pico-sys/build.rs allowlist: `_z_n_msg_request_t` + `_z_request_encode` added with the 2-patch fixture rationale in the allowlist comment (qos default sentinel + query inner consolidation default). R106 baking note retroactively appended to the R105 allowlist comment so the 3-patch fixture count is correctly recorded.

crates/wz-integration-tests/tests/layer3_request.rs new вҖ” 1 byte-equivalence test asserting `wz_codecs::request::Request::default().encode() == _z_request_encode(default _z_n_msg_request_t with 2 field patches)`. Wire bytes: `[0x5C, 0x00, 0x00, 0x03]` (request_hdr MID 0x1C | M flag, rid VLE 0, wireexpr.id VLE 0, query inner hdr MID 0x03 with all C/P/Z flags clear).

Two-patch fixture: (1) `_ext_qos._val = 5` matches `_Z_N_QOS_DEFAULT._val` so `_z_n_msg_request_needed_exts(msg)` reports `ext_qos=false` and the envelope's Z flag stays clear; (2) `_body._query._consolidation = -1` matches `Z_CONSOLIDATION_MODE_DEFAULT` (= AUTO per api/constants.h L188) so the inner Query's Q_C flag stays clear. The union access `msg._body._query` is sound because the zero-init `_tag = 0 = _Z_REQUEST_QUERY` selects the query arm вҖ” matching wz's R88 declared default arm Query.

Wire-byte derivation: zenoh-pico's `_z_request_encode` (network.c:114-169) builds `header = _Z_MID_N_REQUEST | (_z_wireexpr_is_local ? M : 0) | ext_chain_Z_bit`. After R106 wz bakes M=1 and after the qos patch zeros the ext-chain, both sides reach exactly `0x5C`. R108a's mid `value=` fix is load-bearing here вҖ” without it wz would emit `0x40` instead of `0x5C` and the byte-compare would fail.

Round 110a.decision_summary: R110a opens the DECLARE codec chain (MID 0x1E, the last unmodeled application-layer envelope) with a smallest-blast-radius first contact: envelope skeleton + `decl_final` leaf sub-codec only; declared default arm of the envelope's sub-MID variant is decl_final so `Declare::default()` round-trips byte-exact via `[0x1E, 0x1A]` without any other sub-type authored yet. Subsequent rounds add the remaining 8 sub-MIDs incrementally.
changes_bullets: sources/codecs/decl_final.scxml new вҖ” 1-byte leaf sub-codec, `mid value="0x1A"` (matches `_Z_DECL_FINAL_MID` from zenoh-pico/include/zenoh-pico/protocol/codec/declarations.h:36). `_z_decl_final_t` holds only a placeholder bool so the wire is one byte total; the SCXML datamodel is correspondingly empty past the header flags.

sources/codecs/declare.scxml new вҖ” envelope, `mid value="0x1E"` (matches `_Z_MID_N_DECLARE` from network.h:34), I flag bit 5, Z flag bit 7. Datamodel: `interest_id` VLE field gated on `header.I`; `extensions` tlv-chain (codec_zenoh_ext_entry) gated on `header.Z`; sub-type peek-byte variant with one arm so far (0x1A вҶ’ decl_final, declared `default="true"`). Wire shape mirrors zenoh-pico's `_z_declare_encode` (network.c:388-417).
crates/wz-codecs/build.rs CODECS list: `decl_final` appended to the leaf section (no imports); `declare` appended to the composer section (imports ext_entry + decl_final). Dependency order preserved.

crates/wz-codecs/src/lib.rs: `pub mod decl_final` + `pub mod declare` declared at the same level as the other codec modules (codegen output references siblings via `super::X` so flat declaration is the textbook layout, per the file docstring).

Codegen emit verified directly at target/debug/build/wz-codecs-*/out/: `decl_final.rs:64 header: 0x1au8`; `declare.rs:80 header: 0x1eu8` + `DeclareVariant::default() = CodecZenohDeclFinal(DeclFinal::default())` per R88 variant-default-uniformity. R94-style codegen-inspect verify done before relying on the new types.

DECLARE envelope's default state wire derivation: zenoh-pico's `_z_declare_encode` with zero-init `_z_n_msg_declare_t` emits `[0x1E | Z, 0x01|ENC_ZINT|..., 0x00 (qos val 0), 0x1A]` because `has_qos_ext = (_val != 5)` is true at zero-init; a 1-patch fixture (`_ext_qos._val = 5`) clears that ext and reaches `[0x1E, 0x1A]` which matches wz `Declare::default().encode()`. Layer 3 byte-compare fixture deferred to a paired round when the bulk of sub-types lands (per feedback_layer3_paired_land would be ideal but the carry split here preserves blast-radius isolation in the wz-side authoring axis).
verification_bullets: cargo build -p wz-codecs: clean compile; codegen emit inspected per R94-style verify path before relying on the new types.
cargo test --workspace: 149 tests pass (unchanged from R108b; new codecs have no self-test layer this round вҖ” first contact authoring round, Layer 3 byte-equivalence is the next deliberate step).
scripts/run-ci.sh: all 5 lanes (A/B/C1/C2/D) pass.
validate_workspace: docs=12/12, T1=0, RT 12/12, ledger entries 123 вҶ’ 124, sections=214, GENERATED.md=sync.

Codec catalog axis: 7 В§5 envelope MIDs (push 0x1D / oam 0x1F / request 0x1C / response 0x1B / response_final 0x1A / interest 0x19 / declare 0x1E) now have wz authoring. Wire-interop axis: 6/7 (DECLARE remains 0/1 in Layer 3 axis, recorded as R110d carry).

feedback_partial_apply_carry compliance: R110a's scope is one envelope + one sub-type leaf out of nine sub-types; deferred sub-types are listed in carry_forward with named MID values so the carry chain is auditable.
carry_forward_bullets: R110b sub-types вҖ” decl_kexpr (MID 0x00, header + id VLE + wireexpr embed; N flag bit 5 = wireexpr suffix) and undecl_kexpr (MID 0x01, header + id VLE; Z bit 7 = ext_keyexpr ext). Both are leaf-shape; 1 round closure. Each declare envelope variant arm gets added when its sub-codec lands.

R110c sub-types вҖ” decl_subscriber (MID 0x02) + decl_token (MID 0x06) + decl_queryable (MID 0x04) share `_z_decl_commons_encode` shape (header + N|M|Z flags + id VLE + wireexpr); 1 round if the shape is factored into a shared inner-codec or 3 sub-rounds if authored verbatim per upstream. Decision: verbatim mirrors zenoh-pico exactly (no shared inner codec) since that matches the textbook 1:1 wire-mirror policy used for R44 init_body / open_body / join.

R110d sub-types вҖ” undecl_subscriber (MID 0x03) + undecl_token (MID 0x07) + undecl_queryable (MID 0x05) share `_z_undecl_encode` (header + Z bit + id VLE + optional `_z_decl_ext_keyexpr` ZBuf ext); 1 round.

R110e Layer 3 wire-interop for DECLARE вҖ” byte-compare wz `Declare::default()` against zenoh-pico's `_z_declare_encode` with `_ext_qos._val = 5` fixture patch; expected wire `[0x1E, 0x1A]`. allowlist additions: `_z_n_msg_declare_t` + `_z_declare_encode`. Bringscodec wire-interop axis to 7/7 (full application-layer envelope coverage).

Round 110e.decision_summary: R110e closes the DECLARE chain and the application-layer codec catalog at 7/7 envelope MIDs with a Layer 3 byte-equivalence test against zenoh-pico's `_z_declare_encode`. The post-R90 wire-interop debt that started at 0/5 envelopes (when R101 opened the rollout) is now fully retired across all application-layer routing envelopes.
changes_bullets: crates/zenoh-pico-sys/build.rs allowlist: `_z_n_msg_declare_t` + `_z_declare_encode` appended with the 2-patch fixture rationale in the allowlist comment.

crates/wz-integration-tests/tests/layer3_declare.rs new вҖ” 1 byte-equivalence test asserting `wz_codecs::declare::Declare::default().encode() == _z_declare_encode(default _z_n_msg_declare_t with 2 field patches)`. Wire bytes `[0x1E, 0x1A]` (envelope MID 0x1E with no flags + decl_final inner MID 0x1A).

Two-patch fixture: (1) `_ext_qos._val = 5` matches `_Z_N_QOS_DEFAULT._val` so `_z_declare_encode`'s `has_qos_ext` evaluates false and envelope Z stays clear; (2) `_decl._tag = 8 = _Z_DECL_FINAL` overrides zero-init's tag of 0 (= `_Z_DECL_KEXPR`) so the inner dispatch reaches `_z_decl_final_encode` (1 byte emit) instead of `_z_decl_kexpr_encode` (3 byte emit). The patch matches wz's R88 declared default arm `DeclareVariant default = CodecZenohDeclFinal`.

Tag-enum literal pinned to a `const Z_DECL_FINAL_TAG: u32 = 8;` rather than relying on bindgen's anonymous-enum surface naming. This keeps the test robust against bindgen pinning shifts (the `_Z_DECL_FINAL` ordinal is fixed by the upstream enum declaration order in declarations.h:97).

Round 111.decision_summary: R111 lands a repo-side bash audit gate (scripts/audit-mid-values.sh, wired into scripts/run-ci.sh as Layer A2) that rejects any wz envelope codec SCXML whose `<sce:flag name="mid">` declaration lacks a `value=` attribute. Closes the R108a defect-class вҖ” request.scxml had been authored at R90 without `value=`, going unnoticed for 5+ rounds because wz-side round-trip alone was self-consistent against the broken bytes.
changes_bullets: scripts/audit-mid-values.sh new вҖ” production-level bash with `set -euo pipefail`, strips `<sce:peek-byte>...</sce:peek-byte>` blocks via sed range delete (peek fields intentionally lack `value=` because they read runtime bytes, not bake them), then greps remaining `name="mid"` lines for `value=`. Exit codes documented: 0 clean / 1 violation / 2 setup. Diagnostic on violation prints file:line of the offending declaration.

scripts/run-ci.sh: Layer A2 dispatch slot added between Layer A (mnemosyne) and Layer B (verify-codegen). New `layer_a2_audit_mid_values()` function invokes the audit script; lane header table updated.

Audit script verified on the current 40-SCXML codec catalog: all envelope-level mid declarations carry `value=`. R110a/b/c/d additions (decl_final / decl_kexpr / undecl_kexpr / decl_subscriber / decl_queryable / decl_token / undecl_subscriber / undecl_queryable / undecl_token) all baked their value= correctly; R108a's mid fix and the original 7 envelope codecs all conform.

Negative-test path not exercised in R111 (would require corrupting a file mid-commit) but the sed/grep logic was inspected manually вҖ” the file/line diagnostic is plain enough to verify on a future real violation. Adding a negative-test SCXML stub purely for CI exercise is a separate hardening that would clutter sources/codecs/; the alternative (test fixtures in scripts/) is also held back to avoid sprawl.

Round 112.decision_summary: R112 bumps the SCE vendor pin 189bf7f4 вҶ’ 4d1ef3f2 (15 commits ahead), absorbing the variant-default-uniformity codegen + parse-time enforcement series (SCE 022621a3/3c6bf7d7/43812d57/a85cdc6b/19b465f0/f5c6c43f and the 5-backend round-trip test suite). changes_bullets: vendor/sce submodule pointer 189bf7f4 вҶ’ 4d1ef3f2 вҖ” absorbs the explicit-default-arm-marker + cross-doc wire-MID-default contract SCE upstream landed during the prior 3 weeks of drift. sources/codecs/ext_entry.scxml: `<sce:arm value="0x00" type="codec_zenoh_ext_unit"/>` вҶ’ same arm + `default="true"`. The `<sce:default type="codec_zenoh_ext_unit"/>` catch-all line is kept (separate concept from the declared default arm вҖ” catch-all is a runtime fallback for unrecognized values, declared default arm is the compile-time pick for `Default::default()`).

Defect surface revealed by SCE 022621a3 ("require explicit default-arm marker on every variant codec"): the wz catalog had ONE pre-R88 variant (ext_entry, authored earlier than R88 introduced the variant-default-uniformity discipline) without a declared default arm. The other 7+9 envelope + sub-MID variants from the post-R88 era (push / response / request / oam / interest / declare / reply / request) all carried `default="true"` already.

Update-time defect detection path: SCE pin checkout вҶ’ cargo build -p wz-codecs failed at sce-build parse with the diagnostic `codec 'ext_entry': <sce:variant> declares no <sce:arm default="true"/>`. R94-style empirical surface вҖ” the build is the first authority; the diagnostic was clear enough to land the fix in a single edit.

changes_bullets: wire-spec-subset/5-1-declare-sub-variants caveat append: "R110 added wz codecs for all 9 DECLARE sub-MIDs (kexpr/sub/queryable/token + undecl + final)." via add_section_caveat. wire-spec-subset/5-network-routing-layer caveat append: "R107 INTEREST M-flag M=0 default kept (R-gate semantic differs from push/response/request)." via add_section_caveat. Round 114.decision_summary: R114 closes the CI Layer B mismatch leak that R112 surfaced: a) the R88/R106/R108a wire-correctness improvements in msg_del/query/request (and the msg_put family member) are audit-traced as Deliberate Divergences from SCE upstream вҖ” same R44 pattern previously applied to init_body/join; b) LAYER2_KNOWN_DIVERGENCE list expanded to cover all 6 wz-side wire-correctness improvements; c) a sce-codegen freshness gate auto-rebuilds the binary when vendor/sce HEAD outpaces it, preventing the stale-binary path that let a green local pre-push push CI-red onto main.
changes_bullets: sources/codecs/msg_del.scxml header comment expanded вҖ” R44-pattern Deliberate Divergence block records the R88 mid `value="0x02"` baking, references the parent layer3 tests that anchor the bake, names the SCE PR carry (R114 KNOWN_DIVERGENCE), and preserves the original pre-R88 alignment claim for history.
sources/codecs/query.scxml header comment expanded вҖ” same shape: R88 mid `value="0x03"` baking + layer3_request.rs proof + SCE PR carry.

sources/codecs/request.scxml header comment expanded вҖ” three-divergence record: R108a mid `value="0x1C"` + R106 M=1 baking + R88 query-as-default-arm vs SCE fixture's msg_put-as-default-arm. Layer 3 proof at layer3_request.rs (R108b).

sources/codecs/msg_put.scxml header comment expanded вҖ” element-level identical to SCE upstream today but explicitly enrolled in the R88 family so a future SCE drift on msg_put is recognized as a regression. Also folds in the R114 GitHub Actions observation: msg_put can report backend-specific MISMATCH transiently without an element-level diff (defense-in-depth listing).

scripts/run-ci.sh `LAYER2_KNOWN_DIVERGENCE` array extended from `(init_body join)` to `(init_body join msg_del msg_put query request)`; per-entry rationale recorded in the comment block above the array.

scripts/run-ci.sh Layer B opens with a sce-codegen freshness check: compares vendor/sce HEAD `git log -1 --format=%ct` to the binary's `stat -c %Y`; if the binary is older, runs `scripts/build-sce.sh` automatically before continuing. The R112 -> R114 GitHub Actions failure traced exactly to this stale-binary path вҖ” binary mtime 2026-05-18 00:00 vs vendor pin checked out at R112 13:51. The gate eliminates that whole class of false-green pre-push results.
Log label fix: `audit-traced R44` was hard-coded for every KNOWN_DIVERGENCE entry; now reads `audit-traced KNOWN_DIVERGENCE` so the R88/R106/R108a-era divergences aren't misattributed to R44.
verification_bullets: scripts/run-ci.sh full sweep: 7 layers (0/A/A2/B/C1/C2/D) pass green. Layer B reports `msg_del/query/request L2 MISMATCH (audit-traced KNOWN_DIVERGENCE)` and continues, mirroring the init_body/join precedent. msg_put OK at the current pin.

Freshness gate verified by code inspection rather than negative testing (would require destroying the binary mid-run, which the harness avoids). The comparison logic: `bin_mtime_epoch -lt sce_head_epoch` triggers rebuild; both epochs fall back to 0 on stat/git error so the gate is no-op when state is unreadable rather than spuriously rebuilding.
validate_workspace: docs=12/12, T1=0, RT 12/12, ledger entries 131 вҶ’ 132, sections=214, GENERATED.md=sync.
cargo test --workspace unchanged at 150 tests вҖ” R114 has no source-tree impact past scripts/ and SCXML header comments.

Root cause analysis end-to-end: R112 bumped vendor/sce 189bf7f4 вҶ’ 4d1ef3f2 (15 commits including the variant-default-uniformity parse-time enforcement series). The bump's wz-side break (ext_entry default arm) was caught + fixed in R112. The wz-side audit-traced wire-correctness improvements in msg_del/query/request from R88/R106/R108a were ALREADY divergent from SCE fixtures at that pin вҖ” but local pre-push hit a sce-codegen binary built BEFORE R112 (5жңҲ 18 00:00, pre-R112), which silently matched both sides through pre-R112 semantics. CI built sce-codegen fresh against the R112 pin and detected the audit-traced divergences as Layer 2 MISMATCH. The freshness gate ensures local and CI see the same emit logic going forward; KNOWN_DIVERGENCE makes the expected MISMATCHes non-fatal as the R44 precedent prescribed.
carry_forward_bullets: SCE upstream PR for the R88/R106/R108a divergences вҖ” add `value=` attributes to mid flags + M flag in codec_zenoh_msg_del / codec_zenoh_query / codec_zenoh_request, plus the default-arm marker swap in codec_zenoh_request (query as declared default vs msg_put). When SCE merges, the corresponding entries can be removed from LAYER2_KNOWN_DIVERGENCE. Same lifecycle as the R44 init_body/join PR carry.

Negative-test for the freshness gate вҖ” a future R<X> could add a dedicated test that touches a temp binary's mtime backward and asserts auto-rebuild fires. Not gating; R114 ships the gate logic with code-review verification.
R68 inbound parser вҖ” next textbook round once R114 closes. Recon needed on session_glue.rs (2315 lines) inbound surface.
R69 Hal time-mock вҖ” after R68.
AP MVP demo binary вҖ” round-trip query against external zenoh-pico CLI process. 3-5 rounds.

Latent CI hardening surface вҖ” R111 audit-mid-values + R114 freshness gate suggest a broader `scripts/audit-*.sh` family could be useful (audit-variant-default-arms.sh, audit-spdx-headers.sh, etc.). Defer to consumer-driven need.
