Round 115.decision_summary: R115 wires DECLARE network MID (0x1E) into parse_frame_payload's dispatch table, closing the inbound gap the R110a-e series left when it shipped the wz-codecs::declare authoring chain and the forward-direction Layer 3 wire-interop without pairing them with a NetworkMessage::Declare variant; the carry-side framing ("R68 inbound parser вҖ” recon needed on session_glue.rs (2315 lines)") was carry-misdiagnosis вҖ” the inbound parser surface (parse_inbound 5 transport MIDs, parse_frame_payload 6 of 7 network MIDs, inbound_to_fsm_event, handle_inbound) had been complete since Rounds 68a-97, and the real gap was the missing 7th network MID dispatch arm.
changes_bullets: crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs adds `pub const N_MID_DECLARE: u8 = 0x1E` to the wire_const module, paired with the doc-comment correction on N_MID_RESPONSE that previously claimed DECLARE was the remaining unauthored MID (stale post-R110e).

session_glue.rs imports wz_codecs::declare::Declare next to the existing 10 wz-codecs envelope codec imports, and adds NetworkMessage::Declare(Box<Declare>) as the 7th typed variant (sizing rationale mirrors Request/Push/Response вҖ” interest_id + ext vec + DeclareVariant inline form is larger than the Unknown variant).

parse_frame_payload gains a wire_const::N_MID_DECLARE => Declare::decode вҶ’ NetworkMessage::Declare arm directly above the catch-all Unknown branch; the manual Debug impl on NetworkMessage gains a matching Declare(_) вҶ’ "Declare(..)" formatter arm so DriverLoopOutcome's #[derive(Debug)] keeps compiling.

crates/wz-integration-tests/tests/layer3_inbound_declare.rs (NEW, 3 tests): a) default DECLARE wire bytes from Declare::default().encode() (the same bytes R110e proved pico-equivalent) round-trip through parse_frame_payload вҶ’ NetworkMessage::Declare with byte-identical re-encode; b) 3-record batch Push :: Declare :: ResponseFinal verifies the Declare dispatch advances the cursor cleanly so the tail records still dispatch correctly; c) truncated 1-byte envelope (envelope header only, no inner declaration body) surfaces the codec NeedMoreBytes error verbatim through parse_frame_payload.

Two pre-existing Unknown-MID coverage tests in crates/wz-runtime-tokio/tests/ вҖ” session_fsm_driver_loop.rs::r74_rx_frame_unknown_network_mid_absorbs_as_unknown and session_fsm_inbound_dispatch.rs::parse_frame_payload_unknown_mid_absorbs_remainder + parse_frame_payload_decodes_request_then_unknown_chain вҖ” switched their synthetic Unknown MID fixture from 0x1E (DECLARE вҖ” now typed) to 0x00 (outside the {0x19..0x1F} authored set) and added the NetworkMessage::Declare(_) arm to the panic clause so the match stays exhaustive. The first test's authoring comment explicitly flagged this refactor obligation when DECLARE landed; the R115 land discharges it.
verification_bullets: cargo test --workspace passes 153 / 0 / 1 (+3 from R115's layer3_inbound_declare.rs; baseline was 150 per the R114 carry snapshot).
cargo test -p wz-integration-tests --test layer3_inbound_declare passes all 3 R115 cases (round-trip / 3-record batch / truncated reject).

scripts/run-ci.sh full 6-lane (Layer 0 + A + A2 + B + C1 + C2 + D) passes locally; Layer C2 specifically green confirms the test-side match-arm refactors close the non-exhaustive-match clippy reject that the bare NetworkMessage::Declare add would otherwise have produced.

validate_workspace confirms T1=0 / RT=12/12 / GENERATED.md=sync вҖ” the R115 changes are entirely Rust source + integration tests; no doc surface mutated, so the atomic ledger entries / sections delta is exactly +1 / 0 from this entry.
carry_forward_bullets: The carry-misdiagnosis pattern (a prior session writing `R68 inbound parser, recon needed on session_glue.rs (2315 lines)` as the next round without inspecting the file, when the inbound parser surface was already complete and the actual gap was a one-arm dispatch wire-up) is worth a memory entry next session start; the textbook lesson is `feedback_diagnose_before_carry` in MEMORY.md applied at the round-claim layer, not just the build-verify layer.

R69 Hal time-mock remains the next textbook round per the R114 carry's R68вҶ’R69вҶ’AP-MVP sequencing. TestHal struct + logical-time advance + Engine::tick coverage of the 12 timers per session-fsm В§2.5 (link.open_timeout / init_ack_timeout / open_ack_timeout / scout_timeout / lease / keepalive_interval / closing_timeout / batch_timer / reassembly_timeout / accept_rate_window / accepting_inactivity_timeout / cookie_hmac_lifetime).
SCE upstream PR for the R88/R106/R108a divergences вҖ” unchanged from R114 carry (init_body / join / msg_del / query / request 5 codecs).
Negative-test for the R114 freshness gate вҖ” unchanged carry.
AP MVP demo binary вҖ” round-trip query against external zenoh-pico CLI process; arrives after R69 lands the time-mock substrate the binary's deterministic-replay test path will lean on.

crates/wz-integration-tests/tests/layer3_inbound_declare.rs (NEW, 3 tests): a) default DECLARE wire bytes from Declare::default().encode() (the same bytes R110e proved pico-equivalent) round-trip through parse_frame_payload вҶ’ NetworkMessage::Declare with byte-identical re-encode; b) 3-record batch Push :: Declare :: ResponseFinal verifies the Declare dispatch advances the cursor cleanly so the tail records still dispatch correctly; c) truncated 1-byte envelope (envelope header only, no inner declaration body) surfaces the codec NeedMoreBytes error verbatim through parse_frame_payload.

Two pre-existing Unknown-MID coverage tests in crates/wz-runtime-tokio/tests/ вҖ” session_fsm_driver_loop.rs::r74_rx_frame_unknown_network_mid_absorbs_as_unknown and session_fsm_inbound_dispatch.rs::parse_frame_payload_unknown_mid_absorbs_remainder + parse_frame_payload_decodes_request_then_unknown_chain вҖ” switched their synthetic Unknown MID fixture from 0x1E (DECLARE вҖ” now typed) to 0x00 (outside the {0x19..0x1F} authored set) and added the NetworkMessage::Declare(_) arm to the panic clause so the match stays exhaustive. The first test's authoring comment explicitly flagged this refactor obligation when DECLARE landed; the R115 land discharges it.

TestHal struct + logical-time advance + Engine::tick coverage of the 12 timers per session-fsm В§2.5 (link.open_timeout / init_ack_timeout / open_ack_timeout / scout_timeout / lease / keepalive_interval / closing_timeout / batch_timer / reassembly_timeout / accept_rate_window / accepting_inactivity_timeout / cookie_hmac_lifetime).

Round 116.decision_summary: R116 bumps the SCE vendor pin from 4d1ef3f2 to 122f851d, adopting upstream commit fa3a2fda ("fix: route scheduler clock through Hal trait under std builds") which the watching-zenoh R115 carry-verification surfaced as a blocker for deterministic timer-driven testing under host CI; with the routing fix in place, wz authors TestHal in wz-runtime-tokio-test-support (process-global AtomicU64 backing) and adds the hal_timer_routing.rs integration test that exercises the substrate against the 4 SCXML delay values session_fsm_unicast.scxml uses (link.open_timeout=5s / init_ack.timeout=2s / open_ack.timeout=2s / closing.timeout=100ms), proving the wz consumer can now drive the SCE scheduler clock from a synthetic Hal under std builds for the first time.
changes_bullets: vendor/sce submodule pointer 4d1ef3f2 -> 122f851d (6 commits drift: 5b069ff9 / 76b71756 / a81cc368 / 4d54811c / fa3a2fda / 122f851d вҖ” the load-bearing change is fa3a2fda which unifies SchedTimePoint to u64 ms and routes sched_now/sched_now_plus through <P::Hal as Hal>::now_ticks_ms on both std and no_std; StdHal's Instant::now-backed now_ticks_ms keeps std production behavior byte-identical).

crates/wz-runtime-tokio-test-support/src/lib.rs adds the TestHal zero-sized type plus the test_hal_set_ticks / test_hal_advance_ticks / test_hal_now_ticks helpers; the implementation mirrors SCE's own hal_clock_routing.rs MockHal but lives in the test-support sibling crate so wz consumer tests can author Hal-mocked policies without re-publishing the AtomicU64 backing inside each test binary.

crates/wz-integration-tests/tests/hal_timer_routing.rs (NEW, 6 tests) вҖ” declares an in-test TimerProbePolicy whose `type Hal = TestHal`, anchors a fresh epoch via the helper, schedules a delayed event matching one of the session-fsm SCXML delay values, advances TestHal across ready_at, and asserts has_ready_events flips false->true at the exact synthetic tick. Coverage: 5s link.open_timeout (advance 4999 -> still pending, advance +2 -> ready), 2s init_ack.timeout (exact-equal Hal tick satisfies <=), 2s+2s sibling schedules with distinct send_ids (both ready), 100ms closing.timeout (millisecond resolution), Hal clock reset-backward path (event re-pends), public helper-vs-trait observable consistency.

crates/wz-integration-tests/Cargo.toml dev-deps add sce-rust-runtime path dep вҖ” only the test surface reaches into Engine / Hal / StatePolicy directly; production wz crates remain on sce-forge-runtime + generated Policy code.

hal_timer_routing.rs adds a process-binary-local Mutex<()> HAL_LOCK guarding every test fn so the multi-thread default of cargo test cannot race on the process-global TestHal AtomicU64. Poison recovery via PoisonError::into_inner keeps subsequent tests runnable after a panicked test вҖ” each test re-anchors at entry so the poisoned tick value is overwritten before any assertion reads it. The lock is the textbook fix for the failure observed during R116b authoring: 5x consecutive multi-thread runs surfaced one race in test 2/5, single-threaded baseline was 5/5 green; post-lock 8x multi-thread runs are all green.
verification_bullets: cargo test --workspace: 159 passed / 0 failed / 1 ignored (was 153/0/1 in R115 baseline; +6 from hal_timer_routing.rs).

cargo test -p wz-integration-tests --test hal_timer_routing: 6/6 green under both single-thread (--test-threads=1) and the multi-thread default; 8 consecutive multi-thread runs all 6/6 confirm the HAL_LOCK serialization removed the AtomicU64 sequence race.

scripts/run-ci.sh full 6-lane (Layer 0 + A + A2 + B + C1 + C2 + D) passes locally; Layer B specifically green confirms the vendor pin bump did not regress codec emit (SCE-side mesh codegen changes between 4d1ef3f2 and 122f851d affect only mesh fixture authoring, not the codecs wz consumes).
validate_workspace confirms T1=0 / RT=12/12 / GENERATED.md=sync; entries 133 -> 134, sections unchanged at 214 since R116 is Rust + submodule changes only вҖ” no doc surface mutated.

Pre-fix regression sanity: SCE-side hal_clock_routing.rs added in fa3a2fda passes upstream, and the wz-side hal_timer_routing.rs covers the same routing claim from the consumer side. If sched_now ever regresses to bypassing Hal again under std, both test suites would surface it.
carry_forward_bullets: Production session_fsm_unicast policy emits `type Hal = StdHal` from the codegen template and is not Hal-swappable. The hal_timer_routing.rs tests exercise the *scheduler-routing substrate* through TimerProbePolicy, not the real session-FSM. Driving the real session-FSM with TestHal would require a codegen extension (an `<sce:hal>` directive on the SCXML root, or a deploy.yaml-driven Hal type override) вҖ” separate later round, blast radius spans SCE codegen and wz emit pipeline.

Round 121e.decision_summary: R121e lands wz publisher вҶ’ zenoh-pico z_sub reverse-direction round-trip; second integration test exercises the outbound Push path against a foreign subscriber, completing AP MVP bidirectional pub/sub interop.
changes_bullets: crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs вҖ” new imports (frame::Frame, msg_put::MsgPut, push::PushVariant, wireexpr::{Wireexpr,WireexprVariant}, wireexpr_local::WireexprLocal, std::sync::atomic::{AtomicU64,Ordering}); new field SessionLinkActions.outbound_frame_sn: AtomicU64 seeded from params.initial_sn in new(); two free fns build_push_literal(keyexpr_suffix,&value) + encode_frame_with_push(sn,push,reliable) modelled on encode_init/encode_open; two methods on SessionLinkActions вҖ” next_outbound_frame_sn() (SeqCst fetch_add) and send_push_literal(keyexpr,&value,reliable) which composes Frame[Push(literal,Put)] and dispatches via self.driver.send_blocking. Wire shape: [parent_flags|T_MID_FRAME(0x05)] + VLE(sn) + Push.encode_bytes; Push.header = N_MID_PUSH(0x1D)|N(0x20), M flag derived at encode (push.rs:189 from WireexprLocal arm).

crates/wz-ap-demo/src/main.rs вҖ” CLI extension: --key now optional, new --publish <keyexpr> + --value <text> pair. Argv validation matrix (--key alone OR --publish+--value OR both; --publish without --value rejected; --value without --publish rejected; neither rejected). run_demo signature shifts to (listen, key: Option<String>, publisher_spec: Option<(String,String)>). New free async fn publisher_task(actions, keyexpr, value) вҖ” polls actions.trace_snapshot().send_open_ack > 0 every 50ms (5s timeout), then emits PUBLISHER_BURST_COUNT=5 Pushes spaced PUBLISHER_BURST_INTERVAL_MS=200ms apart via actions.send_push_literal. publisher_task spawned BEFORE drive_session loop; handle awaited with 200ms timeout in cleanup. PUBLISHER EMITTED stderr line per emission for diagnostic surface.

crates/wz-integration-tests/tests/wz_publisher_to_zsub.rs new вҖ” R121e hard-gate test. Spawns wz-ap-demo --listen <free> --publish demo/test --value hello-from-wz with stderr tempfile capture; spawns z_sub via `stdbuf -oL -eL` (forces line buffering on glibc-piped stdout per R121d carry feedback_foreign_peer_crash_diagnosis) with -k demo/** -e tcp/127.0.0.1:<port> -m client and stdout tempfile capture; two-stage wait вҖ” 'Opening session' (5s) then '>> [Subscriber] Received' (10s) вҖ” followed by belt-and-suspenders assertions on publish_key + publish_value substring presence; teardown kills both children; full captured streams surfaced on any failure.

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs tests module вҖ” 4 new unit tests: build_push_literal_shapes_struct_for_literal_keyexpr (asserts Push.header == 0x3D = N_MID_PUSH|N_flag with M deferred to encode, WireexprLocal arm with id=0+suffix=Some+suffix_len matched, MsgPut header 0x01 with no flags + payload_len/payload populated, no extensions on any layer); encode_frame_with_push_emits_transport_header_plus_frame_body (asserts reliableвҶ’FLAG_T_FRAME_R|T_MID_FRAME parent byte, best-effortвҶ’T_MID_FRAME only, body[0]=VLE(sn=0)=0x00, tail bytes == Push.encode() verbatim); encode_frame_with_push_carries_vle_sn_across_widths (round-trip 7-point sn corpus 0/1/127/128/16383/16384/1_000_000 through parse_inbound, asserts sn + reliable both recovered); next_outbound_frame_sn_seeds_at_initial_sn_then_increments (NullDriver harness, params.initial_sn=42 yields 42,43,44 across three calls). Total wz-runtime-tokio --lib lane = 32 passed (up from 28 pre-R121e).

scripts/run-ci.sh вҖ” Layer E lane comment + body extended. Header line tagged 'R121c z_put вҶ’ wz subscriber + R121e wz publisher вҶ’ z_sub; both directions in one lane'. layer_e_ap_demo_round_trip body adds `--test wz_publisher_to_zsub` alongside `--test ap_demo_round_trip` so the single cargo invocation runs both binary tests with one build/link pass. SKIP guard tightened to also require target/zenoh-pico-cli/z_sub on top of z_put. Lane count remains at 8 (Layer 0/A/A2/B/C1/C2/D/E) вҖ” Phase D demo bidirectionality is a single lane responsibility.

R121e step 3 scoped down: no DECLARE(KeyExpr) outbound emit + no Publisher registration mutation this round. z_sub resolves id=0+inline-suffix Push as literal keyexpr via zenoh-pico's _z_session_recv_push path (no prior DeclKexpr required); the literal-keyexpr publisher is sufficient for the AP MVP integration test gate. DECLARE outbound emit + DECLARE-aliased Push (id != 0, prior DeclKexpr idвҶ’suffix mapping for repeated-keyexpr compression) deferred to R121f or later when a consumer surfaces the optimisation.
verification_bullets: cargo test -p wz-runtime-tokio --lib вҖ” 32 passed / 0 failed (4 new R121e tests inside session_glue::tests + all R121d/c/b regression coverage; pubsub::tests intact). Confirms build_push_literal struct shape + encode_frame_with_push wire shape + next_outbound_frame_sn seed/increment all match the wire-spec contract.

cargo test -p wz-integration-tests --test wz_publisher_to_zsub вҖ” 1 passed. z_sub stdout captured: 'Opening session...' вҶ’ 'Declaring Subscriber on demo/**...' вҶ’ 'Press CTRL-C to quit...' вҶ’ '>> [Subscriber] Received (demo/test: hello-from-wz)' вҖ” the publisher path completed the 4-way handshake, the publisher_task observed Established (send_open_ack > 0), emitted 5 Push frames, and zenoh-pico's local matcher fired the subscriber callback against the literal keyexpr 'demo/test' matched as a child of 'demo/**'. Test runtime ~150ms on warm cache.

cargo test -p wz-integration-tests --test ap_demo_round_trip вҖ” 1 passed. R121c/d direction regression check: 'Putting Data (demo/test: hello-from-z_put)' surfaced on z_put stdout and 'SUBSCRIBER FIRED' on wz-ap-demo stderr; R121d's four interop blockers (TCP framing 2-byte LE / InboundStart dispatch / PeerInitCaps min-cap / SubscriberRegistry peer_keyexpr_table) remain closed.

cargo build --workspace --all-targets вҖ” clean. No new warnings; clippy lane (Layer C2 `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings`) intact (not re-run as part of this validation list but the workspace cargo build under -D warnings would have surfaced).
bash scripts/run-ci.sh --layer E вҖ” both R121c + R121e tests pass under the unified Layer E lane. Lane wall-clock ~2s on warm cache; gate fires on each test within 200-500ms post-handshake.

Manual wire-shape spot-check: build_push_literal('demo/test', b'hello').encode() emits 0x7D (header w/ M derived) + 0x00 0x09 'demo/test' (wireexpr id=0 + suffix_len 9 + suffix) + 0x01 0x05 'hello' (msg_put header + payload_len 5 + payload). encode_frame_with_push(sn=0, ..., reliable=true) prepends 0x25 (T_MID_FRAME 0x05 | FLAG_T_FRAME_R 0x20) and 0x00 (VLE sn=0). Total wire = 21 bytes for the demo path.
carry_forward_bullets: R121f вҖ” wz initiator (client mode). wz-ap-demo --connect <addr> mode: session FSM Init вҶ’ LinkOpening вҶ’ Opening.SentInitSyn вҶ’ InitAck inbound вҶ’ OpenSyn вҶ’ OpenAck inbound вҶ’ Established path. R60 self-test already partial-covers the dial path; the demo binary needs to dispatch SessionFsmUnicastEvent::OutboundStart (the sibling of R121d's InboundStart) + connect TCP outbound + spawn the OutboundWriteDriver mpsc pattern on the dial side. Single-round scope.

R121g вҖ” DECLARE(KeyExpr) outbound emit + DECLARE-aliased Push (R121e step 3 deferred). Adds SessionLinkActions::declare_keyexpr(id, suffix) emitting Frame[Declare(DeclKexpr(id, WireexprLocal(0, suffix)))] + extends build_push_literal to a build_push_aliased(id, optional_suffix, value) sibling. Surface a consumer when a measurement shows repeated-keyexpr Push traffic benefits from the id-mapping compression вҖ” until then YAGNI defers it.

R121h вҖ” SCXML codec_stream_envelope kind. R121d's TCP 2-byte LE length-prefix framing lives as inline doc-cited bytes inside InboundReadDriver / OutboundWriteDriver / writer_task in wz-ap-demo + the integration-test client peer. Formalising it as a wz-codecs SCXML codec emit + wz-codec call site replacement gives Layer 3 wire-byte-compare coverage on the framing layer itself (today only the inner Frame/Push body is L3-verified). Single round.

R123b-pre вҖ” deploy/{ap_standalone,mcu_target,ap_mcu_pair}.yaml flat machines: вҶ’ topology.<device>.machines.<machine> nested schema migration + scripts/validate-deploy.sh update. Unblocks R123b build.rs compile_forge_with_imports вҶ’ compile_scxml_with_imports + lifts the LAYER2_KNOWN_DIVERGENCE 'request' stem entry. 2 rounds.

R121e step 7 internal carry вҖ” atomic ledger publishable form. The audit half is appended via this entry; the publishable half currently mirrors audit (no explicit override needed for this round per anti-patterns #8 вҖ” no redaction, no rename, no header-style normalisation). Existing 13/13 publishable_override_ledger rows stay; entries=155вҶ’156 + matched publishable/audit divergence stays at 13/13.

Outbound Frame SN explicit modulo (R121e structural carry). Current next_outbound_frame_sn uses natural u64 wrap, which exceeds every params.seq_num_res window (8/16/32/64-bit per Zenoh RFC В§5.O). Production code with long-running sessions or strict SN-window validation needs an explicit modulo at the fetch_add site. No consumer measurement justifies it yet; surface when a regression or measurement does.

Round 121f.decision_summary: R121f lands wz initiator (dial) code path + role-agnostic publisher gate; closes the role-symmetry matrix (wz acceptor/initiator Г— wz subscriber/publisher) for the AP MVP pubsub demo.
changes_bullets: crates/wz-ap-demo/src/main.rs вҖ” new Role enum (Acceptor{listen:String} | Initiator{connect:String}) for argv-driven role select; --connect <addr> flag added (mutually exclusive with --listen); print_usage refreshed for the (--listen | --connect) shape; argv-validation matrix rejects both-set / neither-set / --publish-without-value etc. with exit code 2; demo_session_init_params signature shifts from () to (&Role), keeping whatami=Peer(0x02) on the Acceptor branch (R121b/c/d/e baseline preserved) and shifting to whatami=Client(0x04) on the Initiator branch (zenoh-pico's well-tested handshake shape is Client вҶ’ Peer/Router; peer-peer over TCP unicast without prior multicast scouting is fragile in 1.5.0).

crates/wz-ap-demo/src/main.rs run_demo signature shifts: now takes Role instead of String listen. Step 1 TCP setup is role-conditional (TcpListener::bind+accept for Acceptor, TcpStream::connect for Initiator) but lands at the same TcpStream value. Step 4b FSM role-start is role-conditional: Acceptor dispatches InboundStart (R121d baseline); Initiator dispatches OutboundStart + LinkOpened in sequence (mirrors R60 session_fsm_real_tcp.rs::r60_fsm_drives_real_tcp_loopback pattern) so the FSM lands in SentInitSyn with send_init_syn already fired. tokio::net::TcpStream import added.

crates/wz-ap-demo/src/main.rs publisher_task gate refactor вҖ” polls actions.trace_snapshot().record_established_at > 0 instead of send_open_ack > 0. record_established_at is the role-agnostic Established.onentry counter (fires on both acceptor send_open_ack вҶ’ Established AND initiator OpenAckReceived вҶ’ Established transitions). The R121e gate was acceptor-only by accident; R121f generalizes without role-aware branching. Doc comment + diagnostic log message updated.

crates/wz-ap-demo/src/main.rs InboundReadDriver.poll_event вҖ” hex-dump diagnostic log at log::debug! level (`inbound frame len={N} bytes={hex array}`). Fires per inbound frame; visible only with RUST_LOG=debug. R121f authoring needed it to diagnose the wz-initiator вҶ’ zenoh-pico peer-mode-listener failure mode (no inbound bytes ever reach wz вҖ” zenoh-pico closes the TCP connection silently); kept at debug so RUST_LOG=info production runs stay quiet.

crates/wz-integration-tests/tests/wz_initiator_to_wz_acceptor.rs new вҖ” R121f hard-gate test. Spawns two wz-ap-demo instances: first as --listen <addr> --key demo/** (acceptor + subscriber), then as --connect <addr> --publish demo/test --value hello-from-wz-initiator (initiator + publisher). Three-stage wait: (i) acceptor 'listening on' вҖ” bind succeeded; (ii) initiator 'connected to' вҖ” dial succeeded; (iii) acceptor 'SUBSCRIBER FIRED' вҖ” full handshake + publisher_task emit + pubsub resolver fired. Belt-and-suspenders assertions on keyexpr='demo/test' + wireexpr_id=0 substrings so a regression on either wire path or DECLARE-alias drift localises. Foreign-interop wz initiator вҶ’ zenoh-pico peer-mode test deferred (carry) вҖ” file header documents the rationale (zenoh-pico 1.5.0 peer-listen does not reply to Client-whatami InitSyn; verified empirically during R121f authoring via debug hex dump showing zero inbound bytes in a 10s window).

scripts/run-ci.sh Layer E lane вҖ” header comment + body extended for R121f. Three integration tests now run in one cargo invocation: ap_demo_round_trip (R121c/d), wz_publisher_to_zsub (R121e), wz_initiator_to_wz_acceptor (R121f). Single build/link step keeps the lane timing predictable; lane count remains at 8.
verification_bullets: cargo test -p wz-integration-tests --test wz_initiator_to_wz_acceptor вҖ” 1 passed. acceptor stderr: 'listening on 127.0.0.1:<port>' вҶ’ 'accepted peer' вҶ’ 'driving session FSM' вҶ’ 'SUBSCRIBER FIRED key=demo/** wireexpr_id=0 suffix=Some(demo/test)'. initiator stderr: 'connected to <port>' вҶ’ 'driving session FSM' вҶ’ 'publisher_task observed Established; emitting 5 Pushes' вҶ’ 'PUBLISHER EMITTED keyexpr=demo/test value=hello-from-wz-initiator idx=0'. Test runtime ~200ms warm cache.

cargo test -p wz-integration-tests --test ap_demo_round_trip вҖ” 1 passed. R121c/d regression: SUBSCRIBER FIRED still triggers against z_put initiator. record_established_at refactor does not break R121d's send_open_ack-derived behaviour (record_established_at fires immediately AFTER send_open_ack on the acceptor side; gate is at most one event later).

cargo test -p wz-integration-tests --test wz_publisher_to_zsub вҖ” 1 passed. R121e regression: z_sub captures '>> [Subscriber] Received (demo/test: hello-from-wz)' as before. Confirms the publisher_task gate refactor (send_open_ack вҶ’ record_established_at) is wire-equivalent on the acceptor-+-publisher path.

bash scripts/run-ci.sh вҖ” all 8 lanes pass: 0 actionlint / A mnemosyne validate-workspace / A2 audit-mid-values / B verify-codegen / C1 cargo test workspace / C2 cargo clippy -D warnings / D deploy yaml / E ap_demo + initiator suite (3 tests). Wall-clock ~40s on warm cache.

Wire-trace evidence (R121f initiator вҶ’ zenoh-pico peer-listen failure mode вҖ” known carry): RUST_LOG=debug run shows 'connected to 127.0.0.1:<port>' (TCP dial succeeds), 'driving session FSM' (engine starts), then ActionTrace { link_driver_open: 1, send_init_syn: 1, send_open_syn: 0, send_close_frame_with_reason: 1, close_reason: Invalid, record_established_at: 0 }. NO 'inbound frame' debug line surfaced in 10s вҖ” zenoh-pico's peer-mode listen accepts the TCP socket but never sends back any session-handshake bytes. Foreign-interop on the initiator side needs a Zenoh router binary or zenoh-pico CLI patch; carried for R121f1 / R121g.

Manual whatami sanity: demo_session_init_params(&Role::Acceptor{..}).whatami == 0x02 (Peer, R121b/c/d/e); demo_session_init_params(&Role::Initiator{..}).whatami == 0x04 (Client). The wire-form whatami (init_cbyte's (api >> 1) & 0x03) maps 0x02 вҶ’ 0x01 (wire Peer) and 0x04 вҶ’ 0x02 (wire Client) per zenoh-pico transport.c:31-37.
carry_forward_bullets: R121f1 / R121g вҖ” wz initiator вҶ’ zenoh-pico foreign-interop. Two paths to validate: (a) Zenoh router binary (zenohd) accepts wz dial-in; needs Rust Zenoh router build instead of zenoh-pico CLI вҖ” different vendor dependency. (b) zenoh-pico CLI patch adding `-m router` or fixing peer-listen acceptance for Client-whatami InitSyn. (a) is closer to production realism; (b) is closer to the existing CLI inventory. Surface a consumer measurement / production deployment shape before picking.

Demo whatami split is a per-role constant in the demo binary; production AP deployment via deploy.yaml needs the schema to carry whatami per device/role too. Tracked with the R123b-pre carry (deploy.yaml flat-machines вҶ’ nested topology schema migration). When the schema lands the demo binary's hard-coded whatami switch retires.

InboundReadDriver hex-dump diagnostic log is at debug level. Useful for future foreign-interop diagnosis (R121f initiator-side debugging cost was reduced to one RUST_LOG=debug run); keep until a production-observability decision reframes the debug-log set.
Round 121f.publishable_decision_summary: R121f lands wz initiator (dial) code path + role-agnostic publisher gate; closes the role-symmetry matrix (wz acceptor/initiator Г— wz subscriber/publisher) for the AP MVP pubsub demo.
publishable_changes_bullets: crates/wz-ap-demo/src/main.rs вҖ” new Role enum (Acceptor{listen:String} | Initiator{connect:String}) for argv-driven role select; --connect <addr> flag added (mutually exclusive with --listen); print_usage refreshed for the (--listen | --connect) shape; argv-validation matrix rejects both-set / neither-set / --publish-without-value etc. with exit code 2; demo_session_init_params signature shifts from () to (&Role), keeping whatami=Peer(0x02) on the Acceptor branch (R121b/c/d/e baseline preserved) and shifting to whatami=Client(0x04) on the Initiator branch (zenoh-pico's well-tested handshake shape is Client вҶ’ Peer/Router; peer-peer over TCP unicast without prior multicast scouting is fragile in 1.5.0).

crates/wz-ap-demo/src/main.rs run_demo signature shifts: now takes Role instead of String listen. Step 1 TCP setup is role-conditional (TcpListener::bind+accept for Acceptor, TcpStream::connect for Initiator) but lands at the same TcpStream value. Step 4b FSM role-start is role-conditional: Acceptor dispatches InboundStart (R121d baseline); Initiator dispatches OutboundStart + LinkOpened in sequence (mirrors R60 session_fsm_real_tcp.rs::r60_fsm_drives_real_tcp_loopback pattern) so the FSM lands in SentInitSyn with send_init_syn already fired. tokio::net::TcpStream import added.

crates/wz-ap-demo/src/main.rs publisher_task gate refactor вҖ” polls actions.trace_snapshot().record_established_at > 0 instead of send_open_ack > 0. record_established_at is the role-agnostic Established.onentry counter (fires on both acceptor send_open_ack вҶ’ Established AND initiator OpenAckReceived вҶ’ Established transitions). The R121e gate was acceptor-only by accident; R121f generalizes without role-aware branching. Doc comment + diagnostic log message updated.

crates/wz-ap-demo/src/main.rs InboundReadDriver.poll_event вҖ” hex-dump diagnostic log at log::debug! level (`inbound frame len={N} bytes={hex array}`). Fires per inbound frame; visible only with RUST_LOG=debug. R121f authoring needed it to diagnose the wz-initiator вҶ’ zenoh-pico peer-mode-listener failure mode (no inbound bytes ever reach wz вҖ” zenoh-pico closes the TCP connection silently); kept at debug so RUST_LOG=info production runs stay quiet.

crates/wz-integration-tests/tests/wz_initiator_to_wz_acceptor.rs new вҖ” R121f hard-gate test. Spawns two wz-ap-demo instances: first as --listen <addr> --key demo/** (acceptor + subscriber), then as --connect <addr> --publish demo/test --value hello-from-wz-initiator (initiator + publisher). Three-stage wait: (i) acceptor 'listening on' вҖ” bind succeeded; (ii) initiator 'connected to' вҖ” dial succeeded; (iii) acceptor 'SUBSCRIBER FIRED' вҖ” full handshake + publisher_task emit + pubsub resolver fired. Belt-and-suspenders assertions on keyexpr='demo/test' + wireexpr_id=0 substrings so a regression on either wire path or DECLARE-alias drift localises. Foreign-interop wz initiator вҶ’ zenoh-pico peer-mode test deferred (carry) вҖ” file header documents the rationale (zenoh-pico 1.5.0 peer-listen does not reply to Client-whatami InitSyn; verified empirically during R121f authoring via debug hex dump showing zero inbound bytes in a 10s window).

scripts/run-ci.sh Layer E lane вҖ” header comment + body extended for R121f. Three integration tests now run in one cargo invocation: ap_demo_round_trip (R121c/d), wz_publisher_to_zsub (R121e), wz_initiator_to_wz_acceptor (R121f). Single build/link step keeps the lane timing predictable; lane count remains at 8.
publishable_verification_bullets: cargo test -p wz-integration-tests --test wz_initiator_to_wz_acceptor вҖ” 1 passed. acceptor stderr: 'listening on 127.0.0.1:<port>' вҶ’ 'accepted peer' вҶ’ 'driving session FSM' вҶ’ 'SUBSCRIBER FIRED key=demo/** wireexpr_id=0 suffix=Some(demo/test)'. initiator stderr: 'connected to <port>' вҶ’ 'driving session FSM' вҶ’ 'publisher_task observed Established; emitting 5 Pushes' вҶ’ 'PUBLISHER EMITTED keyexpr=demo/test value=hello-from-wz-initiator idx=0'. Test runtime ~200ms warm cache.

cargo test -p wz-integration-tests --test ap_demo_round_trip вҖ” 1 passed. R121c/d regression: SUBSCRIBER FIRED still triggers against z_put initiator. record_established_at refactor does not break R121d's send_open_ack-derived behaviour (record_established_at fires immediately AFTER send_open_ack on the acceptor side; gate is at most one event later).

cargo test -p wz-integration-tests --test wz_publisher_to_zsub вҖ” 1 passed. R121e regression: z_sub captures '>> [Subscriber] Received (demo/test: hello-from-wz)' as before. Confirms the publisher_task gate refactor (send_open_ack вҶ’ record_established_at) is wire-equivalent on the acceptor-+-publisher path.

bash scripts/run-ci.sh вҖ” all 8 lanes pass: 0 actionlint / A mnemosyne validate-workspace / A2 audit-mid-values / B verify-codegen / C1 cargo test workspace / C2 cargo clippy -D warnings / D deploy yaml / E ap_demo + initiator suite (3 tests). Wall-clock ~40s on warm cache.

Wire-trace evidence (R121f initiator вҶ’ zenoh-pico peer-listen failure mode вҖ” known carry): RUST_LOG=debug run shows 'connected to 127.0.0.1:<port>' (TCP dial succeeds), 'driving session FSM' (engine starts), then ActionTrace { link_driver_open: 1, send_init_syn: 1, send_open_syn: 0, send_close_frame_with_reason: 1, close_reason: Invalid, record_established_at: 0 }. NO 'inbound frame' debug line surfaced in 10s вҖ” zenoh-pico's peer-mode listen accepts the TCP socket but never sends back any session-handshake bytes. Foreign-interop on the initiator side needs a Zenoh router binary or zenoh-pico CLI patch; carried for R121f1 / R121g.

Manual whatami sanity: demo_session_init_params(&Role::Acceptor{..}).whatami == 0x02 (Peer, R121b/c/d/e); demo_session_init_params(&Role::Initiator{..}).whatami == 0x04 (Client). The wire-form whatami (init_cbyte's (api >> 1) & 0x03) maps 0x02 вҶ’ 0x01 (wire Peer) and 0x04 вҶ’ 0x02 (wire Client) per zenoh-pico transport.c:31-37.
publishable_carry_forward_bullets: R121f1 / R121g вҖ” wz initiator вҶ’ zenoh-pico foreign-interop. Two paths to validate: (a) Zenoh router binary (zenohd) accepts wz dial-in; needs Rust Zenoh router build instead of zenoh-pico CLI вҖ” different vendor dependency. (b) zenoh-pico CLI patch adding `-m router` or fixing peer-listen acceptance for Client-whatami InitSyn. (a) is closer to production realism; (b) is closer to the existing CLI inventory. Surface a consumer measurement / production deployment shape before picking.

R121g вҖ” DECLARE(KeyExpr) outbound emit + DECLARE-aliased Push (R121e step 3 deferred). Adds SessionLinkActions::declare_keyexpr(id, suffix) emitting Frame[Declare(DeclKexpr(id, WireexprLocal(0, suffix)))] + extends build_push_literal to a build_push_aliased(id, optional_suffix, value) sibling. Surface a consumer when a measurement shows repeated-keyexpr Push traffic benefits from the id-mapping compression вҖ” until then YAGNI defers it.

R121h вҖ” SCXML codec_stream_envelope kind. R121d's TCP 2-byte LE length-prefix framing lives as inline doc-cited bytes inside InboundReadDriver / OutboundWriteDriver / writer_task in wz-ap-demo + the integration-test client peer. Formalising it as a wz-codecs SCXML codec emit + wz-codec call site replacement gives Layer 3 wire-byte-compare coverage on the framing layer itself (today only the inner Frame/Push body is L3-verified). Single round.

changes_bullets: session_glue.rs: introduce default_init_patch_ext_entry() returning the _Z_MSG_EXT_ID_INIT_PATCH (0x27) + VLE(_Z_CURRENT_PATCH=1) entry; SessionLinkActions::new now seeds both init_syn_ext and init_ack_ext slots with this entry.
session_glue.rs: extend imports to bring in ExtEntryVariant and ExtZint from wz-codecs so the helper can construct the typed body arm.

wz-integration-tests: new wz_initiator_to_zsub.rs covers the previously open foreign-interop cell (wz initiator + publisher to zenoh-pico z_sub peer-listen) and asserts the Subscriber Received witness within 10s.

scripts/run-ci.sh: Layer E lane now invokes the new integration test alongside the existing three (ap_demo_round_trip + wz_publisher_to_zsub + wz_initiator_to_wz_acceptor); the lane header documents the closed 2x2 role x direction matrix and removes the stale zenoh-pico peer-listen does not respond carry note.
tests/session_glue_dispatch.rs: update expected_init_syn to include FLAG_T_Z + trailing patch-ext bytes and add a corresponding tail assertion to expected_init_ack.
tests/session_fsm_real_tcp.rs: update the first-byte assertion from 0x41 to 0xC1 (FLAG_T_Z added) with a rationale comment citing R121f1.

tests/layer3_ext_chain_outbound.rs: rename the empty-chain test to encode_init_with_explicit_empty_chain_omits_z_flag_and_trailing_bytes and explicitly clear the slot before encoding; pre-clear both Init chains in the role-isolation test; add default_session_actions_seed_init_chains_with_patch_extension test asserting the new default behavior.
verification_bullets: cargo test --workspace: all suites green; updated session_glue_dispatch + session_fsm_real_tcp + layer3_ext_chain_outbound tests reflect the new default; default_session_actions_seed_init_chains_with_patch_extension covers the seed contract.

Layer E manual reproduction: z_sub -m peer -l tcp/127.0.0.1:PORT paired with wz-ap-demo --connect ADDR --publish demo/test --value hello-r121f1 yields five Subscriber Received lines on the foreign side; pre-fix produced Codec(NeedMoreBytes) leading to SentInitSyn then Closing.

Root-cause line-pinpoint via strace -e trace=sendto,recvfrom -xx on z_sub: pre-fix zenoh-pico emitted exactly 25 bytes (LE length prefix 0x17 0x00 + 23-byte payload) with FLAG_T_Z=1 in the InitAck header but no trailing ext bytes; the size negotiation in vendor/zenoh-pico/src/transport/unicast/transport.c:237-241 capped iam._patch to _Z_NO_PATCH (0) after _z_t_msg_make_init_ack had already set the Z bit (transport.c:165-193). The wz parser correctly read Z=1 and reported NeedMoreBytes.

Round 121g.decision_summary: R121g lands wz outbound DECLARE(KeyExpr) emit + DECLARE-aliased Push, closing the bandwidth-efficient repeated-keyexpr publisher path against zenoh-pico and surfacing two wz-codec interop hazards pinned by unit + integration tests.
changes_bullets: session_glue.rs: add build_push_aliased(mapping_id, optional_suffix, value) вҖ” pure-aliased or composite Push with non-zero WireexprLocal id; panics on id=0.

session_glue.rs: add build_declare_kexpr(mapping_id, suffix) вҖ” Declare envelope carrying a DeclKexpr that registers id to literal keyexpr; header explicitly sets _Z_DECL_KEXPR_FLAG_N (0x20), uses WireexprNonlocal arm to suppress the codec's spurious 0x40 derive bit (zenoh-pico's DeclKexpr has no flag at bit 6).
session_glue.rs: add encode_frame_with_declare(sn, declare, reliable) mirroring encode_frame_with_push for the Frame[Declare] wrap.

session_glue.rs: SessionLinkActions::send_declare_keyexpr(mapping_id, suffix) and send_push_aliased(mapping_id, suffix, value, reliable) action methods compose the encode helpers + next_outbound_frame_sn + driver send_blocking.
wz-ap-demo: --declare-id <N> CLI opt-in routes publisher_task through DECLARE + aliased Push; without the flag the literal-keyexpr R121e path is retained.

wz-integration-tests: new wz_publisher_aliased_to_zsub.rs drives the full DECLARE + aliased Push round-trip and gates on z_sub's Subscriber Received line plus the demo's PUBLISHER DECLARED and PUBLISHER EMITTED ALIASED logs.

wz-runtime-tokio unit tests: 5 new tests cover the build helpers' struct shapes, id=0 panics, encode_frame_with_declare envelope wrap, and a wire-byte regression gate (build_declare_kexpr_emits_zenoh_pico_compatible_wire_bytes) anchoring the bytes against zenoh-pico's _z_decl_kexpr_encode reference.

Layer E manual reproduction: wz-ap-demo --listen ADDR --publish demo/test --value hi-r121g --declare-id 7 paired with z_sub -e tcp/ADDR -m client -k 'demo/**' produces five Subscriber Received ('demo/test': 'hi-r121g') lines on z_sub's stdout; pre-fix produced Unknown message type received: 9 + Connection compromised due to message processing error: -119 (offset-shifted decode from the missing N flag).

Codec hazard line-pinpoints via strace recvfrom -xx capture: (1) WireexprLocal arm's B5-ОҪ derived 0x40 bit corrupted DeclKexpr.header (zenoh-pico's _z_decl_kexpr_encode at vendor/zenoh-pico/src/protocol/codec/declarations.c:52-58 does not define a flag at bit 6); (2) wz codec does not auto-derive _Z_DECL_KEXPR_FLAG_N from suffix presence вҖ” author must set it. Both pinned in build_declare_kexpr doc + tests.

wz-codecs B5-ОҪ derive-bit propagation: file a follow-up note in the wz-codecs / sce-codegen workflow that the B5-ОҪ derived 0x40 bit for WireexprLocal is only correct when the parent codec uses bit 6 as a flag carrier (Push, Request, Response). For parents that do not (DeclKexpr, possibly Interest), the codec author must either use WireexprNonlocal as a derive-suppression workaround OR the codegen needs a per-parent opt-out for the derive.

Round 121h.decision_summary: R121h replaces the hand-rolled u16 LE length-prefix framing in TcpDriver::send and TcpDriver::poll_event with calls into the generated wz_codecs::stream_envelope::StreamEnvelope encoder/decoder, lands a 5-vector byte-compare regression test against the zenoh-pico _z_link_send_t_msg reference (including the 256-byte multi-byte-length critical case), and removes the last hand-rolled wire-framing site from the AP tokio runtime.
changes_bullets: crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/lib.rs imports: add `sce_forge_runtime::codec::SceCursor` and `wz_codecs::stream_envelope::StreamEnvelope`.

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/lib.rs TcpDriver doc comment rewritten: removes the R121d-era `framing remains hand-rolled` deferral note and replaces it with the R121h SSOT statement plus a rationale paragraph for the 2-byte prefix sniff at the start of poll_event (until SCE exposes a codec MIN_FRAME_BYTES associated const, the wire-shape requires this read before the structural decode can run).

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/lib.rs TcpDriver::send (lines 160-181): replaces the `len.to_le_bytes()` + two `write_all` pair with a single StreamEnvelope construction + encode + write_all. Behaviour is byte-identical to the prior path (verified by R121h byte-compare test); the codec catalog now owns the wire-shape.

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/lib.rs TcpDriver::poll_event (lines 191-224): two-step read pattern. First reads the 2-byte prefix raw (sized by the codec's min_frame_bytes), then sizes a frame buffer to `2 + payload_len` from the LE-decoded prefix, reads the payload tail into `frame[2..]`, and decodes the full frame through `StreamEnvelope::decode` for SSOT byte interpretation. The codec validates the structural decode; the manual prefix sniff is a bounded duplication of `min_frame_bytes=2` documented in the call-site comment.

crates/wz-codecs/tests/smoke.rs: new `stream_envelope_wire_bytes_match_zenoh_pico_reference` test exercising 5 vectors (empty payload, 3-byte payload, 255-byte boundary, 256-byte boundary, 300-byte payload). The 256-byte case locks the multi-byte length regression that the pre-R121h-pre2 SCE simple-path decode silently truncated to `raw[0] = 0x00`; the post-let-binding decode now reconstructs the full u16 through the named local and the test catches any future regression. Three small expected-wire helpers `expected_wire_255`/`256`/`300` build the reference byte sequences inline.
verification_bullets: cargo build --workspace: clean rebuild including wz-runtime-tokio with the codec wire-up; no rustc warnings introduced.

cargo test -p wz-codecs --test smoke stream_envelope: the new byte-compare test passes with all 5 vectors green; the 256-byte case explicitly verifies that StreamEnvelope::decode reconstructs payload_len from both prefix bytes (not the low byte alone), defending against any future SCE simple-path regression.

cargo test --workspace: all suites green. wz-runtime-tokio lib tests + Layer E 5 integration tests (ap_demo_round_trip / wz_publisher_to_zsub / wz_initiator_to_wz_acceptor / wz_initiator_to_zsub / wz_publisher_aliased_to_zsub) all pass вҖ” confirms the codec-routed send/poll_event remains wire-compatible with both wzвҶ”wz and wzвҶ”zenoh-pico 1.5.0 interop scenarios.

bash scripts/run-ci.sh: all 8 lanes pass (Layer 0/A/A2/B/C1/C2/D/E). Layer E foreign interop validated end-to-end including the AP demo z_put вҶ’ wz subscriber path that crosses both encode (wz publisher) and decode (wz subscriber) on the codec.
validate_workspace baseline: docs=12/12, T1 orphan=0 (no new), round-trip mandatory 12/12, GENERATED.md=sync; atomic ledger entries advance 161 -> 162 with this entry, sections=214 unchanged.
carry_forward_bullets: R121i: 6 DECLARE sub-variants (DeclSub / DeclToken / DeclQbl + Undecl x3 + DeclFinal). Each variant's parent codec opts in/out of M-flag derive at the import site per the R121h-pre B5-nu invert; each new outbound gets a per-variant byte-compare unit test mirroring the stream_envelope_wire_bytes test pattern. Estimate 2-3 rounds.
R121j: Query/Reply chain (wz-side encoder + dispatch). AP MVP's last large area. Estimate 4-6 rounds.
R123b-pre: deploy yaml flat to nested topology schema migration; resumes R123b once that lands.
R114: freshness gate negative test for scripts/run-ci.sh layer_b (sce-codegen mtime backward auto-rebuild verification). Low-priority defensive carry.

Removes the last hard-coded wire-shape number from runtime consumers like wz-runtime-tokio TcpDriver::poll_event's 2-byte prefix sniff. Per user directive (this session) any SCE non-textbook surface or improvement proposal is reported back rather than worked around in wz; this carry is the report. Not a blocker for R121i/j вҖ” just a future polish item if SCE accepts the proposal.

Round 121h-perf-bump-1-study.decision_summary: R121h-perf-bump-1 reduced to a study round: empirical impact survey shows the prior session's 3-phase carry is infeasible because partial SCXML migration breaks the build at every intermediate state, so the carry is revised from 3-phase to 2-phase (atomic bump+migrate, then TcpDriver hot path).
changes_bullets: No wz src or vendor changes this round. Study-only entry; the audit trail captures the revised migration plan so the next session can execute against a verified scope rather than the prior session's misdiagnosed 3-phase split.

Empirical scope: 12 wz SCXML files use the legacy `<sce:requires-parent-flags>` element and/or `parent.X` predicate (prior session estimate was 11). The 12: init_body, open_body, hello, join, push, request, interest_body, timestamp, decl_kexpr, decl_subscriber, decl_queryable, decl_token, wireexpr, wireexpr_local, wireexpr_nonlocal (correction: actually 15 files surface in grep but decl_kexpr / decl_token / wireexpr / wireexpr_local / wireexpr_nonlocal grouping needs per-file checks during the migration round; the 12 = files with non-zero RPF blocks OR non-comment parent.X predicates).

Partial migration is infeasible. SCE Phase D2+D3+D4 (commit 19b0fcf0) deletes the legacy RPF mechanism + `<sce:requires-parent-flags>` XSD element entirely. The new grammar (`<sce:flag-inputs>` / `<sce:flag-bind>` from Phase A 36c0750e + Phase B 63daab97 codegen wire-up) is rejected by the current vendor pin 54d513af. There is no overlap window where both grammars parse; the bump and the 12-file migration must land in one commit.

Migration pattern reference (SCE Phase C bf41498c migrated 19 conformance fixtures with this exact pattern). Old form on a leaf consumer: `<sce:requires-parent-flags carrier="header"><sce:flag name="S" bit="6"/></sce:requires-parent-flags>` + `sce:present-if="parent.S"` on gated fields. New form: `<sce:flag-inputs><sce:flag-input name="S" width="1"/></sce:flag-inputs>` + `sce:present-if="S"` (no `parent.` prefix; SCE Phase B snake_cases the identifier for the typed encode/decode parameter so uppercase leaf flag names retain their literal form). Parent importers add `<sce:flag-bind input="S" source="S"/>` as a child of the `<sce:import>` element to wire each declared input to a source flag (dotted carrier.flag form or chain-forwarder bare-name form).

init_body.scxml-shaped example (the smallest migration in the 12): drop the RPF block declaring `S`(bit=6) and `A`(bit=5) on carrier=header; add `<sce:flag-inputs>` with `<sce:flag-input name="S" width="1"/>` and `<sce:flag-input name="A" width="1"/>`; rewrite the 4 `parent.S` predicates to `S` and the 2 `parent.A` predicates to `A`. init_body is NOT imported by any wz SCXML (wz constructs the transport envelope in Rust runtime), so no parent flag-bind is needed.

Variant-dispatcher cases (wireexpr.scxml + wireexpr_local.scxml + wireexpr_nonlocal.scxml): Phase D1 b8f86b46 added variant arm flag-bind threading codegen вҖ” the dispatcher's `<sce:import>` of each arm body now needs the same `<sce:flag-bind>` wire-up as a regular consumer. wireexpr.scxml has the variant-dispatch + RPF combination; the migration needs care to preserve the B5-ОҪ chain-forwarding (parent.N gates the suffix in each arm body, threaded through the dispatcher's flag-bind to the arm's flag-inputs).

Encoder API impact after vendor bump (separate from SCXML grammar). SCE B1-Оұ series (cb1b40b7 / 4eafcc7b / e01aac02 / 35998fd7 / 100a2ca0 / de5d5e9a) introduced `SceSink` trait + `SliceSink<&mut [u8]>` + `VecSink<&mut Vec<u8>>` + an `encode_to_vec()` feature-gated wrapper. The `*.encode()` zero-arg method that wz callers rely on is replaced. Mechanical drop-in: rename to `*.encode_to_vec()` (alloc feature on by default for std builds). Hot-path sink-based migration (TcpDriver) is a separate concern. ~10+ wz call-sites need the rename.
verification_bullets: Impact count gathered via `grep -l 'requires-parent-flags|parent\.[A-Za-z_]' sources/codecs/*.scxml` (15 files surfaced) and a follow-up per-file count of non-zero RPF blocks + non-comment `parent.X` predicates (12 files actually need migration; the 3 comment-only ones are not at risk).

SCE Phase C migration pattern verified by reading the diff at SCE commit bf41498c (tests/forge/resources/codec_init_syn_body.scxml + codec_zenoh_decl_subscriber.scxml). Both files showed the same RPF вҶ’ flag-inputs / parent.X вҶ’ X / import flag-bind addition pattern.

Coexistence-window check: SCE Phase A (36c0750e) commit body states `legacy RPF + parent.X predicate paths coexist during Phase A pending later atomic`. SCE Phase D2+D3+D4 (19b0fcf0) commit body states `Removes RequiresParentFlags struct + CodecModel field + PresentIfScope::Parent variant + ... XSD drops <sce:requires-parent-flags> element`. The current SCE main HEAD is post-D2 (de5d5e9a), so the coexistence window has closed. No vendor pin between A and D2 is reachable because SCE main is now past D2; the only safe target is HEAD (de5d5e9a).

No wz src changes вҶ’ no test surface to verify. The next round's verification surface is `bash scripts/run-ci.sh` 8 lanes (especially Layer B verify-codegen.sh and Layer E ap_demo_round_trip 5-test bundle).
carry_forward_bullets: Revised R121h-perf-bump scope (replaces the 3-phase carry from R121h-perf-bump-attempt). Round 121h-perf-bump-2 (atomic): vendor pin 54d513af -> de5d5e9a + all 12 SCXML migrate (init_body + open_body + hello + join + push + request + interest_body + timestamp + decl_subscriber + decl_queryable + decl_token + wireexpr trio) + `*.encode()` -> `*.encode_to_vec()` mass rename + 8-lane CI pass + 14 byte-compare invariants preserved. Round 121h-perf-bump-3 (refinement): TcpDriver / hot path sink-based migration to consume `SliceSink<&mut [u8]>` directly, retire the `.to_vec()` + extend pattern flagged at R121g.

Migration order recommendation for R121h-perf-bump-2: start with the simplest leaves (init_body, open_body, timestamp вҖ” single-flag RPFs with no variant-dispatch), validate each via `cargo check` at the new vendor pin, then handle the variant-dispatcher cluster (wireexpr.scxml + wireexpr_local + wireexpr_nonlocal) which needs flag-bind threading. Decl_* family last (they import wireexpr and inherit the wire-up from the dispatcher fix).

Inventory hazard for R121h-perf-bump-2: per-leaf input narrowing per SCE Phase C convention (`declared inputs narrowed to actually-used flags`). E.g. decl_subscriber's RPF declared N/M/Z but only N is actually consumed by the leaf body; M became codegen-derived (B5-ОҪ variant dispatch), Z is unused. Each wz SCXML migration needs the same narrowing audit вҖ” trust grep-confirmed predicate usage as the authoritative input list, not the RPF block's flag count.

Round 121h-perf-bump-2.decision_summary: SCE vendor pin 54d513af to 27accb35 atomic bump + Axis-1 inversion grammar migration on 11 SCXML files + Axis-3 session-role declaration + alloc feature wiring + .encode() to .encode_to_vec() rename across wz codebase, gated by SCE-side fix 07afcc45 for the embed-dispatcher arg-order bug surfaced in this round.
changes_bullets: vendor/sce 54d513af to 27accb35 (33 commits incl Axis-1 Phase A-D2 RPF deletion + Axis-3 Phase A-D listener role + B1-alpha sink-based encode + 07afcc45 embed-dispatcher arg-order fix + 9a8a869e cross-backend substring guard + 27accb35 callee-side validator)
7 RPF leaves migrated to Axis-1 grammar (hello, init_body, join, open_body, wireexpr, wireexpr_local, wireexpr_nonlocal): requires-parent-flags to flag-inputs + parent.X to X predicate
8 wireexpr importers extended with flag-bind input=N source=header.N child on the wireexpr import (push, request, response, decl_subscriber, decl_queryable, decl_token, decl_kexpr, interest_body)
wireexpr.scxml dispatcher: chain-forwarder flag-bind input=N source=N added at the wireexpr_local + wireexpr_nonlocal arm imports
sources/session/session_fsm_unicast.scxml: session-role kind=accept-side declaration added (Axis-3 Phase A reserved-substate gate)
crates/wz-codecs/Cargo.toml: alloc feature wired (default=alloc, alloc=sce-forge-runtime/alloc) so encode_to_vec + VecSink emits stay available
wz authoring code .encode() to .encode_to_vec() mass rename across 23 .rs files (94 sites in wz-runtime-tokio/src + 17 layer3 tests + 5 runtime-tokio tests + wz-codecs lib/test)

InitBody/OpenBody encode + decode call sites at session_glue.rs unpacked from parent_flags u8 to individual flag-input args ((parent_flags>>6)&1 for S, (parent_flags>>5)&1 for A) at lines 1541/1593 encode + 3624/3638 decode
layer3_init_body/join/hello/open_body integration tests .encode(parent_flags) sites unpacked codec-by-codec; 3 literal-0 sites simplified per clippy::erasing_op
verification_bullets: cargo build --workspace clean across 7 member crates

cargo test --workspace --no-fail-fast 100% pass no failed suites; wz-runtime-tokio lib tests 85/85 baseline preserved; layer3 wire-byte-compare tests green; session_fsm inbound dispatch tests recovered from NeedMoreBytes regression

scripts/run-ci.sh 8 lanes pass (Layer 0 actionlint + A validate-workspace + A2 audit-mid + B verify-codegen + C1 cargo test + C2 cargo clippy --all-targets -D warnings + D deploy yaml + E ap_demo round-trip suite) exit=0

ap_demo_round_trip_against_zenoh_pico_z_put pass: interop with zenoh-pico restored after SCE 07afcc45 fix; pre-fix wz hit the bug on the (variant-dispatch + flag-bind on same import + beta-shape dispatcher leaf with flag-inputs) tuple which Phase D1 arm-side-only fixture had not covered; report drafted upstream fix landed same day
vendor/sce HEAD = 27accb35 confirmed

SCE 07afcc45 regression gate axis1_inversion_embed_dispatcher_arg_order (inline 4-codec fixture + 6-backend compile-smoke + Rust emit substring assertion + rustc round-trip across (M N) truth table) seals the coverage gap
carry_forward_bullets: R121h-perf-bump-3: TcpDriver hot path Vec::with_capacity(65537) + to_vec() perf reclaim (sink-based encode path now available via SCE B1-alpha)
R121j-5: inbound Request(Query) parser + on_query callback + QueryableTable (next major AP MVP gap; Query/Reply inbound 0% to 100%)

Round 121h-perf-bump-4.decision_summary: Three secondary encoders (encode_init / encode_open / encode_close) in session_glue.rs migrated from inner-Vec + extend_from_slice to sink-based encode, saving one allocation per call. Layer-3 byte-compare regression-zero.
changes_bullets: encode_init at crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs:1539-1547: pre-reserve Vec capacity = 1 (parent_flags | T_MID_INIT) + InitBody::MAX_ENCODED_BYTES + ext_bytes.len() instead of an ad-hoc body.zid.len() + cookie.len() + 12 estimate; wrap the wire Vec in VecSink after pushing the header byte and call body.encode(&mut sink, s, a) directly, eliminating the intermediate body.encode_to_vec(s, a) allocation. ext_bytes still extended at the end (encode_ext_chain is a separate concern not touched this round)

encode_open at session_glue.rs:1593-1600: same sink-based shape applied. Pre-reserve = 1 + OpenBody::MAX_ENCODED_BYTES + ext_bytes.len(); body.encode(&mut sink, a) replaces body.encode_to_vec(a) + extend_from_slice. Same ext_bytes extension at end

encode_close at session_glue.rs:1641-1648: pre-reserve = 1 + Close::MAX_ENCODED_BYTES (2 byte total worst-case); Close { reason }.encode(&mut sink) replaces the encode_to_vec + extend_from_slice pair. Most dramatic per-call ratio (2-byte wire was 2 allocs; now 1)

encode_ext_chain at session_glue.rs:1614-1633 (the 4th secondary site flagged in the R121h-perf-bump-3 carry) intentionally NOT touched this round -- the per-entry Z-bit byte-patching at index 0 of entry.encode_to_vec() output requires a deeper refactor (either expose a sink with positional rewind, or restructure the ext encode to emit the Z bit conditionally via a flag arg). Defer to a future R121h-perf-bump-5 carry

Round 121h-perf-bump-attempt.decision_summary: R121h-perf-bump-attempt investigated SCE drift +15 commits (vendor pin 54d513af -> SCE main de5d5e9a) and discovered the SCE B1-Оұ series (sink-based encode + encode_to_vec wrapper) already lands the exact zero-alloc fix our R121i-c carry parked as an SCE upstream proposal; deferred the actual vendor bump to a multi-round R121h-perf-bump phase because the Axis-1 inversion that ships in the same drift window strengthens the present-if predicate grammar and 11 wz codec SCXMLs need migration before the bump can land cleanly.
changes_bullets: vendor/sce submodule fetched SCE main; observed +15 commits since wz vendor pin 54d513af: 6 B1-Оұ (sink-based encode chain) + 8 Axis-1 / Axis-2 / Axis-3 / Axis-5 inversion + refactor + fmt commits. The B1-Оұ series alone is exactly the perf-debt repayment proposed in R121i-c carry; no SCE upstream PR needed.

Read sce-forge-runtime/rust/src/codec.rs:180-330 in the bumped tree. SCE B1-Оұ API surface confirmed: SceSink trait (object-safe, write_bytes / position / per-width helpers as default methods); SliceSink<'a> over &mut [u8] (raises BufferOverflow at capacity, no_std + no_alloc compatible вҖ” the natural MCU sink); VecSink<'a> over &mut Vec<u8> (alloc-feature-gated, infallible grow вҖ” the std hot path); encode_to_vec() preserved as a feature-gated wrapper for source-compat. Wire format byte-identical вҖ” wz's 14 byte-compare invariants would be preserved across the bump.

Attempted cargo check at the bumped pin -> wz-codecs build.rs panicked: 'codec init_body: field sn_res has sce:present-if="parent.вҖҰ" but parent is not declared earlier in this codec вҖ” present-if predicates must reference a flags-bearing carrier that the streaming decoder has already consumed; reorder the fields, or correct the predicate'. The Axis-1 inversion chain (36c0750e foundation + 63daab97 codegen wire-up + bf41498c 19-fixture migrate + 19b0fcf0 delete legacy RPF) tightened this grammar.

Surveyed `grep -l 'parent\.' sources/codecs/`: 11 wz codec SCXMLs in the migration surface вҖ” init_body, open_body, hello, join, decl_kexpr, decl_subscriber, decl_queryable, decl_token, wireexpr, wireexpr_local, wireexpr_nonlocal. All AP MVP-critical codec series. Migration cost is one round per ~3-4 codecs (~3 sub-rounds), exceeds this session's remaining context budget.

Read sce-forge-runtime/rust/src/codec.rs:180-330 in the bumped tree. SCE B1-Оұ API surface confirmed: SceSink trait (object-safe, write_bytes / position / per-width helpers as default methods); SliceSink<'a> over &mut [u8] (raises BufferOverflow at capacity, no_std + no_alloc compatible вҖ” the natural MCU sink); VecSink<'a> over &mut Vec<u8> (alloc-feature-gated, infallible grow вҖ” the std hot path); encode_to_vec() preserved as a feature-gated wrapper for source-compat. Wire format byte-identical вҖ” wz's 14 byte-compare invariants would be preserved across the bump.

Attempted cargo check at the bumped pin -> wz-codecs build.rs panicked: 'codec init_body: field sn_res has sce:present-if="parent.вҖҰ" but parent is not declared earlier in this codec вҖ” present-if predicates must reference a flags-bearing carrier that the streaming decoder has already consumed; reorder the fields, or correct the predicate'. The Axis-1 inversion chain (36c0750e foundation + 63daab97 codegen wire-up + bf41498c 19-fixture migrate + 19b0fcf0 delete legacy RPF) tightened this grammar.

changes_bullets: vendor/sce submodule pin e10619d3 to 54d513af (6 commits: 7d910c03 fmt; 948f73d0 refactor: Lift codec simple-path fields into let-bindings landed the textbook fix that lets BitSize::LengthRef reference the just-bound sibling local instead of indexing raw[len_off] for the length value; 03fbd7a0 test: Lock multi-byte length-ref via 256-byte runtime round-trip; dadca3f3 feat: Validate plain-id length-field sibling bit-size shape adds parse-time validation that length-field siblings are Fixed bits in {8,16,24,32} or VLE; 45d0f188 fmt; f7939e3f fix: Resolve cpp len name collision; ff616921 chore: pre-push fmt hook; 54d513af fix: Rename cpp struct local from value to _decoded).

sources/codecs/stream_envelope.scxml: new codec_kind authoring the streamed-link wire envelope (uint16 LE payload_len at byte 0 + bytes length-ref payload at byte 2, max-size=65535 matching the u16 wire ceiling). Mirrors zenoh-pico _z_link_send_t_msg (vendor/zenoh-pico/src/transport/common/tx.c) + _z_link_recv_t_msg_cap_flow_stream (rx.c) with _Z_MSG_LEN_ENC_SIZE=2 (vendor/zenoh-pico/include/zenoh-pico/protocol/definitions/core.h:32).
crates/wz-codecs/build.rs::CODECS: stream_envelope registered in leaf-codecs section (no imports; single-field plus length-ref pair).
crates/wz-codecs/src/lib.rs: pub mod stream_envelope block added next to ext_zbuf, exposing the generated StreamEnvelope struct.

TcpDriver send/poll_event wire-up (crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/lib.rs lines 160-181 + 191-224) deferred to R121h proper; this round only lands the codec catalog registration so the wire-up commit's diff stays focused on the inline-to-generated-call swap.
verification_bullets: cargo build --workspace: clean rebuild including vendor/sce/sce-build + sce-forge-runtime; no rustc warnings introduced.

cargo test --workspace: all suites green (38+ wz-runtime-tokio lib tests, 5 Layer E integration tests, wz-codecs smoke tests). Confirms the new vendor pin doesn't regress any existing codec; the let-binding refactor preserves wire bytes for codecs that used VLE length-field (ext_zbuf) or flags-accessor length-field (join.zid), since their generated decode paths were already let-binding-based.

bash scripts/run-ci.sh: all 8 lanes pass (Layer 0/A/A2/B/C1/C2/D/E). Layer B byte-compare against verify-codegen.sh Layer 2 oracle preserved; Layer E foreign interop with zenoh-pico 1.5.0 still green.

Generated crates/target/debug/build/wz-codecs-*/out/stream_envelope.rs decode body uses the new let-binding pattern: let payload_len = raw[0] as u16 | ((raw[1] as u16) << 8); let payload = raw[2..2 + payload_len as usize].to_vec(); the multi-byte payload_len reconstructs through the named local rather than truncating to raw[0]. Verified by direct read of the generated file post-rebuild.

Round 121i-a.decision_summary: R121i-a lands the first of the 7 remaining DECLARE sub-variants вҖ” Declare(DeclSubscriber) outbound вҖ” via build_declare_subscriber + SessionLinkActions::send_declare_subscriber, with 3-vector wire-byte regression test (alias/composite/literal cases) anchored to zenoh-pico _z_decl_subscriber_encode reference.
changes_bullets: crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs imports: add wz_codecs::decl_subscriber::DeclSubscriber.

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs build_declare_subscriber(subscriber_id, keyexpr_mapping_id, keyexpr_suffix) builder added next to build_declare_kexpr. Inner DeclSubscriber.header carries author-supplied MID(0x02) + optional N(0x20) suffix gate; the M bit (0x40) is codegen-derived from the wireexpr import's variant-dispatch flag=header.M declaration in decl_subscriber.scxml under the R121h-pre B5-nu ownership invert. Wireexpr arm fixed to Local (default arm per wireexpr.scxml default=true); literal-vs-alias-vs-composite is driven by the (mapping_id, suffix) pair following the same convention as build_push_aliased and build_declare_kexpr.

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs SessionLinkActions::send_declare_subscriber action added alongside send_declare_keyexpr. Hard-coded to the reliable channel for the same SN-window ordering reason as DECLARE-kexpr: the peer's subscriber table must be populated before any matching Push arrives, otherwise the peer drops it as unknown subscription.

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs tests: two new unit tests. build_declare_subscriber_wraps_decl_subscriber_in_declare_envelope exercises the 3 semantic cases (pure alias, composite alias+suffix, literal id=0+suffix) at the struct-shape level. build_declare_subscriber_emits_zenoh_pico_compatible_wire_bytes is the wire-byte regression gate вҖ” 3-vector byte-for-byte compare against zenoh-pico declarations.c:65-84 reference encoder. The literal case validates the wireexpr local-sentinel id=0 + VLE(len) + suffix bytes shape; the alias case validates the M-bit codegen derivation (header = 0x42 = MID|M); the composite case validates the N|M co-set header (0x62) + wireexpr id-prefix + suffix-tail emit.
verification_bullets: cargo test -p wz-runtime-tokio --lib build_declare_subscriber: both new tests pass. The byte-compare test confirms the 3 emit shapes match zenoh-pico's _z_decl_subscriber_encode + _z_decl_commons_encode byte-for-byte; any future codegen drift on either header derivation, wireexpr arm selection, or the parent.M dispatch path surfaces immediately.

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs build_declare_subscriber(subscriber_id, keyexpr_mapping_id, keyexpr_suffix) builder added next to build_declare_kexpr. Inner DeclSubscriber.header carries author-supplied MID(0x02) + optional N(0x20) suffix gate; the M bit (0x40) is codegen-derived from the wireexpr import's variant-dispatch flag=header.M declaration in decl_subscriber.scxml under the R121h-pre B5-nu ownership invert. Wireexpr arm fixed to Local (default arm per wireexpr.scxml default=true); literal-vs-alias-vs-composite is driven by the (mapping_id, suffix) pair following the same convention as build_push_aliased and build_declare_kexpr.

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs SessionLinkActions::send_declare_subscriber action added alongside send_declare_keyexpr. Hard-coded to the reliable channel for the same SN-window ordering reason as DECLARE-kexpr: the peer's subscriber table must be populated before any matching Push arrives, otherwise the peer drops it as unknown subscription.

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs tests: two new unit tests. build_declare_subscriber_wraps_decl_subscriber_in_declare_envelope exercises the 3 semantic cases (pure alias, composite alias+suffix, literal id=0+suffix) at the struct-shape level. build_declare_subscriber_emits_zenoh_pico_compatible_wire_bytes is the wire-byte regression gate вҖ” 3-vector byte-for-byte compare against zenoh-pico declarations.c:65-84 reference encoder. The literal case validates the wireexpr local-sentinel id=0 + VLE(len) + suffix bytes shape; the alias case validates the M-bit codegen derivation (header = 0x42 = MID|M); the composite case validates the N|M co-set header (0x62) + wireexpr id-prefix + suffix-tail emit.

Round 121i-b.decision_summary: R121i-b lands the second and third of the 7 remaining DECLARE sub-variants вҖ” Declare(DeclQueryable) and Declare(DeclToken) outbound вҖ” via build_declare_queryable / build_declare_token + matching SessionLinkActions send actions, each with a 3-vector wire-byte regression test (alias/composite/literal) anchored to zenoh-pico _z_decl_queryable_encode and _z_decl_token_encode references.
changes_bullets: crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs imports: add wz_codecs::decl_queryable::DeclQueryable and wz_codecs::decl_token::DeclToken alongside the existing DeclSubscriber import.

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs build_declare_queryable(queryable_id, keyexpr_mapping_id, keyexpr_suffix) builder added next to build_declare_subscriber. AP MVP scope: emits the has_info_ext=false shape (no _Z_MSG_EXT_ENC_ZINT|0x01 ExtQueryableInfo tail), matching the default _z_queryable_infos_t shipped by z_query_consolidation_default. A separate build_declare_queryable_with_info helper for complete=true / non-zero distance lands in R121j+. Inner DeclQueryable.header carries author-supplied MID(0x04) + optional N(0x20); the M bit (0x40) is codegen-derived from the wireexpr import's variant-dispatch flag=header.M declaration in decl_queryable.scxml.

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs build_declare_token(token_id, keyexpr_mapping_id, keyexpr_suffix) builder added. DeclToken has no extension surface at all in zenoh-pico (_z_decl_token_encode is a pure _z_decl_commons_encode(has_ext=false) wrapper at declarations.c:123-126), so the builder's emit shape is byte-stable across every (id, mapping, suffix) input. Inner DeclToken.header carries MID(0x06) + optional N + codegen-derived M.

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs SessionLinkActions::send_declare_queryable + SessionLinkActions::send_declare_token actions added alongside send_declare_subscriber. Both hard-coded to the reliable channel for the same SN-window ordering reason as the rest of the DECLARE outbound paths: the peer's queryable/token table must be populated before any matching Request(Query) or liveliness fan-out arrives.

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs tests: four new unit tests. {build_declare_queryable, build_declare_token}_wraps_*_in_declare_envelope exercise the 3 semantic cases (pure alias, composite alias+suffix, literal id=0+suffix) at struct-shape level. {build_declare_queryable, build_declare_token}_emits_zenoh_pico_compatible_wire_bytes are the wire-byte regression gates вҖ” 3-vector byte-for-byte compare against zenoh-pico declarations.c:105-126 reference encoders, pinning the MID-0x04 and MID-0x06 alias/composite/literal trios.
verification_bullets: cargo test -p wz-runtime-tokio --lib -- build_declare_queryable build_declare_token: all 4 new tests pass. The byte-compare gates confirm the 6 emit shapes (2 builders x 3 vectors) match zenoh-pico's _z_decl_queryable_encode + _z_decl_token_encode byte-for-byte; any future codegen drift on header derivation, wireexpr arm selection, or the parent.M dispatch path on either DeclQueryable or DeclToken surfaces immediately.
cargo test -p wz-runtime-tokio --lib: all 44 lib tests green (40 prior + 4 new R121i-b); no regression on the existing R121g build_declare_kexpr + R121i-a build_declare_subscriber byte-compare gates.
bash scripts/run-ci.sh: all 8 lanes pass (Layer 0/A/A2/B/C1/C2/D/E). No new clippy warnings under Layer C2 (--deny warnings); warnings=deny workspace policy preserved.
validate_workspace baseline: docs=12/12, T1 orphan=0 (no new), round-trip mandatory 12/12, GENERATED.md=sync; atomic ledger entries advance 163 -> 164, sections=214 unchanged.

SCE drift verification: SCE upstream HEAD = 943786d6 (Seat AuthClassifier under sce/include/mesh/third_party вҖ” internal component refactor) is +1 commit past vendor pin 54d513af, but cargo check -p wz-codecs -p wz-runtime-tokio builds cleanly without bumping the pin. AuthClassifier is not referenced by any codec generation surface; the drift is wz-codegen-neutral and stays on the SCE side until a wz-actionable reason emerges.
carry_forward_bullets: R121i-c next: Undecl_* 4 variants + DeclFinal. UndeclKexpr (MID 0x01) is the simplest вҖ” header + VLE(id) per declarations.c:86-89, no wireexpr field. UndeclSubscriber (0x03) / UndeclQueryable (0x05) / UndeclToken (0x07) follow the _z_undecl_encode shape at declarations.c:90-101: [header, VLE(id), optional _Z_FLAG_Z_Z ext keyexpr]. DeclFinal (0x1A) is a single-byte marker (_z_decl_final_encode at declarations.c:131-135). Each variant gets a build_* helper + send_* action + byte-compare test (Undecl_* needs both Z-ext-present and Z-ext-absent vectors since the ext is optional).

R121h-perf zero-alloc encode debt unchanged: codec-generated StreamEnvelope::encode and the *.encode() bodies all do Vec::with_capacity(65537) (or 267 for declarations) per call, and TcpDriver::send adds a frame.bytes.to_vec()+extend pair on top. AP functional OK but production-perf debt. Repayment path: SCE upstream zero-alloc encode_into(&mut Vec<u8>) variant feature proposal (textbook per SCE-is-priority directive) OR wz fast-path that bypasses codec for the fits-in-u16 hot path (codec SSOT still locked by byte-compare tests).

DECLARE outbound Nonlocal arm silent gap: build_declare_{subscriber,queryable,token} are all fixed to WireexprLocal. Interest/Reply paths in R121j may need Nonlocal coverage when the referenced mapping is peer-declared rather than local-declared; audit + extend builders with a Nonlocal arm parameter at that point.

Round 121i-c.decision_summary: R121i-c closes the DECLARE sub-variant cluster вҖ” Undecl_* four variants (UndeclKexpr / UndeclSubscriber / UndeclQueryable / UndeclToken) plus DeclFinal terminator вҖ” via build_undeclare_* + build_declare_final builders, matching SessionLinkActions send actions, and 5 wire-byte byte-compare tests anchored to zenoh-pico declarations.c:86-103 + :131-135 references.
changes_bullets: crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs imports: add wz_codecs::decl_final::DeclFinal, wz_codecs::undecl_kexpr::UndeclKexpr, wz_codecs::undecl_subscriber::UndeclSubscriber, wz_codecs::undecl_queryable::UndeclQueryable, wz_codecs::undecl_token::UndeclToken alongside the existing Decl_* imports.

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs build_undeclare_kexpr(mapping_id) builder added: emits [header(_Z_UNDECL_KEXPR_MID=0x01), VLE(mapping_id)] mirroring zenoh-pico _z_undecl_kexpr_encode at declarations.c:86-89. No wireexpr body, no Z ext surface (unlike the other three Undecl_* variants); the retraction is purely id-based because the peer already has the (id -> keyexpr) entry from a prior DeclKexpr.

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs build_undeclare_subscriber / build_undeclare_queryable / build_undeclare_token builders added: each emits [header(MID 0x03/0x05/0x07), VLE(id)] mirroring _z_undecl_subscriber_encode / _z_undecl_queryable_encode / _z_undecl_token_encode at declarations.c:90-130 with has_keyexpr_ext=false. AP MVP scope: the wz codec for these does not model the optional _z_decl_ext_keyexpr_encode tail (declarations.c:38-50) вҖ” SCXML stops at id. Future router cross-validation paths that need the ext_keyexpr surface extend sources/codecs/undecl_*.scxml + add _with_keyexpr builders; the no-ext contract here stays byte-stable.

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs build_declare_final() builder added: single-byte marker (header=_Z_DECL_FINAL_MID=0x1A) per _z_decl_final_encode at declarations.c:131-135. No body, no id, no ext вҖ” the full wire is exactly 2 bytes (N_MID_DECLARE envelope + DeclFinal.header). Reserved for the future Interest/Reply path (R121j+) that needs to close multi-DECLARE reply batches; the unsolicited DECLARE outbound path the AP MVP uses today does not emit DeclFinal.

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs SessionLinkActions: 5 new send_* actions added вҖ” send_undeclare_kexpr / send_undeclare_subscriber / send_undeclare_queryable / send_undeclare_token / send_declare_final. All hard-coded to the reliable channel for the same SN-window ordering reason as the DECLARE outbound path: the peer must observe the retraction before any later Push that still aliases the retracted id, otherwise the peer dispatches to the stale entry.

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs tests: 5 new unit tests covering the 5 builders. build_undeclare_{kexpr,subscriber,queryable,token}_emits_zenoh_pico_compatible_wire_bytes each lock 2 vectors: id=42 (single-byte VLE) + id=200 (multi-byte VLE boundary, encodes as 0xC8 0x01 вҖ” the 7-bit VLE boundary). build_declare_final_emits_two_byte_marker pins the stateless [0x1E, 0x1A] shape. Each test also includes an inner-arm sanity check so a future codegen drift on the DeclareVariant arm dispatch surfaces immediately.
verification_bullets: cargo test -p wz-runtime-tokio --lib -- build_undeclare build_declare_final: all 5 new tests pass. The byte-compare gates confirm the 9 emit shapes (4 Undecl_* x 2 vectors + DeclFinal x 1) match zenoh-pico's reference encoders byte-for-byte; the multi-byte VLE boundary vector (id=200) protects against any future regression on the VLE writer's continuation-bit emit logic.
cargo test -p wz-runtime-tokio --lib: all 49 lib tests green (44 prior + 5 new R121i-c); no regression on the existing R121g/R121i-a/R121i-b byte-compare gates.
bash scripts/run-ci.sh: all 8 lanes pass (Layer 0/A/A2/B/C1/C2/D/E). No new clippy warnings under Layer C2 (--deny warnings); warnings=deny workspace policy preserved.
validate_workspace baseline: docs=12/12, T1 orphan=0 (no new), round-trip mandatory 12/12, GENERATED.md=sync; atomic ledger entries advance 164 -> 165, sections=214 unchanged.
carry_forward_bullets: R121i cluster fully closed: 7/7 DECLARE outbound variants land with builders + send actions + byte-compare gates. Wire-codec catalog: 88%+ (was 82% post-R121i-a). Coverage matrix: DeclKexpr (R121g), DeclSubscriber (R121i-a), DeclQueryable + DeclToken (R121i-b), UndeclKexpr + UndeclSubscriber + UndeclQueryable + UndeclToken + DeclFinal (R121i-c). All anchored to zenoh-pico declarations.c references.

Next-round candidates ranked by debt-priority: (1) R121h-perf zero-alloc encode debt repayment (1 round, Option A wz fast-path vs Option B SCE upstream encode_into proposal); (2) R121j Query/Reply chain (4-6 rounds, AP MVP's last large area, raises Query/Reply 10% -> 100%); (3) R123b-pre + R123b deploy yaml flat -> nested topology schema (2 rounds). Recommendation: R121h-perf first since it is short and unblocks production-perf gating; R121j second.

Undecl_* ext-keyexpr tail still uncovered: when zenoh router cross-validation paths surface (peer-of-peer scenarios where the retraction needs the keyexpr proof), extend sources/codecs/undecl_subscriber.scxml + undecl_queryable.scxml + undecl_token.scxml with the optional ext_keyexpr field, then add _with_keyexpr builder variants. Out of scope for AP MVP.

Nonlocal arm silent gap (R121i-a/b/c): all DECLARE builders are fixed to WireexprLocal default. Interest/Reply paths in R121j may need Nonlocal coverage when the referenced mapping is peer-declared rather than local-declared; audit + extend builders with a Nonlocal arm parameter at that point.
