crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs tests: 5 new unit tests covering the 5 builders. build_undeclare_{kexpr,subscriber,queryable,token}_emits_zenoh_pico_compatible_wire_bytes each lock 2 vectors: id=42 (single-byte VLE) + id=200 (multi-byte VLE boundary, encodes as 0xC8 0x01 вҖ” the 7-bit VLE boundary). build_declare_final_emits_two_byte_marker pins the stateless [0x1E, 0x1A] shape. Each test also includes an inner-arm sanity check so a future codegen drift on the DeclareVariant arm dispatch surfaces immediately.
publishable_verification_bullets: cargo test -p wz-runtime-tokio --lib -- build_undeclare build_declare_final: all 5 new tests pass. The byte-compare gates confirm the 9 emit shapes (4 Undecl_* x 2 vectors + DeclFinal x 1) match zenoh-pico's reference encoders byte-for-byte; the multi-byte VLE boundary vector (id=200) protects against any future regression on the VLE writer's continuation-bit emit logic.
cargo test -p wz-runtime-tokio --lib: all 49 lib tests green (44 prior + 5 new R121i-c); no regression on the existing R121g/R121i-a/R121i-b byte-compare gates.
bash scripts/run-ci.sh: all 8 lanes pass (Layer 0/A/A2/B/C1/C2/D/E). No new clippy warnings under Layer C2 (--deny warnings); warnings=deny workspace policy preserved.
validate_workspace baseline: docs=12/12, T1 orphan=0 (no new), round-trip mandatory 12/12, GENERATED.md=sync; atomic ledger entries advance 164 -> 165, sections=214 unchanged.
publishable_carry_forward_bullets: R121i cluster fully closed: 7/7 DECLARE outbound variants land with builders + send actions + byte-compare gates. Wire-codec catalog: 88%+ (was 82% post-R121i-a). Coverage matrix: DeclKexpr (R121g), DeclSubscriber (R121i-a), DeclQueryable + DeclToken (R121i-b), UndeclKexpr + UndeclSubscriber + UndeclQueryable + UndeclToken + DeclFinal (R121i-c). All anchored to zenoh-pico declarations.c references.

Next-round candidates ranked by debt-priority: (1) R121h-perf zero-alloc encode debt repayment (1 round, Option A wz fast-path vs Option B SCE upstream encode_into proposal); (2) R121j Query/Reply chain (4-6 rounds, AP MVP's last large area, raises Query/Reply 10% -> 100%); (3) R123b-pre + R123b deploy yaml flat -> nested topology schema (2 rounds). Recommendation: R121h-perf first since it is short and unblocks production-perf gating; R121j second.

Undecl_* ext-keyexpr tail still uncovered: when zenoh router cross-validation paths surface (peer-of-peer scenarios where the retraction needs the keyexpr proof), extend sources/codecs/undecl_subscriber.scxml + undecl_queryable.scxml + undecl_token.scxml with the optional ext_keyexpr field, then add _with_keyexpr builder variants. Out of scope for AP MVP.

Nonlocal arm silent gap (R121i-a/b/c): all DECLARE builders are fixed to WireexprLocal default. Interest/Reply paths in R121j may need Nonlocal coverage when the referenced mapping is peer-declared rather than local-declared; audit + extend builders with a Nonlocal arm parameter at that point.

Round 121i-d.decision_summary: R121i-d closes the WireexprNonlocal-arm silent gap for DECLARE builders by adding three companion builders (subscriber / queryable / token), keeping the existing Local-arm builders untouched.
changes_bullets: crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs вҖ” added `use wz_codecs::wireexpr_nonlocal::WireexprNonlocal;` import.

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs вҖ” added `build_declare_subscriber_nonlocal` / `build_declare_queryable_nonlocal` / `build_declare_token_nonlocal` (post-R121i-b `build_declare_token`). Same `(id, mapping_id, suffix)` signature as the Local-arm builders; inner `Wireexpr` arm flipped to `WireexprNonlocal`; codegen-derived M bit (0x00 vs Local's 0x40 вҖ” verified at `wz-codecs-*/out/decl_subscriber.rs` line 134's `_derived_header` match). `build_declare_kexpr` deliberately has no Nonlocal companion вҖ” DeclKexpr's encoder-perspective locality is by construction Local (sender installs the (id, literal) pair into its OWN mapping table).

Each `_nonlocal` builder panics on `keyexpr_mapping_id == 0` mirroring `build_declare_kexpr_rejects_zero_mapping_id`. Rationale: zenoh-pico's `_Z_KEYEXPR_MAPPING_LOCAL` sentinel is `(uintptr_t)0` (core.h:151), so a remote-mapped id=0 refers to the peer's literal-sentinel slot which carries no table entry вҖ” the literal case is only representable in the Local arm via `build_declare_subscriber(_, 0, Some(s))`.

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs вҖ” added six unit tests in the `tests` mod (one byte-compare + one panic-rejection per builder). Each byte-compare locks alias (mapping_id=7, no suffix) / composite (mapping_id=7 + Some("abc")) / multi-byte VLE boundary (mapping_id=200, no suffix) вҖ” three vectors per builder. Locked wire bytes: 0x02 / 0x22 / 0x02 (Nonlocal subscriber header), 0x04 / 0x24 / 0x04 (queryable), 0x06 / 0x26 / 0x06 (token) вҖ” all with the M bit clear (Local-arm counterparts emit 0x42 / 0x62, 0x44 / 0x64, 0x46 / 0x66).

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs вҖ” fixed three pre-existing `clippy::doc_overindented_list_items` errors in the R121j-1 `build_request_query_emits_zenoh_pico_compatible_wire_bytes` doc comment (lines 5139, 5142, 5143) by realigning the bullet continuation lines from 7-space indent to 5-space content-aligned indent. These only surfaced once `--all-targets` was added to the clippy invocation locally; the prior session's `cargo clippy` runs did not include test docs.
verification_bullets: Targeted: `cargo test -p wz-runtime-tokio --lib build_declare_subscriber_nonlocal build_declare_queryable_nonlocal build_declare_token_nonlocal` вҖ” 6 passed, 0 failed (3 byte-compare + 3 panic-rejection).
Regression: `cargo test -p wz-runtime-tokio --lib` вҖ” 60 passed, 0 failed (up from 54; +6 R121i-d tests).
Layer C1 equivalent: `cargo test --workspace` вҖ” 6 suites ok, 0 failed.
Layer C2 equivalent: `cargo clippy -p wz-runtime-tokio --all-targets -- -D warnings` вҖ” clean (after the R121j-1 doc reindent fix above).

Layer E flaky verification (kickoff blocking #0): `bash scripts/run-ci.sh --layer E` at HEAD 83892ab pre-R121i-d вҖ” 5/5 tests pass. The 11th-session pre-push hook FAIL on the ledger-only commit 83892ab was therefore a transient flake, not a wz src regression; no carry-forward needed.
carry_forward_bullets: Inbound DECLARE parser (R121j-5 territory). Outbound 7/7 declarations now cover both M=0 and M=1 arms; once inbound lands, the peer-declared-mapping reception вҶ’ wz Nonlocal-arm reply cross-validation can fire end-to-end.

Request(Query) layered helpers (consolidation / params / Q-level exts / Request-level exts) carry intact from R121j-1.

Round 121j-1.decision_summary: R121j-1 opens the Query/Reply chain вҖ” Request(Query) outbound вҖ” with build_request_query + SessionLinkActions::send_request_query + encode_frame_with_request (PUSH/DECLARE envelope wrapper mirror), each anchored to zenoh-pico _z_request_encode + _z_query_encode references and gated by 3-vector wire-byte regression tests. AP MVP minimal shape only (no consolidation / params / Query-level exts / Request-level exts); layered helpers for consolidation / params / target / budget / timeout_ms land in follow-up R121j sub-rounds.
changes_bullets: crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs imports: replace `use wz_codecs::request::Request;` with `use wz_codecs::request::{Request, RequestVariant};` (the variant enum is needed by the builder) and add `use wz_codecs::query::Query;` for the inner body type.

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs build_request_query(rid, keyexpr_mapping_id, keyexpr_suffix) builder added next to build_declare_final. Emits the minimal AP MVP shape: Request.header = _Z_MID_N_REQUEST(0x1C) | optional N(0x20) suffix gate, with M(0x40) codegen-derived from the WireexprLocal arm via the same <sce:variant-dispatch flag=header.M> mechanism the DECLARE builders use. Inner Query.header = _Z_MID_Z_QUERY(0x03) only вҖ” no C(consolidation) / P(params) / Z(exts) flags. Doc comment enumerates the three layered follow-up helpers (build_request_query_with_consolidation / _with_parameters / _with_exts) that future rounds will add.

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs encode_frame_with_request(sn, request, reliable) helper added: composes the one transport-envelope header byte that lives outside the body codec's scope (parent_flags | T_MID_FRAME) with Frame {sn, payload: request.encode()}.encode() body. Mirror of encode_frame_with_push (R121e) and encode_frame_with_declare (R121g); the reliable-channel default matches the DECLARE outbound contract (SN-window ordering ensures the peer sees the Query before any Response/ResponseFinal that depends on the rid).

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs SessionLinkActions::send_request_query(rid, mapping_id, suffix) action added: build_request_query -> next_outbound_frame_sn -> encode_frame_with_request -> driver.send_blocking(Reliable). Same SN-window ordering reason as the DECLARE outbound path; unreliable Query is unsupported by this minimal builder because the local z_get future would hang on a dropped Query.

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs tests: three new unit tests. build_request_query_wraps_query_in_request_envelope exercises the 3 semantic cases (alias / composite / literal) at struct-shape level, verifying the minimal-shape Query fields (consolidation/params/exts all None). build_request_query_emits_zenoh_pico_compatible_wire_bytes is the wire-byte regression gate вҖ” 3-vector byte-for-byte compare against zenoh-pico network.c:114-169 + message.c:394-451, pinning the alias [0x5C 0x2A 0x07 0x03] / composite [0x7C 0x2A 0x07 0x03 'abc' 0x03] / literal [0x7C 0x2A 0x00 0x09 'demo/test' 0x03] shapes. encode_frame_with_request_wraps_request_in_frame_envelope validates the Frame envelope wrap mirrors encode_frame_with_push/declare for both reliable and best-effort parent-flag paths.
verification_bullets: cargo test -p wz-runtime-tokio --lib -- build_request_query encode_frame_with_request: all 3 new tests pass. The byte-compare gate confirms the 3 emit shapes match zenoh-pico's _z_request_encode + _z_query_encode byte-for-byte; any future codegen drift on Request header derivation, wireexpr arm selection, Query MID emission, or the Z/M/N flag dispatch surfaces immediately.
cargo test -p wz-runtime-tokio --lib: all 52 lib tests green (49 prior + 3 new R121j-1); no regression on the R121g/R121i-a/b/c byte-compare gates.
bash scripts/run-ci.sh: all 8 lanes pass (Layer 0/A/A2/B/C1/C2/D/E). No new clippy warnings under Layer C2; warnings=deny workspace policy preserved.
validate_workspace baseline: docs=12/12, T1 orphan=0 (no new), round-trip mandatory 12/12, GENERATED.md=sync; atomic ledger entries advance 165 -> 166, sections=214 unchanged.
carry_forward_bullets: R121j-2 next: inbound Request(Query) parser + dispatch. zenoh-pico _z_request_decode (network.c:209-238) is the reference. The wz Request codec's generated decode() already handles the byte parse; R121j-2 wires the decoded Request into the on_query callback path on the receive side. Question to resolve: where does the on_query callback registration live in wz вҖ” SessionLinkActions or a new QueryableTable owned by the session?

R121j-3 next: Response(Reply) outbound. zenoh-pico _z_response_encode (network.c:241-321) + _z_reply_encode (message.c reply path). Should be symmetrical to Request(Query) but with the inner body being Reply instead of Query. Reply body carries the actual payload (z_value) so this round wires up the value codec too.

R121j-4 next: Response(Err) + ResponseFinal outbound. ResponseFinal terminates a multi-reply sequence so the local z_get future can resolve. Inbound dispatch for Reply / Err / ResponseFinal on rid follows R121j-5.

Layered Query helpers for full Z_FEATURE_QUERY support: build_request_query_with_consolidation(mode) + _with_parameters(params) + _with_exts(body/info/attachment). Each layered helper extends the byte-compare contract with its own vectors. Out of scope for R121j-1 minimal shape but needed for AP MVP closure.

Query exts (body/info/attachment) require the ExtEntry / Value codec wiring вҖ” verify these codec modules are already published by wz-codecs (lib.rs:98-103 lists ext_entry + ext_envelope; check Value too) before R121j-3 enters.

Round 121j-1a.decision_summary: R121j-1a adds the consolidation layered helper on top of R121j-1's minimal Request(Query) builder, paying down the first quarter of the Q-helpers carry without touching the inbound path.
changes_bullets: crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs вҖ” added `pub enum ConsolidationMode { None, Monotonic, Latest }` mirroring zenoh-pico's `z_consolidation_mode_t` (constants.h:185-187) for the three transmitted values. `Z_CONSOLIDATION_MODE_AUTO = -1` is intentionally NOT representable since zenoh-pico's encoder treats AUTO as the `do not transmit` sentinel вҖ” callers wanting that case use plain `build_request_query`.

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs вҖ” added `ConsolidationMode::wire_byte(self) -> u8` const fn returning 0/1/2 per the zenoh-pico enum integer values. Const-fn so the mapping is verifiable at compile time.

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs вҖ” added `build_request_query_with_consolidation(rid, mapping_id, suffix, consolidation)`. Built on top of `build_request_query` (calls it then mutates the inner `Query` body via the `RequestVariant::CodecZenohQuery` arm match): ORs Q_C(0x20) into `Query.header` and sets `Query.consolidation = Some(consolidation.wire_byte())`. The 1-byte consolidation field then emits at offset 4 of the Request wire shape (after the existing 4-byte minimal-shape prefix). No params, no exts вҖ” those are separate layered helpers (R121j-1b/c/d carry).

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs вҖ” added two unit tests. `build_request_query_with_consolidation_emits_zenoh_pico_compatible_wire_bytes` byte-compare-locks the three modes (alias case, mapping_id=7, no suffix): NONE вҶ’ 0x5C 0x2A 0x07 0x23 0x00; MONOTONIC вҶ’ ...0x23 0x01; LATEST вҶ’ ...0x23 0x02. The 0x23 = MID(0x03) | Q_C(0x20) replaces the minimal-shape 0x03. `consolidation_mode_wire_byte_matches_zenoh_pico_enum_values` independently locks the enum-to-byte mapping so a refactor of the const fn without an encoder change still catches the regression.
verification_bullets: Targeted: `cargo test -p wz-runtime-tokio --lib build_request_query_with_consolidation consolidation_mode` вҖ” 2 passed, 0 failed.

Round 121j-1a.publishable_decision_summary: R121j-1a adds the consolidation layered helper on top of R121j-1's minimal Request(Query) builder, paying down the first quarter of the Q-helpers carry without touching the inbound path.
publishable_changes_bullets: crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs вҖ” added `pub enum ConsolidationMode { None, Monotonic, Latest }` mirroring zenoh-pico's `z_consolidation_mode_t` (constants.h:185-187) for the three transmitted values. `Z_CONSOLIDATION_MODE_AUTO = -1` is intentionally NOT representable since zenoh-pico's encoder treats AUTO as the `do not transmit` sentinel вҖ” callers wanting that case use plain `build_request_query`.

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs вҖ” added `ConsolidationMode::wire_byte(self) -> u8` const fn returning 0/1/2 per the zenoh-pico enum integer values. Const-fn so the mapping is verifiable at compile time.

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs вҖ” added `build_request_query_with_consolidation(rid, mapping_id, suffix, consolidation)`. Built on top of `build_request_query` (calls it then mutates the inner `Query` body via the `RequestVariant::CodecZenohQuery` arm match): ORs Q_C(0x20) into `Query.header` and sets `Query.consolidation = Some(consolidation.wire_byte())`. The 1-byte consolidation field then emits at offset 4 of the Request wire shape (after the existing 4-byte minimal-shape prefix). No params, no exts вҖ” those are separate layered helpers (R121j-1b/c/d carry).

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs вҖ” added two unit tests. `build_request_query_with_consolidation_emits_zenoh_pico_compatible_wire_bytes` byte-compare-locks the three modes (alias case, mapping_id=7, no suffix): NONE вҶ’ 0x5C 0x2A 0x07 0x23 0x00; MONOTONIC вҶ’ ...0x23 0x01; LATEST вҶ’ ...0x23 0x02. The 0x23 = MID(0x03) | Q_C(0x20) replaces the minimal-shape 0x03. `consolidation_mode_wire_byte_matches_zenoh_pico_enum_values` independently locks the enum-to-byte mapping so a refactor of the const fn without an encoder change still catches the regression.

Round 121j-1b.decision_summary: R121j-1b adds the parameters layered helper, paying down the second quarter of the Q-helpers carry (R121j-1a closed consolidation; this closes parameters).
changes_bullets: crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs вҖ” added `pub const REQUEST_QUERY_PARAMETERS_MAX_LEN: usize = 256` mirroring sources/codecs/query.scxml's `sce:max-size="256"` on the parameters field.

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs вҖ” added `build_request_query_with_parameters(rid, mapping_id, suffix, params)`. Built on top of `build_request_query` (same one-shot composition pattern as R121j-1a): ORs Q_P(0x40) into `Query.header`, sets `parameters_len = Some(params.len() as u64)` and `parameters = Some(params.to_vec())`. The codec gates both `parameters_len` and `parameters` fields on `header.P` (query.scxml line 79 / 81), so the flag + paired Optional fields stay byte-coherent.

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs вҖ” added two assertion gates. `assert!(!params.is_empty(), ...)` rejects the empty-slice case (zenoh-pico's `has_params` predicate at message.c:398 is `_z_slice_check && len > 0`, so an empty slice would silently clear Q_P and emit no params вҖ” the caller's intent is then `build_request_query` no-params). `assert!(params.len() <= REQUEST_QUERY_PARAMETERS_MAX_LEN, ...)` rejects oversize at the call site rather than at the peer's decoder.

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs вҖ” added three unit tests in the `tests` mod. Byte-compare locks small-params (k=v) + multi-byte VLE boundary (params_len=128 вҶ’ 0x80 0x01) + max-size (256-byte payload вҶ’ VLE 0x80 0x02). Two `#[should_panic]` tests guard the empty-slice and over-max rejections.

Out of scope (audit-traced carry): `_implicit_anyke` selector convention (zenoh-pico message.c:414-425 prepends `_Z_QUERY_PARAMS_KEY_ANYKE` for `**` / `?` selectors). AP MVP simple `z_get` does not set it; a future helper `build_request_query_with_parameters_and_anyke` layers the anyke-prepend when needed.
verification_bullets: Targeted: `cargo test -p wz-runtime-tokio --lib build_request_query_with_parameters` вҖ” 3 passed, 0 failed (1 byte-compare + 2 panic-rejection).
Regression: `cargo test -p wz-runtime-tokio --lib` вҖ” 65 passed, 0 failed (up from 62 at R121j-1a closure; +3 R121j-1b tests).

Layer C2 equivalent: `cargo clippy -p wz-runtime-tokio --all-targets -- -D warnings` вҖ” clean after a single doc-comment rewrite (line 2609 had `> [REQUEST_QUERY_PARAMETERS_MAX_LEN]` which clippy `doc_lazy_continuation` interpreted as a missing-marker quote; reworded to `above` prose form).
carry_forward_bullets: R121j-1c/d carry intact вҖ” layered helpers for Q-level exts (Q_Z + ext_entry chain for source_info ext_id=0x01 / value ext_id=0x03 / attachment ext_id=0x05; needs Value codec wiring investigation for the value ext) and Request-level exts (outer N_Z + qos / tstamp / target / budget / timeout_ms ext_ids).

Open compositional gap: R121j-1a (consolidation) + R121j-1b (parameters) are one-shot helpers that do not compose. A caller wanting both layered must mutate the Request body manually. If 1c or 1d also need composition with the existing layers, the textbook fix is to introduce a `RequestQueryExt` trait with `with_consolidation` / `with_parameters` / `with_*` fluent chain methods and refactor the existing one-shot helpers into thin wrappers over the trait. Defer the trait extraction until composition is genuinely needed (YAGNI вҖ” R121j-1c may reveal that exts can be set independently without inter-layer composition concerns).

Round 121j-1c.decision_summary: R121j-1c adds the Query attachment-extension layered helper, paying down the third quarter of the Q-helpers carry; source_info and body(Value) exts deferred to separate rounds (sub-codec wiring complexity).
changes_bullets: crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs вҖ” added `use wz_codecs::ext_zbuf::ExtZbuf;` import (joins existing ExtEntry / ExtEntryVariant / ExtZint imports).

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs вҖ” added `pub const QUERY_EXT_ZBUF_MAX_LEN: usize = 32` mirroring sources/codecs/ext_zbuf.scxml's `sce:max-size="32"` on the value field. The wz ExtZbuf is shared across all ZBuf-encoded Query exts (attachment / value-as-payload / source_info-as-payload); a future bump would need either an ExtZbufLarge arm or a codec max-size lift.

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs вҖ” added `build_request_query_with_attachment(rid, mapping_id, suffix, attachment)`. One-shot pattern mirroring R121j-1a/1b: calls `build_request_query` then mutates the Query body to set Q_Z flag (0x80, the network-message-layer extensions-present bit at `_Z_FLAG_Z_Z`) and append a single ExtEntry with header=`ENC_ZBUF(0x40)|id_attachment(0x05)=0x45` and ExtZbuf body. M=0 (attachment is informational, not mandatory), entry-Z=0 (single-ext chain, no successor).

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs вҖ” added two assertion gates. Empty attachment rejected (zenoh-pico's `_z_msg_query_required_extensions` only sets attachment requirement on non-empty bytes вҖ” the empty case is functionally `build_request_query` with no ext). Oversize rejected at QUERY_EXT_ZBUF_MAX_LEN=32 to surface the wz codec bound at the call site rather than as a runtime decoder error.

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs вҖ” added three unit tests in the `tests` mod. Byte-compare locks small attachment (b"hi") + at-max (32-byte sequence 0..32). Two `#[should_panic]` tests guard empty-slice and over-max rejections. Inner-arm sanity check verifies the ExtEntry.header byte + ExtZbuf body shape independently.

Audit-traced carry: source_info ext (ext_id=0x01, body=source_id zid+eid+source_sn struct) and body(Value) ext (ext_id=0x03, body=encoding+payload pair) are scope-deferred. source_info needs the source_info codec wired (currently wz has no SourceInfo codec module вҖ” SCXML authoring required); body(Value) ext needs the Value codec wired. AP MVP client-side z_get does not need either; both are router/queryable surfaces.
verification_bullets: Targeted: `cargo test -p wz-runtime-tokio --lib build_request_query_with_attachment` вҖ” 3 passed, 0 failed.
Regression: `cargo test -p wz-runtime-tokio --lib` вҖ” 68 passed, 0 failed (up from 65 at R121j-1b closure; +3 R121j-1c tests).
Layer C2 equivalent: `cargo clippy -p wz-runtime-tokio --all-targets -- -D warnings` вҖ” clean.
carry_forward_bullets: R121j-1d carry intact вҖ” Request-level exts (outer N_Z(0x80) + Request.extensions tlv-chain for qos / tstamp / target / budget / timeout_ms ext_ids). The wz Request codec already has the `extensions` Optional field generated from request.scxml; the helper pattern mirrors this round. Each ext sub-type (qos / tstamp / etc.) is its own ext_id + body-encoding combination вҖ” likely needs sub-helpers similar to how this round picked attachment-only.

Audit-traced gap: source_info (ext_id=0x01) and body(Value) (ext_id=0x03) Query exts. Routers and queryable-side need them; client AP MVP does not. If a future round adds them, the helper signature is `build_request_query_with_source_info(rid, mid, suf, source_info: SourceInfo)` and `_with_body_value(rid, mid, suf, value: Value)` respectively. Add the wz SourceInfo / Value codec authoring before the helper land.

Open composition gap reaffirmed from R121j-1b: the three layered helpers (consolidation / params / attachment) are one-shot and do not compose. If a caller needs e.g. params + attachment together they must mutate the Request manually. If R121j-1d's Request-level exts also need composition with the existing layers, introduce a `RequestQueryExt` trait at that point and refactor the four one-shot helpers into thin wrappers; document the refactor in the carry.

Round 121j-1d.decision_summary: R121j-1d adds the Request-level timeout extension layered helper, paying down the fourth and final Q-helpers carry quarter; qos / tstamp / target / budget are scope-deferred sub-helpers with the same pattern.
changes_bullets: crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs вҖ” added `build_request_query_with_timeout_ms(rid, mapping_id, suffix, timeout_ms)`. One-shot pattern mirroring R121j-1a/1b/1c: calls `build_request_query` then mutates Request to set the Request-level N_Z flag (0x80, the network-message-layer extensions chain present bit at request.scxml's `<sce:flag name="Z" bit="7"/>`) and append a single ExtEntry with header=`ENC_ZINT(0x20)|id_timeout(0x06)=0x26` and ExtZint body. M=0 (timeout is informational вҖ” the peer may ignore without breaking query semantics; matches zenoh-pico network.c:152 emitting no M, unlike target ext at line 140 which DOES set M).

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs вҖ” doc comment clarifies the two distinct Z bits at two layers (Request.header.Z gates Request-level tlv-chain; ExtEntry.header.Z is the per-entry chain-continuation marker). For single-ext, only the former is set; the latter stays clear. Same shape pattern applies to R121j-1c's Q-level ext chain (Query.header.Z + entry-Z).

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs вҖ” assertion gate: `timeout_ms != 0` rejected. zenoh-pico's encoder predicate at vendor/zenoh-pico/src/protocol/definitions/network.c:29 is `ext_timeout_ms = msg->_ext_timeout_ms != 0`, so a zero value silently clears the ext from the wire вҖ” the helper surfaces this as a panic at the call site rather than letting the caller think their timeout is being honored.

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs вҖ” added two unit tests. Byte-compare locks single-byte VLE (timeout=50ms вҶ’ 0x32), multi-byte VLE boundary (timeout=1000ms вҶ’ 0xE8 0x07), and large VLE (timeout=2^32 ms вҶ’ 5-byte VLE `0x80 0x80 0x80 0x80 0x10`). One panic-rejection test guards the timeout_ms=0 case. Inner-arm sanity verifies the Request-level Extensions vec, the ExtEntry.header byte, and the ExtZint.value independently вҖ” Query body stays minimal (no Q_C / Q_P / Q_Z).

Scope-deferred (audit-traced carry): four other Request-level exts. QoS (ext_id=0x01, ZINT u64, no M): priority + congestion-control + reliability packed into one zint per zenoh-pico session-level helper. Timestamp (ext_id=0x02, ZBUF, no M): timestamp_t struct (zid+time pair). Target (ext_id=0x04, ZINT, M=1!): query target enum (BestMatching / All / AllComplete). Budget (ext_id=0x05, ZINT u64, no M): max replies count. Each follows R121j-1d's pattern with appropriate ENC and ext_id; target's M=1 means the peer MUST handle it or fail the frame.
verification_bullets: Targeted: `cargo test -p wz-runtime-tokio --lib build_request_query_with_timeout_ms` вҖ” 2 passed, 0 failed.
Regression: `cargo test -p wz-runtime-tokio --lib` вҖ” 70 passed, 0 failed (up from 68 at R121j-1c closure; +2 R121j-1d tests).
Layer C2 equivalent: `cargo clippy -p wz-runtime-tokio --all-targets -- -D warnings` вҖ” clean.
carry_forward_bullets: Q-helpers carry now CLOSED across all 4 layered surfaces (consolidation R121j-1a / parameters R121j-1b / Q-level attachment R121j-1c / Request-level timeout R121j-1d). Each is a one-shot helper.

Open Request-level ext sub-helpers carry (lower priority, AP MVP client rarely needs them): `build_request_query_with_target(rid, mid, suf, target_enum)` (M=1!), `_with_qos(rid, mid, suf, qos_packed_u64)`, `_with_budget(rid, mid, suf, max_replies)`, `_with_timestamp(rid, mid, suf, timestamp_t)`. Each follows R121j-1d's pattern. Target is the only mandatory ext (M=1 at network.c:140) вҖ” routers may reject queries that omit it for cross-network forwarding.

Open Query-level ext sub-helpers carry: `build_request_query_with_body_value(rid, mid, suf, value)` (ext_id=0x03, needs wz Value codec authoring вҖ” currently unmapped), `_with_source_info(rid, mid, suf, source_info)` (ext_id=0x01, needs wz SourceInfo codec authoring). Both are router/queryable-side surfaces, not AP MVP client needs.

Composition gap reaffirmed: all 4 layered helpers (R121j-1a/b/c/d) are one-shot and do not compose. A caller wanting consolidation + params + timeout must mutate the Request manually. When the next layered helper need arises (any of the deferred sub-helpers above), introduce a `RequestQueryExt` trait with `with_consolidation` / `with_parameters` / `with_query_attachment` / `with_request_timeout_ms` / etc fluent chain methods and refactor the 4 existing helpers into thin wrappers.

R121h-perf-bump-2 carry intact: blocking on explicit user consent for the atomic 12-SCXML migration + vendor pin bump. Drift state unchanged this session (vendor pin 54d513af vs SCE main de5d5e9a, +15 commits).

R121j-3 (Response/Reply outbound) + R121j-4 (Response/Err outbound) + R121j-5 (inbound parser + QueryableTable) all carry intact вҖ” R121j-1 series closure moves Query/Reply outbound from ~36% (post-R121j-1a) to ~50% (Request layer functional; Response/Reply path still missing). Recommended next attack vector once perf-bump unblocks or before it.
Round 121j-1d.publishable_decision_summary: R121j-1d adds the Request-level timeout extension layered helper, paying down the fourth and final Q-helpers carry quarter; qos / tstamp / target / budget are scope-deferred sub-helpers with the same pattern.
publishable_changes_bullets: crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs вҖ” added `build_request_query_with_timeout_ms(rid, mapping_id, suffix, timeout_ms)`. One-shot pattern mirroring R121j-1a/1b/1c: calls `build_request_query` then mutates Request to set the Request-level N_Z flag (0x80, the network-message-layer extensions chain present bit at request.scxml's `<sce:flag name="Z" bit="7"/>`) and append a single ExtEntry with header=`ENC_ZINT(0x20)|id_timeout(0x06)=0x26` and ExtZint body. M=0 (timeout is informational вҖ” the peer may ignore without breaking query semantics; matches zenoh-pico network.c:152 emitting no M, unlike target ext at line 140 which DOES set M).

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs вҖ” doc comment clarifies the two distinct Z bits at two layers (Request.header.Z gates Request-level tlv-chain; ExtEntry.header.Z is the per-entry chain-continuation marker). For single-ext, only the former is set; the latter stays clear. Same shape pattern applies to R121j-1c's Q-level ext chain (Query.header.Z + entry-Z).

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs вҖ” assertion gate: `timeout_ms != 0` rejected. zenoh-pico's encoder predicate at vendor/zenoh-pico/src/protocol/definitions/network.c:29 is `ext_timeout_ms = msg->_ext_timeout_ms != 0`, so a zero value silently clears the ext from the wire вҖ” the helper surfaces this as a panic at the call site rather than letting the caller think their timeout is being honored.

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs вҖ” added two unit tests. Byte-compare locks single-byte VLE (timeout=50ms вҶ’ 0x32), multi-byte VLE boundary (timeout=1000ms вҶ’ 0xE8 0x07), and large VLE (timeout=2^32 ms вҶ’ 5-byte VLE `0x80 0x80 0x80 0x80 0x10`). One panic-rejection test guards the timeout_ms=0 case. Inner-arm sanity verifies the Request-level Extensions vec, the ExtEntry.header byte, and the ExtZint.value independently вҖ” Query body stays minimal (no Q_C / Q_P / Q_Z).

Scope-deferred (audit-traced carry): four other Request-level exts. QoS (ext_id=0x01, ZINT u64, no M): priority + congestion-control + reliability packed into one zint per zenoh-pico session-level helper. Timestamp (ext_id=0x02, ZBUF, no M): timestamp_t struct (zid+time pair). Target (ext_id=0x04, ZINT, M=1!): query target enum (BestMatching / All / AllComplete). Budget (ext_id=0x05, ZINT u64, no M): max replies count. Each follows R121j-1d's pattern with appropriate ENC and ext_id; target's M=1 means the peer MUST handle it or fail the frame.
publishable_verification_bullets: Targeted: `cargo test -p wz-runtime-tokio --lib build_request_query_with_timeout_ms` вҖ” 2 passed, 0 failed.
Regression: `cargo test -p wz-runtime-tokio --lib` вҖ” 70 passed, 0 failed (up from 68 at R121j-1c closure; +2 R121j-1d tests).
Layer C2 equivalent: `cargo clippy -p wz-runtime-tokio --all-targets -- -D warnings` вҖ” clean.
publishable_carry_forward_bullets: Q-helpers carry now CLOSED across all 4 layered surfaces (consolidation R121j-1a / parameters R121j-1b / Q-level attachment R121j-1c / Request-level timeout R121j-1d). Each is a one-shot helper.

Open Request-level ext sub-helpers carry (lower priority, AP MVP client rarely needs them): `build_request_query_with_target(rid, mid, suf, target_enum)` (M=1!), `_with_qos(rid, mid, suf, qos_packed_u64)`, `_with_budget(rid, mid, suf, max_replies)`, `_with_timestamp(rid, mid, suf, timestamp_t)`. Each follows R121j-1d's pattern. Target is the only mandatory ext (M=1 at network.c:140) вҖ” routers may reject queries that omit it for cross-network forwarding.

Open Query-level ext sub-helpers carry: `build_request_query_with_body_value(rid, mid, suf, value)` (ext_id=0x03, needs wz Value codec authoring вҖ” currently unmapped), `_with_source_info(rid, mid, suf, source_info)` (ext_id=0x01, needs wz SourceInfo codec authoring). Both are router/queryable-side surfaces, not AP MVP client needs.

Composition gap reaffirmed: all 4 layered helpers (R121j-1a/b/c/d) are one-shot and do not compose. A caller wanting consolidation + params + timeout must mutate the Request manually. When the next layered helper need arises (any of the deferred sub-helpers above), introduce a `RequestQueryExt` trait with `with_consolidation` / `with_parameters` / `with_query_attachment` / `with_request_timeout_ms` / etc fluent chain methods and refactor the 4 existing helpers into thin wrappers.

R121h-perf-bump-2 carry intact: blocking on explicit user consent for the atomic 12-SCXML migration + vendor pin bump. Drift state unchanged this session (vendor pin 54d513af vs SCE main de5d5e9a, +15 commits).

R121j-3 (Response/Reply outbound) + R121j-4 (Response/Err outbound) + R121j-5 (inbound parser + QueryableTable) all carry intact вҖ” R121j-1 series closure moves Query/Reply outbound from ~36% (post-R121j-1a) to ~50% (Request layer functional; Response/Reply path still missing). Recommended next attack vector once perf-bump unblocks or before it.

Round 121j-1e.decision_summary: R121j-1e adds the Request-level target extension layered helper; target is the only Request-level ext with M=1 mandatory marker, distinguishing it from timeout/qos/budget which leave M clear.
changes_bullets: crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs вҖ” added `pub enum QueryTarget { All, AllComplete }` mirroring zenoh-pico's `z_query_target_t` for the two transmitted values. `Z_QUERY_TARGET_BEST_MATCHING (0)` is intentionally NOT representable since zenoh-pico's encoder predicate `ext_target != BEST_MATCHING` clears the ext on the default вҖ” callers wanting BEST_MATCHING use plain `build_request_query`.

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs вҖ” added `QueryTarget::wire_byte(self) -> u8` const fn returning 1/2 per the enum integer values (mirroring the ConsolidationMode pattern from R121j-1a). const fn so the mapping is verifiable at compile time.

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs вҖ” added `build_request_query_with_target(rid, mid, suf, target)`. One-shot pattern mirroring R121j-1d: calls `build_request_query` then sets Request.header.Z and appends a single ExtEntry with header=`ENC_ZINT(0x20)|M(0x10)|id_target(0x04)=0x34` and ExtZint body carrying the target enum value. M=1 is the textbook-distinguishing feature: target is the only Request-level ext where zenoh-pico's encoder unconditionally sets the mandatory marker (network.c:140 `extheader |= _Z_MSG_EXT_FLAG_M` is target-specific; qos/tstamp/budget/timeout omit this OR).

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs вҖ” added two unit tests. Wire-byte regression locks both target values (All вҶ’ 0x01, AllComplete вҶ’ 0x02) and verifies the M-bit is set on the ExtEntry.header (`assert!(header & 0x10 != 0)` with explicit docstring about peer-reject semantics on unknown-M-bit exts per ext_entry codec contract). Enum-to-byte mapping invariant tested independently (`query_target_wire_byte_matches_zenoh_pico_enum_values`).
verification_bullets: Targeted: `cargo test -p wz-runtime-tokio --lib build_request_query_with_target query_target_wire_byte` вҖ” 2 passed, 0 failed.
Regression: `cargo test -p wz-runtime-tokio --lib` вҖ” 72 passed, 0 failed (up from 70 at R121j-1d closure; +2 R121j-1e tests).
Layer C2 equivalent: `cargo clippy -p wz-runtime-tokio --all-targets -- -D warnings` вҖ” clean.
carry_forward_bullets: Request-level ext sub-helpers remaining (carry intact, low priority for AP MVP client): `build_request_query_with_qos(rid, mid, suf, qos_packed_u64)` (ext_id=0x01, ZINT, no M; priority + congestion-control + reliability packed per zenoh-pico session helper), `build_request_query_with_budget(rid, mid, suf, max_replies)` (ext_id=0x05, ZINT, no M), `build_request_query_with_timestamp(rid, mid, suf, timestamp_t)` (ext_id=0x02, ZBUF, no M; needs Timestamp codec authoring вҖ” currently unmapped). Each follows R121j-1d/1e's pattern.

Composition refactor carry GROWING (was 4 helpers in R121j-1d, now 5 in R121j-1e): all one-shot helpers reset extensions vec; cannot compose. If R121j-1f adds qos/budget/timestamp (3 more), function count hits 8 вҖ” the textbook RequestQueryBuilder pattern becomes mandatory. Builder structure: per-layer Option fields + .with_*() chained setters + single .build() materializer that orders Request-level exts (qos вҶ’ tstamp вҶ’ target вҶ’ budget вҶ’ timeout per network.c:122-167) and threads Z chain-continuation bits. Defer the refactor until R121j-1f attempt or actual multi-ext caller surfaces.

R121j-3 (Response/Reply outbound) carry intact вҖ” next major AP MVP closure round. Reply codec inspection shows it wraps a push_body (Put/Del) variant similar to Push, so the Reply outbound builder pattern can reuse `build_push_literal` / `build_push_aliased`-shaped helpers for the inner body. Reply-level header has C(consolidation) and Z(exts) flags; the wz codec's `_z_reply_encode` upstream never sets Z (no reply-level exts defined yet) so the wire shape is approximately: [Response.header | N | M | Z=ext] + VLE(request_id) + wireexpr + [Z-gated Response.extensions] + [Reply.header | C] + [C-gated consolidation] + push_body. Estimated 1-2 rounds.

R121j-3 dependency: ext_responder Response-level ext (network.c:268, ext_id=0x01, ZINT) needs investigation вҖ” zenoh-pico responses CAN carry responder zid for reply attribution. AP MVP may or may not need it depending on whether routers expect it on the wire. Audit-traced for the R121j-3 entry round.
R121h-perf-bump-2 carry intact: blocking on explicit user consent for the atomic 12-SCXML migration + vendor pin bump.

Round 121j-1f.decision_summary: RequestQueryBuilder.request_qos(packed) setter -- emits Request-level qos ext (ENC_ZINT|id=1, no M) at the head of the ext chain per zenoh-pico encode order qos to tstamp to target to budget to timeout.
changes_bullets: RequestQueryBuilder in crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs gains the request_qos: Option<u8> field + the .request_qos(packed: u8) setter. Setter takes the pre-packed byte per zenoh-pico _z_n_qos_create at vendor/zenoh-pico/include/zenoh-pico/protocol/definitions/network.h (bits 0-2 priority, bit 3 nodrop, bit 4 express, bits 5-7 reserved); the wz setter is intentionally low-level so callers integrating directly with zenoh-pico-defined constants can pass _z_n_qos_create's output verbatim

RequestQueryBuilder.build() pushes the qos ExtEntry FIRST in request_exts (before target + timeout), matching zenoh-pico _z_request_encode chain order at vendor/zenoh-pico/src/protocol/codec/network.c (qos -> tstamp -> target -> budget -> timeout). The Z chain-continuation loop already in build() sets bit 0x80 on every ext except the last, so the emitted chain shape stays consistent as new sub-setters layer in

qos ExtEntry header = ENC_ZINT(0x20) | id_qos(0x01); NO M(0x10) flag because qos is an informational hint, not mandatory per the ext_qos M=0 convention in zenoh-pico's _z_request_encode. Body is ExtZint { value: packed as u64 }

2 new lib tests pin the wire shape: single-setter case (header = 0x21, value = packed verbatim, N_Z(0x80) on Request.header), three-layer composition (qos + target + timeout with Z continuation bit on indices 0 and 1, no Z on index 2; qos at index 0 confirms encode-order)

build() pushes the budget ExtEntry between target and timeout in the request_exts chain, matching zenoh-pico _z_request_encode at vendor/zenoh-pico/src/protocol/codec/network.c:144-149 (chain order: qos -> tstamp -> target -> budget -> timeout)

budget ExtEntry header = ENC_ZINT(0x20) | id_budget(0x05); no M flag (budget is informational, matching the M=0 emit in network.c:146 unlike target which carries M=0x10). Body widens the u32 budget into ExtZint { value: budget as u64 }

2 new lib tests pin: single-setter case (header = 0x25, value = 0x12345678 lifts verbatim into ZINT u64), four-layer chain composition (qos + target + budget + timeout with Z continuation bit on indices 0/1/2, clear on index 3; budget at index 2 confirms encode-order placement). 1 new should_panic test pins the zero-value validation

cargo test -p wz-runtime-tokio --lib: 96/96 pass (94 baseline + 2 new budget tests)
cargo clippy -p wz-runtime-tokio --all-targets: clean
scripts/run-ci.sh: 8/8 lanes pass on first attempt (no flake this round)

Q-side Request-level ext-chain coverage now complete for the 4 actually-implemented setters: qos / target / budget / timeout. The 5th zenoh-pico-defined Request ext (tstamp at id_tstamp=0x02 ENC_ZBUF) is intentionally deferred -- the wz codec does not currently expose a Timestamp sub-codec on the Request ext path; layering it requires extending the wz_codecs ext_zbuf variant set
publishable_carry_forward_bullets: R121j-1h ergonomic typed wrapper .request_qos_typed(priority, congestion_drop, express) over .request_qos(packed). Low priority; the packed form is production-correct

R121j-tstamp setter (last unimplemented Request ext, id_tstamp=0x02 ENC_ZBUF) needs the wz_codecs ext_zbuf Timestamp sub-codec wiring before it can land. Defer until z_timestamp is needed by an AP MVP path
R121j-3b Reply.source_info setter, R121j-3c Reply.responder setter, R121j-3d Reply.MsgDel body variant still pending on ResponseReplyBuilder
R121j-4b Err.source_info setter, R121j-4c Err.attachment setter still pending on ResponseErrBuilder

R121h-perf-bump-4: 4 secondary encoders (InitBody/OpenBody/DeclFinal-batch/KeepAlive at session_glue.rs lines 1543/1596/1620/1645) still use inner-Vec + extend_from_slice. Apply encode_frame_envelope-style shape for symmetry. Lower priority than R121j-5
R121j-5 inbound Request(Query) parser + on_query dispatch + QueryableTable struct -- largest single AP MVP weighted jump (+5-7%p); 2-3 sub-rounds = 1 session
R121k inbound DECLARE parser silent gap (outbound 9/9 vs inbound 0/8); 4-6 sub-rounds = 1-2 sessions
SliceSink-based zero-alloc hot path deferred to a runtime API design round

Round 121j-2.decision_summary: R121j-2 adds ResponseFinal outbound to close the Query/Reply sequence вҖ” build_response_final + encode_frame_with_response_final + SessionLinkActions::send_response_final вҖ” with 2-vector wire-byte byte-compare tests against zenoh-pico _z_response_final_encode (network.c:368-376) covering both single-byte and multi-byte VLE rid cases.
changes_bullets: crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs build_response_final(request_id) builder added next to build_request_query. Emits the minimal AP MVP shape: ResponseFinal.header = _Z_MID_N_RESPONSE_FINAL(0x1A), Z bit-7 clear (no RF-level exts), extensions = None. zenoh-pico defines no ResponseFinal-level extensions today but the wire format reserves bit 7 for them via _Z_FLAG_Z_Z; future rounds extend with an exts-present variant if a use case emerges.

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs encode_frame_with_response_final(sn, response_final, reliable) helper added: Frame envelope wrap mirror of encode_frame_with_push / encode_frame_with_declare / encode_frame_with_request. ResponseFinal is unconditionally reliable in zenoh-pico's model (losing it would leave the requesting peer's z_get future hung), so the production action layer below hard-codes reliable=true; the helper still accepts the flag for fuzz / negative-test paths.

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs SessionLinkActions::send_response_final(request_id) action added: build_response_final -> next_outbound_frame_sn -> encode_frame_with_response_final -> driver.send_blocking(Reliable). request_id MUST equal the rid from the matching Request(Query); callers are trusted to thread the rid through.

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs tests: two new unit tests. build_response_final_emits_zenoh_pico_compatible_wire_bytes locks 2 vectors (rid=42 single-byte VLE + rid=200 multi-byte VLE boundary that protects against VLE writer continuation-bit regression). encode_frame_with_response_final_wraps_in_frame_envelope validates the Frame envelope wrap behaves identically to the other three encode_frame_with_* helpers for both reliable and best-effort parent-flag paths.
verification_bullets: cargo test -p wz-runtime-tokio --lib -- response_final: both new tests pass. The byte-compare gate confirms the 2 emit shapes match zenoh-pico's _z_response_final_encode byte-for-byte; any future codegen drift on header derivation, VLE writer, or the Z-flag dispatch surfaces immediately.
cargo test -p wz-runtime-tokio --lib: all 54 lib tests green (52 prior + 2 new R121j-2); no regression on the R121g/R121i-a/b/c/R121j-1 byte-compare gates.
bash scripts/run-ci.sh: all 8 lanes pass (Layer 0/A/A2/B/C1/C2/D/E). No new clippy warnings; warnings=deny workspace policy preserved.
validate_workspace baseline: docs=12/12, T1 orphan=0 (no new), round-trip mandatory 12/12, GENERATED.md=sync; atomic ledger entries advance 166 -> 167, sections=214 unchanged.
carry_forward_bullets: R121j-3 next: Response(Reply) outbound. zenoh-pico _z_response_encode (network.c:241-321) + _z_reply_encode (message.c:507+) reference. Response envelope mirrors Request structurally (header / rid / keyexpr / exts) but uses _Z_MID_N_RESPONSE(0x1B) and carries the inner Reply body (MID 0x04). Reply body in turn carries a value payload similar to MsgPut вҖ” needs the Value codec wiring (verify Value module is published by wz-codecs).

Response Exts surface non-trivial: zenoh-pico encodes ext_qos / ext_timestamp / ext_responder for Response (network.c:266-292). AP MVP Reply outbound likely needs ext_responder at minimum because zenoh-pico clients dispatch reply callbacks per-responder; minimal shape may be too sparse for real interop. Verify against zenoh-pico's z_query / on_reply behaviour during R121j-3 entry.

R121j-4: inbound Query parser + dispatch (on_query callback + QueryableTable). Bigger engineering change вҖ” introduces the queryable registration surface. Best entered after R121j-3 lands the Reply outbound so the queryable callback has a place to emit replies.

R121h-perf zero-alloc encode debt still on the carry list вҖ” production-perf gating, parked as SCE-upstream feature proposal (textbook path per SCE-is-priority directive). Not a wz-session deliverable.

Round 121j-2a.decision_summary: R121j-2a introduces the RequestQueryBuilder fluent composition pattern that unifies the 5 one-shot Query helpers (R121j-1a/1b/1c/1d/1e) and unlocks multi-layer composition the one-shots could not express; existing helper signatures unchanged.
changes_bullets: crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs вҖ” added `pub struct RequestQueryBuilder` with per-layer Option fields (consolidation / parameters / query_attachment / request_target / request_timeout_ms). Fluent setters validate per-call (panic on empty / oversize / zero per the corresponding zenoh-pico encoder predicates). `.build()` materialises the Request, applying Query-layer settings to the inner Query body and assembling Request-level extensions in zenoh-pico's emit order (target before timeout per network.c:122-167) with Z chain-continuation bits set on every entry except the last.

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs вҖ” refactored 5 one-shot helpers into thin builder wrappers: `build_request_query_with_consolidation` / `_with_parameters` / `_with_attachment` / `_with_timeout_ms` / `_with_target`. Each is now a 3-line `RequestQueryBuilder::new(вҖҰ).<setter>(вҖҰ).build()` chain. Public signatures unchanged вҖ” callers see the same surface; internal implementation now goes through the builder, eliminating ~150 lines of duplicated Query mutation boilerplate.

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs вҖ” panic message body moved from the helper to the builder setter (e.g. `RequestQueryBuilder::parameters requires a non-empty params slice` replaces `build_request_query_with_parameters requires a non-empty params slice`). The three pre-existing `#[should_panic]` rejection tests were updated to match the new substring; behaviour (panic-on-bad-input) is identical and the max-size tests' panic strings stayed verbatim (`exceeds wz Query codec's max-size (256)` etc.) so those tests required no edits.

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs вҖ” added four new tests in the `tests` mod. `request_query_builder_composes_consolidation_and_parameters` verifies two Query-layer settings combined (the multi-layer case one-shot helpers cannot express). `request_query_builder_composes_all_five_layers` is the full-stack regression: it locks the wire shape with consolidation+parameters+attachment+target+timeout combined, verifies Request-level ext ordering (target вҶ’ timeout), Z chain bit on the target ext, all three Q-flag bits set together, and the Q-attachment ext positioning. Two `#[should_panic]` tests guard the builder's per-setter validation directly.
verification_bullets: Regression: `cargo test -p wz-runtime-tokio --lib` вҖ” 85 passed, 0 failed (up from 81 at R121j-4 closure; +4 R121j-2a tests). All pre-existing byte-compare tests for the 5 one-shot helpers pass unchanged, proving the refactor produces byte-identical wire bytes.
Layer C2 equivalent: `cargo clippy -p wz-runtime-tokio --all-targets -- -D warnings` вҖ” clean.
carry_forward_bullets: R121j-2a closes the composition gap noted in every prior Q-helper entry (R121j-1b/1c/1d/1e all carried it forward). Future Request-level ext sub-helpers (qos / budget / timestamp) add as `.request_qos(...)` / `.request_budget(...)` / `.request_timestamp(...)` setters on the builder + matching one-shot helpers as thin wrappers. The textbook function-count growth is now bounded вҖ” each new layer is one setter, not one helper duplication.

Audit-traced carry: parallel ResponseReplyBuilder for the R121j-3/R121j-4 helpers. Currently Response/Reply has only literal/aliased variants (no layered exts), so the function count there is still 4 helpers вҖ” below the builder threshold. The carry triggers when Reply consolidation / responder / del-body / Err encoding sub-helpers land.

R121j-5 (inbound Request(Query) parser + on_query callback + QueryableTable) remains the next major AP MVP closure target. Now that ALL outbound paths (Query + Reply + Err) are functional and the layered composition pattern is established, the queryable-side request handling is the only major gap. Scope: 2-3 rounds. Recommended next session focus once perf-bump unblocks or independently.
R121h-perf-bump-2 carry intact: blocking on explicit user consent for atomic 12-SCXML migration + vendor pin bump.

Round 121j-2b.decision_summary: ResponseReplyBuilder + ResponseErrBuilder added mirroring R121j-2a RequestQueryBuilder, with Reply.consolidation setter (R121j-3a squeeze) + Err.encoding(id, schema) setter (R121j-4a squeeze).
changes_bullets: ResponseReplyBuilder struct in crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs (request_id + keyexpr_mapping_id + suffix + payload + consolidation). new + .consolidation(ConsolidationMode) + build(); build() delegates to build_response_reply_literal (mapping_id=0+Some) or build_response_reply_aliased (mapping_id!=0) for the minimal-shape baseline, then applies Reply.header.C(0x20) + Reply.consolidation byte if the setter fired

ResponseErrBuilder struct mirroring the Reply side (request_id + keyexpr_mapping_id + suffix + payload + encoding(id + optional schema)). new + .encoding(id, schema) + build(); build() delegates to build_response_err_literal/aliased then applies Err.header.E(0x40) + Encoding { packed_id = (id << 1) | has_schema_bit, schema_len = schema.len(), schema = schema bytes } per zenoh-pico _z_encoding_encode at vendor/zenoh-pico/src/protocol/codec/core.c
+ use wz_codecs::encoding::Encoding to session_glue.rs imports (Encoding now constructed in this file via ResponseErrBuilder)

Both builders enforce literal-path invariant: (mapping_id=0, suffix=None) panics at build() time with the diagnostic 'ResponseXxxBuilder literal path (mapping_id=0) requires a non-empty keyexpr_suffix; use mapping_id != 0 for aliased' so the caller cannot silently produce a zero-id zero-suffix illegal wire shape

7 new lib tests pin the wire-byte invariants: ReplyBuilder no-setters byte-identical to baseline aliased helper; .consolidation(Latest) sets R_C(0x20) on Reply.header + emits 0x02 at offset 4; ErrBuilder no-setters byte-identical to baseline aliased helper; .encoding(4, None) packs to 0x08 (4<<1|0) + omits schema_len/schema; .encoding(4, Some(schema_v1)) packs to 0x09 (4<<1|1) + emits VLE schema_len=9 + 9 schema bytes; both literal-path validation panics caught via should_panic

cargo test -p wz-runtime-tokio --lib: 92/92 pass (85 baseline + 7 new builder tests)

scripts/run-ci.sh: 8/8 lanes pass on first attempt (no flake observed this round)

Byte-identical baseline guarantee: ResponseReplyBuilder.new(...).build() and ResponseErrBuilder.new(...).build() produce the exact wire bytes of build_response_reply_aliased / build_response_err_aliased respectively when no setters fire (pinned by the no_setters_matches_aliased_baseline tests on both builders)
carry_forward_bullets: Reply sub-setters still pending and layer onto ResponseReplyBuilder via the same idiom consolidation introduced this round: R121j-3b source_info (Reply.header.Z + Responder ext on Response.header.Z chain ?? naming TBD by zenoh-pico ext id mapping), R121j-3c responder (Response.header.Z + zid + eid ZBUF ext), R121j-3d MsgDel body variant (swap MsgPut for MsgDel in the inner ReplyVariant arm so a delete-reply is expressible without dropping the builder)
Err sub-setters still pending: R121j-4b source_info (Err.header.Z + ext chain), R121j-4c attachment (Err.header.Z + ExtZbuf payload ext)
R121j-1f Request-level qos setter on RequestQueryBuilder (ext_id=0x01 ZINT no M; zenoh-pico packs priority+congestion+reliability into u8); R121j-1g Request-level budget setter (ext_id=0x05 ZINT)

R121h-perf-bump-4: 4 secondary encoder sites in session_glue.rs (lines 1543/1596/1620/1645 -- InitBody/OpenBody/DeclFinal-batch/KeepAlive) still use the inner-Vec + extend_from_slice pattern; apply the same encode_frame_envelope-style shape for symmetry. Lower priority than R121j-5 on the AP MVP weighted axis
R121j-5 inbound Request(Query) parser + on_query dispatch + QueryableTable struct (largest single AP MVP weighted jump +5-7%p; 2-3 sub-rounds = 1 session)
R121k inbound DECLARE parser silent gap (outbound 9/9 vs inbound 0/8; DeclKexpr inbound first for AP MVP alias-aware DECLARE-then-Push when wz is receiver side; 4-6 sub-rounds = 1-2 sessions)

R121h-perf-bump-4: 4 secondary encoder sites in session_glue.rs (lines 1543/1596/1620/1645 -- InitBody/OpenBody/DeclFinal-batch/KeepAlive) still use the inner-Vec + extend_from_slice pattern; apply the same encode_frame_envelope-style shape for symmetry. Lower priority than R121j-5 on the AP MVP weighted axis
R121j-5 inbound Request(Query) parser + on_query dispatch + QueryableTable struct (largest single AP MVP weighted jump +5-7%p; 2-3 sub-rounds = 1 session)
R121k inbound DECLARE parser silent gap (outbound 9/9 vs inbound 0/8; DeclKexpr inbound first for AP MVP alias-aware DECLARE-then-Push when wz is receiver side; 4-6 sub-rounds = 1-2 sessions)

SliceSink-based zero-alloc hot path (caller-owned reusable buffer plumbed through LinkDriver/TxFrame/tcp_write). Wire bytes computation is now optimal post-R121h-perf-bump-3; remaining alloc is in LinkDriver enqueue layer. Deferred to a runtime API design round

Round 121j-3.decision_summary: R121j-3 opens the Response/Reply outbound chain with the minimal Response(Reply(MsgPut)) shape (literal + aliased keyexpr variants + frame envelope helper). Foundation for queryable-side reply emission; ext_responder / consolidation / Reply-del are scope-deferred sub-helpers.
changes_bullets: crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs вҖ” added `use wz_codecs::reply::{Reply, ReplyVariant};` and updated `use wz_codecs::response::Response` to `use wz_codecs::response::{Response, ResponseVariant};` (the variant enum was needed for the inner-arm construction).

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs вҖ” added `build_response_reply_literal(request_id, keyexpr_suffix, payload)` for literal-keyexpr replies (id=0 + suffix). Wire shape: Response.header = MID(0x1B)|N(0x20)|M(0x40 codegen-derived), VLE(rid), wireexpr Local, Reply.header(0x04), MsgPut.header(0x01), VLE(payload_len), payload. No Response-level exts, no Reply consolidation, no Reply exts, no MsgPut T/E/Z gates. Empty suffix rejected (literal shape's purpose is inline keyexpr).

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs вҖ” added `build_response_reply_aliased(request_id, mapping_id, suffix: Option<&str>, payload)` for peer-declared-mapping replies. Convention matches the DECLARE/Request builders ((N,None) pure alias / (N,Some) compound). Panics on mapping_id=0 (literal-sentinel inversion); use the _literal variant for that case.

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs вҖ” added `encode_frame_with_response(sn, response, reliable)` mirroring the other `encode_frame_with_*` Frame-envelope wrappers (PUSH/DECLARE/REQUEST/RESPONSE_FINAL). Reliable channel is the default вҖ” a dropped Reply hangs the requester's `z_get` since the queryable thinks it sent the reply and won't re-emit.

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs вҖ” added six unit tests. literal byte-compare (small + multi-byte VLE rid=200), aliased byte-compare (pure alias + composite + multi-byte VLE mapping_id=200), two `#[should_panic]` rejections (literal empty suffix, aliased zero mapping_id), and the encode_frame wrap test (reliable + best-effort).

Audit-traced carry: sub-helpers for the Response(Reply) tail. `_with_consolidation(mode)` sets Reply.header.C(0x20) + 1-byte consolidation; `_with_responder(zid, eid)` sets Response.header.Z + Responder ext (ext_id=0x03 ZBUF, custom zid+eid encoding per network.c:281-291); `_with_reply_del(...)` swaps the MsgPut arm for MsgDel.
verification_bullets: Targeted: `cargo test -p wz-runtime-tokio --lib build_response_reply encode_frame_with_response` вҖ” 6 passed, 0 failed (includes 1 pre-existing encode_frame_with_response_final test that the filter caught).
Regression: `cargo test -p wz-runtime-tokio --lib` вҖ” 77 passed, 0 failed (up from 72 at R121j-1e closure; +5 R121j-3 tests).
Workspace: `cargo test --workspace` вҖ” all 20 test suites ok, 0 failed.

Layer C2 equivalent: `cargo clippy -p wz-runtime-tokio --all-targets -- -D warnings` вҖ” clean after a single doc-comment rewrite (lines 3004-3005 had a `_with_responder` / `_with_reply_del` enumeration that clippy `doc_lazy_continuation` interpreted as bullet items; reworded to flowing prose).
carry_forward_bullets: R121j-3 sub-helpers as the next layered work: `build_response_reply_literal_with_consolidation` / `_aliased_with_consolidation` (Reply.header.C + 1-byte consolidation; mirrors the R121j-1a one-shot pattern at the Reply level); `_with_responder(zid, eid)` (Response.header.Z + Responder ext_id=0x03 ZBUF body вҖ” the zid+eid encoding at network.c:281-291 is custom, not a thin ExtZbuf wrap, so needs ZBUF body that contains a sub-encoded header byte + id bytes + eid VLE; non-trivial). `_with_reply_del(...)` (swap MsgPut to MsgDel arm; MsgDel codec authoring may be required if not already generated).

R121j-4 (Response(Err) outbound) is the next sibling round вҖ” same Response envelope, different inner body (Err codec at vendor/zenoh-pico/src/protocol/codec/message.c around line 545+). Err carries an Encoding+Payload pair with optional source_info / attachment exts. AP MVP queryable-side error path.

R121j-5 (inbound Request(Query) parser + on_query callback + QueryableTable) carry intact вҖ” the largest remaining AP MVP gap. Now that outbound Reply (R121j-3) and outbound Err (R121j-4 carry) are in scope, the queryable-side request handling can land once inbound parsing surfaces the Query body for callback dispatch. Estimated 2-3 rounds.

Composition refactor carry GROWING: 5 Query helpers (R121j-1a/1b/1c/1d/1e) + 3 Response helpers (this round) + ext sub-helpers carry = high function count. The textbook RequestQueryBuilder / ResponseReplyBuilder pattern (per-layer Option fields + chained setters + ordered .build()) is the right textbook fix вҖ” defer until 8+ helpers exist OR until a multi-layer caller surfaces.
R121h-perf-bump-2 carry intact: still blocking on explicit user consent for the atomic 12-SCXML migration + vendor pin bump.

Round 121j-3c.decision_summary: R121j-3c .responder(zid, eid) on both Reply+Err builders: envelope-level ExtZbuf ext (id 0x03) on Response.extensions per zenoh-pico network.c:281-291. +7 lib tests.
changes_bullets: crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs ResponseReplyBuilder and ResponseErrBuilder each gain a new responder: Option<(Vec<u8>, u32)> field plus a chainable setter pub fn responder(self, zid: &[u8], eid: u32). Both setters share the same 1..=16 zid-len assertion (zenoh-pico ZenohId wire constraint, transport.h:31-37). The two builders intentionally carry their own copy (rather than a shared trait) because their inner-body invariants differ; the symmetry is on the wire, not on the Rust type.

Both build() bodies grow an envelope-level emit step after the inner-body if-let. When responder is set, response.header |= 0x80 (_Z_FLAG_Z_Z on the Response envelope) and response.extensions = Some(vec![ExtEntry { header: 0x40 | 0x03, body: ExtZbuf { value_len, value } }]) where value = encode_responder_ext_body(&zid, eid). Sole-entry shape (no Z chain-continuation bit); future qos / tstamp envelope exts will plumb chain bits via the same iter-and-set-Z idiom used in RequestQueryBuilder.build at session_glue.rs:2772-2782.

New module-scope helper encode_responder_ext_body(zid: &[u8], eid: u32) -> Vec<u8>: pushes ((zid_len - 1) << 4) leading byte + raw zid + VLE(eid). Reuses encode_vle_u64_into (R121j-4b). Distinct from encode_source_info_ext_body in that no sn trailer is emitted (responder identifies the entity; source_info identifies the entity + per-source sequence position). Capacity = 1 + zid.len() + 5 worst-case VLE u32.

ResponseReplyBuilder docstring updated: prior wording listed Reply source_info as future sub-round R121j-3b without qualifying that it would be Reply-LEVEL ext. The new docstring records R121j-3b retraction (Reply body has no extensions chain per zenoh-pico _z_reply_encode message.c:507-519) and R121j-3c realization. ResponseErrBuilder docstring extended with the same R121j-3c note and an explicit cross-reference to network.c:281-291 documenting that responder applies symmetrically to Reply and Err inner bodies.

No changes to one-shot encode_frame_with_response* helpers or to build_response_reply/err_aliased/literal baselines; the additive shape preserves all R121j-2b / R121j-3d / R121j-4a / R121j-4b wire contracts. response_reply_builder_no_setters_matches_aliased_baseline + response_err_builder_no_setters_matches_aliased_baseline still pass.
verification_bullets: cargo build -p wz-runtime-tokio: clean (incremental 0.29s after edit).

cargo test -p wz-runtime-tokio --lib: 110/110 pass (was 103; +7 new tests: response_reply_builder_responder_emits_envelope_zbuf_ext_entry, response_reply_builder_responder_composes_with_consolidation, response_err_builder_responder_emits_envelope_zbuf_ext_entry, response_err_builder_responder_composes_with_source_info, response_reply_builder_responder_rejects_zid_too_long, response_err_builder_responder_rejects_empty_zid, encode_responder_ext_body_matches_zenoh_pico_layout).
cargo clippy -p wz-runtime-tokio --all-targets: clean (no new warnings, no deny-by-default lints).

cargo test -p wz-integration-tests: pubsub round-trips unchanged (wz_publisher_to_zsub + wz_publisher_aliased_to_zsub still green); layer3 byte-compare baselines untouched because the new envelope-ext path is opt-in via the .responder() setter only.

Wire shape pinned via direct helper test (encode_responder_ext_body_matches_zenoh_pico_layout): zid=[0xCA,0xFE,0xBA], eid=16384 yields [0x20, 0xCA, 0xFE, 0xBA, 0x80, 0x80, 0x01] (leading byte (3-1)<<4 = 0x20, raw zid, VLE(16384) = 0x80 0x80 0x01). Total length 1+3+3 = 7 bytes; builder wire-length delta matches 1+1+7 = 9 envelope ext overhead (header + VLE(value_len=7) + body).

Builder composition correctness verified independently: response_reply_builder_responder_composes_with_consolidation pins that envelope-level Z(0x80) on Response.header and body-level C(0x20) on Reply.header sit on different bytes (orthogonal bit emit); response_err_builder_responder_composes_with_source_info pins the same orthogonality for Err.
carry_forward_bullets: R121j-5b query.rs + QueryableTable + Fn(Query, &QueryResponder) per R121j-5a design вҖ” main AP MVP weighted lever (+5-7%p). QueryResponder.send_reply / send_err can now consume the full builder surface: R121j-4b source_info (Err body), R121j-4a encoding (Err body), R121j-3a consolidation (Reply body), R121j-3d reply_del (Reply body arm), R121j-3c responder (Response envelope). Reply/Err builder closure considered done modulo qos/tstamp envelope exts (separate future round).

Q-side Request-level ext chain coverage status post-R121j-3c: qos / target / budget / timeout = 4/5 (R121j-1f/1g/2a etc.). Tstamp (R121j-tstamp) still gated on wz_codecs ext_zbuf Timestamp sub-codec wiring; not a R121j-5 blocker.

Helper encode_vle_u64_into is now reused by two ext-body builders (source_info + responder). Promote to a module-private `mod ext_body` (alongside encode_source_info_ext_body + encode_responder_ext_body) once a third call site appears вҖ” likely the inbound Query parser's source_info decode reverse (R121j-5b will probably need a decode_source_info_ext_body for symmetry).

R121h-perf-bump-5 (encode_ext_chain Z-bit refactor) decision still pending вҖ” SCE-side option (a) vs wz-side option (b) carried unchanged. Now that Reply/Err builders emit envelope-level ext chains via the same vec![ExtEntry { ... }] pattern as RequestQueryBuilder, the refactor scope is well-defined: encode_ext_chain is called from 3 builder build() sites (Request body, Response envelope x2 via Reply/Err) plus the per-entry Z-bit byte-patching in encode_ext_chain itself.

R121j-3 closure status: Reply/Err builder surface complete for the zenoh-pico-parity subset. Remaining envelope exts (qos / tstamp) are non-blocking carry; their absence does not affect AP MVP because the queryable doesn't need to inject those on Reply (the wire defaults are sensible).

Round 121j-3d.decision_summary: ResponseReplyBuilder.reply_del() setter -- swaps the inner ReplyVariant arm from CodecZenohMsgPut to CodecZenohMsgDel. Inner MID flips 0x01 -> 0x02; payload bytes from constructor are dropped (MsgDel carries no payload).
changes_bullets: + use wz_codecs::msg_del::MsgDel to session_glue.rs imports (MsgDel now constructed in this file via the .reply_del() setter)

ResponseReplyBuilder in crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs gains the body_kind_del: bool field (default false = MsgPut, the existing put-data reply) + the .reply_del() setter that flips the bool to true (the delete-keyexpr reply). Doc-comment captures the payload-drop behaviour: payload supplied to ::new() is unused on the Del path because MsgDel struct has no payload, only an optional timestamp + optional ext chain

build() now branches on body_kind_del after delegating to the existing put one-shot helpers: if Del, the freshly-built MsgPut arm is swapped for ReplyVariant::CodecZenohMsgDel(MsgDel { header: 0x02, timestamp: None, extensions: None }). The delegate-then-swap shape keeps build_response_reply_literal/aliased one-shot helpers unchanged (additive contract); the cost is one wasted MsgPut struct per Del-reply build, acceptable for the additive shape

2 new lib tests pin the wire-level effect: reply_del() flips inner MID from 0x01 to 0x02 and reduces the wire length by exactly 6 bytes (the VLE(5) length prefix + 5 payload bytes of b'hello' that MsgPut would emit but MsgDel does not); composes cleanly with .consolidation(Latest) so Reply.header.C(0x20) + 1 byte consolidation sit between Reply.header and the MsgDel inner MID
verification_bullets: cargo build -p wz-runtime-tokio: clean
cargo test -p wz-runtime-tokio --lib: 98/98 pass (96 baseline + 2 new reply_del tests)
cargo clippy -p wz-runtime-tokio --all-targets: clean
scripts/run-ci.sh: 8/8 lanes pass on first attempt (no flake this round)

Byte-level invariant pin: put_wire.len() - del_wire.len() == 6 confirms the MsgDel payload-omission contract holds at the wire boundary. The +6 difference decomposes as VLE(payload_len=5) = 1 byte + 5 'hello' payload bytes that MsgPut emits and MsgDel does not
carry_forward_bullets: R121j-3b Reply.source_info setter (Reply.header.Z + source-info ZBUF ext) still pending on ResponseReplyBuilder

Round 121j-3d.publishable_decision_summary: ResponseReplyBuilder.reply_del() setter -- swaps the inner ReplyVariant arm from CodecZenohMsgPut to CodecZenohMsgDel. Inner MID flips 0x01 -> 0x02; payload bytes from constructor are dropped (MsgDel carries no payload).
publishable_changes_bullets: + use wz_codecs::msg_del::MsgDel to session_glue.rs imports (MsgDel now constructed in this file via the .reply_del() setter)

ResponseReplyBuilder in crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs gains the body_kind_del: bool field (default false = MsgPut, the existing put-data reply) + the .reply_del() setter that flips the bool to true (the delete-keyexpr reply). Doc-comment captures the payload-drop behaviour: payload supplied to ::new() is unused on the Del path because MsgDel struct has no payload, only an optional timestamp + optional ext chain

build() now branches on body_kind_del after delegating to the existing put one-shot helpers: if Del, the freshly-built MsgPut arm is swapped for ReplyVariant::CodecZenohMsgDel(MsgDel { header: 0x02, timestamp: None, extensions: None }). The delegate-then-swap shape keeps build_response_reply_literal/aliased one-shot helpers unchanged (additive contract); the cost is one wasted MsgPut struct per Del-reply build, acceptable for the additive shape

2 new lib tests pin the wire-level effect: reply_del() flips inner MID from 0x01 to 0x02 and reduces the wire length by exactly 6 bytes (the VLE(5) length prefix + 5 payload bytes of b'hello' that MsgPut would emit but MsgDel does not); composes cleanly with .consolidation(Latest) so Reply.header.C(0x20) + 1 byte consolidation sit between Reply.header and the MsgDel inner MID

Round 121j-4.decision_summary: R121j-4 closes the Response(Err) outbound mirror of R121j-3 (Reply), giving the queryable-side a way to emit error responses for failed Query handling.
changes_bullets: crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs вҖ” added `use wz_codecs::err::Err;` import.

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs вҖ” added `build_response_err_literal(request_id, keyexpr_suffix, payload)` and `build_response_err_aliased(request_id, mapping_id, suffix, payload)`. Mirror R121j-3 Reply builders with `ResponseVariant::CodecZenohErr(Err)` arm instead of `CodecZenohReply(Reply)`. Minimal Err shape (header=0x05 MID only, no E encoding, no Z exts, no payload prefix beyond payload_len + bytes).

Structural difference from Reply documented: Reply wraps a MsgPut which itself carries a MID byte (0x01) before its own payload_len/payload pair, so a literal Response(Reply) wire ends with `[Reply.header(0x04), MsgPut.header(0x01), payload_len, payload]`. Err is a leaf with no inner MID wrap, so a literal Response(Err) wire ends with `[Err.header(0x05), payload_len, payload]` вҖ” the Err MID byte position is identical to where the Reply chain's Reply.header sits, allowing the peer's outer Response decoder to discriminate on that byte.

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs вҖ” added four unit tests. literal byte-compare (small rid + multi-byte VLE rid=200), aliased byte-compare (pure alias + composite), and two `#[should_panic]` rejections (literal empty suffix, aliased zero mapping_id). Inner-arm sanity verifies the Err struct shape independently.

Out of scope (audit-traced carry): `_with_encoding(...)` sets Err.header.E(0x40) + emits the Encoding sub-codec inline; `_with_source_info(...)` sets Err.header.Z + emits a source_info ext entry (ext_id=0x01 ZBUF); `_with_attachment(...)` similar for ext_id=0x05. AP MVP queryable error path does not need encoding hints or source-info exts; the minimal shape here ships first.
verification_bullets: Targeted: `cargo test -p wz-runtime-tokio --lib build_response_err` вҖ” 4 passed, 0 failed.
Regression: `cargo test -p wz-runtime-tokio --lib` вҖ” 81 passed, 0 failed (up from 77 at R121j-3 closure; +4 R121j-4 tests).
Layer C2 equivalent: `cargo clippy -p wz-runtime-tokio --all-targets -- -D warnings` вҖ” clean.
carry_forward_bullets: R121j-3 + R121j-4 outbound layer now complete in minimal shape. Queryable-side reply / err path: caller constructs Response via build_response_reply_* or build_response_err_*, encodes with encode_frame_with_response, hands to the link driver. The actual queryable dispatch wiring (when does wz CHOOSE to emit Reply vs Err) is part of R121j-5 inbound parser + on_query callback territory.

R121j-3/4 sub-helpers carry intact: consolidation / responder / del-body / encoding hints / source-info exts. None are AP MVP-blocking. The textbook RequestQueryBuilder / ResponseReplyBuilder pattern (per-layer Option fields + chained setters + ordered .build() materializer) becomes the right textbook fix when 8+ helpers exist OR when a multi-layer caller surfaces.

R121j-5 (inbound Request(Query) parser + on_query callback + QueryableTable) carry intact. Now that BOTH outbound directions of Query/Reply are functional, R121j-5 is the next major AP MVP closure target. Scope: (1) inbound Request parser that surfaces the Query body to a per-keyexpr callback table, (2) per-session QueryableTable (id вҶ’ callback fn + keyexpr binding) registered via send_declare_queryable, (3) callback signature design (Query handle with reply / err methods that internally call build_response_*). Estimated 2-3 rounds; significant new surface for QueryableTable type and async lifecycle. Recommended as the next non-perf-bump session focus.

Query/Reply outbound now ~75% (was 36% at session start, ~50% after R121j-3, ~75% now with both Reply + Err). Remaining 25% is the layered sub-helpers + composition refactor. AP MVP weighted parity bumps from ~71-75% to ~75-78% with this session's series.
R121h-perf-bump-2 carry intact: blocking on explicit user consent for the atomic 12-SCXML migration + vendor pin bump.

Round 121j-4-retract.decision_summary: R121j-4c/-3b carry retraction: wire-absent. zenoh-pico Err has no attachment ext (message.c:545-573); Reply-body has no extensions chain (message.c:507-519). R121j-4b and -3c remain wire-valid.
changes_bullets: R121j-4c (ResponseErrBuilder.attachment): RETRACTED. zenoh-pico _z_err_encode at src/protocol/codec/message.c:545-573 encodes only encoding (via _Z_FLAG_Z_E_E header bit) and source_info ext (_Z_MSG_EXT_ENC_ZBUF | 0x01). No attachment ext (0x05) emitted. _z_err_decode_extension at message.c:574-589 handles 0x01 only; default arm returns _z_msg_ext_unknown_error(extension, 0x0a). Adding .attachment(...) would emit bytes the peer treats as unknown-mandatory error.

R121j-3b (ResponseReplyBuilder.source_info, as a Reply-body ext): RETRACTED. zenoh-pico _z_reply_encode at message.c:507-519 emits header + optional consolidation byte + inner push_body (MsgPut|MsgDel). No extensions chain at the Reply body level. _z_reply_decode_extension at message.c:520-528 is a stub that always returns _z_msg_ext_unknown_error(extension, 0x0a). Reply-body source_info is wire-absent; source_info on the inner MsgPut body is a separate concern (MsgPut ext, not a Reply-level builder setter).

Wire-valid carries remaining from R121h-perf-bump-4 closure: R121j-4b (Err source_info ext_id 0x01 ZBUF, verified at message.c:554-558 + 565-568) and R121j-3c (Reply responder ext at Response *envelope* level, verified at network.c:281-291 with header _Z_MSG_EXT_ENC_ZBUF | 0x03 + zid+eid encoding). Both mutate the outer Err/Response envelope, not the Reply inner body.

Root cause: R121h-perf-bump-4 closure summary listed 4 active sub-rounds based on the R121j-2b builder shape symmetry instinct (Reply mirrors Query with attachment + source_info), without grep-verifying each named ext against zenoh-pico's _z_reply_encode / _z_err_encode. The closure was authored from outbound builder shape, not from inbound wire spec.
Application of feedback_diagnose_before_carry.md memory: one grep into zenoh-pico/src/protocol/codec/message.c falsified the carries in under 30 seconds. Memory lesson re-validated this round.
verification_bullets: grep -n '_z_err_encode' /home/coin/zenoh-pico/src/protocol/codec/message.c yields line 545; encoder body at 545-573 lists only encoding flag-driven encode + source_info ext (0x01). No attachment branch.
grep -n '_z_reply_encode' /home/coin/zenoh-pico/src/protocol/codec/message.c yields line 507; encoder body at 507-519 is header + consolidation byte + push_body call. No extensions chain emission.

Reply default decode arm (message.c:525) and Err default decode arm (message.c:585) both return _z_msg_ext_unknown_error with reason codes 0x0a вҖ” wire-absent setters would actively break interop, not silently degrade.

R121j-3c ResponseReplyBuilder.responder(zid, eid) вҖ” wire-valid per network.c:281-291. Response-envelope-level (not Reply-body-level) вҖ” ext_id 0x03 ZBUF; sets Response.header.Z. First builder that mutates Response envelope extensions; 1-2 rounds depending on builder-field plumbing.
