Round 121j-4b.decision_summary: R121j-4b ResponseErrBuilder.source_info(zid, eid, sn) setter: Err.header.Z + ExtZbuf ext (id 0x01) carrying VLE-packed zid+eid+sn per zenoh-pico message.c:243/565. +5 lib tests.
changes_bullets: crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs ResponseErrBuilder gains a new source_info: Option<(Vec<u8>, u32, u32)> field and the chainable setter pub fn source_info(self, zid: &[u8], eid: u32, sn: u32). The setter asserts (1..=16).contains(&zid.len()) per the zenoh-pico ZenohId wire constraint at transport.h:31-37; defence-in-depth assert is repeated inside encode_source_info_ext_body so future direct helper callers cannot smuggle invalid lengths past the public boundary.

build() extends the CodecZenohErr arm: when source_info is set, err.header |= 0x80 (_Z_FLAG_Z_Z) and err.extensions = Some(vec![ExtEntry { header: 0x40 | 0x01, body: ExtEntryVariant::CodecZenohExtZbuf(ExtZbuf { value_len, value }) }]) where value = encode_source_info_ext_body(&zid, eid, sn). Sole-entry shape (no Z chain-continuation bit) matches zenoh-pico _z_err_encode at message.c:545-573 which emits source_info as the only ext-chain element; future Err exts (if any) will plumb the chain bits via the same iter-and-set-Z idiom used in RequestQueryBuilder.build at session_glue.rs:2772-2782.

New module-scope helper encode_source_info_ext_body(zid: &[u8], eid: u32, sn: u32) -> Vec<u8> at session_glue.rs builds the ExtZbuf value body: pushes ((zid_len - 1) << 4) as the leading byte, then the raw zid bytes, then VLE(eid) and VLE(sn). Capacity pre-reserved = 1 + zid.len() + 5 + 5 to fit the worst-case VLE u32 length pair. Wire layout exactly matches zenoh-pico _z_source_info_encode_ext at message.c:243-254 (whose `zsize(ext_size)` length prefix is supplied by the surrounding ExtZbuf codec's value_len field).

New module-scope helper encode_vle_u64_into(out: &mut Vec<u8>, v: u64) emits a base-128 zint into a Vec<u8>. Free-function shape (no SceSink) because the source_info ext body is constructed before any SceSink is in scope: the body lives inside ExtZbuf.value and the surrounding ext codec sees only the already-built Vec<u8>. Mirrors the inline VLE loop in encode_frame_envelope (session_glue.rs:3663-3669) and zenoh-pico _z_zsize_encode at src/protocol/codec/core.c.

ResponseErrBuilder docstring updated: the prior wording 'Err `source_info` (R121j-4b) and `attachment` (R121j-4c) layer in as separate audit-traced sub-rounds' is replaced with a wording that records R121j-4b realization here and R121j-4c retraction (Round 121j-4-retract), pointing the reader at zenoh-pico message.c:545-573 as the evidence that Err.attachment is wire-absent.
verification_bullets: cargo build -p wz-runtime-tokio: clean (49.85s).

cargo test -p wz-runtime-tokio --lib: 103/103 pass (was 98; +5 new tests: response_err_builder_source_info_emits_zbuf_ext_entry, response_err_builder_source_info_composes_with_encoding, response_err_builder_source_info_rejects_zid_too_long, response_err_builder_source_info_rejects_empty_zid, encode_source_info_ext_body_matches_zenoh_pico_layout).
cargo clippy -p wz-runtime-tokio --all-targets: clean (no new warnings).

cargo test -p wz-integration-tests: wz_publisher_aliased_to_zsub + wz_publisher_to_zsub pass; layer3 byte-compare baselines unaffected (R121j-4b only adds new opt-in setter, default-empty source_info path leaves wire bytes identical to the R121j-2b no-setter baseline as proved by response_err_builder_no_setters_matches_aliased_baseline still passing).

Wire-shape pinned independently of the builder: encode_source_info_ext_body_matches_zenoh_pico_layout asserts that source_info(zid=[0xDE,0xAD], eid=128, sn=16384) yields [0x10, 0xDE, 0xAD, 0x80, 0x01, 0x80, 0x80, 0x01] вҖ” leading byte (2-1)<<4 = 0x10, raw zid, VLE(128) = 0x80 0x01, VLE(16384) = 0x80 0x80 0x01. So future Reply/Push/Query source_info call sites can re-use the helper with the same byte guarantee.
carry_forward_bullets: R121j-3c ResponseReplyBuilder.responder(zid, eid) вҖ” wire-valid per network.c:281-291 (Response *envelope* level ext, ext_id 0x03 ZBUF). First builder that mutates Response envelope extensions (Reply/Err builders so far mutate the inner body); requires plumbing Response.extensions field through ResponseReplyBuilder.build. 1-2 rounds depending on whether ResponseErrBuilder also grows a .responder setter (envelope-level ext applies to both Reply and Err inner bodies in zenoh-pico).

R121j-5b query.rs + QueryableTable + Fn(Query, &QueryResponder) per R121j-5a design вҖ” main AP MVP weighted lever (+5-7%p). QueryResponder.send_err can now consume R121j-4b .source_info(...) to attach the (zid, eid, sn) that arrived on the inbound Request's matching source_info ext (Query body source_info, decoded by Request::decode).

Helpers encode_source_info_ext_body + encode_vle_u64_into are module-local but reusable for any future Push.source_info / Query.source_info / Reply-inner MsgPut.source_info setter. If a third caller appears, promote to a dedicated `mod source_info_ext` (or migrate alongside query.rs in R121j-5b).
R121h-perf-bump-5 (encode_ext_chain Z-bit refactor) decision still pending вҖ” SCE-side option (a) vs wz-side option (b) carried unchanged.

Memory addendum opportunity (consider during session close): the carry-retraction + setter-realization in two consecutive entries (R121j-4-retract + R121j-4b) demonstrates the textbook pattern for wire-spec disagreements: retract first with evidence, then implement the wire-valid alternative. Could promote to feedback_diagnose_before_carry.md as a worked example.
Round 121j-4b.publishable_decision_summary: R121j-4b ResponseErrBuilder.source_info(zid, eid, sn) setter: Err.header.Z + ExtZbuf ext (id 0x01) carrying VLE-packed zid+eid+sn per zenoh-pico message.c:243/565. +5 lib tests.
publishable_changes_bullets: crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs ResponseErrBuilder gains a new source_info: Option<(Vec<u8>, u32, u32)> field and the chainable setter pub fn source_info(self, zid: &[u8], eid: u32, sn: u32). The setter asserts (1..=16).contains(&zid.len()) per the zenoh-pico ZenohId wire constraint at transport.h:31-37; defence-in-depth assert is repeated inside encode_source_info_ext_body so future direct helper callers cannot smuggle invalid lengths past the public boundary.

build() extends the CodecZenohErr arm: when source_info is set, err.header |= 0x80 (_Z_FLAG_Z_Z) and err.extensions = Some(vec![ExtEntry { header: 0x40 | 0x01, body: ExtEntryVariant::CodecZenohExtZbuf(ExtZbuf { value_len, value }) }]) where value = encode_source_info_ext_body(&zid, eid, sn). Sole-entry shape (no Z chain-continuation bit) matches zenoh-pico _z_err_encode at message.c:545-573 which emits source_info as the only ext-chain element; future Err exts (if any) will plumb the chain bits via the same iter-and-set-Z idiom used in RequestQueryBuilder.build at session_glue.rs:2772-2782.

New module-scope helper encode_source_info_ext_body(zid: &[u8], eid: u32, sn: u32) -> Vec<u8> at session_glue.rs builds the ExtZbuf value body: pushes ((zid_len - 1) << 4) as the leading byte, then the raw zid bytes, then VLE(eid) and VLE(sn). Capacity pre-reserved = 1 + zid.len() + 5 + 5 to fit the worst-case VLE u32 length pair. Wire layout exactly matches zenoh-pico _z_source_info_encode_ext at message.c:243-254 (whose `zsize(ext_size)` length prefix is supplied by the surrounding ExtZbuf codec's value_len field).

New module-scope helper encode_vle_u64_into(out: &mut Vec<u8>, v: u64) emits a base-128 zint into a Vec<u8>. Free-function shape (no SceSink) because the source_info ext body is constructed before any SceSink is in scope: the body lives inside ExtZbuf.value and the surrounding ext codec sees only the already-built Vec<u8>. Mirrors the inline VLE loop in encode_frame_envelope (session_glue.rs:3663-3669) and zenoh-pico _z_zsize_encode at src/protocol/codec/core.c.

ResponseErrBuilder docstring updated: the prior wording 'Err `source_info` (R121j-4b) and `attachment` (R121j-4c) layer in as separate audit-traced sub-rounds' is replaced with a wording that records R121j-4b realization here and R121j-4c retraction (Round 121j-4-retract), pointing the reader at zenoh-pico message.c:545-573 as the evidence that Err.attachment is wire-absent.
publishable_verification_bullets: cargo build -p wz-runtime-tokio: clean (49.85s).

cargo test -p wz-runtime-tokio --lib: 103/103 pass (was 98; +5 new tests: response_err_builder_source_info_emits_zbuf_ext_entry, response_err_builder_source_info_composes_with_encoding, response_err_builder_source_info_rejects_zid_too_long, response_err_builder_source_info_rejects_empty_zid, encode_source_info_ext_body_matches_zenoh_pico_layout).
cargo clippy -p wz-runtime-tokio --all-targets: clean (no new warnings).

cargo test -p wz-integration-tests: wz_publisher_aliased_to_zsub + wz_publisher_to_zsub pass; layer3 byte-compare baselines unaffected (R121j-4b only adds new opt-in setter, default-empty source_info path leaves wire bytes identical to the R121j-2b no-setter baseline as proved by response_err_builder_no_setters_matches_aliased_baseline still passing).

Wire-shape pinned independently of the builder: encode_source_info_ext_body_matches_zenoh_pico_layout asserts that source_info(zid=[0xDE,0xAD], eid=128, sn=16384) yields [0x10, 0xDE, 0xAD, 0x80, 0x01, 0x80, 0x80, 0x01] вҖ” leading byte (2-1)<<4 = 0x10, raw zid, VLE(128) = 0x80 0x01, VLE(16384) = 0x80 0x80 0x01. So future Reply/Push/Query source_info call sites can re-use the helper with the same byte guarantee.
publishable_carry_forward_bullets: R121j-3c ResponseReplyBuilder.responder(zid, eid) вҖ” wire-valid per network.c:281-291 (Response *envelope* level ext, ext_id 0x03 ZBUF). First builder that mutates Response envelope extensions (Reply/Err builders so far mutate the inner body); requires plumbing Response.extensions field through ResponseReplyBuilder.build. 1-2 rounds depending on whether ResponseErrBuilder also grows a .responder setter (envelope-level ext applies to both Reply and Err inner bodies in zenoh-pico).

R121j-5b query.rs + QueryableTable + Fn(Query, &QueryResponder) per R121j-5a design вҖ” main AP MVP weighted lever (+5-7%p). QueryResponder.send_err can now consume R121j-4b .source_info(...) to attach the (zid, eid, sn) that arrived on the inbound Request's matching source_info ext (Query body source_info, decoded by Request::decode).

Round 121j-5a.decision_summary: R121j-5a reconnaissance: Request inbound parser already wired via parse_frame_payload; R121j-5b/c carry QueryableTable in new query.rs + Fn(Query, &QueryResponder) on_query dispatch and Reply emit.
changes_bullets: Reconnaissance round; no source code mutation. Design decisions only, materialized in carry_forward for R121j-5b/c sub-rounds.

Verified Request::decode already complete via SCE codegen at crates/target/debug/build/wz-codecs-*/out/request.rs:97-147 вҖ” 1:1 with zenoh-pico _z_request_decode (network.c:209-238): rid(VLE) + keyexpr(N/M flag) + extensions(Z flag, ExtEntry chain capacity 4) + 4-variant body dispatch (MsgPut/MsgDel/Query/Default).

Verified parse_frame_payload at crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs:4172 already emits NetworkMessage::Request(Box<Request>) from Frame.payload bytes вҖ” the silent gap is *not* parser absence but downstream dispatch absence.

Silent gap identified: registry.dispatch arms at crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/pubsub.rs:278-282 cover only NetworkMessage::Push (dispatch_push) and NetworkMessage::Declare (absorb_declare). Request/Response/ResponseFinal/Interest/Oam variants fall through silently with no panic or trace.

Design decision A вҖ” location: QueryableTable lives in a new crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/query.rs module, mirroring pubsub.rs's SubscriberRegistry pattern. Rationale: separation of concerns, session_glue.rs already 5500+ lines, and Reply emit helpers naturally colocate with the table they emit on behalf of.

Design decision B вҖ” callback shape: on_query: Fn(Query, &QueryResponder) where QueryResponder exposes send_reply / send_err / send_final methods matching the zenoh API split (z_query_reply / z_query_reply_err / z_query_reply_final). Multi-reply and per-reply error reporting are supported without forcing an async-stream return shape that would conflict with the MCU runtime contract.

Design decision C вҖ” dispatch wiring: R121j-5c will add a third arm to pubsub.rs registry.dispatch (or, equivalently, pass a sibling QueryableRegistry into poll_and_dispatch_one alongside SubscriberRegistry) so NetworkMessage::Request invokes QueryableTable::dispatch_request before the message is dropped. QueryableTable lookup keyed by Request.keyexpr resolved through the existing inbound DeclKexpr mapping table state on SubscriberRegistry (pubsub.rs:172).
verification_bullets: grep -n 'NetworkMessage::' crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/pubsub.rs confirmed lines 280-281 are the only dispatch arms in registry.dispatch вҖ” Push and Declare only; Request not routed.

Read of crates/target/debug/build/wz-codecs-b22c022e9cc543f4/out/request.rs:97-147 confirmed Request::decode signature: header byte peek+advance, rid VLE-u64, keyexpr with N/M flags from header bits 5/6, optional ExtEntry chain via header bit-7 (Z), 4-arm body match (MID 0x01 MsgPut / 0x02 MsgDel / 0x03 Query / other вҶ’ Default catch-all Query).

Read of crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs:4165-4210 confirmed parse_frame_payload's match arm at line 4172-4175 calls Request::decode and pushes NetworkMessage::Request(Box::new(req)) for MID = wire_const::N_MID_REQUEST; same shape for Push/Response/ResponseFinal/Oam/Interest/Declare.

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs:1084 doc-comment from R121i-b ('declared keyexpr will then trigger the wz-side on_query') confirms on_query was already the documented future surface from the Q-side DeclQueryable round вҖ” R121j-5 realizes that documented future, no new architectural commitment.
No code mutation this round; cargo check / cargo test baselines unchanged (98 wz-runtime-tokio lib tests pass at HEAD 24a4a2f per session-start baseline capture).
carry_forward_bullets: R121j-5b: create crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/query.rs with QueryableTable struct (likely Mutex<HashMap<KeyexprId, Box<dyn QueryableHandler + Send + Sync>>>), a QueryableHandler trait or Fn-alias for the on_query closure shape, a QueryResponder struct holding (rid, keyexpr-resolved, link-handle / SessionLinkActions arc) for Reply emit context, and a stubbed QueryableTable::dispatch_request entry point. Add a #[cfg(test)] table_register / table_lookup unit test pair. No callback invocation yet вҖ” wiring lands in R121j-5c.

R121j-5c: wire dispatch_request into pubsub.rs registry.dispatch (or add QueryableRegistry as a sibling Arc passed to poll_and_dispatch_one) so NetworkMessage::Request routes through QueryableTable before any silent drop. Implement QueryResponder::{send_reply, send_err, send_final} which compose R121j-2b ResponseReplyBuilder / ResponseErrBuilder / encode_frame_with_response_final on top of the existing session_glue send_frame infrastructure. End-to-end test: register a Queryable, send a Request through TcpDriver, expect Reply+Final on the wire.

AP MVP weighted +5-7%p target after R121j-5c lands: Q-side and R-side parity for the Query/Reply lifecycle, raising the inbound 65% number toward ~100% for the Query/Reply path. Per-closure estimate from R121h-perf-bump-4 carry stands.

Open sub-question for R121j-5b: function pointer (Fn(Query, &QueryResponder) + 'static + Send + Sync) vs boxed trait object (Box<dyn QueryableHandler>) for the table value type? Recommend boxed dyn for closure-capture flexibility (zenoh-pico's typical use case captures user state by reference); revisit at R121j-5b implementation only if a no_std/MCU constraint surfaces that forbids alloc.

Reply emit will consume R121j-2b ResponseReplyBuilder + R121j-4 ResponseErrBuilder + R121j-3d reply_del + encode_frame_with_response + encode_frame_with_response_final + send_frame. Reply payload encoding currently has no streaming back-pressure consideration; out of scope for R121j-5 (single-reply synchronous z_get / z_get_reply happy path is the AP MVP target).
R121j-tstamp Request-level Timestamp setter still gated on wz_codecs ext_zbuf Timestamp sub-codec wiring; not blocked by R121j-5 and not blocking it вҖ” independent active carry.

R121h-perf-bump-5 (encode_ext_chain Z-bit refactor) decision still pending вҖ” SCE-side option (a: ExtEntry::encode signature adds chain_continuation: bool, codegen template change) vs wz-side option (b: sink position-rewind, consumer-only change). Carry from R121h-perf-bump-4.

Round 121j-5b.decision_summary: R121j-5b crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/query.rs new module: QueryableRegistry + QueryResponder + QueryReply (Reply Put/Del + Err) + into_response builder bridge. +14 lib tests. R121j-5c handles inbound wiring.
changes_bullets: New file crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/query.rs (~480 lines) mirrors crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/pubsub.rs SubscriberRegistry surface for the Q-side. Exports QueryableRegistry, QueryableId, QueryableCallback = Box<dyn FnMut(&Query, &mut QueryResponder<'_>) + Send + 'static>, QueryResponder<'a>, QueryReply (Reply { Put|Del } | Err), and ReplyBody. Re-uses pubsub::keyexpr_pattern_matches for the chunk-wildcard matcher so subscriber and queryable patterns are byte-identical in matching semantics.

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/lib.rs registers `pub mod query;` with a doc-comment explaining the R121j-5b vs R121j-5c split (registry + Responder land now; the dispatch_iteration_event wiring that turns the accumulated Vec<QueryReply> into outbound Response frames is R121j-5c scope).

QueryableRegistry::dispatch_request(&mut self, request: &Request, peer_keyexpr_table: &HashMap<u64, String>, replies: &mut Vec<QueryReply>) routes inbound Request(Query) to matched queryables. Non-Query RequestVariant arms (MsgPut|MsgDel|Default) are silently no-ops (matches zenoh-pico _z_handle_request which only routes Query through the queryable callback path). Peer-alias resolution is delegated to a caller-provided &HashMap<u64, String> rather than owning a private copy вҖ” the integration site (R121j-5c) will lend SubscriberRegistry's table so DeclKexpr / UndeclKexpr absorbed by the subscriber path automatically inform queryable resolution. No dual-write bookkeeping; no Arc-shared state required.

QueryResponder<'a> is a short-lived borrow constructed per matched queryable: holds (rid, keyexpr_literal, &mut Vec<QueryReply>). Exposes send_reply(payload), send_reply_del(), send_err(encoding_id, schema, payload), plus rid() / keyexpr_literal() accessors for diagnostic surfaces. Drops before dispatch_request advances to the next queryable so user closures cannot retain the borrow. FnMut signature (not async) keeps the dispatch path suitable for the (c11, bare_metal) runtime crate target.

QueryReply::into_response(self) -> Response composes the wire-form Response by delegating to the existing ResponseReplyBuilder / ResponseErrBuilder. Put path: ResponseReplyBuilder::new(rid, 0, Some(&keyexpr_literal), &payload).build(). Del path: same with .reply_del() flip. Err path: ResponseErrBuilder::new(...) with optional .encoding(id, schema) chained when QueryReply::Err.encoding is Some. Reply keyexpr emitted in literal form (mapping_id=0 + Some(literal)) per zenoh-pico parity вҖ” alias declaration on the responder side is a Phase D+ optimisation.

13 new lib tests (counted lines: empty_registry_dispatch_is_noop_and_no_replies_emitted, register_assigns_monotonic_ids_starting_from_one, unregister_is_idempotent_and_removes_only_matching_id, dispatch_fires_callback_on_literal_match_and_accumulates_reply, dispatch_with_wildcard_pattern_matches_multiple_chunks, dispatch_resolves_mapping_id_against_peer_table, dispatch_ignores_non_query_request_body_arms, responder_send_reply_del_emits_del_arm, responder_send_err_emits_err_with_encoding_tuple, responder_supports_multiple_replies_per_query, query_reply_into_response_put_path_round_trips_via_builder, query_reply_into_response_del_path_flips_inner_arm, query_reply_into_response_err_path_threads_encoding_tuple, multiple_queryables_match_same_keyexpr_fire_in_registration_order). Each pins a specific invariant: dispatch semantics, alias resolution, body-arm filtering, callback shape, replies accumulation, builder round-trip parity, ordering.
verification_bullets: cargo build -p wz-runtime-tokio: clean.
cargo test -p wz-runtime-tokio --lib: 124/124 pass (was 110; +14 new tests confirming the count above).

cargo clippy -p wz-runtime-tokio --all-targets: clean after one doc_lazy_continuation fix (nested-bullet-list followed by paragraph required a blank /// separator on the dispatch_request doc-comment at query.rs:374-381).

cargo test -p wz-integration-tests: pubsub round-trips unaffected (wz_publisher_to_zsub + wz_publisher_aliased_to_zsub still pass); the new query module is unwired from the production poll-loop until R121j-5c, so integration baselines stay byte-identical.

into_response builder-parity tests are the strongest single guarantee: QueryReply::into_response (Put|Del|Err) produces byte-identical encoded Response to a direct ResponseReplyBuilder/ResponseErrBuilder call with the same args, so R121j-5c can drain QueryableRegistry-accumulated replies into the existing encode_frame_with_response helper with zero wire-shape divergence.
carry_forward_bullets: R121j-5c integration wiring: add an iteration adapter on QueryableRegistry (mirror of pubsub::SubscriberRegistry::dispatch_iteration_event) that drains FramePayload, calls dispatch_request on each NetworkMessage::Request, then drains the accumulated Vec<QueryReply> into encode_frame_with_response + final encode_frame_with_response_final on the link's outbound side. Requires passing the SubscriberRegistry's peer_keyexpr_table by reference to QueryableRegistry::dispatch_request вҖ” sites: poll_and_dispatch_one + drive_session_until_terminal in session_glue.rs.

R121j-5c also needs an end-to-end integration test: spin up wz_initiator (sender of Request(Query)) + wz_acceptor with a registered queryable, expect Reply+Final on the wire. Mirror of the existing pubsub round-trip tests (wz_publisher_to_zsub).

ResponseFinal emit currently uses encode_frame_with_response_final at session_glue.rs:1249. R121j-5c will need a helper analogous to QueryReply::into_response for the Final вҖ” one Final per inbound Request (whether the queryable emitted zero, one, or many replies). Already factored: encode_frame_with_response_final exists; only the per-Request invocation site is new.

Peer-alias-table sharing decision: R121j-5b chose parameter-passing over Arc-sharing. If R121j-5c finds the parameter threading too noisy, the textbook upgrade is to introduce a `pub trait PeerKeyexprResolver { fn resolve(&self, id: u64) -> Option<&str>; }` so both registries can hold an Arc<dyn PeerKeyexprResolver + Send + Sync>. Defer until the noise actually surfaces.

Q-side Request-level ext chain coverage: qos / target / budget / timeout = 4/5 (R121j-tstamp still gated on wz_codecs ext_zbuf Timestamp wiring). The QueryResponder API does not yet expose responder ext (R121j-3c .responder() setter) вҖ” if the queryable needs to identify itself as a non-anonymous responder, R121j-5c can add a QueryResponder::with_responder_zid(...) configuration step or expose .responder() on QueryReply::into_response.
R121h-perf-bump-5 (encode_ext_chain Z-bit refactor) decision still pending вҖ” carried unchanged across this round.

QueryableRegistry::dispatch_request(&mut self, request: &Request, peer_keyexpr_table: &HashMap<u64, String>, replies: &mut Vec<QueryReply>) routes inbound Request(Query) to matched queryables. Non-Query RequestVariant arms (MsgPut|MsgDel|Default) are silently no-ops (matches zenoh-pico _z_handle_request which only routes Query through the queryable callback path). Peer-alias resolution is delegated to a caller-provided &HashMap<u64, String> rather than owning a private copy вҖ” the integration site (R121j-5c) will lend SubscriberRegistry's table so DeclKexpr / UndeclKexpr absorbed by the subscriber path automatically inform queryable resolution. No dual-write bookkeeping; no Arc-shared state required.

QueryResponder<'a> is a short-lived borrow constructed per matched queryable: holds (rid, keyexpr_literal, &mut Vec<QueryReply>). Exposes send_reply(payload), send_reply_del(), send_err(encoding_id, schema, payload), plus rid() / keyexpr_literal() accessors for diagnostic surfaces. Drops before dispatch_request advances to the next queryable so user closures cannot retain the borrow. FnMut signature (not async) keeps the dispatch path suitable for the (c11, bare_metal) runtime crate target.

QueryReply::into_response(self) -> Response composes the wire-form Response by delegating to the existing ResponseReplyBuilder / ResponseErrBuilder. Put path: ResponseReplyBuilder::new(rid, 0, Some(&keyexpr_literal), &payload).build(). Del path: same with .reply_del() flip. Err path: ResponseErrBuilder::new(...) with optional .encoding(id, schema) chained when QueryReply::Err.encoding is Some. Reply keyexpr emitted in literal form (mapping_id=0 + Some(literal)) per zenoh-pico parity вҖ” alias declaration on the responder side is a Phase D+ optimisation.

13 new lib tests (counted lines: empty_registry_dispatch_is_noop_and_no_replies_emitted, register_assigns_monotonic_ids_starting_from_one, unregister_is_idempotent_and_removes_only_matching_id, dispatch_fires_callback_on_literal_match_and_accumulates_reply, dispatch_with_wildcard_pattern_matches_multiple_chunks, dispatch_resolves_mapping_id_against_peer_table, dispatch_ignores_non_query_request_body_arms, responder_send_reply_del_emits_del_arm, responder_send_err_emits_err_with_encoding_tuple, responder_supports_multiple_replies_per_query, query_reply_into_response_put_path_round_trips_via_builder, query_reply_into_response_del_path_flips_inner_arm, query_reply_into_response_err_path_threads_encoding_tuple, multiple_queryables_match_same_keyexpr_fire_in_registration_order). Each pins a specific invariant: dispatch semantics, alias resolution, body-arm filtering, callback shape, replies accumulation, builder round-trip parity, ordering.

cargo test -p wz-integration-tests: pubsub round-trips unaffected (wz_publisher_to_zsub + wz_publisher_aliased_to_zsub still pass); the new query module is unwired from the production poll-loop until R121j-5c, so integration baselines stay byte-identical.

into_response builder-parity tests are the strongest single guarantee: QueryReply::into_response (Put|Del|Err) produces byte-identical encoded Response to a direct ResponseReplyBuilder/ResponseErrBuilder call with the same args, so R121j-5c can drain QueryableRegistry-accumulated replies into the existing encode_frame_with_response helper with zero wire-shape divergence.
publishable_carry_forward_bullets: R121j-5c integration wiring: add an iteration adapter on QueryableRegistry (mirror of pubsub::SubscriberRegistry::dispatch_iteration_event) that drains FramePayload, calls dispatch_request on each NetworkMessage::Request, then drains the accumulated Vec<QueryReply> into encode_frame_with_response + final encode_frame_with_response_final on the link's outbound side. Requires passing the SubscriberRegistry's peer_keyexpr_table by reference to QueryableRegistry::dispatch_request вҖ” sites: poll_and_dispatch_one + drive_session_until_terminal in session_glue.rs.

R121j-5c also needs an end-to-end integration test: spin up wz_initiator (sender of Request(Query)) + wz_acceptor with a registered queryable, expect Reply+Final on the wire. Mirror of the existing pubsub round-trip tests (wz_publisher_to_zsub).

ResponseFinal emit currently uses encode_frame_with_response_final at session_glue.rs:1249. R121j-5c will need a helper analogous to QueryReply::into_response for the Final вҖ” one Final per inbound Request (whether the queryable emitted zero, one, or many replies). Already factored: encode_frame_with_response_final exists; only the per-Request invocation site is new.

Peer-alias-table sharing decision: R121j-5b chose parameter-passing over Arc-sharing. If R121j-5c finds the parameter threading too noisy, the textbook upgrade is to introduce a `pub trait PeerKeyexprResolver { fn resolve(&self, id: u64) -> Option<&str>; }` so both registries can hold an Arc<dyn PeerKeyexprResolver + Send + Sync>. Defer until the noise actually surfaces.

Q-side Request-level ext chain coverage: qos / target / budget / timeout = 4/5 (R121j-tstamp still gated on wz_codecs ext_zbuf Timestamp wiring). The QueryResponder API does not yet expose responder ext (R121j-3c .responder() setter) вҖ” if the queryable needs to identify itself as a non-anonymous responder, R121j-5c can add a QueryResponder::with_responder_zid(...) configuration step or expose .responder() on QueryReply::into_response.
R121h-perf-bump-5 (encode_ext_chain Z-bit refactor) decision still pending вҖ” carried unchanged across this round.

Round 121j-5c.decision_summary: R121j-5c wiring: QueryableRegistry.dispatch_messages + _iteration_event + response_final_for + SubscriberRegistry.peer_keyexpr_table() getter. +5 lib tests. e2e TCP deferred.
changes_bullets: crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/pubsub.rs gains `pub fn peer_keyexpr_table(&self) -> &HashMap<u64, String>`. Lends the SubscriberRegistry-owned mapping table to the queryable side so DeclKexpr / UndeclKexpr absorbed by the subscriber path automatically informs queryable resolution вҖ” no dual-write bookkeeping, no Arc<Mutex<...>> needed. Doc-comment makes the cross-registry sharing contract explicit.

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/query.rs gains `pub fn dispatch_messages(&mut self, messages: &[NetworkMessage], peer_keyexpr_table: &HashMap<u64, String>, pending_replies: &mut Vec<QueryReply>, pending_final_rids: &mut Vec<u64>)`. Iterates messages, routes each NetworkMessage::Request through dispatch_request, and вҖ” only when dispatch_request actually emitted at least one Reply for this rid вҖ” enqueues the rid into pending_final_rids so the caller emits exactly one ResponseFinal per Reply chain (zenoh-pico parity: many Reply + one Final per Query). Un-resolvable mapping ids, non-Query body arms, and unmatched keyexprs all yield zero Replies and zero Finals вҖ” no false 'Final without Reply' wire shape.

query.rs also adds `pub fn dispatch_iteration_event(...)` adapter that pulls FramePayload.messages out of IterationEvent::Poll(DriverLoopOutcome::FramePayload {messages, ..}) and forwards to dispatch_messages. Mirror of SubscriberRegistry::dispatch_iteration_event so the queryable integration site uses the same observer-callback shape as the subscriber side.

query.rs gains the module-level helper `pub fn response_final_for(rid: u64) -> ResponseFinal` that constructs a wire-correct ResponseFinal envelope (header = _Z_MID_N_RESPONSE_FINAL = 0x1A; extensions = None today). Uses `..ResponseFinal::default()` spread so the helper stays resilient to future ResponseFinal field additions that land with sensible defaults.

5 new lib tests on the dispatch_messages + response_final_for surface: dispatch_messages_emits_final_for_each_matched_request (two matched + one unmatched -> exactly two Finals for the matched rids), dispatch_messages_skips_final_when_no_queryable_matched, dispatch_messages_skips_final_when_keyexpr_unresolvable, dispatch_messages_ignores_push_response_declare_variants (non-Query body arms produce no Reply / no Final), response_final_for_uses_default_header_and_explicit_rid (header bit + rid + extensions=None invariants).

Integration site (TCP-driven wz_initiator <-> wz_acceptor end-to-end Query/Reply round-trip via a registered queryable) intentionally deferred to a follow-up sub-round. R121j-5c stages the wire-level dispatch primitives and tests them in isolation; the e2e harness layering (LinkDriver send + frame-encode sequencing + sn assignment) requires its own design pass to decide the SessionLinkActions vs application-side ownership of the queryable registry. The lib-test surface here pins the wire-shape contract independent of that decision.

Architectural decision deferred: who owns QueryableRegistry on the acceptor side? Option A: each application creates one and threads it through the dispatch loop directly. Option B: SessionLinkActions grows a queryable: Arc<Mutex<QueryableRegistry>> field analogous to its subscriber: Arc<Mutex<SubscriberRegistry>> (if/when that lands). R121j-5c stays neutral; the e2e harness will surface the natural choice.

QueryResponder does not yet expose a .responder(zid, eid) setter for envelope-level responder ext (R121j-3c). Defer until the e2e flow surfaces the need.

new pub enum CongestionControl (Drop/Block) with wire_bit() mirror of z_congestion_control_t; named wire_bit rather than wire_byte since the value lifts into qos packed byte's bit 3, not a full byte

RequestQueryBuilder.request_qos_typed(priority, congestion, express) packs per `_z_n_qos_create` at network.h:84-89: `(express<<4) | (nodrop<<3) | priority`, then delegates to request_qos so the chain emit path stays uniform with the raw setter
3 new lib tests: packed-byte equivalence for 3 (Priority, Congestion, express) combinations; wire_byte/wire_bit mapping pin; typed-vs-raw byte-equivalent encode_to_vec round trip
clippy unnecessary_cast fix on congestion.wire_bit() (already returns u8); workspace-wide clippy run BEFORE first commit attempt avoided post-push hotfix lane
verification_bullets: cargo test -p wz-runtime-tokio --lib вҶ’ 179 вҶ’ 182 pass (+3 new); no regression
cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings clean after one quick fix iteration
scripts/run-ci.sh вҶ’ 8/8 lanes pass; typed-vs-raw byte-equivalent test pins that the wrapper is purely a packing convenience
carry_forward_bullets: AP MVP weighted ~99% (R121j builder ergonomics fully landed: qos / qos_typed / tstamp / target / budget / timeout)

changes_bullets: crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/keyexpr_canon.rs new module вҖ” pub fn canonize_keyexpr(input: &str) -> Result<String, KeyexprCanonError> + 5-variant KeyexprCanonError (EmptyChunk / ContainsSharpOrQmark / DollarAfterDollarOrStar / StarsInChunk / ContainsUnboundDollar mirroring zenoh-pico's zp_keyexpr_canon_status_t); two internal passes вҖ” collapse_dsl_runs (singleify $*$* runs, mirrors zenoh-pico __zp_singleify line 220-259) and canonize_chunks (per-chunk validation + rewriting, mirrors __zp_canon_prefix line 113-218 + second-pass rewrite line 318-430)

structural-only canonicalization вҖ” NO lowercase, NO NFC, NO Unicode normalization (confirmed via direct grep of zenoh-pico keyexpr.c + keyexpr_match_template.h showing no tolower / no normalize / no nfc references). R220 closure's carry text 'lowercase / NFC' was speculative вҖ” R221 corrects scope to structural canon only per the actual zenoh-pico semantics

ChunkShape internal enum classifies each chunk as SingleStar / DoubleStar / LoneDollarStar / Mixed; analyze_chunk per-char state machine (state values 0/1/3 mirror zenoh-pico's in_dollar 0/1/3 encoding so the cross-reference stays mechanical); chunk-level rewrite rules drop `*` and `**` after a prior `**` and rewrite lone `$*` chunk to `*`

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/pubsub.rs::SubscriberRegistry::register and crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/query.rs::QueryableRegistry::register wired вҖ” input keyexpr is canonicalized via canonize_keyexpr before being split into pattern_chunks; canon success replaces stored chunks with canonical form (lone $* вҶ’ *, **/* вҶ’ **, $*$* run вҶ’ $*), canon failure falls back to raw form with log::warn emit (non-breaking вҖ” promotion to Result-returning register deferred to cluster API rewrite)

Round 222.decision_summary: R222 Sample-type subscriber projection вҖ” Sample/SampleKind in new sample module; SubscriberCallback signature changed FnMut(&Push)->FnMut(&Sample); wz-ap-demo + 18 test sites migrated.
changes_bullets: crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/sample.rs new module вҖ” pub struct Sample { keyexpr: String, kind: SampleKind, payload: Vec<u8> } with #[non_exhaustive] for future-additive extension (timestamp / encoding / qos / attachment / source_info / reliability fields can be added in follow-up rounds without breaking external callers that read existing fields); pub enum SampleKind { Put=0, Del=1 } with repr(u8) matching zenoh-pico's z_sample_kind_t (vendor/zenoh-pico/include/zenoh-pico/api/constants.h lines 165-167); Sample::new_put and Sample::new_del constructors are the only external-construction surface (non_exhaustive prevents struct-literal construction outside the crate)

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/pubsub.rs SubscriberCallback type alias changed from Box<dyn FnMut(&Push) + Send + 'static> to Box<dyn FnMut(&Sample) + Send + 'static>; SubscriberRegistry::register and register_with_locality signatures updated to impl FnMut(&Sample) accordingly; dispatch_push gains a Push -> Sample projection step that matches on push.body's PushVariant (CodecZenohMsgPut -> SampleKind::Put + payload clone; CodecZenohMsgDel -> SampleKind::Del + empty Vec; Default { tag, body } -> silent drop because surfacing an unknown-tag body would semantically lie about the kind)

API-breaking change вҖ” the callback signature is no longer source-compatible. Mitigated by (a) all internal pubsub tests using `move |_push|` (underscore-prefix unused, rename to `_sample` is cosmetic), (b) wz-ap-demo's single subscriber callback was the only production caller, rewritten to use sample.keyexpr / sample.kind / sample.payload directly so the prior `match &push.keyexpr.body` tagged-union extraction is gone, (c) #[non_exhaustive] on Sample protects future extensions from inducing another break

crates/wz-ap-demo/src/main.rs subscriber callback rewritten вҖ” prior boilerplate that matched push.keyexpr.body's WireexprVariant arms to extract (id, suffix) for stderr logging is replaced by a single eprintln using sample.keyexpr (resolved literal), sample.kind, and sample.payload.len(); WireexprVariant import removed since no longer referenced
crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/lib.rs registers the new pub mod sample with module doc explaining the projection rationale and the non-exhaustive future-additive contract
verification_bullets: cargo test -p wz-runtime-tokio --lib: 226 -> 233 (+7 R222 tests вҖ” 4 sample::tests internal (numeric repr match against zenoh-pico's Z_SAMPLE_KIND_* / new_put carries keyexpr+payload / new_del carries empty payload / clone preserves kind+payload) + 3 pubsub::tests dispatch projection (Put push -> Sample with kind=Put + payload bytes / Del push -> Sample with kind=Del + empty payload / Sample.keyexpr is resolved literal not wire id) вҖ” all green; the existing 226 tests stay green confirming the callback signature change is mechanical-rename-only for `_push`-named fixtures)

cargo test -p wz-integration-tests: 65 tests across all binaries all green вҖ” no regression on Push / Del / Query / DECLARE / observer / reply paths; the wz-ap-demo migration is end-to-end verified by the publisher integration tests that spawn wz-ap-demo as a subprocess and assert on its stderr output (the new SUBSCRIBER FIRED format with keyexpr / kind / payload_len fields is asserted in those tests through R219's wz_publisher_*_to_zsub suite)

cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings: clean вҖ” the unused WireexprVariant import in wz-ap-demo/src/main.rs was caught + removed during the clippy pass; the non_exhaustive Sample struct introduces no new lint surface; the PushVariant::Default catch-all in dispatch_push satisfies the exhaustive-match requirement that R218 introduced

structural invariant: numeric_repr_matches_zenoh_pico test pins SampleKind::Put as u8 == 0, Del == 1 so any future wire-side extension that carries a kind byte (zenoh sample-kind extension on Push body) can serialize via `as u8` without translation
carry_forward_bullets: R218 + R219 + R220 + R221 + R222 + R223 closed the Pub/Sub inbound cluster end-to-end. The cluster ratify boundary: subscriber callbacks now receive a fully-projected Sample with resolved keyexpr + kind + payload; canonical pattern storage matches the wire form a peer emits; intra-chunk $* DSL matches zenoh-pico semantics; locality filter surfaces (dormant pre-loopback) match zenoh-pico API; DECLARE absorb covers every variant explicitly. Pub/Sub inbound parity now ~90% (was 70% at R217 start)

Future Sample expansion (additive via #[non_exhaustive]) carry вҖ” timestamp / encoding / qos / attachment / source_info / reliability fields are populated by zenoh-pico's z_sample_t today. Each one requires: (a) extract from MsgPut struct (already decoded by codec), (b) add field to Sample, (c) wire into dispatch_push. No API break since non_exhaustive prevents destructuring; existing callers reading sample.keyexpr / kind / payload unaffected. Estimated 1 round when a real consumer surfaces

Self-publish loopback infrastructure deferred (R223 carry continues) вҖ” currently SubscriberRegistry and QueryableRegistry only see wire-parsed records; Locality::SessionLocal is surface-correct but dispatch-dormant. Future round wires the local-publish path through dispatch_push with is_remote=false flag

PushVariant::Default { tag, body } silent-drop policy is conservative вҖ” a future round could surface the unknown-tag body via a new SampleKind::Unknown { tag } variant if applications need to log or forward unknown-kind samples. Defer until a real telemetry use case surfaces. Currently the drop is invisible to applications (no callback fires) which is the safe behavior for forward-compatibility

Round 224.decision_summary: R224 pre-push hook: stdin ref-tuple short-circuit + flock advisory lock close 22-session false-negative on Everything-up-to-date + concurrent push race
changes_bullets: `.githooks/pre-push` reads the git protocol stdin (`<local_ref> <local_sha> <remote_ref> <remote_sha>` per ref) and fast-exits with status 0 when no row has a non-zero `local_sha` вҖ” i.e. empty stdin (redundant push after another invocation updated the remote) or delete-only push (no new commit to validate).

`.githooks/pre-push` acquires a non-blocking `flock(1)` advisory lock on `$GIT_DIR/run-ci.lock` before entering `scripts/run-ci.sh`; failure exits 1 with an explicit `another run-ci already running` message + lock path so concurrent push attempts fail loud instead of silently racing `crates/target` cargo lock + sce-codegen freshness rebuild.
`flock(1)` presence guard via `command -v flock` so the advisory lock degrades to a no-op on slimmed containers without util-linux instead of hard-failing the hook.

Header rationale block extended with R224 retrospect documenting the 22-session false-negative root cause (a) Everything-up-to-date pushes still firing the full ~50s gate against zero work and (b) concurrent run-ci.sh races surfacing phantom Layer X FAIL on a clean tree.
verification_bullets: Test 1 (empty stdin): `echo -n '' | .githooks/pre-push origin git@example.com:repo.git` exits 0 with `pre-push: nothing to push (no non-delete refs); skipping CI gate`.
Test 2 (delete-only ref): stdin row with `local_sha = 40*'0'` exits 0 with the same fast-exit message вҖ” no ~50s gate spent on ref deletion.

Test 3 (concurrent lock): sibling process holds `$GIT_DIR/run-ci.lock` via `flock 200; sleep 10` background; hook with normal-ref stdin exits 1 with `another run-ci already running (lock: .git/run-ci.lock)`.

set -euo pipefail compatibility verified: `while read` EOF returns 1 inside the while-condition (not under `set -e` reject scope), `local_sha` only referenced inside the loop body so unset-variable not triggered.

`flock` advisory check via `command -v flock` keeps the guard optional; degraded path on slimmed containers still runs the original race-prone scripts/run-ci.sh path (transparent fallback, no new hard dependency).
carry_forward_bullets: R225 вҖ” Sample-type subscriber 5+ field unified surface. R222 lesson #5 (`#[non_exhaustive]` struct is not a partial-surface escape hatch) demotes the closure's R225+R226 split plan to anti-pattern; merge into a single round that adds timestamp + encoding (MsgPut/MsgDel already decode these) + qos + attachment + source_info + reliability (Push outer extensions require an extraction pass). Carry breaks naturally only if the wire-ext decode infra is genuinely missing for some field; otherwise unify.
R121h-perf-bump-5 SCE-a vs wz-b decision still pending user confirm вҖ” surface at an appropriate point in 23-session before further outbound-perf work.
Self-publish loopback infrastructure (Locality::SessionLocal dispatch path) вҖ” depends on Sample completeness from R225; 2-3 rounds estimated.
SCE upstream drift re-check at each kickoff. 23-session start: SCE origin/main unchanged at ebf3b3ff (local has 14 clippy/refactor commits ahead, none pushed); vendor pin remains ebf3b3ff.

Round 225.decision_summary: R225 Sample 6-field expansion: timestamp + encoding + qos + attachment + source_info + reliability surface mirrors zenoh-pico _z_sample_t in one shot; no partial-surface split
changes_bullets: `crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/sample.rs` extends `Sample` from 3 fields (keyexpr / kind / payload) to 9 fields by adding `timestamp: Option<TimestampHint>`, `encoding: Option<EncodingHint>`, `qos: Option<QosLevel>`, `attachment: Option<Vec<u8>>`, `source_info: Option<SourceInfo>`, `reliability: Reliability` вҖ” the full `_z_sample_t` parity surface in a single round per R222 lesson #5 (`#[non_exhaustive]` is not a partial-surface escape hatch).

New wz-owned mirror types `TimestampHint` / `EncodingHint` wrap codec `Timestamp` / `Encoding` (both `#[derive(Default)]`-only) so `Sample` can carry `Clone` / `Debug` / `PartialEq` derives the SCE codegen layer omits; the established wz pattern at `reply.rs` ~286-305 (codec value вҶ’ wz-owned mirror) is reused here verbatim.

New types `QosLevel { raw: u8 }`, `SourceInfo { zid: [u8;16], eid: u32, sn: u32 }`, `Reliability { BestEffort=0, Reliable=1 }` mirror zenoh-pico `_z_qos_t._val`, `_z_source_info_t`, and `z_reliability_t` respectively; `Reliability::default() == Reliable` matches `Z_RELIABILITY_DEFAULT`.

Three projection helpers `extract_qos` / `extract_attachment` / `extract_source_info` walk an `ExtEntry` chain matching on `(ext_id, enc)` tuples per zenoh-pico's dispatch switches вҖ” `_z_push_decode_ext_cb` for outer QoS (`ext_id=0x01, enc=ZInt`) and `_z_push_body_decode_extensions` for body attachment (`0x03, ZBuf`) + source_info (`0x01, ZBuf`).

`decode_source_info_payload` parses the source-info ZBuf shape per `_z_source_info_decode` (`vendor/zenoh-pico/src/protocol/codec/message.c` 196-231): 1 byte header (high nibble = zidlen-1), zidlen bytes zid right-padded into the [u8; 16] buffer, VLE-encoded eid (u32 overflow rejected), VLE-encoded sn (u32 overflow rejected). Carry text recorded an incorrect '16 bytes zid + VLE eid + VLE sn' shape; falsified before implementation by direct read of message.c вҖ” lesson recorded below.

`crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/pubsub.rs::dispatch_push` now projects the full Sample literal: body-level timestamp/encoding via `TimestampHint::from_codec` / `EncodingHint::from_codec` from `MsgPut.timestamp` / `MsgPut.encoding` (Put) or `MsgDel.timestamp` (Del, encoding = None); body-level attachment + source_info via the helpers on `MsgPut.extensions` / `MsgDel.extensions`; outer-level QoS via helper on `Push.extensions`; `reliability = Reliability::default()` (transport-context wire-up = R226+ carry).

Builder setters `with_timestamp` / `with_encoding` / `with_qos` / `with_attachment` / `with_source_info` / `with_reliability` keep external `Sample::new_put` / `new_del` callers source-compatible while allowing intra-crate `dispatch_push` to construct via struct literal.
verification_bullets: `cargo test -p wz-runtime-tokio --lib`: 233 вҶ’ 252 (+19) all pass. New tests cover the 9 Sample fields default state (`new_put` + `new_del` baselines), `Reliability::default() == Reliable` (zenoh-pico parity), `QosLevel::is_express` bit (1<<4) per zenoh-pico `_Z_N_QOS_IS_EXPRESS_FLAG`, builder chain through all 6 new metadata setters, `TimestampHint::from_codec` / `EncodingHint::from_codec` field round-trip (incl. absent-schema case), each `extract_*` helper's empty-chain / non-matching-tuple / first-match projection paths, `decode_source_info_payload` happy path (5-byte zid + single-byte VLEs) + multi-byte VLE path (200 / 16384) + truncated zid window + empty payload, and the `read_vle_u64` private helper's single-byte / multi-byte / truncation branches.

`cargo test -p wz-integration-tests`: 65 / 65 pass, regression 0. e2e suite (wz_publisher_*_to_zsub family + wz_initiator_to_* + ap_demo_round_trip + remote_declare + reply / queryable) continues to exercise the wire round-trip with the Sample callback signature now carrying the full 9-field projection; the `Sample` re-shape is non-breaking for existing subscribers because the load-bearing fields (keyexpr / kind / payload) keep their position and the new fields slot in as `Option<_>` / `Reliability` defaults.

`cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings`: clean across all crates including wz-codecs / wz-runtime-tokio / wz-integration-tests / wz-ap-demo / wz-runtime-tokio-test-support / zenoh-pico-sys.

Carry-text falsify discipline (R221 textbook applied here): before implementation, carry's '16 bytes zid + VLE eid + VLE sn' source-info wire shape was checked against `_z_source_info_decode` directly and corrected to the actual '1 byte zidlen header + zidlen bytes zid + VLE eid + VLE sn' shape. Discovered during step 3; the corrected shape is what landed in `decode_source_info_payload`. Confirms the operating pattern surfaces real bugs cheaply.
carry_forward_bullets: R226 reliability transport-context wire-up. `Sample::reliability` is filled with `Reliability::default() == Reliable` in R225 because `dispatch_push` has no transport-context handle; the link-layer reliability value (best-effort vs reliable channel) needs to thread from the link / session layer through the registry so subscribers can observe the actual delivery class. zenoh-pico passes `z_reliability_t` as an explicit argument to `_z_trigger_push` (`src/session/push.c` 25-39) вҖ” wz mirror is the same shape (extra dispatch_push argument or per-link default stored in registry).

Operating pattern extension вҖ” 'carry text falsify' broadens beyond grep into type-compatibility falsification. R225 entry discovered that the closure's 'MsgPut struct has timestamp + encoding already decoded вҶ’ immediate surface possible' assumption was false at the Rust level because the codec structs derive only `Default` (no `Clone`); the wz reply.rs ~286-305 wrap pattern resolves it. Future kickoffs: when carry text claims 'X struct exposes Y field already', verify both the field exists (grep) AND that the field is in a `Clone`-able container (read the struct's derive line); a `.clone()` plan fails the type-compat check if the container is not Clone.

New wz-owned mirror types `TimestampHint` / `EncodingHint` wrap codec `Timestamp` / `Encoding` (both `#[derive(Default)]`-only) so `Sample` can carry `Clone` / `Debug` / `PartialEq` derives the SCE codegen layer omits; the established wz pattern at `reply.rs` ~286-305 (codec value вҶ’ wz-owned mirror) is reused here verbatim.

New types `QosLevel { raw: u8 }`, `SourceInfo { zid: [u8;16], eid: u32, sn: u32 }`, `Reliability { BestEffort=0, Reliable=1 }` mirror zenoh-pico `_z_qos_t._val`, `_z_source_info_t`, and `z_reliability_t` respectively; `Reliability::default() == Reliable` matches `Z_RELIABILITY_DEFAULT`.

Three projection helpers `extract_qos` / `extract_attachment` / `extract_source_info` walk an `ExtEntry` chain matching on `(ext_id, enc)` tuples per zenoh-pico's dispatch switches вҖ” `_z_push_decode_ext_cb` for outer QoS (`ext_id=0x01, enc=ZInt`) and `_z_push_body_decode_extensions` for body attachment (`0x03, ZBuf`) + source_info (`0x01, ZBuf`).

`decode_source_info_payload` parses the source-info ZBuf shape per `_z_source_info_decode` (`vendor/zenoh-pico/src/protocol/codec/message.c` 196-231): 1 byte header (high nibble = zidlen-1), zidlen bytes zid right-padded into the [u8; 16] buffer, VLE-encoded eid (u32 overflow rejected), VLE-encoded sn (u32 overflow rejected). Carry text recorded an incorrect '16 bytes zid + VLE eid + VLE sn' shape; falsified before implementation by direct read of message.c вҖ” lesson recorded below.

`crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/pubsub.rs::dispatch_push` now projects the full Sample literal: body-level timestamp/encoding via `TimestampHint::from_codec` / `EncodingHint::from_codec` from `MsgPut.timestamp` / `MsgPut.encoding` (Put) or `MsgDel.timestamp` (Del, encoding = None); body-level attachment + source_info via the helpers on `MsgPut.extensions` / `MsgDel.extensions`; outer-level QoS via helper on `Push.extensions`; `reliability = Reliability::default()` (transport-context wire-up = R226+ carry).

Builder setters `with_timestamp` / `with_encoding` / `with_qos` / `with_attachment` / `with_source_info` / `with_reliability` keep external `Sample::new_put` / `new_del` callers source-compatible while allowing intra-crate `dispatch_push` to construct via struct literal.
publishable_verification_bullets: `cargo test -p wz-runtime-tokio --lib`: 233 вҶ’ 252 (+19) all pass. New tests cover the 9 Sample fields default state (`new_put` + `new_del` baselines), `Reliability::default() == Reliable` (zenoh-pico parity), `QosLevel::is_express` bit (1<<4) per zenoh-pico `_Z_N_QOS_IS_EXPRESS_FLAG`, builder chain through all 6 new metadata setters, `TimestampHint::from_codec` / `EncodingHint::from_codec` field round-trip (incl. absent-schema case), each `extract_*` helper's empty-chain / non-matching-tuple / first-match projection paths, `decode_source_info_payload` happy path (5-byte zid + single-byte VLEs) + multi-byte VLE path (200 / 16384) + truncated zid window + empty payload, and the `read_vle_u64` private helper's single-byte / multi-byte / truncation branches.

`cargo test -p wz-integration-tests`: 65 / 65 pass, regression 0. e2e suite (wz_publisher_*_to_zsub family + wz_initiator_to_* + ap_demo_round_trip + remote_declare + reply / queryable) continues to exercise the wire round-trip with the Sample callback signature now carrying the full 9-field projection; the `Sample` re-shape is non-breaking for existing subscribers because the load-bearing fields (keyexpr / kind / payload) keep their position and the new fields slot in as `Option<_>` / `Reliability` defaults.

`cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings`: clean across all crates including wz-codecs / wz-runtime-tokio / wz-integration-tests / wz-ap-demo / wz-runtime-tokio-test-support / zenoh-pico-sys.

Carry-text falsify discipline (R221 textbook applied here): before implementation, carry's '16 bytes zid + VLE eid + VLE sn' source-info wire shape was checked against `_z_source_info_decode` directly and corrected to the actual '1 byte zidlen header + zidlen bytes zid + VLE eid + VLE sn' shape. Discovered during step 3; the corrected shape is what landed in `decode_source_info_payload`. Confirms the operating pattern surfaces real bugs cheaply.
publishable_carry_forward_bullets: R226 reliability transport-context wire-up. `Sample::reliability` is filled with `Reliability::default() == Reliable` in R225 because `dispatch_push` has no transport-context handle; the link-layer reliability value (best-effort vs reliable channel) needs to thread from the link / session layer through the registry so subscribers can observe the actual delivery class. zenoh-pico passes `z_reliability_t` as an explicit argument to `_z_trigger_push` (`src/session/push.c` 25-39) вҖ” wz mirror is the same shape (extra dispatch_push argument or per-link default stored in registry).

Operating pattern extension вҖ” 'carry text falsify' broadens beyond grep into type-compatibility falsification. R225 entry discovered that the closure's 'MsgPut struct has timestamp + encoding already decoded вҶ’ immediate surface possible' assumption was false at the Rust level because the codec structs derive only `Default` (no `Clone`); the wz reply.rs ~286-305 wrap pattern resolves it. Future kickoffs: when carry text claims 'X struct exposes Y field already', verify both the field exists (grep) AND that the field is in a `Clone`-able container (read the struct's derive line); a `.clone()` plan fails the type-compat check if the container is not Clone.
R121h-perf-bump-5 SCE-a vs wz-b decision still pending user confirm вҖ” surface at an appropriate point in 23-session.
SCE upstream drift carry: SCE origin/main unchanged at ebf3b3ff; SCE local 14 commits ahead (clippy / refactor cleanup, none pushed). 23-session kickoff confirmed no vendor pin bump needed.

Round 226.decision_summary: R226 unifies Reliability enum + threads link-layer reliability from FramePayload.reliable through dispatch chain into Sample.reliability, closing R225 carry
changes_bullets: `crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/lib.rs` extends the crate-root `Reliability` enum with `Default` / `Hash` / `repr(u8)` / explicit discriminants (`BestEffort=0`, `Reliable=1`, default `Reliable`) so the outbound driver hint and inbound `Sample.reliability` share a single typed surface. New `Reliability::from_reliable_bool` adapter maps the `DriverLoopOutcome::FramePayload.reliable` discriminator to the typed enum.

`crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/sample.rs` retires the duplicate `Reliability` enum that R225 introduced under the sample module; the previous `pub enum Reliability { ... }` is replaced with `pub use crate::Reliability;` so the sample-module re-export keeps `crate::sample::Reliability` source-compatible for callers while collapsing to one definition.

`crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/pubsub.rs::dispatch_iteration_event` now destructures `FramePayload.reliable`, converts it via `Reliability::from_reliable_bool`, and threads the value through every per-message `dispatch` call. `dispatch(message, reliability)` and `dispatch_push(push, reliability)` grow an explicit `Reliability` argument so the link-layer classification reaches the `Sample` literal instead of the R225 placeholder `Reliability::default()`.

All 30 in-crate `registry.dispatch(...)` call sites (28 in pubsub.rs tests + 2 in the ResponseFinal / undeclare paths) now pass `Reliability::Reliable` explicitly. The Declare-arm absorb stays reliability-agnostic (declarations always travel on the reliable channel per session-fsm В§6); the argument is consumed only by the Push arm via `dispatch_push`.
verification_bullets: `cargo test -p wz-runtime-tokio --lib`: 252 -> 256 (+4) all pass. New tests cover `dispatch(_, Reliable)` -> `Sample.reliability == Reliable`, `dispatch(_, BestEffort)` -> `Sample.reliability == BestEffort`, `dispatch_iteration_event(FramePayload { reliable: false, .. })` -> `Sample.reliability == BestEffort` (end-to-end frame -> sample projection), and `Reliability::from_reliable_bool(true|false)` adapter pairing.

`cargo test -p wz-integration-tests`: 65 / 65 pass, regression 0. The full e2e suite (pubsub round-trips against zenoh-pico, queryable / reply, remote declare, ap_demo) continues to interop with the now-threaded reliability argument because `dispatch_iteration_event` keeps its external signature; the new argument lives only on `dispatch` / `dispatch_push` whose external callers are limited to internal unit tests (already updated).

`cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings`: clean across wz-codecs / wz-runtime-tokio / wz-runtime-tokio-test-support / wz-integration-tests / wz-ap-demo / zenoh-pico-sys. The duplicate-enum cleanup did not surface any new lints; `pub use crate::Reliability` re-export is unambiguous.
carry_forward_bullets: R227+ Self-publish loopback infrastructure (R222 cluster carry). `dispatch_push(push, reliability)` now has a stable shape; the self-publish path will reuse it by routing the local Sample through the same dispatcher with the registry's default reliability (or a per-publisher override). Locality::SessionLocal short-circuit in the allows_remote() branch from R223 is the matching dormant surface that activates with this work.
R121h-perf-bump-5 SCE-a vs wz-b decision still pending user confirm.
SCE upstream drift carry: re-check at next kickoff. SCE origin/main unchanged at ebf3b3ff at the start of this session.

Operating pattern (R225 lesson #4 next-step refinement): cluster-level enum / type-surface review BEFORE adding a new enum. R225 added `sample::Reliability` without first searching for an existing crate-level enum of the same semantic; R226 had to retire it within one round. Future kickoffs: when introducing a typed surface that maps a wire concept (reliability, locality, kind, priority, etc.), grep `pub enum <Name>` and `pub use ... as <Name>` across the crate root + every module before authoring; if the concept already exists, extend it rather than introducing a sibling.

Round 227.decision_summary: R227 self-publish loopback: SubscriberRegistry::local_publish surfaces wire-equivalent dispatch with is_remote=false; SessionLocal activates, Remote suppressed, single fire_to_subscribers gate
changes_bullets: `crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/pubsub.rs::SubscriberRegistry` adds public method `local_publish(&mut self, sample: &Sample) -> usize` вҖ” self-publish loopback entry point. Routes the caller-built `Sample` through `fire_to_subscribers` with `is_remote = false`, applying `Locality::allows_local()` so `SessionLocal` + `Any` fire and `Remote` is suppressed. Returns the count of subscriber callbacks that fired so the caller can verify loopback delivery. Mirrors zenoh-pico `_z_session_deliver_push_locally` (`vendor/zenoh-pico/src/session/loopback.c` 70-100) which is routed from `_z_write` (`vendor/zenoh-pico/src/net/primitives.c` 198-202) when the publisher's `allowed_destination.allows_local()` holds.

`pubsub.rs::dispatch_push` signature extends from `(push, reliability)` to `(push, reliability, is_remote: bool)`; the private fn is the wire-arrived dispatch entry, so the change is internal-only and the public `dispatch` / `dispatch_iteration_event` surfaces are unchanged. The `dispatch` body now passes `true` for `NetworkMessage::Push` per R227 single-dispatch-path semantic. The user pre-confirmed (a) `dispatch_push` signature extension over (b) a sibling `local_dispatch_push` entry point вҖ” the (a) shape preserves zenoh-pico's `_z_handle_network_message` single-handler invariant.

New helper `fire_to_subscribers(&mut self, sample: &Sample, is_remote: bool) -> usize` factors out the locality-predicate + pattern-match + callback-fire loop previously inlined at the end of `dispatch_push`. Selects `Locality::allows_remote()` when `is_remote=true` and `Locality::allows_local()` otherwise, then matches each subscriber's pattern chunks against `sample.keyexpr` via `keyexpr_pattern_matches`. Returns the fired count; the wire path discards it, `local_publish` returns it to the caller. The helper is the single source of truth for subscriber filtering вҖ” both wire and loopback paths converge here so the locality + pattern-match invariants are enforced exactly once.

`crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/locality.rs` module doc retires the pre-R227 'carry: not in any current cluster' wording. The new doc states both invariants: wire-arrived Push routes through `dispatch_push` with `is_remote=true` вҶ’ `allows_remote()` (`SessionLocal` suppressed, `Any`/`Remote` fire), and `local_publish` routes through `fire_to_subscribers` with `is_remote=false` вҶ’ `allows_local()` (`Remote` suppressed, `Any`/`SessionLocal` fire). QueryableRegistry-side loopback parity is noted as deferred until the use case surfaces.

`pubsub.rs` module-level Scope section extends with the R227 loopback bullet pointing at `SubscriberRegistry::local_publish`, the `Locality::SessionLocal` activation, the `Locality::Remote` loopback suppression, and the zenoh-pico mirror citations (`loopback.c` 70-100 + `primitives.c` 198-202).

`LocalPublishRequest` builder NOT introduced вҖ” `Sample` is already `#[non_exhaustive]` with `new_put` / `new_del` + 6 `with_*` setters covering all 9 fields, so reusing `Sample` as the publish input is the textbook choice (R225 lesson #5 surface-all-at-once already satisfied without a new sibling type).

12 new R227 unit tests on `local_publish`: `Locality::Any` fires; `Locality::SessionLocal` fires (R227 activation guard вҖ” was dormant before); `Locality::Remote` suppressed (zenoh-pico `_z_locality_allows_local(Z_LOCALITY_REMOTE)` parity); mixed-locality isolation (3 subscribers on the same keyexpr вҖ” loopback fires `Any` + `SessionLocal`, wire-arrived dispatch fires `Any` + `Remote`, same registry, single source of truth); empty-registry returns 0; pattern-mismatch returns 0; multi-subscriber count returns total fired (`fired = 2` for two overlapping matches); `**` zero-or-more wildcard matches on loopback; `$*` intra-chunk DSL (R220) matches on loopback; `Sample` fields propagate verbatim (registry does not synthesize); `SampleKind::Del` routes through the same path as Put with empty payload; locality predicate runs before keyexpr match (regression guard).

`pubsub.rs::register_with_locality_session_local_does_not_fire_on_inbound` test body comment updated to reflect post-R227 semantic. The test assertion is unchanged (wire-arrived `SessionLocal` subscriber still does not fire); the comment now states the predicate-level reason (`is_remote=true` вҶ’ `allows_remote()` is false for `SessionLocal`) rather than the pre-R227 'no loopback exists yet' rationale.
verification_bullets: `cargo test -p wz-runtime-tokio --lib`: 256 -> 268 (+12) all pass. 256 pre-R227 lib tests unchanged; 12 new R227 tests cover the `local_publish` surface and the dual-origin locality matrix (Any / SessionLocal / Remote across wire and loopback paths).

`cargo test -p wz-integration-tests`: 65 / 65 pass across all binaries, regression 0. The `dispatch_push` signature change is internal (private fn); external observers (`dispatch` / `dispatch_iteration_event`) keep their shapes, so the wire-side interop suite (wz_publisher_* / wz_initiator_* / ap_demo_round_trip / remote_declare / reply / queryable) continues to exercise the full e2e round-trip unchanged.

`cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings`: clean across wz-codecs / wz-runtime-tokio / wz-runtime-tokio-test-support / wz-integration-tests / wz-ap-demo / zenoh-pico-sys. No new lints surfaced from the helper refactor or the new method.

zenoh-pico mirror trace confirmed by direct read: `_z_session_deliver_push_locally` (`vendor/zenoh-pico/src/session/loopback.c` 70-100) is the upstream's separate-entry-point loopback shape (option (b) in the user pre-confirm). wz adopts option (a) (extend `dispatch_push` with `is_remote`) per the user's preview-selected choice; the single-dispatch-path semantic is preserved via the `fire_to_subscribers` helper that both wire and loopback paths converge on, exactly mirroring zenoh-pico's `_z_handle_network_message` single-handler invariant.

Pre-confirm discipline (R222 lesson) applied: although `dispatch_push` is a private fn (signature change has zero external impact), pre-confirm was requested because the substantive change is the user-visible `local_publish` surface + `Locality::SessionLocal` activation semantic. Option (a) vs (b) preview rendered side-by-side so the user could compare the two trade-offs before committing.

Cluster-level enum / type-surface pre-grep (R225 lesson #4 textbook applied): before authoring `local_publish`, ran `grep -rn 'pub enum Locality | pub use .* as Locality | pub struct Locality' crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/` to confirm no duplicate Locality surface exists, plus `grep -rn 'impl SubscriberRegistry | pub fn' pubsub.rs` to confirm no `local_publish` method already exists. Both checks clean вҖ” no sibling surface to retire.
carry_forward_bullets: R228+ publisher-side loopback wiring. R227 surfaces the `SubscriberRegistry::local_publish` infrastructure but no publisher path invokes it yet вҖ” an outbound publish over the wire does not also call `local_publish`, so an `Any` subscriber on the same session as a publisher does not see the publish unless the wire echoes back. R228+ candidate: wire `local_publish` into the outbound publish path (either by extending `_z_write`'s wz mirror with the `allows_local()` branch, or by introducing a session-level Publisher surface that fans out to both wire and loopback). zenoh-pico's `_z_write` symmetric branch at `vendor/zenoh-pico/src/net/primitives.c` 198-202 is the textbook mirror.

QueryableRegistry self-loopback parity. R227 covers the SubscriberRegistry side only; the symmetric `_z_session_deliver_query_locally` (`vendor/zenoh-pico/src/session/loopback.c` 121-142) + `_z_session_deliver_reply_locally` (`loopback.c` 166-198) + `_z_session_deliver_reply_err_locally` + `_z_session_deliver_reply_final_locally` shapes have no wz mirror yet. Surfaces when a self-query use case lands (no current demo path).

Reliability default for self-publish: caller-owned by design. `local_publish(&sample)` reads `sample.reliability` as-is; the registry does not synthesize a default. zenoh-pico's `_z_session_deliver_push_locally` takes reliability as an explicit argument from `_z_write`, which derives it from the publisher's per-publisher reliability config. When a publisher API lands (R228+), it must pick the per-publisher default (Reliable per `Z_RELIABILITY_DEFAULT`) and let callers override via `Sample::with_reliability`.
R121h-perf-bump-5 SCE-a vs wz-b decision still pending user confirm; not in R227 scope. Surface at an appropriate point in 24-session.

SCE upstream drift carry: at R227 start, SCE origin/main unchanged at `ebf3b3ff` (matches wz vendor pin). SCE local has ~20 unpushed commits (refactor: Bundle args into context structs + clippy cleanup, none pushed). 24-session kickoff confirmed no vendor pin bump needed. Re-check at next kickoff.

Operating pattern textbook applied + new lesson recorded (R225 lesson #3 carry-text falsify extension): R226 closure carry text claimed 'dispatch_push(push, reliability) now has a stable shape; the self-publish path will reuse it by routing the local Sample through the same dispatcher'. R227 falsified the 'stable shape' portion вҖ” adding `is_remote` to the private fn was the textbook choice (single dispatch path with locality discriminator) over fabricating a sibling `local_dispatch_push`. The carry text's underlying claim (single dispatcher reuse) was correct; the 'stable shape' surface was the falsified piece. Future kickoffs: carry-text claims about API stability are particularly worth falsifying because they tend to mask the locality / discriminator semantic that the next round actually adds. The dual-form preview in `AskUserQuestion` made the falsification cheap (option (a) vs (b) side-by-side).
