12 new R227 unit tests on `local_publish`: `Locality::Any` fires; `Locality::SessionLocal` fires (R227 activation guard вҖ” was dormant before); `Locality::Remote` suppressed (zenoh-pico `_z_locality_allows_local(Z_LOCALITY_REMOTE)` parity); mixed-locality isolation (3 subscribers on the same keyexpr вҖ” loopback fires `Any` + `SessionLocal`, wire-arrived dispatch fires `Any` + `Remote`, same registry, single source of truth); empty-registry returns 0; pattern-mismatch returns 0; multi-subscriber count returns total fired (`fired = 2` for two overlapping matches); `**` zero-or-more wildcard matches on loopback; `$*` intra-chunk DSL (R220) matches on loopback; `Sample` fields propagate verbatim (registry does not synthesize); `SampleKind::Del` routes through the same path as Put with empty payload; locality predicate runs before keyexpr match (regression guard).

`pubsub.rs::register_with_locality_session_local_does_not_fire_on_inbound` test body comment updated to reflect post-R227 semantic. The test assertion is unchanged (wire-arrived `SessionLocal` subscriber still does not fire); the comment now states the predicate-level reason (`is_remote=true` вҶ’ `allows_remote()` is false for `SessionLocal`) rather than the pre-R227 'no loopback exists yet' rationale.
publishable_verification_bullets: `cargo test -p wz-runtime-tokio --lib`: 256 -> 268 (+12) all pass. 256 pre-R227 lib tests unchanged; 12 new R227 tests cover the `local_publish` surface and the dual-origin locality matrix (Any / SessionLocal / Remote across wire and loopback paths).

`cargo test -p wz-integration-tests`: 65 / 65 pass across all binaries, regression 0. The `dispatch_push` signature change is internal (private fn); external observers (`dispatch` / `dispatch_iteration_event`) keep their shapes, so the wire-side interop suite (wz_publisher_* / wz_initiator_* / ap_demo_round_trip / remote_declare / reply / queryable) continues to exercise the full e2e round-trip unchanged.

`cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings`: clean across wz-codecs / wz-runtime-tokio / wz-runtime-tokio-test-support / wz-integration-tests / wz-ap-demo / zenoh-pico-sys. No new lints surfaced from the helper refactor or the new method.

zenoh-pico mirror trace confirmed by direct read: `_z_session_deliver_push_locally` (`vendor/zenoh-pico/src/session/loopback.c` 70-100) is the upstream's separate-entry-point loopback shape (option (b) in the user pre-confirm). wz adopts option (a) (extend `dispatch_push` with `is_remote`) per the user's preview-selected choice; the single-dispatch-path semantic is preserved via the `fire_to_subscribers` helper that both wire and loopback paths converge on, exactly mirroring zenoh-pico's `_z_handle_network_message` single-handler invariant.

Pre-confirm discipline (R222 lesson) applied: although `dispatch_push` is a private fn (signature change has zero external impact), pre-confirm was requested because the substantive change is the user-visible `local_publish` surface + `Locality::SessionLocal` activation semantic. Option (a) vs (b) preview rendered side-by-side so the user could compare the two trade-offs before committing.

Cluster-level enum / type-surface pre-grep (R225 lesson #4 textbook applied): before authoring `local_publish`, ran `grep -rn 'pub enum Locality | pub use .* as Locality | pub struct Locality' crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/` to confirm no duplicate Locality surface exists, plus `grep -rn 'impl SubscriberRegistry | pub fn' pubsub.rs` to confirm no `local_publish` method already exists. Both checks clean вҖ” no sibling surface to retire.
publishable_carry_forward_bullets: R228+ publisher-side loopback wiring. R227 surfaces the `SubscriberRegistry::local_publish` infrastructure but no publisher path invokes it yet вҖ” an outbound publish over the wire does not also call `local_publish`, so an `Any` subscriber on the same session as a publisher does not see the publish unless the wire echoes back. R228+ candidate: wire `local_publish` into the outbound publish path (either by extending `_z_write`'s wz mirror with the `allows_local()` branch, or by introducing a session-level Publisher surface that fans out to both wire and loopback). zenoh-pico's `_z_write` symmetric branch at `vendor/zenoh-pico/src/net/primitives.c` 198-202 is the textbook mirror.

QueryableRegistry self-loopback parity. R227 covers the SubscriberRegistry side only; the symmetric `_z_session_deliver_query_locally` (`vendor/zenoh-pico/src/session/loopback.c` 121-142) + `_z_session_deliver_reply_locally` (`loopback.c` 166-198) + `_z_session_deliver_reply_err_locally` + `_z_session_deliver_reply_final_locally` shapes have no wz mirror yet. Surfaces when a self-query use case lands (no current demo path).

Reliability default for self-publish: caller-owned by design. `local_publish(&sample)` reads `sample.reliability` as-is; the registry does not synthesize a default. zenoh-pico's `_z_session_deliver_push_locally` takes reliability as an explicit argument from `_z_write`, which derives it from the publisher's per-publisher reliability config. When a publisher API lands (R228+), it must pick the per-publisher default (Reliable per `Z_RELIABILITY_DEFAULT`) and let callers override via `Sample::with_reliability`.
R121h-perf-bump-5 SCE-a vs wz-b decision still pending user confirm; not in R227 scope. Surface at an appropriate point in 24-session.

SCE upstream drift carry: at R227 start, SCE origin/main unchanged at `ebf3b3ff` (matches wz vendor pin). SCE local has ~20 unpushed commits (refactor: Bundle args into context structs + clippy cleanup, none pushed). 24-session kickoff confirmed no vendor pin bump needed. Re-check at next kickoff.

Operating pattern textbook applied + new lesson recorded (R225 lesson #3 carry-text falsify extension): R226 closure carry text claimed 'dispatch_push(push, reliability) now has a stable shape; the self-publish path will reuse it by routing the local Sample through the same dispatcher'. R227 falsified the 'stable shape' portion вҖ” adding `is_remote` to the private fn was the textbook choice (single dispatch path with locality discriminator) over fabricating a sibling `local_dispatch_push`. The carry text's underlying claim (single dispatcher reuse) was correct; the 'stable shape' surface was the falsified piece. Future kickoffs: carry-text claims about API stability are particularly worth falsifying because they tend to mask the locality / discriminator semantic that the next round actually adds. The dual-form preview in `AskUserQuestion` made the falsification cheap (option (a) vs (b) side-by-side).

Round 228.decision_summary: R228 Session bundle: Arc<SessionLinkActions> + Arc<Mutex<ApplicationLayerObserver>>; publish() fans wire + loopback per PublishOptions.allowed_destination Locality predicate
changes_bullets: New module `crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session.rs` introduces `Session` вҖ” the application-level bundle mirroring zenoh-pico's `_z_session_t`. `Session` owns `Arc<SessionLinkActions>` (outbound handle) and `Arc<Mutex<ApplicationLayerObserver>>` (inbound registries bundle). A single `Session::publish(keyexpr, payload, opts)` call fans both branches: wire-side `send_push_literal` / `send_push_del_literal` under `Locality::allows_remote()`, and loopback `SubscriberRegistry::local_publish` under `Locality::allows_local()`. Mirrors `_z_write` (`vendor/zenoh-pico/src/net/primitives.c` 170-205) which is the single zenoh-pico entry point that fans both branches off a single record.

`PublishOptions` is the new options bundle for `Session::publish`. `#[non_exhaustive]` to allow R229+ additions without breaking external callers. R228 scope: 3 fields (`allowed_destination: Locality`, `reliability: Reliability`, `kind: SampleKind`). Builder API: `put()` / `del()` constructors (defaults to `Any` locality + `Reliable` + matching kind) + `with_locality(...)` / `with_reliability(...)` / `with_kind(...)` chainable setters. The remaining 5 `Sample` body fields (`qos`, `attachment`, `timestamp`, `encoding`, `source_info`) are deferred to R229+ because the wire-side `send_push_literal` does not accept them either вҖ” propagating them through `Session::publish` now would silently fork wire (loses metadata) vs loopback (preserves metadata) symmetry.

`Session::publish` reads `opts.allowed_destination` and routes: `allows_remote()` вҶ’ wire send through the existing `SessionLinkActions` API (Put picks `send_push_literal`, Del picks `send_push_del_literal`; the `payload` argument is ignored on Del kind вҖ” zenoh-pico `_z_n_msg_make_push_del` does not carry payload). `allows_local()` вҶ’ build a `Sample` via `Sample::new_put` / `Sample::new_del` + `with_reliability(opts.reliability)` and dispatch through `observer.lock().unwrap().subscribers.local_publish(&sample)`. Returns the loopback `fired` count (0 when wire-only or no matching subscriber).

`Session::actions(&self) -> &Arc<SessionLinkActions>` and `Session::observer(&self) -> &Arc<Mutex<ApplicationLayerObserver>>` accessors expose the handles for non-publish call sites (e.g. `send_declare_subscriber` / `send_request_query` still go through `actions()`, callback registration goes through `observer().lock().unwrap().subscribers.register(...)`). This keeps `Session` from monopolizing the API surface during the migration window.

`crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/lib.rs` adds `pub mod session;` with a 9-line doc block citing R228 scope, the `_z_session_t` zenoh-pico mirror, and the R229+ carry list. The module slot lives after `observer` so the dependency order (`observer::ApplicationLayerObserver` defined first, then `session::Session` bundles it) is reflected in lib-level ordering.

Locking discipline (module-level doc): `Arc<Mutex<ApplicationLayerObserver>>` uses `std::sync::Mutex` (not `tokio::sync::Mutex`) because the loopback branch fires subscriber callbacks synchronously вҖ” exactly the semantic of zenoh-pico's `_z_session_deliver_push_locally` which fires callbacks in-line under the session lock. A `tokio::sync::Mutex` would force `publish` to be `async`, propagating an unnecessary coloring change across every call site for no measurable benefit (the lock window is one subscriber-table walk).

13 new R228 unit tests on `Session::publish` + `PublishOptions`: defaults yield Put/Any/Reliable; `put()` / `del()` constructor parity; `with_*` builder chain through all 3 setters; `Locality::Any` fires both branches (wire frame count = 1, loopback fired = 1); `Locality::Remote` fires wire only (frame count = 1, fired = 0); `Locality::SessionLocal` fires loopback only (frame count = 0, fired = 1); Sample reliability + kind propagation through loopback callback; Del-kind routes to Del wire builder + Del-kind Sample loopback with empty payload; reliability translates to wire-frame reliability hint for both `Reliable` and `BestEffort`; empty-registry returns 0; Remote-only-with-matching-subscriber returns 0 (loopback branch never entered); multi-subscriber count returns total fired; mixed-locality isolation (Any + SessionLocal + Remote on same keyexpr, Session::publish(SessionLocal) fires Any + SessionLocal, suppresses Remote, wire frame count = 0).

Tests reuse the existing `RecordingDriver` mock shape from `session_glue.rs` test module so the wire branch is observable without requiring real TCP setup. `fixture_params()` helper produces a minimal `SessionInitParams` (zid + lease + cookie + 32-byte signing key) sufficient for the action-layer's outbound paths.

Out of R228 scope: aliased publish (`mapping_id != 0`) is R229 carry вҖ” the symmetric `publish_aliased(mapping_id, suffix, payload, opts)` will mirror `SessionLinkActions::send_push_aliased`. wz-ap-demo migration to `Session` is R230 carry вҖ” wz-ap-demo + integration suite continue to use the legacy direct-`ApplicationLayerObserver` pattern so the existing 65 / 65 e2e tests stay byte-identical.
verification_bullets: `cargo test -p wz-runtime-tokio --lib`: 268 -> 281 (+13) all pass. The 13 new tests cover the locality x kind x reliability matrix exhaustively, the builder API, and the mixed-locality isolation scenario.

`cargo test -p wz-integration-tests`: 65 / 65 pass across all binaries, regression 0. Integration tests continue to use the legacy direct-`ApplicationLayerObserver` pattern unchanged вҖ” `Session` is introduced as a NEW parallel surface so existing e2e wiring is unaffected.

`cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings`: clean. First pass surfaced `doc_list_item_without_indentation` on two doc-string continuation lines starting with `[`-prefixed intra-doc links; resolved by re-flowing the docstring so the continuation does not begin with `[` (clippy's heuristic for list-item detection). After the re-flow, every crate passes.

The user pre-selected option (B) higher-level Session type over (A) extending send_push_* signatures or (C) callback-hook pattern, with the explicit reasoning that long-term zenoh-pico mirror + extensibility (priority, congestion control, security) wins over minimal-blast-radius shortcuts.

Pre-grep clean.

Carry-text falsify discipline (R227 lesson textbook applied): R227 carry text claimed R228+ may need 'extending `_z_write`'s wz mirror with the `allows_local()` branch, OR introducing a session-level Publisher surface'. Pre-implementation read of `session_glue.rs::SessionLinkActions::send_push_literal` confirmed that the existing publisher surface lives on `SessionLinkActions` (not `SessionGlue` which doesn't exist as a distinct struct), and the closest higher-level bundle (`ApplicationLayerObserver`) covers inbound only. The 'introduce session-level Publisher surface' branch of the carry is the textbook fit for the actual current architecture; the 'extend _z_write's wz mirror' branch would require plumbing `Arc<Mutex<SubscriberRegistry>>` through every `send_push_*` call site (the leaky-abstraction concern noted in the R228 pre-confirm preview for option A).
carry_forward_bullets: R229 candidate: aliased publish (mapping_id != 0). `Session::publish_aliased(mapping_id, suffix, payload, opts)` symmetric counterpart to `SessionLinkActions::send_push_aliased`. Same locality routing as R228's literal publish; the loopback branch will need keyexpr resolution against the local-side mapping table OR the keyexpr_suffix can be the literal already (caller-resolved). zenoh-pico `_z_write` accepts a `_z_declared_keyexpr_t` which carries the resolved form; wz mirror will probably do likewise.

R229+ candidate: full Sample metadata propagation through `PublishOptions`. R228 only carries 3 fields (allowed_destination, reliability, kind). Add `qos` / `attachment` / `timestamp` / `encoding` / `source_info` to PublishOptions; extend `send_push_literal` / `send_push_del_literal` on the wire side to accept the same metadata so both branches see the same Sample shape. Without this, an `Any` subscriber that receives the same publish twice (loopback now + wire echo later via a roundtrip) would see two different Samples вҖ” the loopback one with caller-set metadata, the wire one with defaults. zenoh-pico's `_z_write` mirror takes all 9 fields, so the wz parity surface lands when these are threaded through.

R230 candidate: wz-ap-demo migration to `Session`. The R228 introduction is parallel-surface вҖ” wz-ap-demo continues to use `observer.subscribers.register(...)` + `actions.send_push_literal(...)` direct calls. R230 wraps these in a `Session::new` + `session.publish(...)` adoption, also exercising the Session through the integration suite. Likely 1-2 rounds for wz-ap-demo + integration test sweep.

Surfaces when a self-query use case lands. Same `is_remote` flag treatment as R227.

Round 229.decision_summary: R229 Session::publish_aliased: mapping_id + suffix + caller-asserted loopback_keyexpr; mirrors send_push_aliased on wire side, fires local_publish on loopback with the asserted literal
changes_bullets: `crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session.rs::Session` adds `publish_aliased(mapping_id, inline_suffix, loopback_keyexpr, payload, opts)` вҖ” aliased-keyexpr counterpart to R228's `publish` for literal keyexpr. Wire branch routes through `SessionLinkActions::send_push_aliased` (Put) or `send_push_del_aliased` (Del) so the peer resolves the keyexpr through its inbound mapping table (populated by an earlier outbound `send_declare_keyexpr`). Loopback branch builds a `Sample` with `loopback_keyexpr` as the literal and dispatches through the same `SubscriberRegistry::local_publish` the R227 wire-path-equivalent does. Returns the loopback fired count, same shape as `publish`.

Caller precondition documented inline: `loopback_keyexpr` MUST equal the literal form the peer would resolve `(mapping_id, inline_suffix)` to. Typical usage: (1) `send_declare_keyexpr(7, "home/temp")` registers `7 -> "home/temp"` on the peer; (2) `publish_aliased(7, None, "home/temp", payload, opts)` carries `(id=7, suffix=None)` on the wire and fires loopback on `"home/temp"`. With an inline suffix: `publish_aliased(7, Some("/kitchen"), "home/temp/kitchen", payload, opts)` carries `(id=7, suffix="/kitchen")` on the wire and fires loopback on `"home/temp/kitchen"` вҖ” the caller resolves the composite form explicitly.

Caller-resolved `loopback_keyexpr` choice over wz-side outbound mapping table: zenoh-pico's `_z_session_t._local_resources` is the local-side resolution table populated by self-declarations (`vendor/zenoh-pico/include/zenoh-pico/session/resource.h`). wz does not yet have an equivalent outbound table вҖ” introducing one is the natural R230+ carry. Until then, the caller is the single source of truth for the loopback literal; documented inline.

Implementation re-uses R228 patterns verbatim: locality predicate selects branches; `opts.kind` selects Put-vs-Del routing; `Sample::with_reliability(opts.reliability)` propagates the link-layer hint into `Sample.reliability`. The two branches are independent (wire branch ignores the loopback_keyexpr argument; loopback branch ignores the mapping_id + inline_suffix pair) so callers can intentionally decouple the wire form from the loopback form, which the regression-guard test pins.

9 new R229 unit tests on `publish_aliased`: Locality::Any fires both wire + loopback; Locality::Remote fires wire only; Locality::SessionLocal fires loopback only; Del kind routes to `send_push_del_aliased` + Del Sample loopback with empty payload + caller-resolved keyexpr; reliability propagates to both wire-frame hint and Sample.reliability for BestEffort; inline_suffix passes through to wire while loopback uses the caller-resolved literal verbatim (no auto-concatenation); empty registry returns 0; loopback_keyexpr structurally independent of the wire pair (regression-guard pinning the documented contract); mixed-locality isolation matches R228's `publish` symmetric test (Any + SessionLocal + Remote on same loopback literal, SessionLocal publish fires Any + SessionLocal, suppresses Remote, no wire frame).
verification_bullets: `cargo test -p wz-runtime-tokio --lib`: 281 -> 290 (+9) all pass. The 9 R229 tests exhaustively cover the Locality x kind x reliability x suffix matrix for the aliased path, plus the regression-guard for the loopback-keyexpr-independence contract.

`cargo test -p wz-integration-tests`: 65 / 65 pass, regression 0. publish_aliased is a new additive method on Session, so the integration suite (which still uses the legacy direct-`SessionLinkActions` send_push_* path) is unaffected.
`cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings`: clean across all crates.

Cluster-level surface pre-grep (R225 lesson #4 textbook): before adding `publish_aliased`, ran `grep -n 'pub fn publish' crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session.rs` to confirm no existing `publish_aliased` method shadows. Pre-grep clean.

Carry-text falsify discipline (R227 lesson textbook): R228 carry text claimed R229 candidate scope as 'aliased publish (mapping_id != 0). publish_aliased symmetric counterpart to send_push_aliased'. Pre-implementation read of `send_push_aliased` confirmed signature `(mapping_id: u64, suffix: Option<&str>, value: &[u8], reliable: bool)` matches the carry expectation; no architectural surprise. The carry's 'keyexpr resolution against the local-side mapping table' suggestion was falsified вҖ” wz has no such table yet, so R229 ships with caller-asserted `loopback_keyexpr` and the table is moved to R230+ carry.
carry_forward_bullets: R230 candidate: wz-side outbound mapping table on `SessionLinkActions` (or a sibling `OutboundResources` struct). Populated by `send_declare_keyexpr(mapping_id, suffix)` invocations, queried by a future `publish_aliased_resolved` variant that auto-derives `loopback_keyexpr` from `(mapping_id, inline_suffix)`. Mirrors zenoh-pico's `_z_session_t._local_resources`. The R229 `publish_aliased` keeps the caller-asserted form working in parallel as the production-safe default until the table proves out.

R229+ candidate: full Sample metadata propagation through PublishOptions вҖ” still deferred from R228. Adding qos / attachment / timestamp / encoding / source_info to PublishOptions + extending send_push_literal / send_push_aliased / send_push_del_* to accept them. Without this, loopback Sample is richer than the wire-side equivalent.

R230 candidate: wz-ap-demo migration to Session. wz-ap-demo's publish loop in main.rs (lines 1452 / 1459 / 1465 / 1471) currently calls `actions.send_push_*` directly with 4 sites; migrating to `session.publish` / `session.publish_aliased` exercises the full R228+R229 surface through the integration suite. wz-ap-demo also has 11 `observer.*.register` sites that would need `observer.lock().unwrap().*` wrapping if observer goes behind Arc<Mutex<>>.

Round 245.decision_summary: R245 Subscriber + SubscribeOptions + declare_subscriber{_aliased}: RAII handle on Drop, aliased variant resolves outbound mapping at declare time so retracts after declare do not break the subscriber.
changes_bullets: session.rs SubscribeOptions { allowed_origin: Locality } non_exhaustive + new() + with_allowed_origin.
session.rs Subscriber struct (Session, SubscriptionId, keyexpr: String, SubscribeOptions) non_exhaustive + !Clone.
session.rs Subscriber::id() + keyexpr() + options() accessors.
session.rs Subscriber::undeclare() consumes self + std::mem::forget to skip Drop.
session.rs Subscriber Drop impl: lock observer (poison-recover), unregister id, ignore returned bool.
session.rs Session::declare_subscriber(keyexpr, options, callback) -> Subscriber wraps register_with_locality.
session.rs Session::declare_subscriber_aliased(mapping_id, suffix, options, callback) -> Result<Subscriber, SubscribeAliasError> resolves outbound mapping at declare time and stores the literal.
session.rs SubscribeAliasError::UnknownMapping(u64) + Display + std::error::Error (PublishAliasError/QueryAliasError mirror).
Resolution-once-at-declare contract: subsequent send_undeclare_kexpr does NOT affect already-registered Subscriber (zenoh-pico _z_register_subscription mirror).
Mirrors zenoh-pico z_subscriber_t + z_declare_subscriber + z_undeclare_subscriber.

changes_bullets: session.rs LivelinessOptions empty non_exhaustive + Default + new() (z_liveliness_token_options_t mirror, future-additive placeholder).
session.rs LivelinessToken { session, id: u64, keyexpr: String, options } non_exhaustive + !Clone + id() + keyexpr() + options() + undeclare(self) consumes + std::mem::forget.

session.rs LivelinessToken Drop impl emits send_undeclare_token wire (differs from Subscriber/Queryable Drop which only unregister local registry; zenoh-pico _z_undeclare_liveliness_token mirror вҖ” retraction MUST notify peer's liveliness subscribers).
session.rs Session::declare_token(keyexpr, options) -> LivelinessToken: allocates token_id via alloc_next_token_id, emits send_declare_token(id, 0, Some(literal)) literal-form wire.

session.rs LivelinessAliasError::UnknownMapping(u64) + Display + std::error::Error (mirror of Subscribe/Queryable/Query/PublishAliasError on the token side).

session_glue.rs SessionLinkActions::next_outbound_token_id AtomicU64 init 0 + alloc_next_token_id() Relaxed fetch_add (zenoh-pico _z_get_entity_id mirror, independent counter from request_id/subscriber_id/queryable_id spaces вҖ” distinct intmaps on peer receiver).

Round 250.decision_summary: R250 pre-push hook re-sources scripts/run-ci.sh from HEAD content; defends against self-add-Layer-X chicken-and-egg from R247 carry.
changes_bullets: .githooks/pre-push: replaced direct `scripts/run-ci.sh` invocation with `git show HEAD:scripts/run-ci.sh` extraction to a mktemp tmpfile + `bash $tmpfile` execution; CWD stays at repo_root so relative paths inside the script (scripts/audit-mid-values.sh, scripts/verify-codegen.sh, deploy/*.yaml) continue to resolve against the working tree.

Fallback path: when `git show HEAD:scripts/run-ci.sh` returns no content (first-bootstrap push or HEAD lacks the script), the hook reverts to invoking the working-tree `scripts/run-ci.sh` so the first push after `mnemosyne-init` / fresh clone still works.
trap-on-EXIT cleans up the tmpfile regardless of pass/fail/early-exit; lock acquisition (R224 advisory flock) sequence preserved.

Inline comment block explains the R247 retrospect, the Git-protocol contract that HEAD already points at the to-be-pushed tip in pre-push, and the specific failure mode being defended (working tree dirtied between commit and push).
verification_bullets: Manual hook simulation: `echo 'refs/heads/main <HEAD-sha> refs/heads/main 0*40' | bash .githooks/pre-push` ran all Layer A-E lanes successfully against the extracted HEAD:scripts/run-ci.sh.
pre-commit hook + commit-msg hook passed at commit time (validate_workspace clean; COMMIT_FORMAT.md 72-byte gate clean).

No regression in lane behaviour: same Layer A-E pass output observed via simulated push as via direct `scripts/run-ci.sh` invocation.
carry_forward_bullets: R251+ CI yaml mirror: `.github/workflows/ci.yml` runs `scripts/run-ci.sh` directly without the HEAD-extraction defence; same chicken-and-egg theoretically applies there but the CI runner always checks out a clean tree at HEAD so the working-tree-vs-HEAD divergence cannot fire вҖ” mirror change is therefore not required for parity, only the local pre-push hook needed the defence.

Round 251.decision_summary: R251 Phase W entry: wz-runtime-core trait skeleton landed (Runtime + TimeSource + Allocator + RuntimeError). R230 В§5.P spec moves from 0% impl to skeleton-only; AP/MCU dual-target compile path opened.
changes_bullets: crates/wz-runtime-core/ new crate (no_std default + std/alloc opt-in features). Workspace member added; MSRV 1.75 retained.

src/runtime.rs Runtime trait: type JoinHandle<T: Send + 'static>: Future<Output = Result<T, RuntimeError>> + Send + 'static GAT + spawn<F: Future + Send + 'static>(fut: F) -> Self::JoinHandle<F::Output>. Mirrors tokio JoinHandle as a Future contract so tokio impl can satisfy directly via JoinError -> RuntimeError::JoinFailed wrap.
src/time.rs TimeSource trait: now_monotonic_ms(&self) -> u64 + sleep(&self, ms: u64) -> impl Future<Output=()> + Send + '_ (RPITIT shape user-confirmed; Rust 1.75 stable).

src/allocator.rs Allocator trait: alloc(&self, Layout) -> Option<NonNull<u8>> + unsafe dealloc(&self, NonNull<u8>, Layout). Local trait (NOT core::alloc::Allocator which is nightly-only on MSRV 1.75) with explicit Option-on-OOM failure surface; buffer-pool / TLSF allocator pivot space preserved.

src/error.rs RuntimeError enum: JoinFailed + Shutdown variants, non_exhaustive. Display impl. core::error::Error impl deferred (requires MSRV 1.81); std::error::Error impl behind std feature deferred to R252+ AP integration round.
src/lib.rs cfg_attr no_std gate + alloc extern + module re-exports. deny(missing_docs) for trait-surface doc completeness.

Documentation throughout the crate cites R230 В§5.P (parent spec), R63 (anti-stub rule: no NOP impl ships), В§5.I (intrinsics tier вҖ” distinct layer), 111 std/tokio call site R230 inventory baseline (the R252+ migration target).

Carries explicit in lib.rs module doc: Mutex/RwLock shape deferred (HKT-vs-type-alias-vs-cfg-cond design tied to actual MCU work shape); TokioRuntime impl deferred (R252+); LwipRuntime + EmbassyRuntime deferred (Phase W lwIP integration round).
0 production site change; 0 wz upper layer reparameterization; 0 impl. Pure type-level scaffold (per user-confirmed scope).

cargo build -p wz-runtime-core --features alloc: clean.
cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings: clean across 7 crates (now including wz-runtime-core).
bash scripts/run-ci.sh: all required layers pass (Layer A mnemosyne + A2 audit + B verify-codegen + C1 cargo test 464 + C2 clippy + D deploy + E binary-dep e2e 9/9).
pre-commit hook: validate_workspace clean (T1=0, RT 12/12, sync preserved).
pre-push hook simulation passed at commit time via the R250 HEAD-extracted run-ci.sh path.
commit a8c7094 (feat(runtime-core): R251 Phase W entry wz-runtime-core trait skeleton; 8 new files +381 / -0).
carry_forward_bullets: R252+ TokioRuntime concrete impl in wz-runtime-tokio: spawn via tokio::task::spawn returning tokio::task::JoinHandle wrapped to convert JoinError -> RuntimeError::JoinFailed. TimeSource via tokio::time::Instant + tokio::time::sleep. impl-with-no-caller pattern from R244/R245/R248 quartet вҖ” next round must wire one caller to avoid dead-code rot.

R253+ first caller migration: pick a leaf wz-runtime-tokio site (likely a tokio::time::sleep call in a timeout path) to validate the trait contract carries actual production semantics. В§5.P guidance: 'leaf crates first, Session struct last' вҖ” do not start with Session reparameterization.

Mutex / RwLock contract decision: deferred from R251 design. Pick between (a) per-runtime `Mutex<T>` type alias re-exported from runtime crate, (b) `MutexFamily` GAT trait, or (c) cfg-conditional direct std vs embassy use. Best resolved when first real Mutex-using site needs to compile under MCU profile.

Allocator first concrete impl: deferred until MCU buffer-pool round (Phase W lwIP integration). AP profile keeps std GlobalAlloc until then вҖ” no impl needed in wz-runtime-tokio because tokio + std default heap already covers AP.
core::error::Error impl on RuntimeError: gated on MSRV bump to 1.81+. Until then, std::error::Error impl behind `std` feature is the cleaner path (lands with R252 TokioRuntime).
wz-runtime-lwip crate re-introduction: tracking the R63 removal carry; the empty-stub anti-pattern is preserved вҖ” lwip crate reintroduces only when there is actual lwIP integration code to ship.
Liveliness Token wz-ap-demo migration (R250 carry).
ReplyRegistry timeout sweep (needs TimeSource concrete impl from R252+, so naturally chains).
Q_B / Q_E wire codec slots (SCE-side codec extension).
LivelinessToken local_tokens table for history fan-out.
LivelinessToken Drop catch_unwind defensive wrap.

R251 lesson: Phase W entry is the smallest defensible architectural commitment вҖ” trait skeleton + cfg gating only, 0 impl, 0 caller. The 26-session textbook anchor (AskUserQuestion + code preview for architectural decisions) was applied: user confirmed (1) scope = trait skeleton only, (2) sleep shape = RPITIT. Trait shape decisions that genuinely require caller-shape evidence (Mutex/RwLock) were explicitly deferred rather than guessed. The R63 anti-stub rule held: no NOP impl shipped; the contract exists, the first impl waits for the first caller.

carry_forward_bullets: R255+ Instant-arithmetic trait gap: declare_task + query_task + publisher_task all still use `std::time::Instant::now() + Duration::from_millis(...)` for deadline math. Two design choices: (a) extend TimeSource with a `now() -> impl Instant`-shaped API that supports SubAssign<Duration> + Ord, (b) wrap now_monotonic_ms() arithmetic so deadlines become u64 ms comparisons. Option (b) is MCU-friendlier (no Instant type on bare-metal) and aligns with the trait's current millisecond grid; option (a) preserves the existing call-site shape. Decision waits on R255+ Session migration shape.

R255+ Session struct reparameterisation: В§5.P 'leaf crates first, Session struct last' вҖ” with wz-ap-demo leaf tasks now migrated, the next textbook step is Session itself. Either (i) Session: Generic<R: Runtime, T: TimeSource> or (ii) a single Bundle trait fusing both. Tied to Mutex / RwLock decision (R251 carry) because Session holds Arc<Mutex<ApplicationLayerObserver>>.
R255+ wz-runtime-tokio internal tokio::time::sleep sites: 1 site in session_glue.rs (lease-deadline select branch). Migration requires Session-or-actions to carry a TimeSource handle.
R255+ Mutex / RwLock contract decision (R251 carry) actively constrains the Session reparameterisation shape вҖ” the two carries unify in R255+.

changes_bullets: crates/wz-runtime-core/src/error.rs: RuntimeError::JoinCancelled variant added (mirror of tokio::task::JoinError::is_cancelled()). JoinFailed doc-comment narrowed: 'spawned task panicked' (was: panic+cancellation union). Display strings updated to match.

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/runtime_impl.rs TokioJoinHandle: new pub fn abort(&self) struct-level method (NOT trait-level вҖ” the Runtime trait skeleton scoped abort out per R251; this is the pragmatic AP-profile escape hatch zenoh-pico shutdown paths need).

TokioJoinHandle Future impl: poll() now disambiguates JoinError. is_cancelled() routes to RuntimeError::JoinCancelled; is_panic() routes to JoinFailed; is_cancelled wins when both true (shutdown intent dominates).
Two new lib tests: (1) abort + await resolves to JoinCancelled; (2) side-by-side panic + abort spawns surface the two variants distinctly (panic_outcome != cancel_outcome).
Existing panic test (tokio_runtime_spawn_panic_resolves_to_join_failed) untouched: still asserts JoinFailed, just with the narrowed semantic.
Doc-comment list formatting: clippy::doc_list_item_without_indentation surfaced + fixed by adding blank doc-line terminator after bullet block (textbook rustdoc pattern).
non_exhaustive shield protects external callers from match-arm fragility: the new JoinCancelled variant does not break existing exhaustive matches because the enum is already #[non_exhaustive].

Round 258.decision_summary: R258 pins Phase W public contract trait bounds at compile time + smoke-tests <R: Runtime, T: TimeSource> generic composition; defensive structural fixity layer over the R251-R257 trait surface.
changes_bullets: crates/wz-runtime-core/src/error.rs: compile_time_assertions module added. _assert_send/_assert_sync/_assert_clone/_assert_eq fn-bound check pattern. runtime_error_trait_bounds_compile() exercises Send+Sync+Clone+Eq on RuntimeError. Functions never called; their bodies are bound-checks that fail the build if any trait impl regresses.

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/runtime_impl.rs: compile_time_assertions module added. TokioRuntime + TokioTime asserted Send+Sync; TokioJoinHandle<()> + TokioJoinHandle<String> asserted Send-only (NOT Sync вҖ” mirrors tokio::task::JoinHandle which is Send + !Sync; single-consumer join semantic).

Generic composition smoke fn runtime_and_time_compose_in_generic_code<R: Runtime, T: TimeSource + Clone + Send + 'static>: exercises rt.spawn(async block) returning R::JoinHandle<u64>, captures clock by clone into the spawned task, calls clock.sleep(1).await + clock.now_monotonic_ms() inside the task. Validates the trait pair is actually usable in production-shape generic code (the trajectory toward Session<R, T> reparameterisation).

Pattern is dead-code by design вҖ” #[allow(dead_code)] on the assertion functions because cargo would warn about uncalled test-module functions otherwise. Trade-off accepted: the build-time check is more valuable than the unused-fn lint.

No production code changed; no test count change (475 lib tests in wz-runtime-tokio unchanged). The compile-time assertions live in #[cfg(test)] modules so they only build under `cargo test` / `cargo check --tests` вҖ” zero footprint in the release binary.

R258 lesson: compile-time trait-bound assertions (the _assert_send<T: Send>() pattern) are zero-cost production-grade contract fixity. They surface regressions earlier than runtime tests and require no runtime resources вҖ” textbook pattern for any public trait crate. The generic-composition smoke test similarly validates that the trait surface composes in real generic code without consuming actual scheduling resources.
Round 258.publishable_decision_summary: R258 pins Phase W public contract trait bounds at compile time + smoke-tests <R: Runtime, T: TimeSource> generic composition; defensive structural fixity layer over the R251-R257 trait surface.
publishable_changes_bullets: crates/wz-runtime-core/src/error.rs: compile_time_assertions module added. _assert_send/_assert_sync/_assert_clone/_assert_eq fn-bound check pattern. runtime_error_trait_bounds_compile() exercises Send+Sync+Clone+Eq on RuntimeError. Functions never called; their bodies are bound-checks that fail the build if any trait impl regresses.

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/runtime_impl.rs: compile_time_assertions module added. TokioRuntime + TokioTime asserted Send+Sync; TokioJoinHandle<()> + TokioJoinHandle<String> asserted Send-only (NOT Sync вҖ” mirrors tokio::task::JoinHandle which is Send + !Sync; single-consumer join semantic).

Generic composition smoke fn runtime_and_time_compose_in_generic_code<R: Runtime, T: TimeSource + Clone + Send + 'static>: exercises rt.spawn(async block) returning R::JoinHandle<u64>, captures clock by clone into the spawned task, calls clock.sleep(1).await + clock.now_monotonic_ms() inside the task. Validates the trait pair is actually usable in production-shape generic code (the trajectory toward Session<R, T> reparameterisation).

Pattern is dead-code by design вҖ” #[allow(dead_code)] on the assertion functions because cargo would warn about uncalled test-module functions otherwise. Trade-off accepted: the build-time check is more valuable than the unused-fn lint.

No production code changed; no test count change (475 lib tests in wz-runtime-tokio unchanged). The compile-time assertions live in #[cfg(test)] modules so they only build under `cargo test` / `cargo check --tests` вҖ” zero footprint in the release binary.

Round 259.decision_summary: R259 closes the R257 abort contract by pinning two edge cases: abort against an already-completed task is a no-op (Ok value preserved); repeated aborts collapse to a single JoinCancelled outcome (idempotent).
changes_bullets: crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/runtime_impl.rs: two new #[tokio::test] cases. tokio_runtime_abort_after_completion_is_noop verifies that calling abort() on a completed task does NOT flip Ok(value) into Err(JoinCancelled). tokio_runtime_abort_is_idempotent_across_repeated_calls verifies triple-abort still resolves to a single Err(JoinCancelled).

Both tests pin tokio contract from the wz wrapper perspective: 'If the task has already completed, calling abort will not have any effect' (tokio JoinHandle::abort doc) + the repeated-abort silent-no-op convention.

Round 260.decision_summary: R260 routes drive_session_until_terminal lease-branch sleep through TimeSource::sleep; last internal tokio::time::sleep site in wz-runtime-tokio gone; storage unchanged.
changes_bullets: crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs: drive_session_until_terminal signature adds <T: TimeSource> and clock: &T as the 5th positional param between max_iters and on_event. The tokio::select! lease branch now awaits clock.sleep(remaining_ms) (u64 ms cast from saturating_duration_since(now).as_millis()) instead of direct tokio::time::sleep(remaining). use wz_runtime_core::TimeSource import added.

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/tests/session_fsm_drive_session.rs: 9 callers across r76b_returns_terminated_when_engine_already_final / r76b_iteration_limit_when_loop_cannot_terminate / r76b_link_lost_event_drives_loop_to_terminated / r76b_lease_branch_fires_with_silent_peer / r85_unlimited_iters_terminates_on_finite_event_sequence / r83_observer_captures_framepayload_and_linklost_in_order / r83_observer_reads_framepayload_messages_through_reference / r99_subscriber_registry_routes_framepayload_push_to_callback / r83_observer_fires_on_lease_branch each construct let clock = TokioTime::new() and pass &clock between max_iters and the closure. use wz_runtime_tokio::runtime_impl::TokioTime added.

crates/wz-ap-demo/src/main.rs: production caller constructs a dedicated clock_for_drive: TokioTime alongside the existing R253/R254 clock_for_declare / clock_for_query / clock_for_publisher instances. Each TokioTime samples its own monotonic epoch; the drive instance does not need to share epoch with the task instances because the lease branch uses clock.sleep(ms) only, not cross-instance now_monotonic_ms comparison.

Instant storage in SessionLinkActions (last_inbound_keepalive_at: Mutex<Option<Instant>> and established_at: Mutex<Option<Instant>>) is unchanged this round; the local Instant::now() / saturating_duration_since lease comparator path stays as before. Full Instant -> u64 ms storage migration deferred to a future round because of cross-consumer reach (check_lease_deadline, handle_inbound write site at line 960, record_established_at Lua action, and all observer consumers that read these stamps).

wz-runtime-tokio's last internal tokio::time::sleep direct call site closes. The only remaining tokio::time::sleep / Instant usage in wz-runtime-tokio is runtime_impl::TokioTime (the trait impl itself; expected) and tests/echo_demo*.rs (50ms test budget waits in integration test infra). Production runtime code is fully on the В§5.P trait-mediated time-dispatch path.

When the next round considers storage-type migration (Instant -> u64 ms) the fan-out is much wider (~30+ consumer sites) and the same single-round cadence will not fit вҖ” that round needs its own audit decomposition (write sites first, read sites second, lease comparator third).

Round 261.decision_summary: R261 adds ReplyRegistry::sweep_timed_out + Pending.deadline_ms Option<u64>; production callers pass None (Session::query clock plumbing deferred to R262).
changes_bullets: crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/reply.rs: Pending struct gains deadline_ms: Option<u64> field (absolute monotonic-ms deadline; None = never expire). ReplyRegistry::register signature adds 3rd positional argument deadline_ms: Option<u64> between expected_finals and on_reply.

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/reply.rs: New ReplyRegistry::sweep_timed_out(&mut self, now_ms: u64) -> usize method. Drains pending entries whose deadline_ms <= now_ms, fires on_final per entry, returns swept count. Same drain-then-fire pattern as fire_final_for (partition first, fire after borrow release; panicking on_final leaves no half-swept state).

Architectural pick R261 (option A confirmed by user): on_final fires with opaque cause. FinalCallback signature unchanged from FnMut(u64). Callers cannot distinguish 'timed out' from a normal Final via the callback signature this round; R261 carry records the FinalCause-enum extension path if a concrete user need arises.
Boundary semantics: deadline_ms <= now_ms is inclusive (entry at exact deadline tick is swept). None deadline (timeout_ms == 0 path) is skipped from every sweep, indefinitely.

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session.rs: 2 production register sites (Session::query at line 847 + Session::query_aliased at line 944) pass deadline_ms = None this round. R261 comment block on each site flags R262 as the round that will thread a clock through Session::query to compute deadline_ms from opts.timeout_ms.
crates/wz-ap-demo/src/main.rs: 1 production register site (line 993) passes deadline_ms = None with R261 comment flagging R263 as the round that wires demo timeout_ms exercise through QueryOptions.

23 test callers across crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/reply.rs all pass None (13 single-line via sed + 10 multi-line via Python regex; no test-behaviour change because every existing test predates the timeout contract).

New unit tests for sweep_timed_out (crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/reply.rs): 7 tests pin (1) expired+swept+on_final; (2) unexpired kept; (3) None skipped at u64::MAX; (4) mixed batch partitions correctly; (5) inclusive boundary at deadline==now_ms; (6) idempotent across repeated calls + 2nd sweep returns 0; (7) duplicate-rid same-deadline drops both entries independently in registration order.

commit 1ffe104 (feat(runtime-tokio): R261 ReplyRegistry timeout sweep API; 3 files +279 / -15). Pre-commit commit-msg gate passed (subject 57 bytes, all body lines <= 72 bytes, 3 bullets).
carry_forward_bullets: R262 Session::query / query_aliased API change: thread a clock (either clock: &impl TimeSource or now_ms: u64 caller-precomputed) through both functions so deadline_ms = clock.now_monotonic_ms() + opts.timeout_ms can be computed at register time. The architectural decision (clock vs now_ms) needs sub-decision because Session::query fan-out is ~25 sites (2 production + 23+ test). Recommended approach: clock: &impl TimeSource so caller does not need a pre-call clock sample.

R262 drive_session_until_terminal sweep hookup: call replies.sweep_timed_out(clock.now_monotonic_ms()) on each iteration top (or per-poll-cycle) so timed-out queries surface as on_final fires from the production loop. The clock used at sweep MUST share monotonic epoch with the clock used at Session::query register time (different TokioTime instances have different epochs because TokioTime::epoch samples Instant::now() per construction; a shared Arc<TokioTime> across query + drive sites is the natural pattern, or both sites take the same clock arg from the caller).

R263 wz-ap-demo end-to-end timeout exercise: integration test where wz-ap-demo issues a z_get with a short timeout_ms against a peer that never replies, and the on_final callback fires via sweep_timed_out within wall-clock budget. Likely requires reply_log_spec extension + new fixture.

R261 option B path (FinalCallback gains FinalCause: Normal | TimedOut): hold as a YAGNI-tagged future round; the user explicitly picked option A (opaque cause) this round. If R262/R263 integration testing surfaces a concrete need to distinguish (e.g. wz-ap-demo wants to log 'TIMEOUT' vs 'FINAL' differently), promote at that time.

R261 lesson: API-breaking changes to a widely-called surface (register has 23 call sites in this codebase) benefit from a 2-phase rollout: (phase 1) add new param with sentinel default (None) at every site so the feature is wired but inert; (phase 2) connect the upstream consumer (Session::query) and the downstream consumer (drive_session sweep) in subsequent rounds. R261-R263 is this 2-phase pattern in practice; the alternative single-round approach would have touched 25+ sites + introduced clock-epoch-sharing decision + new tests + production-caller deadline math all at once.

R261 sed + Python multi-edit pattern: sed for line-local single-line patterns (low risk), Python re.sub for multi-line indentation-sensitive patterns (the cleanest tool for indent-preserving block insertion). Both were used here for the 23-site test caller update; both passed cargo build clean on first attempt.

Round 262.decision_summary: R262 threads clock: &T (TimeSource) through Session::query / query_aliased / query_aliased_auto / Querier handles; drive_session adds on_tick 7th param; deadline_ms now computed at register.
changes_bullets: crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session.rs: Session::query / query_aliased / query_aliased_auto each grow a generic <T: TimeSource> and a positional clock: &T parameter. Each function internally computes deadline_ms = (opts.timeout_ms > 0).then(|| clock.now_monotonic_ms() + opts.timeout_ms as u64) and threads it into ReplyRegistry::register, replacing the R261 'always None' shim.

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session.rs: Querier::get and QuerierAliased::get each grow the same generic + clock parameter and forward to the underlying Session::query / query_aliased_auto. This keeps the Querier/QuerierAliased declare-side state (keyexpr + options) stable while propagating the call-side clock through the per-get fan-out.

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs: drive_session_until_terminal grows a new generic <G: FnMut(u64)> + on_tick: G as the 7th positional parameter, between on_event and the where clause. The loop fires on_tick(clock.now_monotonic_ms()) at the top of every iteration after the final-state + iteration-limit checks, BEFORE the tokio::select! poll/lease race. Production callers wrap a ReplyRegistry::sweep_timed_out call into on_tick; test callers pass |_| {}.

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session.rs query_aliased gets #[allow(clippy::too_many_arguments)] (8 distinct atomic args вҖ” mapping_id + inline_suffix + loopback_keyexpr + opts + clock + 2 closures = load-bearing) with a doc-comment paragraph explaining each arg's purpose so the allow does not become a maintenance hazard.

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/tests/session_fsm_drive_session.rs: all 9 prior drive_session callers updated to pass an |_| {} on_tick closure (machine-edited via Python regex covering both multi-line and single-line shapes). New R262 integration test r262_drive_session_on_tick_sweep_surfaces_expired_pending_on_final wires a stand-alone ReplyRegistry into the on_tick closure with a pre-registered entry whose deadline_ms = 0 (always expired) + QueueDriver staging a single Lost event; asserts the sweep surfaces on_final exactly once + the registry is empty after the loop terminates.

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session.rs tests module: 14 session.query / query_aliased / query_aliased_auto call sites + 4 querier.get + 5 qa.get sites all updated via Python regex + 4 manual fixes (multi-line dot-chain forms the regex did not catch). Each affected test function gains let clock = TokioTime::new(); near its top so the clock variable is in scope.

crates/wz-ap-demo/src/main.rs: drive_session_until_terminal caller adds the on_tick closure that locks the observer.clone() handle and calls observer.replies.sweep_timed_out(now_ms). The direct ReplyRegistry::register call at line 993 still passes deadline_ms = None вҖ” wz-ap-demo's z_get does not yet honour QueryOptions.timeout_ms (R263 carry). Comment block flags this state explicitly so a reader knows the on_tick sweep is wired but inert at the demo level this round.

Epoch-sharing contract: the clock used at Session::query register-time MUST share monotonic epoch with the clock used at drive_session's on_tick sweep-time. TokioTime is Copy/Clone with a per-instance epoch field, so a caller that wants epoch-sharing constructs ONE TokioTime and shares it (clone or &-borrow) between both sites. Doc-comments on drive_session_until_terminal + Session::query call this out explicitly so future maintainers do not accidentally construct independent TokioTime instances at register vs sweep sites.

cargo test --manifest-path crates/Cargo.toml -p wz-runtime-tokio --lib: 484/484 pass (R261 baseline preserved вҖ” the additional surface tests live in the integration suite, not the lib).

cargo test --manifest-path crates/Cargo.toml -p wz-runtime-tokio --test session_fsm_drive_session: 10/10 pass (was 9; +1 R262 sweep integration test). The new test r262_drive_session_on_tick_sweep_surfaces_expired_pending_on_final completes in <1ms wall time (no real waits; staged Lost terminates the loop after one iteration).

commit 5d8b484 (feat(runtime-tokio): R262 Session/Querier clock + drive_session on_tick; 4 files +315 / -75). Pre-commit commit-msg gate passed (subject 71 bytes, all body lines <= 72 bytes, 3 bullets).
carry_forward_bullets: R263 wz-ap-demo end-to-end timeout exercise: the direct ReplyRegistry::register at wz-ap-demo/main.rs:993 still passes deadline_ms = None. R263 will thread the demo's QueryOptions.timeout_ms (or equivalent reply_log_spec field) through that register call and consolidate the demo's 4 TokioTime instances (clock_for_declare / clock_for_query / clock_for_publisher / clock_for_drive) into a single session_clock so the register-time deadline shares monotonic epoch with the on_tick sweep. An integration fixture should exercise the path: wz-ap-demo issues a z_get with timeout=N ms against a peer that never replies; the on_tick sweep surfaces on_final within N+driver-loop-quantum ms of register.

R263 sweep wall-time guarantee documentation: the doc-comment on drive_session_until_terminal currently says the sweep fires 'within one driver-loop tick of the deadline expiry'. R263 should pin a numeric upper bound by measuring the typical wall-clock latency between deadline ms and on_final fire вҖ” likely dominated by the lease branch sleep duration in the absence of poll-arm activity. The bound matters for application authors picking timeout_ms values.

R263 wz-ap-demo session_clock consolidation: today's demo creates 4 separate TokioTime instances. R263 should refactor to one Arc<TokioTime> shared across the 4 task call sites + the Session::query register + drive_session on_tick sweep. The Arc form keeps the API uniform with future MCU profiles that might also share clock instances across tasks.

R264+ FinalCallback FinalCause extension: hold as YAGNI tagged. Once R263 wz-ap-demo timeout fires in practice and the user inspects 'TIMEOUT vs FINAL' distinguishability needs, decide whether to promote FinalCallback to FnMut(u64, FinalCause) or leave the opaque-cause shape.

R262 lesson: API-breaking change to 5 public functions (Session::query / query_aliased / query_aliased_auto + Querier::get + QuerierAliased::get) cascades into ~35 caller sites. The 2-phase rollout (R261 None shim вҶ’ R262 generic plumbing) worked well вҖ” each round's tests stayed green throughout. The Python regex multi-edit pattern for multi-line call sites caught ~28 of ~35 sites cleanly; 4 sites required manual intervention because of method-chain dot syntax that the regex did not cover. For future surface changes of this size, plan ~30 minutes of caller-site reconciliation regardless of automation quality.

R260-R262 lesson: clock-epoch sharing emerged as a contract concern late in R261 design. The clean fix (each TokioTime carries its own epoch + Clone copies share it) is correct but subtle вҖ” caller discipline matters. The Arc<TokioTime> form proposed for R263 makes this discipline structurally enforceable rather than convention-only.

changes_bullets: crates/wz-ap-demo/src/main.rs: new CLI flag --query-timeout-ms <ms> parsed via parse_pair + u32 validation. timeout_ms > 0 with no --query is rejected with a textual error matching the on_query_reply_log / on_query_final_log pattern (same require --query rule applies because the timeout binds to the outbound Query's pending entry).
crates/wz-ap-demo/src/main.rs: ReplyConsumerSpec gains a query_timeout_ms: u32 field (default 0 = no timeout, preserving pre-R263 behaviour for callers that do not pass the new flag).

crates/wz-ap-demo/src/main.rs: session_clock TokioTime instance constructed once near the top of run_demo (right after the observer Arc + before the inner observer_lock block) and shared by value-copy (TokioTime is Copy) across 4 prior sites: publisher_task / query_task / declare_task each receive session_clock instead of constructing their own TokioTime::new(); drive_session_until_terminal receives &session_clock; the QUERY_RID register call computes deadline_ms = session_clock.now_monotonic_ms() + query_timeout_ms when timeout > 0.

crates/wz-ap-demo/src/main.rs: QUERY_RID observer_lock.replies.register call now passes deadline_ms = (query_timeout_ms > 0).then(|| session_clock.now_monotonic_ms() + query_timeout_ms as u64) instead of the R261 sentinel None. drive_session on_tick sweep is now load-bearing for this register site вҖ” a peer that never replies will surface on_final within (timeout_ms + driver-loop-tick) wall time.

crates/wz-ap-demo/src/main.rs: print_usage and OPTIONS doc updated to document the new --query-timeout-ms flag including the require-query precondition + the 0 = no timeout default + the (timeout_ms + driver-loop-tick) wall-time bound description.

Epoch contract honoured: session_clock is a single TokioTime constructed once. The QUERY_RID register-time deadline_ms reads session_clock.now_monotonic_ms() at register; the drive_session on_tick sweep reads now_monotonic_ms() from the same session_clock copy via &session_clock. Both reads share the original epoch field even though they are value-copies (TokioTime stores epoch as tokio::time::Instant which is itself Copy).

Likely dominated by the lease branch sleep duration in the absence of poll-arm activity.

R263 lesson: CLI surface extension (1 new flag + 1 new struct field) cascaded into 6 distinct call-site updates (CLI parse, validation, struct definition, struct construction, register call deadline computation, usage doc). All 6 are mechanical and clustered in one file. The session_clock consolidation (4 -> 1 TokioTime) is a separate semantic-preserving refactor that opportunistically rode this round because R262's clock-epoch contract made consolidation load-bearing. Bundling both into R263 worked because they share the same review boundary (wz-ap-demo only).

R263 lesson: TokioTime is Copy + 'static. Passing it by value-copy is the natural sharing pattern вҖ” no Arc<TokioTime> needed. The session_clock construction site can be moved up to before the inner observer_lock block freely; the copy semantics keep all downstream sites' references valid. This is the pattern future MCU profiles' shared-clock impls should target.

Round 264.decision_summary: R264 closes the R263 verification debt by adding a Layer E fixture that spawns 2x wz-ap-demo (silent acceptor + initiator with --query-timeout-ms 500 --on-query-final-log) and asserts FINAL RECEIVED rid=1 fires within 400-2000 ms wall-time; the fixture surfaced an architectural gap where the R262 in-loop on_tick sweep could not fire sub-second because (a) the lease-bound sleep arm cadence is ~10 s on production AP params and (b) clamping the sleep arm with a sweep_tick_ms parameter inside drive_session_until_terminal cancels poll_and_dispatch_one mid-frame which is NOT cancel-safe for length-prefixed link drivers such as wz-ap-demo's InboundReadDriver; the R264 fix moves the sweep to a dedicated spawned task driven by TimeSource::sleep(100) so the drive_session loop's poll future runs to completion without competing select arms.
changes_bullets: crates/wz-integration-tests/tests/wz_ap_demo_query_timeout.rs: new Layer E fixture #[ignore]-gated. Spawns acceptor `wz-ap-demo --listen <port> --key demo/timeout` (subscriber declared, no queryable so the inbound Query has no responder and silently sinks at the peer), waits 5 s for 'listening on' witness, drops PortReservation, spawns initiator `wz-ap-demo --connect <port> --query demo/timeout --query-timeout-ms 500 --on-query-final-log`, waits 2 s for 'FINAL RECEIVED rid=1' stderr witness. Wall-time elapsed since initiator_start asserted 400 ms <= elapsed <= 2000 ms: 400 ms lower bound catches a deadline_ms=0 register-time regression; 2000 ms upper bound catches sweep cadence stall.

crates/wz-ap-demo/src/main.rs: new sweep_task spawned BEFORE drive_session_until_terminal. The task is an unbounded `loop { sweep_clock.sleep(100).await; <lock observer; sweep_timed_out(now_monotonic_ms()); drop lock> }` using the shared session_clock TokioTime (Copy + 'static). Implementation uses TimeSource::sleep only (no tokio::time::interval) so MCU profiles inherit the same code-shape via their concrete TimeSource impl. After drive_session_until_terminal returns the test calls sweep_task.abort(); abort() is sufficient because the task body holds no shared mutable state beyond the observer Arc<Mutex<>> (cleanly dropped on task join) and has no on-Drop cleanup requirements.

crates/wz-ap-demo/src/main.rs: drive_session on_tick closure becomes a no-op `|_now_ms| {}`. The R263 in-loop sweep call inside on_tick is removed because single-source sweep cadence in the dedicated task is cleaner than double-sourced (B' option chosen in R264 kickoff). The on_tick parameter is preserved on the drive_session API surface for future per-iteration observability uses.

crates/wz-ap-demo/src/main.rs: R260/R263 comment block above the drive_session call refreshed to reflect single-source sweep architecture; the R262/R263 reference to in-loop sweep removed; new R264 sweep_task comment block (~12 lines) documents the cancel-safety rationale + sweep cadence sizing + MCU-portability shape choice.

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs: drive_session_until_terminal doc-comment adds a 'R264 cancel-safety carry' note explaining that poll_and_dispatch_one is NOT cancel-safe for length-prefixed drivers (wz-ap-demo InboundReadDriver reads u16 length then payload via two read_exact calls; select! cancel between the two consumes the length bytes from the socket but drops the captured len, the next iteration's poll then mis-aligns by 2 bytes). The note states explicitly that sub-second sweep cadence belongs in an external task. drive_session API surface remains at 7 args (R262 shape preserved); no #[allow(clippy::too_many_arguments)] needed.

Architectural micro-decision sequence (recorded for posterity): R264 kickoff first asked between (a) 2x wz-ap-demo (b) wz-ap-demo + raw TCP stub (c) wz-ap-demo + z_sub вҖ” chose (a). Then asked between bounds (400+2000) / (2000-only) / (none) вҖ” chose (400+2000). After the fixture surfaced the sweep cadence gap a second AskUserQuestion presented B (drive_session sweep_tick_ms param) / C (shorter lease) / A (lease-bound + 11s upper bound) вҖ” initially chose B. After B's implementation surfaced the poll cancel-safety regression in wz_query_reply_round_trip a third AskUserQuestion presented B' (external sweep_task in wz-ap-demo) / B'' (make InboundReadDriver cancel-safe) / B''' (B' + retain in-loop sweep) вҖ” chose B'. The final shape is the B' implementation.

cargo clippy --manifest-path crates/Cargo.toml --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings: clean across 7 crates (no #[allow(too_many_arguments)] introduced because drive_session API stayed at 7 args).

First commit attempt rejected by commit-msg hook for indented continuation lines (multi-line bullet wrap); reformatted to single-line bullets per COMMIT_FORMAT.md rule and re-committed cleanly. R260-R263 commit-msg-gate-rework=0 streak now broken at 1 cumulative R264 rework вҖ” root cause: drafted 11-12 line body with 4 indented continuations; mechanical mitigation is the awk-line-length check that R263 introduced, which catches >72 byte lines but does not catch indented-continuation rule. R265+ commit drafts must self-check both rules together.
carry_forward_bullets: R264 lesson: any architectural decision that adds select! arms to drive_session_until_terminal must FIRST audit poll_and_dispatch_one's cancel-safety against the concrete LinkDriver impls in scope (wz-ap-demo InboundReadDriver uses length-prefixed read_exact x2 вҖ” NOT cancel-safe). The original R264 attempt to add a sweep_tick_ms parameter that clamps the lease sleep arm tripped this hazard and broke wz_query_reply_round_trip. Future architectural changes touching the inner select! must include the cancel-safety audit in the design step.

R264 lesson: AskUserQuestion sequence (3 rounds of questions in one round of work) is the correct pattern when an architectural attempt surfaces a downstream regression mid-execution. Each AskUserQuestion presented previews of code shape so the user could evaluate the trade-off without reading source. The textbook beats 'silently revert and pick a different approach' because it leaves audit trace of the decision chain.

InboundReadDriver poll_event cancel-safety hazard (R264 surfaced): the function reads u16 length then variable-length payload via two read_exact calls. select! cancel between the calls drops the captured length and leaves the socket cursor 2 bytes ahead of where the next iteration expects. This was acceptable under R263 lease=10 s cadence (cancellation rare in steady-state traffic) but blocks any future sub-second sweep cadence work that would race poll_and_dispatch. Textbook fix: make poll_event truly cancel-safe by buffering partial-read state in InboundReadDriver. Scope estimate: 1-2 rounds (read state machine + matching changes in any other length-prefixed driver impl). Tag: cancel-safety carry, R265+.

R264 production-grade follow-ups (deferred to dedicated rounds): (a) FinalCallback FinalCause Normal vs TimedOut differentiation вҖ” wz-ap-demo's FINAL RECEIVED log line currently logs the same shape for both peer-final and timeout-sweep-final; if a deploy use case needs to differentiate, promote FinalCallback to FnMut(u64, FinalCause). YAGNI hold pending user inspection. (b) sweep cadence configurability via deploy.yaml вҖ” the 100 ms constant is currently a hardcoded literal in wz-ap-demo run_demo; production AP deploys may want to tune per topology. Topology-schema migration carry (R123b-pre) already tracks this surface.

R264 carry: sweep_task graceful shutdown is via abort() which is fine for current state (no async-cleanup-needed). If sweep_task ever owns external resources (open files, network sockets, etc.) abort() may bypass Drop guarantees; the textbook upgrade is a CancellationToken + select! { tick | cancel } shape. Not needed now; documented for the cancel-safety carry round.

R264 carry: drive_session_until_terminal's on_tick API parameter is now a no-op closure in wz-ap-demo (the only production caller). The R262 API surface is preserved for future per-iteration observability uses but currently has no consumer. YAGNI hold вҖ” if a future round confirms no consumer materializes by R270+, consider removing on_tick and the 11 caller sites (single revert via reverse-of-R262 commit shape).

The original R264 attempt to add a sweep_tick_ms parameter that clamps the lease sleep arm tripped this hazard and broke wz_query_reply_round_trip. Future architectural changes touching the inner select! must include the cancel-safety audit in the design step.

R264 lesson: AskUserQuestion sequence (3 rounds of questions in one round of work) is the correct pattern when an architectural attempt surfaces a downstream regression mid-execution. Each AskUserQuestion presented previews of code shape so the user could evaluate the trade-off without reading source. The textbook beats 'silently revert and pick a different approach' because it leaves audit trace of the decision chain.

InboundReadDriver poll_event cancel-safety hazard (R264 surfaced): the function reads u16 length then variable-length payload via two read_exact calls. select! cancel between the calls drops the captured length and leaves the socket cursor 2 bytes ahead of where the next iteration expects. This was acceptable under R263 lease=10 s cadence (cancellation rare in steady-state traffic) but blocks any future sub-second sweep cadence work that would race poll_and_dispatch. Textbook fix: make poll_event truly cancel-safe by buffering partial-read state in InboundReadDriver. Scope estimate: 1-2 rounds (read state machine + matching changes in any other length-prefixed driver impl). Tag: cancel-safety carry, R265+.

R264 production-grade follow-ups (deferred to dedicated rounds): (a) FinalCallback FinalCause Normal vs TimedOut differentiation вҖ” wz-ap-demo's FINAL RECEIVED log line currently logs the same shape for both peer-final and timeout-sweep-final; if a deploy use case needs to differentiate, promote FinalCallback to FnMut(u64, FinalCause). YAGNI hold pending user inspection. (b) sweep cadence configurability via deploy.yaml вҖ” the 100 ms constant is currently a hardcoded literal in wz-ap-demo run_demo; production AP deploys may want to tune per topology. Topology-schema migration carry (R123b-pre) already tracks this surface.

R264 carry: sweep_task graceful shutdown is via abort() which is fine for current state (no async-cleanup-needed). If sweep_task ever owns external resources (open files, network sockets, etc.) abort() may bypass Drop guarantees; the textbook upgrade is a CancellationToken + select! { tick | cancel } shape. Not needed now; documented for the cancel-safety carry round.

R264 carry: drive_session_until_terminal's on_tick API parameter is now a no-op closure in wz-ap-demo (the only production caller). The R262 API surface is preserved for future per-iteration observability uses but currently has no consumer. YAGNI hold вҖ” if a future round confirms no consumer materializes by R270+, consider removing on_tick and the 11 caller sites (single revert via reverse-of-R262 commit shape).

All prior R263 carries except 'R264 end-to-end timeout integration fixture' remain (the latter closes this round). Specifically still open: Instant -> u64 ms storage migration (3-5 round) / Cancellable trait promotion (R257 carry, YAGNI hold) / RuntimeError inner-cause variants behind alloc/std (1 round) / Session<R, T> reparam (urgency accumulating; 3-7 round + design) / core::error::Error replacement (MSRV 1.81+, 1 round) / Liveliness Token wz-ap-demo migration (R250 carry, 1-2 round) / Q_B Q_E wire codec slots (SCE-side codec extension, 5-10 round) / Querier-Publisher get_matching_status (1-2 round) / Phase W full lwIP (20-50 round) / SCE drift (vendor pin ebf3b3ff vs upstream e8cfe14c, ~90 commits, wz call sites=0 so pin bump still deferred).

Round 265.decision_summary: R265 closes the R264 cancel-safety carry by refactoring TcpDriver::poll_event (lib) and InboundReadDriver::poll_event (wz-ap-demo) from a read_exact-based shape (which dropped in-flight bytes on tokio::select! cancellation, leaving the next iteration's poll re-syncing from a mis-aligned socket cursor) into a ReadState state machine where each .await is a single .read() syscall (cancel-safe per tokio's documented contract) and the partial-read buffer survives cancellation in &mut self.read_state; the impl now matches the cancel-safety contract that drive_session_until_terminal's doc-comment had been claiming since R76b, and a new tcp_driver_cancel_safety.rs integration suite exercises the contract end-to-end against a real loopback TcpStream pair with 4 scenarios.
changes_bullets: crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/lib.rs: new ReadState enum (Idle / Length{prefix: [u8; 2], offset: usize} / Payload{frame: Vec<u8>, offset: usize}) with Default derived on Idle. TcpDriver gains a read_state field; from_stream constructor initialises it to Idle. poll_event becomes `loop { match &mut self.read_state { ... } }` where each .await is a single stream.read(...) call. EOF (Ok(0)) and OS error branches reset read_state to Idle. The frame-complete branch takes the buffer out via std::mem::take, resets state, only then runs StreamEnvelope::decode.

crates/wz-ap-demo/src/main.rs: new InboundReadState enum (same Idle/Length/Payload shape, locally defined so the binary does not depend on a library-internal type). InboundReadDriver gains read_state; construction site passes InboundReadState::Idle. poll_event refactored to mirror TcpDriver. log::debug 'inbound frame' line moves into the frame-complete branch (only fires when a frame is reassembled).

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/tests/tcp_driver_cancel_safety.rs: new integration test file (4 scenarios). loopback_pair() helper builds a TcpListener-backed (writer: TcpStream, driver: TcpDriver) pair with TCP_NODELAY enabled on both sides so test write_all + flush sequences hit the wire promptly. envelope_bytes() helper encodes a payload through StreamEnvelope::encode_to_vec for byte-identical wire shape vs production. assert_rx_payload() re-encodes the received bytes back through StreamEnvelope::decode as a double-check.

tcp_driver_cancel_safety scenarios: (1) partial-length-prefix-survives-cancel: write 1 of 2 length-prefix bytes, race poll_event against 100 ms sleep (sleep wins), write remainder, verify Rx with full payload. (2) partial-payload-survives-cancel: write prefix + 5 of N payload bytes, race+sleep, write more, race+sleep again, write remainder, verify Rx вҖ” exercises multi-cancellation resilience. (3) eof-mid-frame-surfaces-PeerClosed: write partial frame + shutdown writer, verify Lost{PeerClosed}. (4) back-to-back-frames-with-intermediate-cancel: write entire first frame + second frame prefix only, first poll returns first frame, second poll parks (race+sleep wins), write second-frame payload, second poll returns second frame.
verification_bullets: cargo build --manifest-path crates/Cargo.toml --workspace --all-targets: clean across 7 crates after both refactors.

cargo test --manifest-path crates/Cargo.toml -p wz-runtime-tokio --test tcp_driver_cancel_safety -- --nocapture: all 4 new scenarios pass in 0.24 s. Each asserts both that cancellation did not return prematurely AND that the eventual Rx event carries the byte-correct payload, so a regression losing bytes on cancel fails loudly.

cargo test --manifest-path crates/Cargo.toml --workspace: all pre-existing suites pass; wz-runtime-tokio lib 484 invariant; integration suites 10+ invariant. No regression in any layer3_* / session_fsm_* suite, including session_fsm_real_tcp.rs which exercises TcpDriver against a real loopback pair via the FSM.
cargo clippy --manifest-path crates/Cargo.toml --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings: clean across 7 crates. No new #[allow(...)] attributes introduced.

bash scripts/run-ci.sh: Layer A-E 9/9 pass. Layer E specifically includes wz_ap_demo_query_timeout.rs (R264) which still passes вҖ” confirms the InboundReadDriver state-machine refactor preserves the wz-ap-demo timeout path end-to-end against the silent peer pattern.

Pre-commit hook validate_workspace clean: T1=0 (ledger=1 R193), RT 12/12, sections=215, GENERATED.md=sync. Commit bbbc088 created with COMMIT_FORMAT.md gate (subject 57 bytes, all body lines <= 72 bytes, 5 single-line bullets); 0 commit-msg rework this round.
carry_forward_bullets: R265 lesson: tokio's cancel-safety contract is per-method; AsyncReadExt::read is cancel-safe (single syscall, no bytes consumed if dropped), but AsyncReadExt::read_exact is NOT (loops over .read internally, partial-read bytes lost on outer-future drop). Any future driver impl over a length-prefixed wire shape must use the single-.read state-machine pattern, not read_exact.

R265 lesson: when refactoring a public function for cancel-safety, the integration test should drive the production wire shape (StreamEnvelope encode/decode here) rather than synthetic byte sequences вҖ” catches any subtle codec-vs-state-machine misalignment that a unit-level state-machine snapshot would miss.

R265 follow-up (deferred): drive_session_until_terminal's R264 cancel-safety carry note can be tightened вҖ” with R265 established, a future round could re-introduce sweep_tick_ms in drive_session safely. YAGNI hold: wz-ap-demo's external sweep_task pattern is already production-grade and MCU-portable.

R265 carry: UdpDriver poll_event was not refactored (carrier-class-different; UDP is datagram-bounded, no length prefix needed). Future round adding UDP fragmentation must audit cancel-safety of any future multi-datagram read path.
