Round 266.decision_summary: R266 lands the panic_payload capture path on RuntimeError::JoinFailed under feature = "alloc": tokio's JoinError::into_panic() result is now propagated through to AP-profile callers as Box<dyn Any + Send + 'static>, accessible via a new RuntimeError::panic_payload() accessor + a Display impl that extracts String / &'static str payload bytes as a "panicked: <msg>" suffix; MCU no-alloc builds keep JoinFailed as a unit variant (no heap to box onto, no panic-infrastructure parity); R258's compile-time Send/Sync/Clone/Eq pin gates Sync/Clone/Eq on cfg(not(feature = "alloc")) because Box<dyn Any + Send> is not Sync (tokio surfaces Send-only payloads), not Clone (Any erases the concrete type's Clone) and not PartialEq; tokio test sites switch from assert_eq! against PartialEq-bound Result to matches! against variant shape (preserving R257 panic-vs-cancel split semantics) and unwrap-+-value-compare for Ok(value) checks.
changes_bullets: crates/wz-runtime-core/src/error.rs: RuntimeError enum body cfg-gated. Under `feature = "alloc"` JoinFailed becomes struct-like with `payload: Option<Box<dyn core::any::Any + Send + 'static>>`; under !alloc it stays as a unit variant. `#[derive(Debug)]` only at the type-level; `#[cfg_attr(not(feature = "alloc"), derive(Clone, PartialEq, Eq))]` re-adds the no-alloc trait surface. JoinCancelled / Shutdown unchanged.

crates/wz-runtime-core/src/error.rs: new smart constructors. `RuntimeError::join_failed()` available in both builds (None-payload under alloc; unit variant under no-alloc); `RuntimeError::join_failed_with_payload(Option<Box<dyn Any + Send + 'static>>)` available only under alloc. The constructor pair lets runtime impls produce the variant without `#[cfg]` switches at the call site (constructor itself dispatches the cfg internally).

crates/wz-runtime-core/src/error.rs: new `RuntimeError::panic_payload(&self) -> Option<&(dyn Any + Send + 'static)>` accessor. Returns Some(&borrowed Box content) for JoinFailed-with-payload; None for JoinFailed-without-payload, JoinCancelled, Shutdown, or under no-alloc (where the slot does not exist). Doc-comment includes the textbook downcast snippet for the two common panic payload types (String / &'static str).

crates/wz-runtime-core/src/error.rs: Display impl extended. Under alloc the JoinFailed arm tries `payload.downcast_ref::<String>()` first then `payload.downcast_ref::<&'static str>()`; on match Display emits `RuntimeError: spawned task panicked: <msg>`. Unknown payload type or no-payload falls back to the plain prefix string (preserves log-grep stability for callers that pattern-match on the prefix).

crates/wz-runtime-core/src/error.rs: std::error::Error impl's source() chain stays None вҖ” the panic payload is type-erased Any (not dyn Error), so threading it through `source()` would mislead callers about what the chain carries. Doc-comment documents the rationale + points at panic_payload() as the explicit accessor.

crates/wz-runtime-core/src/error.rs: compile_time_assertions module updated. _assert_send still pins Send (preserved in both builds; Result<T, RuntimeError> must cross thread boundaries via Future::Output). _assert_sync / _assert_clone / _assert_eq gated on `cfg(not(feature = "alloc"))`. The function calling them gates the alloc-mode assertions accordingly.

crates/wz-runtime-core/src/error.rs: 7 new tests under cfg(all(test, feature = "std")). Cover Box<dyn Error> compatibility (existing); source-chain-is-empty (extended to all variants); panic_payload accessor returns None for non-JoinFailed variants and for JoinFailed-no-payload; payload downcast to String (round-trip from Box<String>); payload downcast to &'static str; Display extracts String payload as suffix; Display extracts static str payload as suffix.

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/runtime_impl.rs: TokioJoinHandle Future poll impl extracts the panic payload via `join_error.into_panic()` (only safe in the !is_cancelled branch where is_panic() is guaranteed true) and stuffs it into RuntimeError::join_failed_with_payload(Some(payload)). Doc-comment adds a R266 section explaining the capture path + downcast targets.

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/runtime_impl.rs: existing 4 assert_eq! sites that relied on RuntimeError PartialEq for Err comparison switch to matches! against the variant shape. The 4 assert_eq! sites that compared Ok(value) switch to result.expect(...) + value compare (unwrap surfaces the actual error variant in the test output on regression). The R257 assert_ne! sanity check rewrites as two matches! negations (panic_outcome not JoinCancelled; cancel_outcome not JoinFailed).

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/runtime_impl.rs: 2 new tokio integration tests. `tokio_runtime_panic_payload_round_trips_through_join_handle` spawns a task that `panic!("literal")`s, asserts the resulting RuntimeError::JoinFailed carries the &'static str payload, downcasts back, and verifies Display embeds the marker. `tokio_runtime_panic_string_payload_round_trips` covers the formatted-message path (panic!("{}", dynamic_string)) which produces a String payload.

cargo test --manifest-path crates/Cargo.toml -p wz-runtime-tokio --lib: 486 tests pass (R265 484 baseline + 2 new R266 panic-payload integration tests).

carry_forward_bullets: R266 closure of the R263 'RuntimeError inner-cause variants (panic_payload behind alloc/std)' carry. The original 1-round estimate turned out accurate; the scope expansion (R258 contract reshape from Send/Sync/Clone/Eq pin to Send-only pin under alloc, plus 8 caller-site assertion migrations) was mechanical and contained within the runtime-impl test module.

R266 lesson: when adding a payload field to a non_exhaustive enum, audit downstream `assert_eq!` patterns that implicitly relied on the type's PartialEq impl. The Rust compiler surfaced 4 hit sites cleanly; matches! / expect+compare migration is mechanical. Future enum-payload additions should grep for `assert_eq!.*EnumName::` + `assert_ne!.*EnumName::` before designing the new variant.

R266 lesson: panic infrastructure produces Send-only payloads (Box<dyn Any + Send>), not Send + Sync. The pre-R266 R258 contract pinned Send + Sync at the RuntimeError level; adding the payload necessarily relaxes Sync under alloc. The relaxation is correct (no caller-site relied on shared-by-reference RuntimeError across threads) but it is a contract change worth documenting in the audit entry, not a silent reshape. The Send invariant is preserved (Result<T, RuntimeError> in Future::Output must be Send).

R266 follow-up (deferred): the std::error::Error::source() chain still returns None. An alternative design would wrap the panic payload in a dyn Error newtype that downcasts to the original Box<dyn Any> internally. The chosen design rejects this because (a) Any -> Error is an honest type erasure step that hides the downcast requirement from callers, and (b) the panic_payload() accessor is the textbook explicit-API shape. If a future round adds a richer source chain (e.g. wrapping a tokio-specific JoinError variant in JoinFailed) it should extend source() at that time.

R266 carry: core::error::Error replacement still depends on MSRV 1.81+ bump. R266 std::error::Error impl is unchanged; when MSRV bumps, the std-gated impl can be replaced with a default-on core::error::Error impl (which is no_std-friendly). 1 round of mechanical work; no architectural blockers.

publishable_carry_forward_bullets: R266 closure of the R263 'RuntimeError inner-cause variants (panic_payload behind alloc/std)' carry. The original 1-round estimate turned out accurate; the scope expansion (R258 contract reshape from Send/Sync/Clone/Eq pin to Send-only pin under alloc, plus 8 caller-site assertion migrations) was mechanical and contained within the runtime-impl test module.

R266 lesson: when adding a payload field to a non_exhaustive enum, audit downstream `assert_eq!` patterns that implicitly relied on the type's PartialEq impl. The Rust compiler surfaced 4 hit sites cleanly; matches! / expect+compare migration is mechanical. Future enum-payload additions should grep for `assert_eq!.*EnumName::` + `assert_ne!.*EnumName::` before designing the new variant.

R266 lesson: panic infrastructure produces Send-only payloads (Box<dyn Any + Send>), not Send + Sync. The pre-R266 R258 contract pinned Send + Sync at the RuntimeError level; adding the payload necessarily relaxes Sync under alloc. The relaxation is correct (no caller-site relied on shared-by-reference RuntimeError across threads) but it is a contract change worth documenting in the audit entry, not a silent reshape. The Send invariant is preserved (Result<T, RuntimeError> in Future::Output must be Send).

R266 follow-up (deferred): the std::error::Error::source() chain still returns None. An alternative design would wrap the panic payload in a dyn Error newtype that downcasts to the original Box<dyn Any> internally. The chosen design rejects this because (a) Any -> Error is an honest type erasure step that hides the downcast requirement from callers, and (b) the panic_payload() accessor is the textbook explicit-API shape. If a future round adds a richer source chain (e.g. wrapping a tokio-specific JoinError variant in JoinFailed) it should extend source() at that time.

R266 carry: core::error::Error replacement still depends on MSRV 1.81+ bump. R266 std::error::Error impl is unchanged; when MSRV bumps, the std-gated impl can be replaced with a default-on core::error::Error impl (which is no_std-friendly). 1 round of mechanical work; no architectural blockers.

changes_bullets: crates/Cargo.toml: [workspace.package] rust-version bumped 1.75 -> 1.81. crates/wz-runtime-core/src/error.rs: `#[cfg(feature = "std")] impl std::error::Error for RuntimeError {}` becomes `impl core::error::Error for RuntimeError {}` (no feature gate). R267 carry: the `feature = "std"` flag on wz-runtime-core is now exclusively a 'pull std into scope' marker (e.g. for std::time, std-only convenience impls if they ever land). If a future round adds another std-only convenience, that's the right place for the gate; otherwise the std feature is YAGNI hold and could be considered for removal post-R270 if no consumer materialises.

With the Error impl now no-feature-gated, the same test could be made `cfg(all(test, feature = "alloc"))` to also exercise the no_std + alloc MCU profile path. R269 carry: when a future feature flag on wz-runtime-core is added, gate tests on the same feature the code uses, not on a parent feature that pulls it in via chain. R266/R267's `feature = "std"` gate over alloc APIs was a `wider-gate-than-necessary` antipattern that hid the CI gap for two rounds; the post-R269 invariant is `cfg(test, feature = X)` where X is the narrowest feature the test body depends on.

R269 lesson: when a feature flag becomes a marker for a feature-set that no longer has code, remove it in the same round another change touches the gate. Carry-as-future-cleanup accumulates dead surface that future readers must reason about (here it took R267 + R268 + R269 = 3 rounds touching the same area before the dead flag was retired). Textbook trigger: `cargo metadata + grep` shows zero non-marker uses for one full round.

Round 270.decision_summary: R270 adds --sweep-cadence-ms CLI flag to wz-ap-demo replacing the hardcoded 100 ms sweep_task sleep; default 100 preserves existing behaviour, 0 explicitly rejected. Closes R264 carry.
changes_bullets: crates/wz-ap-demo/src/main.rs: --sweep-cadence-ms <ms> CLI parse added (lines ~663-687) following the --query-timeout-ms pattern; value 0 explicitly rejected with `wz-ap-demo: --sweep-cadence-ms must be > 0 (0 would busy-loop)` + ExitCode::from(2); non-numeric input rejected with `must be a u32`; default 100 preserves the pre-R270 hardcoded constant.

crates/wz-ap-demo/src/main.rs ReplyConsumerSpec gains a `sweep_cadence_ms: u32` field with doc-comment explaining the wall-time bound + the parser-side 0-rejection invariant (the struct holds an unwrapped u32 because main's parse path guarantees > 0). The R121j-6-e2e-bundle approach keeps run_demo's clippy::too_many_arguments threshold satisfied вҖ” the new knob piggybacks on the existing reply-side spec struct (semantic grouping: sweep cadence is only meaningful when reply consumption is active).

crates/wz-ap-demo/src/main.rs run_demo sweep_task body (lines ~1456-1475): hardcoded `sweep_clock.sleep(100)` replaced with `sweep_clock.sleep(sweep_cadence_ms)` where `sweep_cadence_ms = u64::from(reply_log_spec.sweep_cadence_ms)`. The u32 -> u64 widening matches TimeSource::sleep's u64 milliseconds argument. R264 sweep_task preamble comment updated to reference --sweep-cadence-ms instead of the now-removed hardcoded 100.

crates/wz-ap-demo/src/main.rs: --help / print_usage updated with the new flag listing in the USAGE summary + a 6-line OPTIONS block explaining the tick-period role + lower-is-tighter trade-off + R264 sweep_task reference. The OPTIONS block phrasing mirrors the --query-timeout-ms surrounding entry for consistency.

R264 carry `sweep cadence deploy.yaml knob (0.5R)` CLOSED via the CLI-flag path. Deploy.yaml schema integration is intentionally out of scope for wz-ap-demo today: wz-ap-demo's config surface is CLI flags (mirroring zenoh-cpp's z_get_options_t.timeout_ms pattern), and the existing deploy/*.yaml schema is centred on MCU/AP platform descriptors (cores, SRAM regions, link configs) rather than application-tunable knobs. A future production AP runtime that consumes deploy.yaml can reuse ReplyConsumerSpec.sweep_cadence_ms via a yaml -> struct deserializer; the field is already u32-typed.

cargo clippy --manifest-path crates/Cargo.toml --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings: clean across 7 crates.

bash scripts/run-ci.sh: 10/10 lanes pass (Layer 0/A/A2/B/C0/C1/C1b/C2/D/E). Layer E sweep_task fixture (wz_ap_demo_query_timeout_fires_final_callback) still passes against the post-R270 binary with the default 100 ms cadence; the test does not pass --sweep-cadence-ms explicitly so it exercises the default path.
Manual --help inspection: `--sweep-cadence-ms <ms>` appears in the USAGE summary + OPTIONS block; the OPTIONS prose matches the printed help with cargo run wz-ap-demo -- --help.

Manual rejection-path verification: `cargo run --bin wz-ap-demo -- --connect 127.0.0.1:9999 --query demo/test --sweep-cadence-ms 0` exits with code 2 + stderr `wz-ap-demo: --sweep-cadence-ms must be > 0 (0 would busy-loop)`; the same invocation with `--sweep-cadence-ms abc` exits with code 2 + stderr `wz-ap-demo: --sweep-cadence-ms must be a u32 (got "abc")`. Both error paths surface before any TCP connect attempt (parsing happens in main pre-runtime-spawn).

R270 carry: a future production AP runtime that consumes deploy.yaml can reuse ReplyConsumerSpec.sweep_cadence_ms via a yaml -> struct deserializer; the field is already u32-typed and the parser-side 0-rejection invariant is documented in the struct doc-comment. Phase W lwIP integration will inherit the same knob shape (MCU profile constant via deploy.yaml schema once the schema gains an application-knob section).

R270 lesson: when a `deploy.yaml knob` carry is small enough, the textbook scope respect is to first surface the knob at the existing application config layer (CLI flags here) and defer the deploy.yaml schema integration until the consuming production runtime exists. Reverse order (schema first, application later) would create dead schema fields that no code reads вҖ” same anti-pattern class as `feature = "std"` with no std-only code (closed at R269).

R270 lesson: when a `deploy.yaml knob` carry is small enough, the textbook scope respect is to first surface the knob at the existing application config layer (CLI flags here) and defer the deploy.yaml schema integration until the consuming production runtime exists. Reverse order (schema first, application later) would create dead schema fields that no code reads вҖ” same anti-pattern class as `feature = "std"` with no std-only code (closed at R269).

R273 lesson: when a scope-descriptor language change crosses both the human-facing markdown surface (README) and the atomic Section bodies, doing the markdown surface first (R273) creates a temporary divergence with the atomic store. The divergence is observable only via direct query_section (the parser-side round-trip stays green because round-trip parses the markdown, not the atomic body), so it does not surface as a validate_workspace fail. The fix is a follow-up typed-primitive round; the divergence is acceptable as a transitional state.

Round 28.decision_summary: Phase A control-plane SCXML round вҖ” 3 new algorithm-kind validators (whatami / payload-ext-id / transport-ext-id) covering В§3 Hello body, В§7.1 payload-layer per-context extension allowlist, В§4.4 transport-layer per-control-message extension allowlist.
changes_bullets: sources/algorithms/whatami_v1_valid.scxml new вҖ” В§3 Hello + В§4.1 Init/Join cbyte whatami 2-bit field validator. Allowlist {0=router, 1=peer, 2=client}; rejects wire value 3 (would decode to API enum 0x08 outside constants.h:50-54 surface).

sources/algorithms/payload_ext_id_v1_valid.scxml new вҖ” В§7.1 per-body-kind extension FULL_ID allowlist. 2-param (z_mid, ext_header) вҶ’ bool. Z_PUT/Z_DEL allow {0x41, 0x43}; Z_QUERY allows {0x41, 0x43, 0x45}; Z_REPLY allows {}; Z_ERR allows {0x41}. Source: zenoh-pico src/protocol/codec/message.c per-body decode_extensions switches.

sources/algorithms/transport_ext_id_v1_valid.scxml new вҖ” В§4.4 per-control-message extension FULL_ID allowlist. 2-param (t_mid, ext_header) вҶ’ bool. T_INIT allows {0x27 INIT_PATCH}; T_OPEN allows {} (skip-all per transport.c:328); T_JOIN allows {0x27 JOIN_PATCH, 0x51 JOIN_QOS}. Fragment (В§4.2) extensions out of scope (extension_v1_dispatch R19 covers).

Round 284.decision_summary: R284 lands SessionLinkActions::send_close_with_reason Rust-side primitive + wires it into wz-ap-demo signal-cancel arm so peer sees graceful Close handshake instead of bare EOF.
changes_bullets: crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs вҖ” add SessionLinkActions::send_close_with_reason(reason: CloseReason) Rust-side primitive. Mirrors the Lua-bound send_close_frame_with_reason action's wire behaviour (encode_close + driver.send_blocking on Reliability::Reliable), but takes reason as explicit parameter (the Lua-side reads from ActionTrace::close_reason which is set by set_close_reason_* actions; the Rust-side caller is outside the FSM scxml engine). Bumps ActionTrace::send_close_frame_with_reason for trace symmetry with the Lua-bound action. Independent of FSM state.

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs (tests) вҖ” send_close_with_reason_emits_zenoh_pico_compatible_wire_bytes 4-vector lock across all CloseReason variants (Generic=0, Invalid=1, Expired=2, Unresponsive=3). Asserts: outer header byte = T_MID_CLOSE | FLAG_T_CLOSE_S; body byte = reason discriminator; Reliability::Reliable; trace counter bumps once per call.

crates/wz-ap-demo/src/main.rs вҖ” import CloseReason from wz_runtime_tokio::session_glue::*; insert actions.send_close_with_reason(CloseReason::Generic) on the signal-cancel arm (outcome.is_none() branch) AFTER sweep_task.abort and BEFORE the writer-task drain phase, so peer observes Close ahead of EOF; idempotent against the natural-exit path (script-driven Close already fires from the FSM Closing state).

crates/wz-ap-demo/src/main.rs вҖ” update the R278 select-arm comment block to reflect the R284 reality: Rust-side primitive bypasses the FSM driver, no drive-loop re-entry needed, signal-cancel and natural-exit paths produce equivalent wire-side handshakes. Log message on the cancel arm references the Close + UndeclToken + tail frame drain ordering.

crates/wz-ap-demo/src/runner.rs body вҖ” teardown sequence after drive_session ends remains inline in run_demo (publisher/query/declare join with 200ms timeout each, LivelinessToken receive + drop, signal-cancel-arm Close(Generic) emit, drop(actions), 50ms writer drain). Round 288.decision_summary: R288 lands Querier::get_matching_status вҖ” mirror of zenoh-pico's z_querier_get_matching_status; consults the per-session RemoteQueryableRegistry membership against the querier's keyexpr under the bidirectional asymmetric pattern-match approximation.
changes_bullets: crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/declare/queryable.rs вҖ” RemoteQueryableRegistry gains a `declared: HashMap<u64, String>` field tracking peer-declared queryables by id (populated on DeclQueryable, removed on UndeclQueryable in dispatch_declare). New pub fns: declared_count (membership size), iter_declared (id -> resolved keyexpr enumeration), has_matching(query_keyexpr) returning bool. Match algorithm uses crate::pubsub::keyexpr_pattern_matches bidirectionally so the registry catches `peer-pattern + querier-literal`, `querier-pattern + peer-literal`, and both-literal cases; honest two-pattern wildcard intersection is documented as a future-round carry.

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session.rs вҖ” new pub Querier::get_matching_status -> MatchingStatus method; new pub MatchingStatus { matching: bool } struct (#[non_exhaustive] for future field expansion). The method locks the session's observer, consults remote_queryables.has_matching against the querier's literal keyexpr, and releases the lock before returning the by-value MatchingStatus. No wire emit; consult-only.

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/declare/queryable.rs (tests) вҖ” 7 new unit tests: queryable_declared_count_starts_at_zero_and_tracks_decl_undecl_lifecycle (membership lifecycle), queryable_has_matching_false_on_empty_registry (no peers), queryable_has_matching_true_on_literal_keyexpr_equality (literal match), queryable_has_matching_true_when_peer_pattern_covers_query_literal (peer pattern arm), queryable_has_matching_true_when_query_pattern_covers_peer_literal (query pattern arm), queryable_has_matching_false_after_undeclare (Undecl removes membership), queryable_has_matching_with_mixed_peers_finds_any_match (any-of-many match).

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session.rs (tests) вҖ” 8 new unit tests: 7 querier_get_matching_status_* scenarios (fresh / peer literal match / peer pattern arm / querier pattern arm / Undecl flip / non-matching peer / mixed-peers) plus querier_clone_shares_matching_status_view (clones see the same registry view). Two local helper fns (make_decl_queryable / make_undecl_queryable) constructed inline because the declare/test_helpers module is pub(super)-scoped and not visible from session.rs tests.

Approximation boundary documented inline on has_matching: two patterns with partial overlap where neither contains the other (e.g. home/*/temp vs */sensor/temp) are NOT caught by either asymmetric arm. Honest wildcard intersection is a future-round carry; the wz keyexpr v1 spec (RFC В§5.A line 311) currently locks intersect to exact uint32 ID equality for MVP, so the bidirectional pattern-match shape used here strictly extends the spec for the practical declare-then-match production patterns.

Brief's `test_helpers visibility мӨ‘ліө` carry (R290 closure recommendation) retired as misdiagnosis: session.rs `make_decl_queryable` / `make_decl_subscriber` return `DeclareVariant` envelope variants, while `crate::declare::test_helpers::decl_*` return raw codec records (DeclQueryable / DeclSubscriber). The signatures and return types differ; R288 (665b32d) and R290 (e8a6568) doc-comments at session.rs:5152-5156 + 5774-5778 explicitly chose inlining over visibility promotion ("inlining is cheaper than relaxing the helper visibility"). To genuinely consolidate would require adding new convenience helpers (`literal-suffix вҶ’ DeclareVariant`) in test_helpers, which is more work than the carry framed.

Round 292.decision_summary: R292 lifts the R277/R278/R284 run_demo teardown ordering invariant from doc-only to compile-time enforcement via a six-state typestate sequence wrapper in the new `crate::teardown` module.
changes_bullets: crates/wz-ap-demo/src/teardown.rs (new, 196 lines) вҖ” six typestates (TeardownInitial -> TasksJoined -> TokenDropped -> CloseEmitted -> ActionsDropped -> WriterDrained) with consume-self transitions encode the canonical chain. Per-step doc-comments anchor each transition to its originating round: TeardownInitial::abort_sweep_join_tasks owns R264 sweep_task.abort + R121e 200ms publisher/query/declare task join; TasksJoined::drop_liveliness_token owns R277 token_rx receive + RAII drop that enqueues Declare(UndeclToken) on the writer channel; TokenDropped::emit_close_if_cancelled owns R284 graceful Close(Generic) emit gated on was_cancelled; CloseEmitted::drop_actions owns Arc<SessionLinkActions> drop so the writer-task mpsc sender clones drain; ActionsDropped::drain_writer owns the 50ms writer_handle drain. Reaching WriterDrained is the compile-time witness that all seven steps ran in order.

crates/wz-ap-demo/src/runner.rs вҖ” pre-R292 inline Step 2-7 body (sweep_task.abort + 3x 200ms task join + token_rx receive+drop + outcome.is_none() Close emit + drop(actions) + 50ms writer_handle drain, ~80 lines of doc-comment-anchored R264/R277/R284 invariants) replaced by the fluent typestate chain. The R292 call-site binding `let _: teardown::WriterDrained = TeardownInitial { ... }.abort_sweep_join_tasks().await.drop_liveliness_token().await.emit_close_if_cancelled().drop_actions().drain_writer().await;` makes the canonical-order witness explicit. run_demo's outer doc-comment redirects readers to crate::teardown for per-step rationale; module-level doc-comment updated to advertise R292 invariant promotion.

crates/wz-ap-demo/src/main.rs вҖ” `mod teardown;` added so the new sibling module enters the crate root (slot 6 of the seven sibling modules now in wz-ap-demo per R285-R287 decomposition + R292 addition).
Net diff: +257 / -102 across 3 files; the runner.rs side shrinks by ~80 lines as the inline R264/R277/R284 doc-comment block migrates to per-typestate doc-comments in teardown.rs.
verification_bullets: `cargo build / cargo build --release -p wz-ap-demo` clean. The typestate chain compiles to identical machine code as the previous inline form (each consume-self transition is a struct move + field unpack; LLVM optimises to the same instruction sequence as the pre-R292 inline body).

`(cd crates && cargo test --workspace --quiet)` pass; `(cd crates && cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --no-deps --quiet -- -D warnings)` clean after resolving one initial `doc_lazy_continuation` lint by flattening the run_demo doc-comment's nested list into prose.

`scripts/run-ci.sh --skip-codegen` вҖ” Layer 0 / A / A2 / C0 / C1 / C1b / C2 / D / E all green. Layer E specifically re-runs the wz_liveliness_subscriber_round_trip_against_wz_acceptor e2e gate that R284 ratified as the regression detector for reversed UndeclToken/Close ordering; pass confirms R292 preserves the behaviour the typestate now also enforces structurally.
`cargo fmt --all --manifest-path crates/Cargo.toml -- --check` exit=0 post-edit; the new teardown.rs and runner.rs edits stay within R291's mandatory Layer 0 fmt gate.

Round 293.decision_summary: R293 honest wildcard-vs-wildcard keyexpr intersection: new keyexpr_intersect_patterns helper + has_matching swap on Q-side + Pub-side registries; R288 bidirectional asymmetric approximation retired.
changes_bullets: crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/pubsub.rs вҖ” new `pub fn keyexpr_intersect_patterns(a_chunks: &[&str], b_chunks: &[&str]) -> bool` helper. Algorithm: recursive descent over both chunk slices with symmetric handling of `**` (zero-or-more chunks, with backtracking on either side), `*` (exactly-one chunk on either side), `$*` intra-chunk DSL (single-side reuses chunk_matches_with_dsl, two-side $* uses leading + trailing anchor prefix/suffix compatibility check as a sound over-approximation), and literal chunks (byte equality after each side's chunk_intersects accepts). Sibling private fns `intersect_chunks` (recursive driver) + `chunk_intersects` (single-chunk router) keep the public surface a single function. Module-level doc-comment names the approximation boundary explicitly.

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/declare/queryable.rs вҖ” `RemoteQueryableRegistry::has_matching` swaps the pre-R293 two-arm asymmetric matcher (`peer-pattern over literal-query OR query-pattern over literal-peer`) for a single `keyexpr_intersect_patterns(&peer_chunks, &query_chunks)` consult. Doc-comment rewritten: removed the `approximation boundary` paragraph; added the honest two-pattern overlap case as a first-class supported semantic; clarified the wz spec / peer-declared bifurcation (wz-own KE = compile-time fixed per RFC Appendix C; peer-declared KE = runtime string-based, matcher lives in the wz-runtime-tokio layer).

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/declare/subscriber.rs вҖ” Pub-side mirror swap on `RemoteSubscriberRegistry::has_matching`; same single-line consult shape. Doc-comment redirects readers to the Q-side has_matching for the per-case textbook expansion (matcher is symmetric so a single statement of semantics suffices).

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/pubsub.rs tests вҖ” 12 new unit tests under `intersect_*`: literals_equal, literals_differ, pattern_covers_literal_either_side, two_patterns_share_literal_via_mid_star (the textbook R288-miss case `home/*/temp вҲ© */sensor/temp`), two_patterns_no_shared_literal, double_star_consumes_zero, double_star_both_sides_overlap_mid, double_star_disjoint_anchors, double_star_match_anywhere, dsl_one_side_matches_other_literal_chunk, dsl_both_sides_over_approx_documented (pins the $*-$* over-approximation contract), and symmetry_under_swap (spot-checks `(a,b) == (b,a)` over 8 representative pairs).

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/declare/queryable.rs tests + subscriber.rs tests вҖ” 3 new tests on each side (6 total) exercising the new honest-intersection path at the registry surface: `*_has_matching_true_when_two_patterns_share_literal_via_mid_star` (the R288 false-negative case), `*_has_matching_false_when_two_patterns_have_disjoint_anchors` (negative-side coverage for the same domain), and `*_has_matching_true_when_double_star_intersects_either_direction` (`** вҲ© **` symmetric backtracking). Pre-existing R288/R290 tests stay green unchanged.
verification_bullets: cargo test -p wz-runtime-tokio --lib pubsub::tests::intersect вҖ” 12/12 pass (literals_equal, literals_differ, pattern_covers_literal_either_side, two_patterns_share_literal_via_mid_star, two_patterns_no_shared_literal, double_star_consumes_zero, double_star_both_sides_overlap_mid, double_star_disjoint_anchors, double_star_match_anywhere, dsl_one_side_matches_other_literal_chunk, dsl_both_sides_over_approx_documented, symmetry_under_swap). Initial draft over-approximation for $*-$* failed `pre$* вҲ© $*post`; tightened to leading+trailing anchor compat check before final commit.

cargo test -p wz-runtime-tokio --lib (full lib) вҖ” 562 pass / 0 fail / 0 ignored (pre-R293 baseline = 544; R293 delta = +18: 12 intersect + 3 Q-side + 3 Pub-side). All R288/R289/R290 has_matching tests stay green under the new matcher, confirming the textbook one-side-pattern + both-literal cases still fire identically.

scripts/run-ci.sh --skip-codegen вҖ” Layer 0 / A / A2 / C0 / C1 / C1b / C2 / D / E all green. Layer E (`wz_liveliness_subscriber_round_trip_against_wz_acceptor` + other e2e) pass confirms peer-declared keyexpr matching at the wire level still works.

cargo fmt --all -- --check exit=0 + cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --no-deps -- -D warnings clean after one initial `needless_lifetimes` lint on the `split` test-helper (resolved by lifetime elision) and one fmt drift on the new test struct literal (resolved by `cargo fmt --all`).
pre-commit `mnemosyne-cli validate-workspace` fired green on feat commit `6c0a52f` вҖ” entries=291 / sections=215 / T1=0 / RT 12/12 / GENERATED.md=sync, unchanged from R292 closure baseline.
carry_forward_bullets: Appendix C clarification (R293-carry from R293) вҖ” the Appendix C `What watching-zenoh will NOT deliver:` list item `Full KeyExpr wildcard at runtime вҖ” KeyExpr set is compile-time fixed; runtime matching is an O(1) table lookup built at build time (В§5.F const-fold)` is technically accurate only for wz's *own* declared keyexprs (compile-time fixed). Peer-declared keyexprs arrive via DeclSubscriber / DeclQueryable wire as runtime strings and the R293 matcher handles them honestly. The Appendix C wording does not currently distinguish the two domains; a clarifying amend (split into two list items) is a textbook spec-text fix. Blocked on the section's atomic-decomposition status (the 6-bullet body lives in raw markdown round-trip, not in atomic fields) вҖ” amend requires either (a) Appendix C atomic decompose first then set_section_*, or (b) explicit user override for direct .md edit. Carry to R294.

Two-side `$*`-DSL exact matcher (R293-carry from R293) вҖ” the current chunk_intersects implementation over-approximates when both chunks contain `$*` (compatibility check on leading + trailing anchors only; middle sub-parts assumed interleavable). Sound for has_matching callers (false-positive drives at most a redundant wire emit). Lifting to the textbook exact form (sub-part order matching DP per zenoh-pico `_z_chunk_intersect_left_with_right_substar_substar`) is a 1-2R refinement when a consumer surfaces. Currently no production caller exercises $*-$* (zenoh AP keyexprs use $* on single side at most).

Layer 3 wire-interop fixture (R293-carry from R293 + R100 deferred) вҖ” wz keyexpr_intersect_patterns vs zenoh-pico's `_z_keyexpr_intersect_*` symbols via zenoh-pico-sys FFI. Proves cross-stack semantic compatibility beyond unit-test pairs. ~1R once the FFI surface exposes the keyexpr matcher.

Closed R293 carry from R290/R291 вҖ” honest two-pattern keyexpr intersection algorithm. has_matching consult now uses keyexpr_intersect_patterns directly on both Q-side and Pub-side registries; the R288 bidirectional asymmetric pattern-match approximation is retired. The four matching_status API handles (Querier / QuerierAliased / Publisher / PublisherAliased) benefit transparently because their backend RemoteQueryableRegistry::has_matching / RemoteSubscriberRegistry::has_matching consults are the only consumers.

Round 294.decision_summary: R294 `Instant -> u64 ms` storage migration: SessionLinkActions gains a TokioTime clock field, the two stamp slots + check_lease_deadline + drive_session_until_terminal switch to pure u64 ms arithmetic.
changes_bullets: crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs вҖ” SessionLinkActions struct: `last_inbound_keepalive_at` + `established_at` retyped from `Mutex<Option<Instant>>` to `Mutex<Option<u64>>` (storage is now u64 ms since the shared monotonic epoch); new `pub clock: TokioTime` field carries the shared epoch. `SessionLinkActions::new` signature gains a `clock: TokioTime` parameter so production callers (runner.rs) thread the same `TokioTime` value to actions + drive_session for R263 single-shared-epoch invariant. The two stamp-recording sites (`InboundFrame::KeepAlive` arm of `handle_inbound`, `record_established_at` Lua-bound action) read `self.clock.now_monotonic_ms()`. Doc-comments rewritten across both fields + the new field.

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs вҖ” `check_lease_deadline` signature: `now: Instant` -> `now_ms: u64`. Body rewritten as pure u64 arithmetic: `lease_ms` is the `lease` field times 1000 when `lease_in_seconds`, otherwise as-is; baseline is `max(established_ms, keepalive_ms)`; comparator is `now_ms.saturating_sub(stamp_ms) >= lease_ms`. The `Duration::from_secs/from_millis` calls retired.

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs вҖ” `drive_session_until_terminal` body: `lease_ms` + `lease_deadline_ms: Option<u64>` (`stamp_ms.map(|s| s.saturating_add(lease_ms))`), `remaining_ms = deadline_ms.saturating_sub(now_ms)` for the `clock.sleep(remaining_ms)` race arm, and the lease-branch `check_lease_deadline(actions, engine, clock.now_monotonic_ms())` call. The R260 doc-comment paragraph that flagged the storage migration as a `future round` carry is updated to record the R294 closure.

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs imports: `std::time::{Duration, Instant}` retired (the file no longer references either); `crate::runtime_impl::TokioTime` added for the new struct field type.

crates/wz-ap-demo/src/runner.rs вҖ” `SessionLinkActions::new(outbound, params, session_clock)`. The pre-existing `session_clock = TokioTime::new()` value at run_demo scope is threaded into the actions ctor so the actions' clock matches the drive_session_until_terminal `clock` param + the sweep_task / spawn_background_tasks clones. R294 textbook: one TokioTime per session, shared by every consumer.

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session.rs tests + 10 test files in crates/wz-runtime-tokio/tests/ + 2 layer3 integration tests in crates/wz-integration-tests/tests/ вҖ” 33 `SessionLinkActions::new` call sites get a `TokioTime::new()` 3rd argument via a single perl regex (balanced-parens aware: `s/SessionLinkActions::new\(([^()]*(?:\([^()]*\)[^()]*)*)\)/SessionLinkActions::new($1, TokioTime::new())/g`). The 12 lease-deadline tests + 2 inbound-dispatch tests + 2 drive-session tests + 1 session.rs `mark_session_established` helper update their Instant-based time arithmetic (`stamp + Duration::from_secs(N)`, `Instant::now()`, etc.) to the u64 ms form (`stamp + N * 1000`, `actions.clock.now_monotonic_ms()`).

Tests + integration test imports updated: `use wz_runtime_tokio::runtime_impl::TokioTime;` added to 12 files; `use wz_runtime_core::TimeSource;` added where the test body calls `actions.clock.now_monotonic_ms()` (R263 trait-method-not-in-scope fix); stale `use std::time::{Duration, Instant};` lines retired where the file no longer needs them.
verification_bullets: cargo build --tests вҖ” clean compile after the 33 `SessionLinkActions::new` call-site updates + 8 test-body Instant/Duration arithmetic conversions. The compiler-driven fan-out caught every site (E0061 + E0308 errors surfaced exactly the call sites the perl regex needed + the test bodies that did Instant comparison or `Duration::from_*` arithmetic).

cargo test --workspace --quiet вҖ” pass (562 wz-runtime-tokio lib + integration test suites). The R77 + R84 lease-deadline tests (12 in session_fsm_lease_deadline.rs) all pass with the new u64 ms arithmetic; both the WithinLease + Expired + NoBaseline arms keep their semantics, and the established_at-only baseline fallback (R84 scenario A/B) still drives Established -> Closing past the lease.

scripts/run-ci.sh --skip-codegen вҖ” Layer 0 / A / A2 / C0 / C1 / C1b / C2 / D / E all green. Layer E `wz_liveliness_subscriber_round_trip_against_wz_acceptor` + the four other binary-dep e2e tests exercise the real keepalive + lease deadline code paths against the wz-ap-demo binary; pass confirms the u64 ms storage form preserves the wire-level invariants.

cargo fmt --all --manifest-path crates/Cargo.toml -- --check вҖ” exit=0 after one explicit `cargo fmt --all` pass to canonicalise the multi-line struct-literal formats rustfmt prefers over the dense per-arg `SessionLinkActions::new(driver, params, TokioTime::new())` perl emits.

cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --no-deps -- -D warnings вҖ” clean after removing the few unused `wz_runtime_core::TimeSource` imports the bulk-add introduced in tests that did not end up calling `now_monotonic_ms()`. The remaining TimeSource imports are load-bearing and the lint surface stays empty.

Round 295.decision_summary: R295 RFC Appendix C wildcard bifurcation вҖ” wz-own KE (compile-time fixed declare_* surface) vs peer-declared KE (runtime intersection matcher) clarified across both `WILL deliver` + `will NOT deliver` sub-sections.
changes_bullets: docs/rfc-sce-protocol-synthesis.md вҖ” Appendix C `What \`watching-zenoh\` will NOT deliver` sub-section: the single `Full KeyExpr wildcard at runtime вҖ” KeyExpr set is compile-time fixed; runtime matching is an O(1) table lookup built at build time (В§5.F const-fold)` bullet is rewritten to scope the limitation to wz-own keyexprs only: `Full KeyExpr wildcard *authoring* for `watching-zenoh`-owned keyexprs вҖ” the wz-own KE set is compile-time fixed; the runtime `declare_*` API accepts only literal keyexprs registered at build time (В§5.F const-fold). Peer-declared keyexpr wildcards arrive as wire-runtime strings and are matched via the runtime intersection algorithm (see *What `watching-zenoh` WILL deliver*).` The other five `will NOT deliver` bullets (router mode / aggregation / oversize fragment / auth-crypto / ABI compat) are byte-preserved.

docs/rfc-sce-protocol-synthesis.md вҖ” Appendix C `What `watching-zenoh` WILL deliver when all phases land` sub-section: new sixth bullet appended after the five pre-existing ones, recording the peer-declared KE matching commitment that R293's honest 2-pattern intersection landed: `Honest peer-declared KeyExpr intersection at runtime вҖ” incoming `DeclSubscriber` / `DeclQueryable` / `Interest` wire frames carry arbitrary zenoh-style chunked patterns (literal / `*` / `**` / `$*` DSL mixes), matched against the local wz-owned KE registry via a dedicated chunk-intersect algorithm.` The other five `WILL deliver` bullets (Zenoh wire pin / leaf-peer mode / zenohd interop / QoS / zero-copy) are byte-preserved.

Both edits land via `mnemosyne-cli set-section-body --doc docs/rfc-sce-protocol-synthesis.md --section <atomic-id> --body-file /tmp/r295/{will_not_deliver,will_deliver}_body.md` вҖ” typed primitive path, T1/T2 + structural verification + round-trip pass at each invocation. The two sub-sections previously had empty atomic typed fields (intent / rationale / etc) and the raw markdown body was the source of truth; R295 keeps that hybrid shape вҖ” typed fields remain unpopulated, body now reflects the bifurcated text.

Upstream mnemosyne dependency: mutate.rs `find_section_heading` / `find_first_heading_after` / `find_section_end_position` previously lacked CommonMark code-fence detection symmetric with parser.rs:64-75. Set-section-body on any RFC sub-section failed StructuralVerificationFailed because the doc's fenced `#define` / `# Generated by sce-codegen` lines (1295-1307, 1396-1402, 1773) corrupted the parent-prefix stack. Fix landed in mnemosyne commit 1f81f51 (`fix(validator): make section-range scanners code-fence-aware`) вҖ” shared `is_code_fence_line` helper + fence-state threading through all three scanners + 3 unit tests; cargo install --path crates/mnemosyne-cli --force pulled 0550aa7 вҶ’ 0550aa7f-dirty into PATH before the wz set-section-body retries succeeded.
verification_bullets: mnemosyne-cli set-section-body л‘җ лІҲ лӘЁл‘җ success вҖ” affected_sections л‘җ sub-section, round_trip_diff_count=0 each. post_mutate_style_summary T3 warn=706 / T4 info=362 on the rfc-sce-protocol-synthesis.md doc itself (workspace-wide T3 warn=855 unchanged, T4 info=427 unchanged at validate_workspace post-mutation).

No other regressions across the workspace.

Spec text post-R295 reads cleanly against R293 has_matching impl вҖ” the wz-runtime-tokio `keyexpr_intersect_patterns` helper, RemoteSubscriberRegistry::has_matching, RemoteQueryableRegistry::has_matching, and matching_status 4-handle surface are all peer-declared KE consumers; the new Appendix C `WILL deliver` bullet is the spec-side anchor for them. wz-own KE `declare_*` literal-only contract (В§5.F const-fold) stays separately captured in the bifurcated `will NOT deliver` bullet.

The set_section_body raw-md path was the correct primitive once the upstream mutate.rs code-fence asymmetry was fixed.

Round 296.decision_summary: R296 closes R293-carry (b) two-side $* matcher вҖ” lead/trail anchor pair is exact (zenoh-pico intersects-mode equivalent), not an over-approximation; +8 verify tests + chunk_intersects doc-comment correction.
changes_bullets: crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/pubsub.rs::chunk_intersects вҖ” the (true, true) branch's doc-comment and the surrounding `keyexpr_intersect_patterns` doc are rewritten. The previous text labelled the lead/trail anchor pair check as `R293 over-approximation for two-side `$*`` and pointed at zenoh-pico's `_z_chunk_intersect_left_with_right_substar_substar` as the carry refinement. R296 closure: the lead/trail anchor pair is the exact necessary-and-sufficient condition. Middle `$*`-separated sub-parts on both sides always admit a common chunk literal via alternating interleaving (any two ordered sub-part sequences `[A1..AN]` and `[B1..BM]` are realised by `A1 B1 A2 B2 вҖҰ`, with each side independently `$*`-floating its own sub-parts). zenoh-pico's `chunk_special_intersects` reaches the same answer through the `right contains $*` over-approximation branch (line 156 of `keyexpr_match_template.h`); every (true, true) input lands there because by precondition both chunks carry at least one `$*`. So wz's algorithm and zenoh-pico's intersects-mode chunk matcher agree on every (true, true) input under canonical wire form.

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/pubsub.rs::tests вҖ” 8 new verification tests pinning the exact algorithm boundary: `intersect_dsl_both_sides_lead_anchor_mismatch_rejects` (byte-distinct lead anchors `A` vs `B`), `..._trail_anchor_mismatch_rejects` (byte-distinct trail anchors), `..._lead_prefix_compatible_accepts` (lead `A` is a prefix of `AB`), `..._trail_suffix_compatible_accepts` (trail `C` is a suffix of `BC`), `..._middle_sub_parts_always_fit` ([A,B] vs [B,A] interleave in `BABA`), `..._distinct_middles_accept` (`$*ABC$*` вҲ© `$*XYZ$*` shares `ABCXYZ`), `..._lead_prefix_with_trail_suffix_overlap` (combined prefix + suffix anchor checks), `..._byte_overlap_lead_rejects` (`AB` vs `AX` share first byte but diverge вҖ” neither is a prefix). The pre-existing `intersect_dsl_both_sides_over_approx_documented` is renamed to `intersect_dsl_both_sides_lead_trail_anchor` and its body retained as the trivial `pre$*` вҲ© `$*post` case; net wz-runtime-tokio lib test growth 562 вҶ’ 570 (+8).
verification_bullets: cargo test -p wz-runtime-tokio --lib intersect_dsl_both_sides вҖ” 9 pass / 0 fail / 0 ignored (eight new R296 tests + the renamed lead_trail_anchor case). cargo test -p wz-runtime-tokio --lib total вҖ” 570 pass (562 R293 closure baseline + 8 net new; rename is net-zero).

cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --no-deps -- -D warnings вҖ” clean across the full workspace. cargo test --workspace вҖ” every result line passes; no regressions outside wz-runtime-tokio. cargo fmt --all --manifest-path crates/Cargo.toml -- --check вҖ” exit=0.

Round 297.decision_summary: R297 closes R293-carry (c) вҖ” Layer 3 keyexpr intersect fixture cross-validates wz vs zenoh-pico: zenoh-pico-sys binding + 7 test fns Г— ~50 chunk pairs all byte-equal.
changes_bullets: crates/zenoh-pico-sys/wrapper.h + build.rs вҖ” expose `_z_keyexpr_forward_intersects` (chunk-level forward-scan matcher that `_z_keyexpr_intersects` in session/keyexpr.c:581 dispatches into). Forward declaration added in wrapper.h (with `#include <stdbool.h>` for the return-type), bindgen allowlist gains `.allowlist_function("_z_keyexpr_forward_intersects")`. The symbol is generated by `_ZP_CAT(_z_keyexpr_forward, intersects)` macro expansion in keyexpr_match_template.h:472 вҖ” already extern in libzenohpico.a, declaration only widens visibility for bindgen + the Rust linker. Side-steps the `_z_keyexpr_t` / `_z_string_t` composite that the public-ish `_z_keyexpr_intersects` requires (those structs are not in the existing allowlist) by passing raw `(ptr, len)` slices the worker accepts natively.

crates/wz-integration-tests/tests/layer3_keyexpr_intersect.rs вҖ” new Layer 3 wire-interop fixture (208 lines). 7 test fns: keyexpr_intersect_literal_pairs (7 cases), keyexpr_intersect_single_chunk_wildcard (9), keyexpr_intersect_double_star (12), keyexpr_intersect_single_side_dsl (9), keyexpr_intersect_two_side_dsl_anchor_pairs (10), keyexpr_intersect_mixed_wildcards (10), keyexpr_intersect_depth_edge_cases (6) вҖ” totalling ~63 chunk-pair invocations. Each case calls both implementations via `assert_agree(a, b)` (wz: split on `/` вҶ’ keyexpr_intersect_patterns; pico: raw ptr+len вҶ’ _z_keyexpr_forward_intersects), asserts wz/pico byte-equal on the forward pair AND the reverse pair (cross-checks intersect symmetry on both sides).

R296 equivalence claim cross-validated against the actual upstream binary (zenoh-pico 1.5.0 vendored static lib). The R296 algorithm analysis pinned the wz/pico equivalence at the algorithm level; R297 closes the empirical loop вҖ” every fixture case the analysis predicted would agree does agree at runtime. No wz algorithm change. The fixture is property-style in coverage breadth (7 chunk-pattern classes spanning the matcher's full input variety) but handcrafted in case selection (deterministic, no random generation вҖ” keeps the gate flaky-free and runs in <1ms total).
verification_bullets: cargo test -p wz-integration-tests --test layer3_keyexpr_intersect вҖ” 7 pass / 0 fail / 0 ignored. Every assert_agree call holds: wz and zenoh-pico return byte-equal boolean for all ~63 chunk pair invocations, both forward and reverse direction (~126 total binary comparisons). No mismatch in any class.

cargo build -p zenoh-pico-sys вҖ” incremental rebuild green after wrapper.h + build.rs allowlist additions. Generated bindings.rs contains `_z_keyexpr_forward_intersects` symbol (`grep -l _z_keyexpr_forward_intersects $(ls -t target/debug/build/zenoh-pico-sys-*/out/bindings.rs | head -1)` returns SYMBOL_PRESENT). Two-stage cmake вҶ’ bindgen build cached, ~1s incremental.

cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --no-deps -- -D warnings вҖ” clean across the full workspace post-R297 (no new lint surface from the unsafe FFI block in the fixture, no new warnings from the allowlist expansion). scripts/run-ci.sh --skip-codegen вҖ” all required layers pass (Layer 0 / A / A2 / C0 / C1 / C1b / C2 / D / E).
carry_forward_bullets: Closed R297 carry from R293 вҖ” (c) Layer 3 keyexpr intersect wire-interop fixture. The R293/R296 wz algorithm now has both algorithm-level (R296) and binary-level (R297) equivalence evidence against zenoh-pico's intersects-mode chunk matcher.

Optional R297b extension вҖ” property-based test using a `proptest` or `quickcheck` chunk-pattern generator (random canonical chunks with bounded alphabet + bounded `$*` count + bounded depth). The R297 handcrafted fixture is deterministic and runs in <1ms total; random shrinking could surface boundary cases the handcrafted corpus missed. Not gated on anything; defer until a regression suggests the corpus is insufficient.

Round 298.decision_summary: R298 closes R297 optional carry вҖ” proptest property layer (512 cases) over canonical keyexpr generator; wz/zenoh-pico agree on every shrunken pair, zero divergence found.
changes_bullets: crates/wz-integration-tests/Cargo.toml dev-dependencies вҖ” add `proptest = "1"` (resolved to 1.11.0). The note records the generator scope: bounded alphabet [a,b,c], 1..=4 chunks, per-chunk literal / `*` / `**` / `$*`-DSL (1..=2 `$*` runs).

crates/wz-integration-tests/tests/layer3_keyexpr_intersect.rs вҖ” append R297b property-fuzz block after the seven R297 handcrafted test fns. Adds `alpha_char_strategy` / `lit_strategy(min,max)` / `dsl_chunk_strategy` (n=1: `lead$*trail`, n=2: `lead$*mid$*trail` with mid вүҘ1 char to keep `$*$*` non-canonical runs out of the corpus) / `chunk_strategy` (weights 3/1/1/3 across literal/`*`/`**`/DSL) / `keyexpr_strategy` (1..=4 chunks joined by `/`, consecutive `**` chunks deduped in-generator so shrinking always lands on a canonical string). The property `keyexpr_intersect_wz_pico_property` runs `ProptestConfig { cases: 512, .. }` and asserts wz/pico byte-equal on forward + reverse + wz symmetry + pico symmetry per random `(a, b)` keyexpr pair.

R297b extension closes the R297 optional carry without altering wz's R293/R296 matcher вҖ” the algorithm and its handcrafted corpus are unchanged; only the fuzz coverage layer is added. The property is constructive (no `prop_filter` rejects) so proptest's shrinking always converges on a minimal counter-example if a divergence ever surfaces.

Round 299.decision_summary: R299 lands Layer 3 keyexpr canonicalization fixture; cross-validates wz canonize_keyexpr against zenoh-pico _z_keyexpr_canonize and surfaces three pico canon bugs as documented divergences (incl. one SIGABRT denial-of-service path).
changes_bullets: crates/zenoh-pico-sys/wrapper.h + build.rs вҖ” expose `_z_keyexpr_canonize(char *start, size_t *len)` and the `zp_keyexpr_canon_status_t` enum. The symbol is declared in `vendor/zenoh-pico/include/zenoh-pico/session/keyexpr.h:69`; the enum lives in `api/constants.h` and is already transitively included via `zenoh-pico.h`. Bindgen allowlist gains the function + the status enum; the wrapper header adds an explicit `#include <stddef.h>` + `#include "zenoh-pico/session/keyexpr.h"` to keep the keyexpr-domain FFI surface visible at one site (R297 intersect symbol lives next to it).

crates/wz-integration-tests/tests/layer3_keyexpr_canon.rs вҖ” new Layer 3 fixture (~330 lines). 9 tests: `canon_identity_on_already_canonical_input`, `canon_singleify_collapses_dollar_star_runs_in_dsl_chunks`, `canon_lone_dollar_star_alone`, `canon_drops_double_star_after_double_star`, `canon_rejects_invalid_grammar_single_violation` (agreed-subspace cross-validation), `canon_known_pico_anomaly_star_after_double_star`, `canon_known_pico_anomaly_dsl_rewrite_chunk_walk_overrun`, `canon_known_pico_anomaly_double_star_literal_star_aborts` (divergence pin), `keyexpr_canon_wz_pico_property` (proptest 512-case constrained generator). The status-code 1:1 map (wz `KeyexprCanonError` вҶ” pico `zp_keyexpr_canon_status_t`) lives at `wz_error_to_pico_status` per the wz module doc-comment line 64-65.

Three wz/pico canon divergences discovered + documented in the fixture module doc-comment (lines 25-66): (1) `**` followed by `*`-shape chunk вҖ” pico's case-1 unconditional write + deferred `in_big_wild` produces `*/**/...` instead of `**/...`; (2) initial `$*` rewrite + any later chunk with `*` вҖ” pico's main-canonize char-walk uses `c < end` (should be `c < chunk_end`) so the per-chunk validation reads past the boundary and trips STARS_IN_CHUNK on a later chunk's `*`; (3) `**` + literal + `*` вҖ” pico's `__zp_canon_prefix` case-1 else-continue path skips `in_big_wild = false` reset, so a subsequent `*` returns SINGLE_STAR_AFTER_DOUBLE_STAR with `*len` pointing at the `/` between literal and `*`, then main canonize fails its `chunk_end - reader == 2` precondition and triggers `assert(false)` at `keyexpr.c:340` (process aborts via SIGABRT вҖ” wz peer sending such a keyexpr could DoS a pico client). Wz handles all three cleanly per spec.

Property generator constrained to AGREED subspace вҖ” R298-style canonical chunk strategy (alphabet [a,b,c], 1..=4 chunks, literal / `*` / `**` / flanked-DSL) with an in-generator post-process that drops every `*`-shape or `$*`-prefixed chunk appearing AFTER any `**` chunk in the sequence (avoids pico bugs #1 and #3 deterministically; #2 is avoided because dsl_chunk_strategy emits only flanked DSL chunks that stay in case-1/case-2 of the chunk handler, never falling through to the buggy char-walk). The constructive filter keeps shrinking on canonical inputs.
verification_bullets: cargo test -p wz-integration-tests --test layer3_keyexpr_canon вҖ” 9 pass / 0 fail / 0 ignored. Property fn ran 512 random canonical cases in <100ms; zero divergences found within the constrained subspace.

cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --no-deps -- -D warnings вҖ” clean across the full workspace. No new lint surface from the wrapper.h additions, the bindgen allowlist expansion, or the proptest macro expansion.
scripts/run-ci.sh --skip-codegen вҖ” Layer 0 / A / A2 / C0 / C1 / C1b / C2 / D / E all green; `run-ci: all required layers pass`.

Pico bug discoveries reproducibly trigger вҖ” bug #1 (`**/*` вҶ’ `*/**`) and bug #2 (`$*/a/*` вҶ’ STARS_IN_CHUNK) pinned by `canon_known_pico_anomaly_star_after_double_star` and `_dsl_rewrite_chunk_walk_overrun` respectively; bug #3 SIGABRT path verified by hand (`**/c/*` aborts the process via assert(false) at keyexpr.c:340) and is intentionally NOT runtime-asserted (would kill the test binary) вҖ” the `_double_star_literal_star_aborts` test instead locks wz's identity output on these inputs and documents the pico abort analytically.
carry_forward_bullets: R299 surfaced THREE wz/zenoh-pico canon divergences вҖ” wire-interop hazards that affect any wz вҶ” pico session where keyexprs containing `**`, `$*`, or specific chunk-shape combinations are exchanged. R300+ candidate (architectural): decide between (a) wz tracks pico's buggy output (lossy but interop-safe), (b) wz adds an inbound-side normalization shim that fixes pico's output before storage, or (c) wz upstreams patches to zenoh-pico (best long-term, requires merge cycle). User confirmation needed вҖ” architectural decision with cross-repo coordination if option (c).

Bug #3 (`**` + literal + `*` SIGABRT path) is a DoS risk: a malicious or buggy wz peer sending such a keyexpr to a pico client triggers a process abort. wz-side mitigation: caller MUST canonize_keyexpr() before sending Declare(DeclKexpr) so the post-canon string can never trip pico's assert. Add a pre-send canonize-or-reject gate in wz's send-declare path as R300+ candidate.

R284 architectural carry from R283 вҖ” uniform Established gate across declare_token / declare_subscriber / declare_queryable / declare_liveliness_subscriber non-aliased declare_* methods. 3-5R, signature break + caller fan-out on runner.rs::install_session_handles + e2e harnesses. R288 spec-first lesson applies; user confirmation needed вҖ” install_session_handles deliberately registers pre-Established per the design doc at runner.rs:280-293.
R283 architectural carry вҖ” peer-side non-final Interest inbound (bilateral). wz Liveliness 100% bilateral path remains gated on this. 2-3R + design. User confirmation needed.

R267 architectural carry вҖ” Session<R,T> reparameterisation. Mutex/RwLock contract precondition; 3-7R + design. R260-R263 + R294 single-shared-epoch invariant is a precondition for the Phase W lwIP/embassy/FreeRTOS port вҖ” TimeSource is the seam. Mutex/RwLock trait abstraction has empirical impedance (guard vs closure, sync vs async) that needs MCU call-site shape before design lock. User confirmation needed.

Round 300.decision_summary: R300 closes R299 carry #1 (DoS mitigation): adds outbound DECLARE-side keyexpr gate that rejects R299 bug #3 SIGABRT pattern family pre-emit, plus argv eager-reject in wz-ap-demo.
changes_bullets: crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/keyexpr_canon.rs вҖ” adds `OutboundKeyexprError` enum (NotCanonical(KeyexprCanonError) + PicoBugThreeFamily { keyexpr, offending_chunk }) and `check_outbound_keyexpr_pico_safe(input: &str) -> Result<(), OutboundKeyexprError>`. The gate first runs canonize_keyexpr for structural validity, then walks chunks with a 2-flag state machine (seen_double_star + seen_literal_after_double_star, reset on every fresh `**` chunk). Reject fires when both flags are set and the current chunk's canonical form is `*`-shape (single `*`, `**`, or any `$*`-only run via `chunk_canonizes_to_star_shape` private helper). Conservative scope: also rejects trailing `**` after a literal-after-`**` segment (`**/a/**` etc.) вҖ” R299 fixture empirically pins SIGABRT only for trailing `*`/`$*` (cannot cross-validate `**` trailing without process abort), but the underlying `in_big_wild` stale-state mechanism does not distinguish the closer's exact star-shape. False-positive zone documented; narrowing pending empirical fork-based pico abort probe.

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs вҖ” adds `SendDeclareError` enum (Keyexpr(OutboundKeyexprError) + ReservedMappingIdZero + UnknownMappingId(u64) + MissingKeyexpr) with Display/Error/From impls. Adds private `SessionLinkActions::reconstruct_outbound_keyexpr(mapping_id, suffix) -> Result<String, SendDeclareError>` covering all four wire shapes ((0,None)=MissingKeyexpr / (0,Some(s))=literal / (id,None)=pure-alias prefix lookup / (id,Some(tail))=composite prefix||tail). Changes `send_declare_keyexpr`, `send_declare_subscriber`, `send_declare_queryable`, `send_declare_token` signatures from `()` to `Result<(), SendDeclareError>`. Each method gate-checks BEFORE wire emit or mapping-table side effect; send_declare_keyexpr also rejects mapping_id=0 (mirrors build_declare_kexpr's assertion as typed error). send_undeclare_kexpr unchanged (no keyexpr input). 9 new unit tests: reserved-id-zero / bug-three / structurally-invalid / missing-keyexpr / unknown-mapping / cross-boundary-bug-three / safe-alias-form / queryable+token gate inheritance / reconstruct shape table.

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session.rs вҖ” `Session::declare_token` signature now `Result<LivelinessToken, OutboundKeyexprError>` (literal-mode, only Keyexpr variant reachable; unreachable! guards future refactors). `Session::declare_token_aliased` now also returns the existing `LivelinessAliasError` extended with `InvalidKeyexpr(OutboundKeyexprError)` variant + Display + Error::source impl. 13 declare_token test callsites updated to `.expect("hardcoded canonical literal keyexpr")`. 23 direct `send_declare_keyexpr` test callsites updated to chained `.expect()`.

crates/wz-ap-demo/src/main.rs вҖ” adds eager argv-level R300 validation loop for the three DECLARE-side flags (--declare-subscriber/-queryable/-token); rejected keyexprs exit code 2 with a diagnostic stderr line BEFORE the tokio runtime / driver loop is built. Receive-side keyexprs (--key/--queryable/--query/--liveliness-subscribe) deliberately not gated вҖ” they register local pattern slots that match inbound peer keyexprs and are never emitted on the outbound wire.

crates/wz-ap-demo/src/tasks.rs вҖ” declare_task and publisher_task callsites for send_declare_subscriber/queryable/keyexpr + session.declare_token now `if let Err(e) = ...` вҶ’ log::warn + eprintln + early return (CLI bad-input path, no panic). Mirrors the argv-eager path so library API users still get the same gate at the runtime layer.

crates/wz-integration-tests/tests/r300_outbound_keyexpr_gate.rs вҖ” new Layer E fixture (4 tests, all #[ignore = "binary-dep e2e ..."]). Pins exit-code 2 + stderr diagnostic for the 3 R299 bug #3 family inputs via --declare-subscriber, plus uniform gate firing across --declare-queryable / --declare-token, plus structural reject path (empty chunk). Uses --connect 127.0.0.1:1 (unreachable target) so the argv-eager gate fires before any connect attempt вҖ” fast + peer-independent.
verification_bullets: cargo test --workspace вҖ” 589 lib tests pass / 0 fail (was 579 pre-R300; +10 = 9 SessionLinkActions gate tests + 1 reconstruct shape table test). 23 keyexpr_canon tests pass (was 15 pre-R300; +8 R300-specific). Layer E `cargo test -p wz-integration-tests --test r300_outbound_keyexpr_gate -- --ignored` вҖ” 4 pass / 0 fail in <1s (no peer dependency).

cargo build --workspace --tests вҖ” clean across wz-runtime-tokio, wz-runtime-tokio-test-support, wz-ap-demo, wz-integration-tests. Cascade scope: 13 declare_token + 23 send_declare_keyexpr test callsites + 4 wz-ap-demo prod callsites, all updated to handle Result.

validate_workspace baseline preserved вҖ” docs=12/12, T1 orphan total=0 (ledger=1 R193 known-stale unchanged), round-trip mandatory=12/12, T3 warn=855 / T4 info=427 unchanged, atomic ledger entries=298/sections=215/sync. R300 commit pre-commit hook passed.

Gate semantic correctness вҖ” R299 fixture 3 bug #3 cases (`**/c/*`, `**/foo/*`, `**/a/b/*`) reject; bug #1 cases (`**/*`, `home/**/*/temp`, `**/$*/temp`) pass (no false positive; wz canon converts these to safe wire form internally per R221, separate wire-drift handling deferred to R301+). Cross-boundary case verified: prefix `**` registered via send_declare_keyexpr, suffix `/c/*` via send_declare_subscriber вҶ’ gate reports reconstructed full keyexpr `**/c/*` in the error (a suffix-only check would have missed this вҖ” the gate's raison d'etre).

No regression on R299 layer3_keyexpr_canon fixture (the 8 documented divergence pins + proptest 512-case property continue to pass вҖ” R300 added a separate safety layer above canonize_keyexpr without touching canonize_keyexpr itself, preserving R221 spec-mirror contract intact).
carry_forward_bullets: R299 carry #1 (DoS mitigation gate) вҖ” CLOSED by R300.

R299 carry #2 (other wz/pico equivalence inventory) вҖ” still open; recommend R301 to grep wz-runtime-tokio for `mirror` / `equivalent` / `zenoh-pico's` doc-comments and inventory each module's empirical re-verification need. R299 retrospect lesson: every 'functionally equivalent' claim deserves an R299-style cross-validation fixture.

R299 carry #3 (wzвҶ”pico canon divergence handling вҖ” bug #1 wire-interop drift) вҖ” still open; R300 narrowed scope to SIGABRT-only, leaving bug #1 family allowed. Decision among (a) wz traces pico bug / (b) wz inbound normalization shim / (c) zenoh-pico upstream patch still requires user input.

R300 conservative-reject false-positive zone (`**/a/**`-shape inputs are semantically valid zenoh keyexpr вҖ” 'a appears somewhere' вҖ” but the gate rejects them defensively). Empirical SIGABRT verification via a fork()/wait()-based pico subprocess probe would let R301+ narrow the gate to the literal-trailing-* subset. Currently conservative.

wz-ap-demo eager argv gate is DECLARE-side only. R301 could evaluate whether --key / --queryable / --query / --liveliness-subscribe (receive-side keyexprs) should also be argv-validated for grammar correctness (not pico-safety вҖ” they never leave wz) so CLI users get the same UX as DECLARE flags. Likely a low-effort UX tweak, not a correctness concern.

Round 301.decision_summary: R301 first-pass composable-framework feature catalog: docs/feature_inventory.md 13th workspace doc + 144 atomic features (19 domains) + 6 semver preset contracts.
changes_bullets: docs/feature_inventory.md NEW вҖ” H1 + 9 H2 (В§1 Purpose / В§2 Naming convention / В§3 Three-test definitions / В§4 Conflict policy / В§5 Atomic feature catalog / В§6 Presets / В§7 Cargo feature emission mechanism / В§8 SCE feature gate mechanism / В§9 Change log) + 28 H3 sub-sections (В§2.1-2.2 + В§3.1-3.3 + В§5.1-5.19 + В§6.1-6.6). ~600 lines of prose authored with Status/Scope/Inputs/Outputs/Non-outputs front-matter. Cross-feature consistency anchor is zenoh-cpp public API shape (R267 north star).

mnemosyne.toml::workspace.docs extended 12вҶ’13 вҖ” new entry `docs/feature_inventory.md` inserted in alpha order between README.md and docs/intrinsics-runtime-symbols.md. default_doc unchanged (ARCHITECTURE.md). No other config changes.

144 add_inventory_entry calls landed atomic features across 19 domains. Counts per domain: transport=16, locator=9, scouting=6, session=9, link=5, keyexpr=8, declare=7, pubsub=11, query=10, liveliness=5, storage=4, codec=16, runtime=6, platform=7, routing=6, access=6, attachment=2, time=4, encoding=7. Naming convention `<domain>-<capability>` strict kebab-case ASCII, max 3 segments.

6 add_inventory_entry calls landed preset semver-named contracts at v0.1.0: preset-mcu-minimal (35 atomic feat smallest viable MCU client) / preset-mcu-extended (~57 atomic feat adds query+liveliness+wildcards) / preset-ap-client (~65 atomic feat Linux client) / preset-ap-router (~78 atomic feat adds router mode+gossip+multicast) / preset-ap-full (~123 atomic feat kitchen sink) / preset-zenoh-cpp (preset-ap-full minus MCU subset, cross-feature consistency anchor).

Status labels per inventory entry: ~62 active (wz R300 implementation snapshot with citation pointing at wz source file) / ~88 reserved (zenoh / zenoh-pico / zenoh-cpp roadmap with citation pointing at upstream module). All entries carry source + reason field вҖ” reason summarizes Footprint/Plausibility/Coherence 3-test verdict in F=...; P=...; C=... triple.

В§7 Cargo feature emission mechanism (R302+ open carry) вҖ” Cargo.toml [features] table layout + default feature set + feature-implication edges + #[cfg] gate placement deferred. Reference points cited: Linux kconfig, Zephyr west config, Buildroot, NixOS USE flags.

В§8 SCE feature gate mechanism (R302+ open carry) вҖ” SCXML `<sce:requires feature=...>` / `<sce:requires preset=...>` attribute design pending SCE codegen ratify. First cross-repo deliverable on composable-framework track.
verification_bullets: validate_workspace post-doc-register: docs=12вҶ’13, T1 orphan=0 (no new orphans), round-trip mandatory=13/13 (new doc parses+emits clean), T3 warn 855вҶ’862 (+7 style flags from new doc prose, expected), T4 info 427вҶ’429 (+2), atomic ledger entries=299 unchanged, sections=215 unchanged, GENERATED.md=sync.

validate_workspace post-inventory (150 add_inventory_entry calls): docs=13/13, T1=0, RT=13/13, GENERATED.md=sync. sidecar grew 2.63 MB вҶ’ 2.66 MB (~30 KB across 150 entries). Each call cascaded GENERATED.md atomically; no manual edits to .atomic.json or GENERATED.md.

SSOT contract honored per CLAUDE.md вҖ” all 150 inventory mutations routed through `add_inventory_entry` primitive. mnemosyne.toml workspace.docs edit was the only direct file mutation; this is the textbook scope-extension path (workspace.docs grows monotonically; no orphan-silencing intent).

Atomic feature naming consistency check вҖ” all 144 atomic ids match `<domain>-<capability>` or `<domain>-<capability>-<modifier>` kebab-case pattern; all 6 preset ids match `preset-<target>-<level>`. No duplicates (add_inventory_entry rejects on duplicate inventory_id).

wz R300 implementation snapshot grounding вҖ” active labels cite concrete wz source paths: crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/{session,session_glue,keyexpr_canon,pubsub,query,reply,sample}.rs + sources/{algorithms,codecs,session}/ SCXMLs. Reserved labels cite zenoh / zenoh-pico / zenoh-cpp module names per the inventory.source field.
carry_forward_bullets: R302+ Cargo feature emission mechanism вҖ” design `Cargo.toml::[features]` layout, default-feature set, implication edges, #[cfg(feature)] gate placement. Reference points: kconfig / west / Buildroot / NixOS. This is the WHAT-cargo-does layer of the composable framework.

R302+ SCE feature gate mechanism вҖ” ratify SCXML `<sce:requires feature="X"/>` / `<sce:requires preset="Y"/>` attribute with SCE codegen. First cross-repo deliverable on the composable framework track; requires vendor/sce write-side coordination.

R303+ atomic Section decompose for feature_inventory.md вҖ” В§5.1-5.19 + В§6.1-6.6 currently parser-detected only (sections=215 unchanged); typed intent/rationale/inputs/outputs population deferred per R25 register-only precedent. Once decomposed, back-fill section_ref on all 150 inventory entries.

R303+ Footprint empirical re-evaluation вҖ” ~62 active entries marked F=measured against wz R300 source but no actual LOC/binary-byte/RAM-byte numbers captured. A measurement round would produce the first quantitative Footprint baseline; reserved entries get estimates from upstream code size.
R299 carry #2 (wz/pico equivalence inventory) вҖ” still open. R301 catalog adjacent but does not address per-module mirror claim audit; recommend separate grep+verify round.

R299 carry #3 (wzвҶ”pico canon divergence handling, bug #1 wire-interop drift) вҖ” still open; user input needed among (a) wz traces pico bug / (b) wz inbound normalization shim / (c) zenoh-pico upstream patch.
R300 conservative-reject narrow (false-positive zone in DECLARE-side keyexpr gate) вҖ” still open low-priority; fork/wait pico subprocess probe deferred.
R300 receive-side argv UX (wz-ap-demo --key/--queryable/--query/--liveliness-subscribe argv-level grammar validation) вҖ” still open low-priority UX tweak.

Architectural carries unchanged: R284 uniform Established gate, R283 peer Interest inbound bilateral, R267 Session<R,T> reparam (composability direct prerequisite), Querier::get_matching_status, Q_B/Q_E codec slots, Phase W full lwIP.

19 domain-aggregate features `domain-<name>` = union of each domain's atomic set. Granularity layer between hand-picking atomic and pick-a-preset. Used as `cargo add wz --features domain-pubsub` for whole-domain opt-in.

6 preset features as EXPLICIT per-preset atomic lists; no preset-extends-preset. Rationale: v0.1.0 is a frozen semver contract вҖ” atomic catalog growth cannot silently shift preset membership. Round 303.decision_summary: R302b first source-level #[cfg(feature)] gates: 17 gate points in wz-runtime-tokio liveliness paths + wz facade forwarding via `wz-runtime-tokio?/feature` syntax.
changes_bullets: wz-runtime-tokio/Cargo.toml [features] section added: default=["liveliness-token", "liveliness-subscriber"], liveliness-token=[], liveliness-subscriber=[]. Backward-compat: existing in-workspace consumers (wz-ap-demo, integration tests, session_fsm tests) build unchanged because defaults stay on.
src/declare.rs: gate `mod liveliness` + `mod liveliness_subscriber` declarations + their `pub use` re-exports on respective features. Queryable + subscriber sub-modules stay unconditional.

src/observer.rs: gate `ApplicationLayerObserver::liveliness` field on `liveliness-token`, `::liveliness_subscribers` field on `liveliness-subscriber`. Same gates on the `new()` initializers and the `dispatch_iteration_event()` calls. Import statements split вҖ” `use crate::declare::LivelinessRegistry` now `#[cfg(feature = "liveliness-token")]`, same shape for the subscriber registry.

src/session.rs 17 gate points: 4 methods (declare_token, declare_token_aliased, declare_liveliness_subscriber, declare_liveliness_subscriber_aliased) + 4 structs (LivelinessOptions, LivelinessToken, LivelinessSubscriberOptions, LivelinessSubscriber) + 4 impl/Drop blocks (impl LivelinessToken, Drop for LivelinessToken, impl LivelinessSubscriber, Drop for LivelinessSubscriber) + 5 enum/Display/Error pairs (LivelinessAliasError enum + Display + Error, LivelinessSubscriberAliasError enum + Display + Error). Use statements split so OutboundKeyexprError/SendDeclareError/LivelinessSample/LivelinessSampleCallback become conditional.

src/declare/cross_tests.rs: gate the `three_registries_share_a_message_stream_independently` test on liveliness-token. The two-registry test (subscriber + queryable) stays unconditional. Test count diff: feature-off mode loses the 3-registry test, keeps the 2-registry test.

src/declare/test_helpers.rs: gate `decl_token` / `undecl_token` / `declare_envelope_decl_token` / `declare_envelope_undecl_token` helpers + their underlying `use wz_codecs::decl_token::DeclToken` / `use wz_codecs::undecl_token::UndeclToken` imports on `any(feature = "liveliness-token", feature = "liveliness-subscriber")` вҖ” either feature needs the helpers (liveliness.rs uses them with -token; liveliness_subscriber.rs uses them with -subscriber). Imports + helpers stay symmetric so no half-active state.

wz/Cargo.toml: [dependencies] wz-runtime-tokio = { path = "../wz-runtime-tokio", optional = true, default-features = false }. Forwarding: runtime-tokio = ["dep:wz-runtime-tokio"]. liveliness-token = ["wz-runtime-tokio?/liveliness-token"]. liveliness-subscriber = ["wz-runtime-tokio?/liveliness-subscriber"]. Cargo `?` syntax means liveliness-* enabled WITHOUT runtime-tokio stays a label (no crate pull); WITH runtime-tokio it forwards the sub-feature into wz-runtime-tokio.

carry_forward_bullets: cargo test --no-default-features cargo gotcha вҖ” wz-runtime-tokio-test-support's Cargo.toml line 47 has `wz-runtime-tokio = { path = "../wz-runtime-tokio" }` WITHOUT `default-features = false`. Cargo feature-unification then forces wz-runtime-tokio defaults (both liveliness ON) during `cargo test` even with --no-default-features. Production `cargo check --no-default-features` works correctly (verified). Fix: future round mark test-support's wz-runtime-tokio dep as default-features=false + explicit feature pulling.
