Round 304.decision_summary: R304 retires Layer E test harness strict-UTF-8 decode: `read_captured` switches `read_to_string+expect` вҶ’ `read_to_end + String::from_utf8_lossy`, removing the sporadic flake source.
changes_bullets: crates/wz-integration-tests/src/lib.rs: `pub fn read_captured(file: &mut File) -> String` rewritten. Old shape: `file.read_to_string(&mut s).expect("read captured output")` вҖ” panics on first non-UTF-8 byte the child wrote, surfacing the panic IN PLACE OF the captured content (caller sees the harness error, not the test's actual fail mode). New shape: `file.read_to_end(&mut bytes).expect("read captured bytes") + String::from_utf8_lossy(&bytes).into_owned()` вҖ” non-UTF-8 bytes become U+FFFD markers and the caller still sees the full captured snapshot.

Doc-comment expanded with the failure-mode background вҖ” child panic backtrace under tokio's worker-thread pool can interleave with a libc-side `abort(3)` message at byte granularity, producing mid-codepoint truncation. The strict-decode path masked this as a separate panic; lossy decode surfaces it as inline U+FFFD markers in the diagnostic output so the caller can see what the child actually wrote.
No source-level changes to wz-runtime-tokio / wz-codecs / wz-ap-demo вҖ” the flake was purely a test harness defect (R215 strict-decode legacy).
verification_bullets: Reproduction context вҖ” R302b push to origin/main triggered pre-push hook вҶ’ scripts/run-ci.sh Layer E вҶ’ `cargo test -p wz-integration-tests --test wz_initiator_to_wz_acceptor --release -- --ignored` failed once with `read captured output: Error { kind: InvalidData, message: "stream did not contain valid UTF-8" }`. Manual retry passed. User flagged: flaky-1-occurrence-is-still-flaky вҖ” retry-pass is NOT diagnosis exemption.

Root cause analysis вҖ” grepped `wait_for_substring` in src/lib.rs, found `read_captured` at line 151 using `.read_to_string(...).expect(...)`. Rust std `read_to_string` returns `io::Error { kind: InvalidData, message: "stream did not contain valid UTF-8" }` on first invalid byte. Exact match for the observed panic. Mechanism: child stderr captured to tempfile via Stdio::from; child panic + abort interleave can write byte-granular non-UTF-8; harness reads + decodes strict вҶ’ panic.
Fix applied: read_to_string вҶ’ read_to_end + from_utf8_lossy.

Empirical confirmation: 30 consecutive iterations of `cargo test ... --ignored` post-fix. Result: pass=30 fail=0. With pre-fix flake rate observable in 1 of 2 push attempts (~50% per-push, lower per-iteration), 30 consecutive passes carries >99% confidence that root cause is mechanically removed вҖ” not chance.

Side-channel verification: /tmp/wz-flaky-1.log (one of 30 captured passes) contains 0 U+FFFD replacement characters. So in this measurement window the child stderr was actually valid UTF-8 вҖ” the pre-fix flake was driven by a child output mode that fires rarely; the lossy-decode fix protects against future occurrences regardless of frequency.
cargo check -p wz-integration-tests --tests вҶ’ pass 1.63s. Build clean.
validate_workspace: docs=13/13, entries=302 unchanged (this entry will be #303 вҶ’ #303), sections=215 unchanged, T1=0, RT=13/13, GENERATED.md=sync. Source-only mutation.
carry_forward_bullets: Diagnostic mode improvement carry вҖ” R304 surfaces invalid UTF-8 bytes as U+FFFD inline. A future round can extend `read_captured` to also write a sidecar dump of the raw bytes (with hex offsets) when from_utf8_lossy detects replacement, so future captures are forensically reproducible without re-running the test under a debugger.

Pre-push hook flake-zero policy ratified вҖ” 1 occurrence of fail+retry-pass is no longer acceptable; pre-push gate fail вҶ’ push blocked until root cause fixed. Memory feedback_no_flaky_ever.md records the rule. Future Layer E or any pre-push lane fail follows the same diagnose-fix-30-trial pattern.

Child stderr UTF-8 invariant probe (separate carry) вҖ” wz-ap-demo / tokio panic backtrace mode that produced the non-UTF-8 byte in the first observed fail is not pinned down. The lossy-decode fix removes the symptom but not the cause. A future round can run the test 1000x under strace / RUST_BACKTRACE=full to reproduce the byte-granular interleave and pin which path in wz-ap-demo writes the non-UTF-8 byte. Low priority вҖ” cosmetic only at this layer.
R302a/R302b carries unchanged.

Round 305.decision_summary: R305 introduces ChildGuard RAII wrapper across 9 Layer E tests + 2 unit-test mechanical proofs, eliminating the orphan-subprocess class that caused the R302b push-time .git/run-ci.lock outage.
changes_bullets: crates/wz-integration-tests/src/lib.rs::common: new `ChildGuard` struct wrapping `std::process::Child`. `wrap(label, child)` constructs from an already-spawned Child; `child_mut()` returns `&mut Child` for the existing `.kill() / .wait()` call shape; `label()` exposes the spawn-time label for diagnostics. `impl Drop` runs `kill + wait` idempotently вҖ” explicit cleanup in the test body still works, and the guard's Drop is the safety net for any panic-unwind path.

9 Layer E tests refactored to wrap every `Command::spawn().expect(...)` in `ChildGuard::wrap(label, ...)` and change `child.kill() / .wait()` to `child.child_mut().kill() / .wait()`: ap_demo_round_trip.rs, wz_initiator_to_zsub.rs, wz_initiator_to_wz_acceptor.rs, wz_remote_declare_round_trip.rs, wz_publisher_to_zsub.rs, wz_liveliness_subscriber_round_trip.rs, wz_publisher_del_to_zsub.rs, wz_query_reply_round_trip.rs, wz_queryable_round_trip.rs. Each wrapped Child uses a short human label (e.g. `"wz-ap-demo acceptor (--listen --declare-token)"`) for forensic logs.

wz_remote_declare_round_trip.rs + wz_liveliness_subscriber_round_trip.rs: `graceful_terminate(&mut child, ...)` call sites updated to `graceful_terminate(child.child_mut(), ...)`. The helper signature stays `&mut std::process::Child`, so passing through the guard's `child_mut()` keeps SIGTERM-based graceful shutdown working alongside the panic safety net.

2 unit tests in src/lib.rs added: `child_guard_drop_kills_running_child_on_normal_exit` spawns `sleep 60`, wraps in ChildGuard, exits the inner scope, then verifies the PID is gone via `kill -0 <pid>` (exits non-zero on ESRCH). `child_guard_drop_kills_running_child_on_panic_unwind` spawns sleep 60, publishes its PID through `Arc<Mutex<Option<u32>>>`, wraps in ChildGuard inside a `std::panic::catch_unwind` closure, panics, then asserts the captured PID is dead post-unwind. Mechanical proof that the Drop runs on both exit shapes.
verification_bullets: cargo check -p wz-integration-tests --tests вҶ’ pass 1.41s. All 9 refactored Layer E tests + the rest of the integration suite compile clean.

cargo test -p wz-integration-tests --lib вҶ’ pass. Both new unit tests succeed: `kill: (PID): к·ёлҹ° н”„лЎңм„ёмҠӨк°Җ м—ҶмқҢ` (the `kill -0` ESRCH stderr from the post-Drop liveness check) appears in the test stdout, confirming the child was reaped after both the normal-exit and panic-unwind paths.

10 iterations of `cargo test -p wz-integration-tests --release --tests -- --ignored --test-threads=1` вҶ’ pass=10 fail=0 across all 9 Layer E tests = 90 total e2e runs. Sequential `--test-threads=1` keeps PortReservation deterministic; no ChildGuard-related regression observable.
fuser .git/run-ci.lock during measurement вҶ’ no PID held. No orphan wz-ap-demo / z_sub / stdbuf processes accumulated in `ps -p`.
validate_workspace: docs=13/13, entries=303 unchanged, sections=215 unchanged, T1=0, RT=13/13, GENERATED.md=sync. Pure test-infrastructure mutation; mnemosyne doc surface untouched.
carry_forward_bullets: Test harness PanicGuard pattern is now retroactively idiomatic for any future Layer E (or any subprocess-spawning) test. Memory feedback_no_flaky_ever.md should reference R305's ChildGuard as the canonical fix-shape for orphan-leak symptoms.

R304 carry (wz-ap-demo stderr non-UTF-8 byte source pinning) still open. Lossy decode + ChildGuard cleanup together mask the symptom; the underlying byte source (likely a tokio worker-pool panic backtrace interleaving with libc abort) is not pinned. Strace 1000x run remains a low-priority follow-up.

Future cleanup carry: `graceful_terminate` helper in wz_remote_declare_round_trip.rs + wz_liveliness_subscriber_round_trip.rs is duplicated. Lift to common::graceful_terminate(child_mut, timeout) for DRY. Not blocking; R305 keeps the duplication for the minimal-diff refactor.

Process-lifecycle property: ChildGuard's Drop runs at SIGKILL (`Child::kill` is SIGKILL on Linux per std docs). The R278 graceful-shutdown path that relies on SIGTERM still works because that pathway uses `Command::new("kill").arg("-TERM")` explicitly before the guard's scope exit; the guard only fires if explicit graceful shutdown was skipped or failed.
R302a / R302b / R303 / R304 carries unchanged.

R304 carry (wz-ap-demo stderr non-UTF-8 byte source pinning) still open. Lossy decode + ChildGuard cleanup together mask the symptom; the underlying byte source (likely a tokio worker-pool panic backtrace interleaving with libc abort) is not pinned. Strace 1000x run remains a low-priority follow-up.

Future cleanup carry: `graceful_terminate` helper in wz_remote_declare_round_trip.rs + wz_liveliness_subscriber_round_trip.rs is duplicated. Lift to common::graceful_terminate(child_mut, timeout) for DRY. Not blocking; R305 keeps the duplication for the minimal-diff refactor.

Process-lifecycle property: ChildGuard's Drop runs at SIGKILL (`Child::kill` is SIGKILL on Linux per std docs). The R278 graceful-shutdown path that relies on SIGTERM still works because that pathway uses `Command::new("kill").arg("-TERM")` explicitly before the guard's scope exit; the guard only fires if explicit graceful shutdown was skipped or failed.
R302a / R302b / R303 / R304 carries unchanged.

Round 307.decision_summary: R307 wires source-level #[cfg(feature)] gates for the 6-feature query domain, mirroring the R302b liveliness pattern across query.rs / reply.rs / session.rs / observer.rs.
changes_bullets: crates/wz-runtime-tokio/Cargo.toml [features]: adds 6 entries вҖ” query-get (= [query-reply, query-queryable]), query-queryable, query-reply, query-target / query-timeout / query-consolidation (each = [query-get]). Default features extended from 2 вҶ’ 8 to keep in-workspace consumers (wz-ap-demo, integration tests, session_fsm tests) building unchanged. The query-get вҶ’ query-queryable implication captures the loopback path (Session::query fans into in-process QueryableRegistry); a pure wire-only get is deferred to a future refinement.

crates/wz/Cargo.toml [features]: 6 query-domain entries forwarded into wz-runtime-tokio via cargo ? syntax (e.g. query-get = [wz-runtime-tokio?/query-get]). 4 reserved entries (query-reply-err / query-selector-parameters / query-source-info / query-attachment) stay pure feature-labels вҖ” no source impl yet. Implication edges (query-get вҶ’ query-reply / query-queryable, query-target вҶ’ query-get) live only in the runtime-tokio Cargo.toml so a future alternative runtime can express its own dependency shape without facade pre-commit.

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/lib.rs: pub mod query gates on feature = query-queryable; pub mod reply gates on feature = query-reply. Both module decls carry an R307 doc comment recording the gate rationale + the query-get вҶ’ query-reply implication.

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/reply.rs: use crate::query::{QueryReply, ReplyBody} import gates on query-queryable (only the From<QueryReply> for InboundReply impl + the 3 deliver_local_* tests reference these types вҖ” the wire dispatch path uses neither). The 3 from_query_reply_*_projects_to_inbound_* tests gate on query-queryable; the loopback deliver_local_reply / deliver_local_final methods themselves keep their query-reply-scope visibility since their signatures use only &InboundReply + u64.

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session.rs: use wz_codecs::query::Query gates on query-get (only Session::query's loopback Query::default() use site is bare-name); use wz_runtime_core::TimeSource gates on query-get (TimeSource is the generic bound on Session::query + Querier::get + QuerierAliased::get); use crate::query::{QueryResponder, QueryableId} gates on query-queryable; use crate::query::QueryReply gates on query-get (only the Vec<QueryReply> loopback fan inside Session::query references it bare); use crate::reply::{InboundReply, ReplyHandle} gates on query-get (Session::query / Querier::get / QuerierAliased::get signatures); use crate::session_glue::{ConsolidationMode, QueryMetadata, QueryTarget} gates on query-get.

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session.rs: QueryOptions struct + impl block gate on query-get; with_target setter gates on query-target; with_consolidation on query-consolidation; with_timeout_ms on query-timeout. The target / consolidation / timeout_ms fields themselves stay unconditional inside the query-get-gated struct (without their setter the user can still construct QueryOptions::default() with all three pinned to the default None / 0).

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session.rs: Session::query<T> / query_aliased<T> / query_aliased_auto<T> / declare_querier / declare_querier_aliased all gate on query-get; Session::declare_queryable / declare_queryable_aliased gate on query-queryable. Each carries an R307 doc-comment line recording the gate.

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session.rs: standalone items split by gate. QueryAliasError + Display + Error impls + Querier struct + impl + QuerierAliased struct + impl gate on query-get. QueryableOptions struct + impl + Queryable struct + impl + Drop + QueryableAliasError + Display + Error impls gate on query-queryable. MatchingStatus stays unconditional because it is also returned by Publisher::get_matching_status.

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/observer.rs: use crate::query::{QueryReply, QueryableRegistry} gates on query-queryable; use crate::reply::ReplyRegistry gates on query-reply. ApplicationLayerObserver::queryables field + pending_replies / pending_final_rids buffers gate on query-queryable; replies field gates on query-reply. ApplicationLayerObserver::new() / dispatch_event() / flush_pending() carry inline cfg gates on the relevant arms. pending_reply_count() / pending_final_count() accessors gate on query-queryable. Three tests gate accordingly: new_observer_starts_empty keeps unconditional shape with per-field cfg-gated assertions; dispatch_event_routes_event_into_all_consumer_registries_without_cross_talk gates on liveliness-token (the test exercises the liveliness arm); flush_pending_clears_queryable_staged_buffers gates on query-queryable.

Build-matrix verification вҖ” 11-path check matrix: (1) cargo check -p wz-runtime-tokio (default 8 features) (2) --no-default-features (3) --features query-get (4) --features query-queryable (5) --features query-reply (6) --features query-target (7) --features query-timeout (8) --features query-consolidation (9) --features liveliness-token (10) --features liveliness-subscriber (11) --all-features. Plus the wz facade with each of the 6 forwards (runtime-tokio + query-*) and preset-mcu-minimal / preset-ap-client / preset-mcu-extended вҖ” all 17 paths pass.

Net diff: +145 lines across 5 files (8 lines wz facade, 18 lines wz-runtime-tokio Cargo, 11 lines lib.rs, 47 lines session.rs use-stmt + cfg attribute additions, 36 lines observer.rs, 14 lines reply.rs). No body changes; all edits are cfg attribute additions + doc comment additions.

scripts/run-ci.sh --layer 0 (fmt + actionlint) pass. --layer A (validate-workspace) pass. --layer C1 (cargo test --workspace) pass on retry вҖ” first run hit the pre-existing keyexpr_canon proptest flake (random input occasionally tripping pico assert; not introduced by R307). --layer E pass on first run.

Layer E 30-trial flake measurement: R307 = 29/30 pass (1 fail: wz_liveliness_subscriber_round_trip at trial 19 вҖ” stderr interleaving race on the wz-ap-demo callback eprintln! vs env_logger writes). Baseline-comparison: identical 30-trial run after git stash of R307 changes recorded 28/30 pass (2 fails: wz_queryable_round_trip at trial 2 + wz_initiator_round_trip at trial 18). Different tests fail across baseline and R307 вҖ” confirms the e2e race is environmental wz-ap-demo test-harness flake, not introduced by R307. R307 source diff is purely additive cfg attributes; with default features the compiled binary is structurally identical to the pre-R307 build.

Atomic ledger: validate_workspace baseline (R306 close) = entries=305 / sections=215 / T1=0 / RT=13/13 / GENERATED.md=sync / T3 warn=862 / T4 info=429. This entry will be #306 after append; sections unchanged (no section mutation); GENERATED.md cascade re-render expected.
carry_forward_bullets: wz-ap-demo stderr-interleaving e2e flake (~5% per trial across both baseline and R307; 3 different tests observed flaking: wz_queryable_round_trip / wz_initiator_round_trip / wz_liveliness_subscriber_round_trip). Root cause is concurrent stderr writes from eprintln! (LIVELINESS SAMPLE log lines etc.) racing with env_logger output вҖ” both go to stderr but their stream-level locking is not coordinated. Mechanical fix: route the demo eprintln! lines through log::info! so env_logger handles serialization, OR wrap eprintln! calls in std::io::stderr().lock() for the duration of the line. Either approach is wz-ap-demo-side, R307-orthogonal. Estimated 1-2R.

pre-existing keyexpr_canon proptest flake вҖ” Layer C1 random input occasionally trips a zenoh-pico assert (signal 6 SIGABRT). The proptest filter strategy is supposed to exclude this pattern; reaching the assert means the strategy has a hole. Estimated 1R analysis to locate the filter gap + tighten the strategy.

R307 query-get вҶ’ query-queryable implication may be too restrictive long-term. A pure wire-only query-get (without query-queryable's loopback path) is a valid composition for a thin AP query client that has no in-process queryable. Refining this to remove the implication while keeping AP MVP tests green is a follow-up вҖ” touches Session::query's loopback if allows_local block with cfg(feature = query-queryable) and adjusts QueryOptions::expected_finals to only count remote when query-queryable is off. Estimated 1-2R.

R307 internal-type elision is incomplete by design: QueryTarget / ConsolidationMode types live in session_glue.rs and stay compiled unconditionally (their only public bare-name reference under the gates is via QueryOptions / Session::query, which are gated). A future round can move these types behind cfg(feature = query-target) / cfg(feature = query-consolidation) to push gating one level deeper. Low priority вҖ” internal symbols only, not user-API.

R308 next mechanical track: declare domain #[cfg(feature)] gates (R302d sequence). 7 active features (declare-final, declare-interest, declare-keyexpr, declare-queryable, declare-subscriber, declare-token, declare-undeclare). Blast radius likely larger than R307 query because declare touches every wire-side outbound Declare(*) builder + every inbound observer arm. Estimated 3-5R for a textbook split following the R307 pattern.

Round 308.decision_summary: R308 converts wz-ap-demo eprintln! вҶ’ log::info! across runner.rs + tasks.rs to unify stderr writes through env_logger lock, closing the 5% Layer E e2e flake measured at R307.
changes_bullets: crates/wz-ap-demo/src/runner.rs: 11 eprintln! sites converted to log::info!. Observer-callback closures (REMOTE SUBSCRIBER / QUERYABLE / TOKEN DECLARED + UNDECLARED, REPLY RECEIVED, FINAL RECEIVED, SUBSCRIBER FIRED, LIVELINESS SAMPLE, QUERYABLE FIRED) all route through env_logger's BufferWriter + io::stderr().lock() path. Pre-R308 the mixed eprintln! / log::info! writes serialised in theory (both acquire the same io::stderr() Mutex) but the empirical 30-trial measurement showed ~3-5% byte-interleave at the LIVELINESS SAMPLE callback fire site. Routing every callback line through env_logger collapses the writers onto a single Mutex<BufferWriter> + write_all(buf) discipline.

crates/wz-ap-demo/src/tasks.rs: 5 eprintln! sites converted. declare_task / publisher_task previously had a dup-write pattern on the error path (log::warn! + eprintln! emitting the same message) plus eprintln! on the success-emission lines (DECLARED SUBSCRIBER / QUERYABLE / TOKEN, QUERY EMITTED, PUBLISHER DECLARED, PUBLISHER EMITTED). R308 drops the duplicate eprintln! on error paths (log::warn! already covers them) and converts the success eprintln! to log::info!. Net: 5 eprintln! removed; 5 log::info! added (3 of those replace removed-eprintln on success paths; 0 net-add).

Integration test substring matching preserved by design вҖ” env_logger renders each record as `[<ts> <LEVEL> <module>] <message>` and `<message>` carries the pre-R308 line verbatim. Test fixtures (e.g. wz_liveliness_subscriber_round_trip.rs's wait_for_substring(`LIVELINESS SAMPLE PUT`)) match the substring inside the env_logger-prefixed line without modification.

The 5Вұ2% flake rate measured on the same suite across both baseline and R307 collapses to 0% under R308, confirming the eprintln!/env_logger byte-interleave was the load-bearing cause.

carry_forward_bullets: R308 didn't touch main.rs eprintln! sites вҖ” those run BEFORE env_logger::init() (lines ~142-310 are argv-validation paths that exit non-zero). They stay as eprintln! because no env_logger lock exists yet. Plus the ABOUT-line eprintln! at line 379 (immediately after env_logger::init()) stays because it's single-threaded (no race target). No follow-up needed.

Root-cause of the eprintln!/env_logger interleave under default Stderr mutex вҖ” both writers theoretically share `io::stderr().lock()`, but the empirical 5% interleave rate indicates the lock is NOT actually shared, OR the env_logger BufferWriter releases the lock between formatting + writing the record's bytes. R308 sidesteps the question by routing every wz-ap-demo write through env_logger, but the underlying std/env_logger interleave question is interesting enough to track. Low priority вҖ” 30/30 measurement says the practical fix works. 0.5R analysis follow-up if a future round has spare cycles.

changes_bullets: crates/wz-runtime-tokio/Cargo.toml [features]: adds 7 declare-* entries. Default extended 8 вҶ’ 15 to keep in-workspace consumers unchanged. liveliness-token = ['declare-token', 'declare-undeclare'] so LivelinessToken::Drop's send_undeclare_token stays valid. liveliness-subscriber = ['declare-interest', 'declare-final'] so the R280 send_interest_* path stays valid.

crates/wz/Cargo.toml [features]: 7 declare-* entries forwarded into wz-runtime-tokio via cargo ? syntax. declare-keyexpr keeps the pre-R309 keyexpr-mapping facade-internal implication; the other 6 are pure forwards.

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs: 11 wire-emit methods gated. send_declare_keyexpr on declare-keyexpr; send_declare_subscriber on declare-subscriber; send_declare_queryable on declare-queryable; send_declare_token on declare-token; send_declare_final on declare-final; send_interest_liveliness_subscriber + send_interest_final on declare-interest; send_undeclare_kexpr on all(declare-keyexpr, declare-undeclare); send_undeclare_subscriber on all(declare-subscriber, declare-undeclare); send_undeclare_queryable on all(declare-queryable, declare-undeclare); send_undeclare_token on all(declare-token, declare-undeclare).

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs: supporting helpers gated for -D warnings cleanliness. `use check_outbound_keyexpr_pico_safe` gates on any(declare-keyexpr, declare-subscriber, declare-queryable, declare-token). `fn reconstruct_outbound_keyexpr` gates on any(declare-subscriber, declare-queryable, declare-token, test) so the cfg(test) shape-table companion still compiles under cargo test --no-default-features.

R309 scope is wire-emit only. Application-layer gating (declare.rs sub-modules, observer.rs remote_* fields, Session::declare_subscriber on declare-subscriber) deferred to R310 to keep R309 contained. The wire-emit layer alone gives meaningful footprint reduction for embedded deploys that pin a static declare-set and never reach for runtime undeclare.

Round 40.decision_summary: Phase 2 walking skeleton lands вҖ” `crates/` Cargo workspace + `wz-codecs` crate that hosts sce-codegen Rust output for the В§6 payload trio (msg_put + msg_del + 5 deps). First piece of running code in the project вҖ” cargo build passes, smoke test exercises encode/decode round-trip on default instances. 38 rounds of spec authoring now have a Rust binary that compiles.
changes_bullets: crates/Cargo.toml new вҖ” wz Rust workspace root. resolver=2; members=[wz-codecs]. Workspace package shares license + edition + rust-version. [workspace.lints.rust] warnings = "deny" for production gate parity with SCE workspace.

crates/wz-codecs/ new (4 files): Cargo.toml + build.rs + src/lib.rs + tests/smoke.rs. Cargo.toml declares deps on sce-forge-runtime (path) and sce-build (build-dep, path) into vendor/sce. lib.rs declares 8 `pub mod <stem> { include!() }` blocks at crate root for codegen-output sibling resolution.

crates/wz-codecs/build.rs invokes sce-build::compile_forge_with_imports() in-process at compile time, mirroring vendor/sce/sce-forge-runtime/rust/build.rs pattern. 8 SCXMLs compiled in dep-order: timestamp/encoding/ext_unit/ext_zint/ext_zbuf/ext_entry/msg_put/msg_del. cargo:rerun-if-changed wired on sources/codecs/.

SCE codegen template bug workaround in build.rs post-process: SCE emits `#![doc = "SCE-MAP: stem:line"]` as a file-leading inner attribute. When wz includes the file inside `pub mod X { include!(...); }`, rust rejects (E0658 / inner-attribute-not-permitted at item position). SCE's own `cargo test -p sce-forge-runtime --features alloc` hits the same wall вҖ” verified R40. Workaround strips the redundant inner-attr line; the SCE-MAP info itself is also emitted on the very next line as a regular `// SCE-MAP: stem:line` comment, so zero info loss. Proper fix is an SCE upstream PR (R41 carry вҖ” see below).

tests/smoke.rs (66 lines, 2 tests): msg_put + msg_del DefaultвҶ’encodeвҶ’decodeвҶ’assert-fields-match round-trip. Establishes the encode/decode self-consistency baseline that Layer 3 (wire-interop against zenoh-pico bytes) will graduate from in R41/R42.

.gitignore extended: `crates/target/` ignored (workspace build output). `crates/Cargo.lock` COMMITTED per Cargo's binary-producing-workspace recommendation for reproducible Layer 3 builds across CI/dev/prod.
verification_bullets: cargo build -p wz-codecs: pass (Compiling wz-codecs v0.1.0, Finished 'dev' profile, 0 errors, 0 warnings).

cargo test -p wz-codecs: 2 passed / 0 failed / 0 ignored. Both msg_put + msg_del default encodeвҶ’decode round-trips assert all fields match (header byte, payload_len, payload bytes, present-if Optional gates all None).

sce-codegen output (in OUT_DIR): 8 .rs files (timestamp.rs, encoding.rs, ext_unit.rs, ext_zint.rs, ext_zbuf.rs, ext_entry.rs, msg_put.rs, msg_del.rs). All `use super::X::Y;` cross-module references resolve through wz_codecs::lib's `pub mod X { include!() }` declarations.
validate_workspace: T1 orphan=0, round-trip 12/12, GENERATED.md=sync, atomic ledger entries 39вҶ’40, T3 warn 856 unchanged.
First running Rust code in the project. Prior 38 rounds were spec authoring (12 atomic docs, 36 SCXML, vendored SCE, mnemosyne adoption); R40 is the FIRST round whose deliverable executes.
carry_forward_bullets: SCE upstream PR carry вҖ” `tools/codegen/templates/forge/rust/<kind>.rs.jinja2` change template to emit SCE-MAP info as `/// SCE-MAP: stem:line` (outer doc, attaches to first struct) or just `// SCE-MAP: stem:line` (regular comment) instead of `#![doc = ...]` (inner attribute). Inner attribute on a file consumed via `include!(...)` inside `pub mod X` is rejected by rust E0658 вҖ” broken in SCE's own `--features alloc --tests` build too, just not caught because default build doesn't trigger include!(). Single-line template change; would let wz-codecs/build.rs drop the post-process strip.

R41 = `crates/zenoh-pico-sys` вҖ” vendor zenoh-pico as git submodule (sibling to vendor/sce/), build via cc/cmake, generate FFI bindings via bindgen, expose `_z_put_encode` + `_z_keep_alive_encode` + `_z_init_encode` + sibling `_*_decode` Rust signatures. This is the foundation for Layer 3 wire-interop tests.

R42 = `crates/wz-integration-tests` вҖ” Layer 3 test crate. For each codec in wz-codecs, construct a logical message instance, encode via wz-codecs Rust path AND via zenoh-pico-sys FFI path, byte-compare. First REAL validation that the codec SCXMLs match zenoh-pico wire-format. This is the round that PROVES the 36 SCXML rounds correctly modeled the wire protocol; if it fails, the diagnosis loop is short because each codec is independently testable.
R43+ = expand wz-codecs to host all 21 codec SCXMLs (currently only 8 of 21 wired). Layer 3 coverage grows in lockstep.
R45+ (post-Phase 2) = runtime crates wz-runtime-tokio + wz-runtime-lwip per the design docs. Session FSM (R29 statechart) wiring becomes possible once runtime trait surface lands.

Production gate carry: GitHub Actions CI (validate_workspace + verify-codegen.sh + cargo test + Layer 3 interop) lands in Phase 4 (R46+ after walking skeleton + a few codec/FSM expansion rounds), per the R39 architectural roadmap.

Round 41.decision_summary: Phase 2 walking-skeleton FFI bridge lands вҖ” `vendor/zenoh-pico` submodule + `crates/zenoh-pico-sys` cmake+bindgen crate. First FFI call from wz Rust into zenoh-pico C library succeeds (4/4 smoke tests on `_z_id_len`). Layer 3 wire-interop foundation in place; R42 can now construct byte-equivalence assertions between wz-emit and zenoh-pico-emit for the same logical message.
changes_bullets: vendor/zenoh-pico submodule added (eclipse-zenoh/zenoh-pico) pinned at 3b3ab65c (1.9.0+10 commits). Matches the R5 zenoh-pico reference state used throughout R29+ SCXML authoring evidence chain. .gitmodules updated.

crates/zenoh-pico-sys/ new crate (Cargo.toml + build.rs + src/lib.rs + tests/ffi_smoke.rs). Build-deps: cmake 0.1 + bindgen 0.71. Crate-level `links = "zenohpico"` declares the static library to cargo's link DAG.

build.rs two-stage: (1) cmake::Config invokes vendor/zenoh-pico/CMakeLists.txt with BUILD_SHARED_LIBS=OFF + minimum-features flag set, produces libzenohpico.a under OUT_DIR/lib/; (2) bindgen parses include/zenoh-pico.h with allowlisted symbols only (`_z_id_t` + `_z_id_len` for R41 smoke). Linux/macOS platform macro (`ZENOH_LINUX` / `ZENOH_MACOS`) derived from cargo TARGET env var вҖ” zenoh-pico's platform.h errors out on missing macro at clang parse time.

src/lib.rs `include!(concat!(env!("OUT_DIR"), "/bindings.rs"))` exposes the FFI surface with the bindgen-required `#![allow(non_*case*)]` set. Documented as raw FFI вҖ” NOT a safe wrapper. Layer 3 test harness (R42+) is the intended consumer.

tests/ffi_smoke.rs (4 tests): _z_id_len exercised across all four `_z_id_t.id[16]` topologies (all-zero / partial-trailing-zeros / all-non-zero / mid-buffer-trailing-nonzero). Each asserts the C function's strip-trailing-zeros loop behavior matches expected length, proving the FFI bridge transfers struct-by-value correctly across the C ABI boundary.
crates/Cargo.toml workspace members extended to include zenoh-pico-sys.
verification_bullets: cargo build -p zenoh-pico-sys: pass. CMake configures + builds + installs libzenohpico.a (with all upstream platform support layers compiled), bindgen emits bindings.rs into OUT_DIR, rustc links the static lib into the test binary. ~30s cold-build, sub-second warm.
cargo test -p zenoh-pico-sys: 4/4 passed / 0 failed. All four _z_id_len cases (lengths 0/5/16/10) match the expected output of zenoh-pico's strip-trailing-zeros loop at src/protocol/core.c:35-45.
validate_workspace: T1 orphan=0, round-trip 12/12, GENERATED.md=sync, atomic ledger entries 40вҶ’41, T3 warn 856 unchanged.

FFI integrity proven empirically: (a) bindgen-emitted `_z_id_t` struct layout matches C's `struct { uint8_t id[16] }` (4 different non-zero patterns transfer correctly); (b) static linker resolves _z_id_len from libzenohpico.a; (c) C function called by Rust returns correct uint8_t values across the FFI boundary.
carry_forward_bullets: R42 = `crates/wz-integration-tests` Layer 3 wire-interop tests. Expand zenoh-pico-sys allowlist to include `_z_keep_alive_encode` (simplest body, single MID + 0 body bytes вҖ” matches the keep_alive.scxml codec wz emit). Then expand to msg_put / msg_del / scout / hello / etc. Each new codec verified = one new allowlist entry + one new test. Per-codec round-trip: (a) construct a known logical message, (b) encode via wz_codecs::X::encode(), (c) encode via zenoh-pico's _z_*_encode FFI, (d) byte-compare. If they differ, the wz codec SCXML's spec mapping has a bug вҖ” first REAL spec validation.

SCE upstream PR carry (R40) still pending: codegen template `#![doc]` inner-attribute fix in `tools/codegen/templates/forge/rust/<kind>.rs.jinja2`. Drop-in fix when SCE ships it; until then wz-codecs/build.rs's strip workaround stays.

R44+ = `sce-codegen build deploy/ap_standalone.yaml` end-to-end exercise. Validates the deploy-driven build pipeline produces a coherent `out/ap/` Cargo crate consumable by AP runtimes.

R45+ = `crates/wz-runtime-tokio` (sce_link_runtime_tokio impl per docs/runtime-crate-tokio.md). Session FSM script-placeholder wiring round (R29 carry) becomes viable once the runtime trait surface lands.

Phase 4 CI (R46+): GitHub Actions running validate_workspace + verify-codegen.sh + cargo test (all crates) + Layer 3 interop. The Layer 3 lane is what makes the CI gate meaningful вҖ” prior to R42, CI would only catch codegen regressions, not wire-format divergence.

First FFI call from wz Rust into zenoh-pico C library succeeds (4/4 smoke tests on `_z_id_len`). Layer 3 wire-interop foundation in place; R42 can now construct byte-equivalence assertions between wz-emit and zenoh-pico-emit for the same logical message.
publishable_changes_bullets: vendor/zenoh-pico submodule added (eclipse-zenoh/zenoh-pico) pinned at 3b3ab65c (1.9.0+10 commits). Matches the R5 zenoh-pico reference state used throughout R29+ SCXML authoring evidence chain. .gitmodules updated.

crates/zenoh-pico-sys/ new crate (Cargo.toml + build.rs + src/lib.rs + tests/ffi_smoke.rs). Build-deps: cmake 0.1 + bindgen 0.71. Crate-level `links = "zenohpico"` declares the static library to cargo's link DAG.

build.rs two-stage: (1) cmake::Config invokes vendor/zenoh-pico/CMakeLists.txt with BUILD_SHARED_LIBS=OFF + minimum-features flag set, produces libzenohpico.a under OUT_DIR/lib/; (2) bindgen parses include/zenoh-pico.h with allowlisted symbols only (`_z_id_t` + `_z_id_len` for R41 smoke). Linux/macOS platform macro (`ZENOH_LINUX` / `ZENOH_MACOS`) derived from cargo TARGET env var вҖ” zenoh-pico's platform.h errors out on missing macro at clang parse time.

src/lib.rs `include!(concat!(env!("OUT_DIR"), "/bindings.rs"))` exposes the FFI surface with the bindgen-required `#![allow(non_*case*)]` set. Documented as raw FFI вҖ” NOT a safe wrapper. Layer 3 test harness (R42+) is the intended consumer.

tests/ffi_smoke.rs (4 tests): _z_id_len exercised across all four `_z_id_t.id[16]` topologies (all-zero / partial-trailing-zeros / all-non-zero / mid-buffer-trailing-nonzero). Each asserts the C function's strip-trailing-zeros loop behavior matches expected length, proving the FFI bridge transfers struct-by-value correctly across the C ABI boundary.
crates/Cargo.toml workspace members extended to include zenoh-pico-sys.
publishable_verification_bullets: cargo build -p zenoh-pico-sys: pass. CMake configures + builds + installs libzenohpico.a (with all upstream platform support layers compiled), bindgen emits bindings.rs into OUT_DIR, rustc links the static lib into the test binary. ~30s cold-build, sub-second warm.
cargo test -p zenoh-pico-sys: 4/4 passed / 0 failed. All four _z_id_len cases (lengths 0/5/16/10) match the expected output of zenoh-pico's strip-trailing-zeros loop at src/protocol/core.c:35-45.
validate_workspace: T1 orphan=0, round-trip 12/12, GENERATED.md=sync, atomic ledger entries 40вҶ’41, T3 warn 856 unchanged.

FFI integrity proven empirically: (a) bindgen-emitted `_z_id_t` struct layout matches C's `struct { uint8_t id[16] }` (4 different non-zero patterns transfer correctly); (b) static linker resolves _z_id_len from libzenohpico.a; (c) C function called by Rust returns correct uint8_t values across the FFI boundary.
publishable_carry_forward_bullets: R42 = `crates/wz-integration-tests` Layer 3 wire-interop tests. Expand zenoh-pico-sys allowlist to include `_z_keep_alive_encode` (simplest body, single MID + 0 body bytes вҖ” matches the keep_alive.scxml codec wz emit). Then expand to msg_put / msg_del / scout / hello / etc. Each new codec verified = one new allowlist entry + one new test. Per-codec round-trip: (a) construct a known logical message, (b) encode via wz_codecs::X::encode(), (c) encode via zenoh-pico's _z_*_encode FFI, (d) byte-compare. If they differ, the wz codec SCXML's spec mapping has a bug вҖ” first REAL spec validation.

SCE upstream PR carry (R40) still pending: codegen template `#![doc]` inner-attribute fix in `tools/codegen/templates/forge/rust/<kind>.rs.jinja2`. Drop-in fix when SCE ships it; until then wz-codecs/build.rs's strip workaround stays.
R43+ = expand wz-codecs to wire all 21 codec SCXMLs (currently 8 of 21). Each new codec lands a paired Layer 3 test in wz-integration-tests.
R44+ = `sce-codegen build deploy/ap_standalone.yaml` end-to-end exercise. Validates the deploy-driven build pipeline produces a coherent `out/ap/` Cargo crate consumable by AP runtimes.

R45+ = `crates/wz-runtime-tokio` (sce_link_runtime_tokio impl per docs/runtime-crate-tokio.md). Session FSM script-placeholder wiring round (R29 carry) becomes viable once the runtime trait surface lands.

Phase 4 CI (R46+): GitHub Actions running validate_workspace + verify-codegen.sh + cargo test (all crates) + Layer 3 interop. The Layer 3 lane is what makes the CI gate meaningful вҖ” prior to R42, CI would only catch codegen regressions, not wire-format divergence.

Round 42.decision_summary: Layer 3 wire-interop verification lands. First objective evidence in 42 rounds that a watching-zenoh codec SCXML is byte-equivalent to zenoh-pico 1.9.0's wire encoding: close codec emits identical bytes to _z_close_encode across 10 reason-byte test cases. The spec is no longer just an internally-consistent artifact вҖ” it is wire-format-validated against the canonical reference implementation for the first codec.

zenoh-pico-sys/build.rs allowlist expanded with _z_t_msg_close_t + _z_wbuf_t + _z_zbuf_t + _z_close_encode + _z_wbuf_make + _z_wbuf_len + _z_wbuf_clear + _z_wbuf_to_zbuf + _z_zbuf_clear. Each entry justified by the close Layer 3 test.

Round 43.decision_summary: Layer 3 expansion вҖ” fragment + frame codecs validated against zenoh-pico 1.9.0 wire format across VLE width boundaries (1/2/3+ byte forms). VLE u64 primitive byte-correctness now empirically verified; downstream codecs that consume VLE (sn, lease, batch_size, payload_len, ~20 others) inherit confidence transitively. Cross-codec frameвҶ”fragment body-equivalence pinned.
changes_bullets: zenoh-pico-sys/build.rs allowlist gains: _z_t_msg_frame_t + _z_t_msg_fragment_t + _z_slice_t + _z_delete_context_t + _z_frame_encode + _z_fragment_encode + _z_slice_as_zbuf. All justified by R43 Layer 3 tests.

tests/layer3_fragment.rs (130 lines, 2 tests): VLE width boundary corpus (sn=0/1/127/128/256/16383/16384/1M/u32::MAX) Г— payload variants (empty / 1-byte / multi-byte). Non-owning _z_slice_t wrap of Rust &[u8]; empty payload skipped via NULL start pointer + _z_slice_check short-circuit.

tests/layer3_frame.rs (130 lines, 2 tests): same VLE corpus via the frame path. Construction shape differs from fragment вҖ” _z_t_msg_frame_t._payload is *mut _z_zbuf_t (NOT _z_slice_t by value); zbuf built via _z_slice_as_zbuf wrapping a Rust slice, stored in MaybeUninit to avoid unused_assignments lint on the empty-payload null branch. Empty payload: _payload=null short-circuit per upstream code.

Cross-codec frameвҶ”fragment body-equivalence: when header=0 (no Z flag, no first/drop extensions), frame and fragment produce identical body bytes for the same (sn, payload) input. The transport-layer MID distinction (T_FRAME 0x05 vs T_FRAGMENT 0x06) is written by the envelope encoder, not the body codecs.
verification_bullets: cargo test --workspace: 12/12 pass / 0 fail. Breakdown by test target: wz-codecs smoke 2/2 (R40), zenoh-pico-sys ffi_smoke 4/4 (R41), wz-integration-tests layer3_close 2/2 (R42), layer3_fragment 2/2 (R43 NEW), layer3_frame 2/2 (R43 NEW).

VLE u64 primitive validated across width boundaries: 1-byte VLE (sn=0/1/127), 2-byte VLE (sn=128/256/16383), 3-byte VLE (sn=16384/1M), 4+-byte VLE (sn=u32::MAX). Both encoders produce identical byte sequences at every boundary вҖ” the most pervasive codec primitive (used by ~20 wire fields across the catalog) has now passed objective Layer 3 verification.

Cross-codec invariant pinned: wz_codecs::frame::Frame { sn, payload }.encode() == wz_codecs::fragment::Fragment { sn, payload }.encode() when extensions are off (header=0). Caught at test time if the codec SCXMLs ever diverge on the shared В§4.2 body shape.

Round 44.decision_summary: Layer 3 handshake codec expansion вҖ” scout / open_body / init_body / join validated. **Layer 3 caught the first real spec drift**: SCE codec_zenoh_init_body + codec_zenoh_join wrongly inherited `sce:default-endian="big"` while zenoh-pico's `_z_uint16_encode` is LE (LSB-first). wz init_body + join SCXMLs corrected to `default-endian="little"`; SCE fixture upstream PR carried for matching fix. Production-grade validation now empirically catching incorrect spec assumptions.

zenoh-pico-sys/wrapper.h gains forward declarations for _z_scout_encode + _z_hello_encode (file-private in zenoh-pico's message.c, no header decl upstream). Symbols already in libzenohpico.a; declaration only changes bindgen + linker visibility.

tests/layer3_open_body.rs (110 lines, 3 tests) вҖ” parent.A NEGATION gate (B5-О»). Cookie present iff parent.A=0; VLE width boundary corpus for lease + initial_sn.
tests/layer3_init_body.rs (190 lines, 4 tests) вҖ” parent.S + parent.A positive gates. **CAUGHT SPEC DRIFT**: wz BE batch_size diverged from zenoh-pico LE. Fix applied + audit-traced.
tests/layer3_join.rs (185 lines, 3 tests) вҖ” parent.S gate + multi-VLE next_sn chain (3 VLE u64 fields). Same LE batch_size fix as init_body.

Diagnosed via vendor/zenoh-pico/src/protocol/codec.c:82-85 (_z_uint16_encode writes LSB first). Fixed via wz SCXML root sce:default-endian change. Same bug latent in codec_zenoh_join (paired R44 PR).

Total Layer 2: 120/132 match (down from 132/132 by exactly the 12 init_body+join cells; not a regression, audit-traced divergence).

changes_bullets: scripts/verify-codegen.sh: header comment Layer 3 section rewritten (no longer NOT-YET-IMPLEMENTED). Implementation block at script tail invokes `cargo test -p wz-integration-tests --test layer3_<stem>` for the input SCXML's stem. Three result modes: pass (test exists + passes), FAIL (test exists + fails вҖ” script exits 1), skip (no test file).

Verification stack now end-to-end automated for any wz codec: one `bash scripts/verify-codegen.sh <wz.scxml> <sce.scxml>` invocation exercises Layer 1 (6-backend emit) + Layer 2 (body-golden vs SCE fixture) + Layer 3 (byte-equiv vs zenoh-pico FFI). Single-command production gate.

R47b carry list of codecs without Layer 3 tests is now visible as `layer3=skip` lines.
publishable_changes_bullets: scripts/verify-codegen.sh: header comment Layer 3 section rewritten (no longer NOT-YET-IMPLEMENTED). Implementation block at script tail invokes `cargo test -p wz-integration-tests --test layer3_<stem>` for the input SCXML's stem. Three result modes: pass (test exists + passes), FAIL (test exists + fails вҖ” script exits 1), skip (no test file).

Verification stack now end-to-end automated for any wz codec: one `bash scripts/verify-codegen.sh <wz.scxml> <sce.scxml>` invocation exercises Layer 1 (6-backend emit) + Layer 2 (body-golden vs SCE fixture) + Layer 3 (byte-equiv vs zenoh-pico FFI). Single-command production gate.

Empirical validation: close.scxml verify reports `Layer 1: 6/6 / Layer 2: golden match=6 / Layer 3: pass (tests=2)`. timestamp.scxml (no Layer 3 test yet) reports `Layer 3: skip (no tests/layer3_timestamp.rs)` вҖ” the skip signal makes the R47b carry list mechanically observable.

keep_alive.scxml: Layer 1 6/6 + Layer 3 1/1 pass (no upstream pair for Layer 2 here вҖ” keep_alive has no SCE codec_zenoh_keep_alive counterpart in the R47 set).
timestamp.scxml: skip path verified вҖ” `layer3=skip (no tests/layer3_timestamp.rs)` correctly identifies the missing test. R47b carry visibility achieved.

changes_bullets: .github/workflows/ci.yml new (~150 lines). Single ubuntu-22.04 runner job with: checkout (submodules: recursive) + apt cmake/libclang/clang/perl + rust toolchain (stable, with rustfmt+clippy) + cache (Swatinem/rust-cache v2 for crates + vendor/sce) + mnemosyne-cli install (graceful skip on failure) + build-sce.sh + mnemosyne validate-workspace + per-codec verify-codegen.sh sweep with audit-traced LE-divergence allowlist for init_body+join + cargo test --workspace + cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings.

wz-codecs/src/lib.rs gains crate-level `#![allow(clippy::all)]` вҖ” the entire crate body is sce-codegen include!()'d output, so clippy style lints are SCE template authoring concerns not consumer-tunable. Workspace rustc `warnings = "deny"` policy stays in force (rustc warnings ARE consumer-actionable).
zenoh-pico-sys/src/lib.rs gains `#![allow(clippy::all)]` for the same reason вҖ” entire crate is bindgen FFI output.

wz-integration-tests test files cleaned: `manual_range_contains` (3 places) вҖ” `zid_len >= 1 && zid_len <= 16` вҶ’ `(1..=16).contains(&zid_len)`; `unnecessary identity map` (msg_put: `(0..255u8).map(|i| i).collect()` вҶ’ `.collect()`); doc list indentation in fragment + init_body. All clippy lints in human-authored code resolved at source, not muted.

Round 51.decision_summary: Phase Z entry вҖ” FIRST OBSERVABLE COMMUNICATION milestone reached. wz-runtime-tokio crate lands with LinkDriver trait + TcpDriver impl + echo_demo integration test. Two tokio tasks on a single process exchange a real wz-codecs-encoded MsgPut body over TCP loopback; subscriber decodes back to a structurally-equivalent MsgPut. The R28 user question "when will we see communication?" is concretely answered at R51.
changes_bullets: crates/wz-runtime-tokio/ new crate (3 files): Cargo.toml + src/lib.rs + tests/echo_demo.rs. Workspace member added.

src/lib.rs: LinkDriver trait (4 methods open/send/close/poll_event matching docs/runtime-crate-tokio.md В§2.1 baseline) + TxFrame/RxFrame/LinkEvent/Reliability enums + TcpDriver impl (4-byte BE length-prefixed framing over TcpStream).

tests/echo_demo.rs: publisher + subscriber tokio tasks on loopback TCP. Publisher: MsgPut header=0x01 + payload=[0xCA,0xFE,0xBA,0xBE,0x42] encoded via wz-codecs, sent via TcpDriver. Subscriber: accept + poll_event Rx + decode via wz-codecs::msg_put::MsgPut::decode + assert equality. PASS.

Workspace: 33/33 tests pass + clippy --deny warnings clean across all 4 crates.
verification_bullets: R28 question answered: cargo test -p wz-runtime-tokio --test echo_demo passes. MsgPut wire bytes flow through TCP loopback and decode back successfully.
cargo test --workspace: 33/33 pass / 0 fail. +1 echo_demo vs R47.
cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings: clean.

What this proves: encodeвҶ”decode self-consistency + Layer 3 zenoh-pico byte-equivalence are not just in-memory properties вҖ” they survive a real TCP socket boundary. The bytes Layer 3-validated against zenoh-pico are the SAME bytes flowing on the wire.
What this does NOT yet prove: zenoh-pico endpoint interop (needs session-FSM handshake вҖ” R54); multi-message + reliability + extensions; framing-error paths.

Round 52.decision_summary: Echo demo expansion вҖ” UdpDriver impl added + msg_del/TCP + msg_put/UDP round-trip tests. Phase Z reaches 35 workspace tests. Both push-body codec variants (msg_put + msg_del) now demonstrably survive a real network boundary across BOTH transport classes (TCP + UDP). Reliability::BestEffort hint wired (UDP semantic).
changes_bullets: wz-runtime-tokio/src/lib.rs: UdpDriver impl added. from_socket(socket, peer) constructor + open/send/close/poll_event implementations. Honors Reliability::BestEffort silently (UDP is inherently best-effort); Reliability::Reliable hint is forwarded but UDP can't enforce вҖ” the session FSM's responsibility per docs/runtime-crate-tokio.md В§6.

tests/echo_demo_del_and_udp.rs (130 lines, 2 tests). echo_msg_del_tcp_round_trip: MsgDel header=0x02 + no flags + no payload across TCP loopback. echo_msg_put_udp_round_trip: MsgPut header=0x01 + 3-byte payload across UDP datagram вҖ” datagram preserves message boundary so no 4-byte length prefix needed.
Both codec variants of the В§6.1 PushBody family validated end-to-end through both link transport classes the project supports for AP linux.
verification_bullets: cargo test --workspace: 35/35 pass / 0 fail. R51 33 + R52 2 new tests = 35.
cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings: clean.
validate_workspace: T1 orphan=0, round-trip 12/12, GENERATED.md=sync, atomic ledger entries 51вҶ’52, T3 warn 856 unchanged.

Phase Z scope at end of R52: LinkDriver trait + TcpDriver + UdpDriver + msg_put/msg_del round-trip tests across both transports. Remaining Phase Z: R53 (wz-runtime-lwip C11 sibling) + R54 (session_fsm R29 placeholder wiring).
carry_forward_bullets: R53 (Phase Z) = crates/wz-runtime-lwip C11 sibling per docs/runtime-crate-lwip.md. Opaque sce_link_t handle + 6-event sce_link_event_t enum + 3 outbound functions (send_init_syn / send_payload / send_close). FFI-bridged from generated C11 codec code.

R54 (Phase Z closure) = R29 session_fsm_unicast.scxml script-action placeholder wiring. Bind link_driver_open() / send_init_syn() / etc. to actual wz-runtime-tokio LinkDriver calls. After R54 the FSM drives encoded handshake bytes вҖ” zenoh-pico endpoint interop becomes possible.

R52b (deferred) = 2-PROCESS echo (separate src/bin/publisher.rs + src/bin/subscriber.rs). Validates that the codec stack survives a true process boundary (no shared address space, only the kernel TCP/UDP socket). Lower priority than R53/R54 since the current tokio-task-on-loopback already crosses the kernel socket boundary.
After Phase Z: Phase W (MCU hardening: no_std + alloc cfg-flag + fuzz harness + clippy --all-features) + Phase V (user-facing README + rustdoc + quickstart).

Round 53.decision_summary: SCE vendor pin 11f1032dвҶ’2ff9d7a9 + wz-runtime-lwip C11 sibling vertical slice вҖ” first round consuming B6 link-kind C11 emitter end-to-end.
changes_bullets: vendor/sce submodule bumped 11f1032d вҶ’ 2ff9d7a9 (13 commits); c56161ea resolves R52.1+ NEW issues (workspace-root flag + 4-layer resolution chain, --version, stale 'C11 emitter lands in M2+' help text strip).
Verified post-bump: sce-codegen --version returns 0.1.0; CLI emit embeds real template-hash 9faef237... (zero regression closed for CLI path).
crates/wz-runtime-lwip/ created (Cargo.toml + build.rs + src/sce_link_runtime_lwip.c + src/wz_runtime_lwip.h + src/lib.rs); workspace members 4 вҶ’ 5.

sources/links/lwip_udp_scout.scxml authored (sce:kind='link', link-class=udp, framer=frame, backpressure=drop) вҖ” first link-kind SCXML in the workspace, mapped to SCE B6-Оұ self-test fixture shape (vendor/sce/sce-build/src/lib.rs:5524).

build.rs invokes sce-build's compile_forge_with_imports(Language::C11) in-process; emitted lwip_udp_scout.h composed with cc-compiled sce_link_runtime_lwip.c (vtable skeleton вҖ” host-build NOP stubs counting rx/tx/poll calls).

Discarded an earlier hand-authored sce_link_runtime.h drafted against docs/runtime-crate-lwip.md В§2 spec when the user flagged that the B6 emitter was already live; reframed R53 to consume the canonical emit instead of hand-locking a divergent contract.
doc В§6 atomic intent updated via set_section_intent to retire the 'design-only until C11 emitter exists' stale claim and record the emitter-live + skeleton-landed state.
verification_bullets: cargo build --workspace clean; cargo test --workspace 39/39 pass (35 baseline + 4 new wz-runtime-lwip ABI smoke tests).

Smoke tests (crates/wz-runtime-lwip/src/lib.rs::tests): LinkStatus repr size = 4; LinkOps vtable size = 3 pointers; factory_handle_round_trips_vtable_calls exercises rx/tx/poll through the FFI surface and verifies state counter side effects; tx_default_status round-trips ErrBackpressure variant.
cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings clean.
validate_workspace post-mutations: T1=0, RT 12/12, GENERATED.md=sync, atomic ledger entries 53 вҶ’ 54, sections 214 unchanged.

sce-codegen --language c11 against sources/links/lwip_udp_scout.scxml emits include/lwip_udp_scout.h with sce_forge_link_t wrapper struct + 4 inline functions + LINK_CLASS/FRAMER_REF/BACKPRESSURE macros; #include 'sce/forge/link.h' resolves against vendor/sce/sce-forge-runtime/c/include/.
carry_forward_bullets: R54 = R29 session_fsm script-action placeholder wiring to wz-runtime-tokio LinkDriver (23-round stale carry; final Phase Z closure).
R55+ = lwip_tcp + serial_uart + raw_eth sibling SCXMLs in sources/links/; concrete lwIP API wiring inside src/sce_link_runtime_lwip.c (currently NOP stubs); MCU cross-compile target plumbing.

R53b = docs/runtime-crate-lwip.md В§2 spec divergence вҖ” hand-authored 6-event enum + 3-outbound function contract differs from B6 emitter's 3-function vtable shape. Decide whether В§2 prose is rewritten to match the emitter or retained as a higher-level session-layer contract above the B6 driver vtable.

R40 carry reframe вҖ” SCE-side fix is not the path: sce_map_marker.jinja2 emits BOTH `#![doc=SCE-MAP]` and `// SCE-MAP` per RFC В§5.O spec lines 3135-3136 'MUST emit BOTH' obligation. The include!() consumption pattern in wz-codecs is the incompatibility surface; options are (a) keep build.rs strip workaround, (b) switch to per-codec sub-crates, (c) SCE PR adding `--no-inner-attr` carve-out.

R44 carry still open вҖ” vendor/sce/tests/forge/resources/codec_zenoh_init_body.scxml + codec_zenoh_join.scxml retain sce:default-endian='big'; no upstream change between 11f1032d and 2ff9d7a9 touching those files.
R50 carry still open вҖ” sce-codegen lacks a `build <deploy.yaml>` subcommand; the new `orchestrate` subcommand is a weaker multi-doc form that does not consume deploy.yaml.

In-process API anchor gap вҖ” sce-build's compile_forge_with_imports omits the SCE-GENERATED + source-hash + template-hash + generated-at 4-line block that the CLI binary emits, so wz build.rs outputs (wz-codecs, wz-runtime-lwip) lack RFC В§5.O traceability anchors entirely. Separate from the CLI workspace-root fix; needs an in-process anchor-emit pass.
53 + R53 commits unpushed; pre-push hook re-runs validate_workspace + commit-msg formatter at push time. Push remains explicit-consent only.

Round 54.decision_summary: R29 session_fsm.scxml script-action placeholders wired to wz-runtime-tokio LinkDriver via sce-rust-lua + register_global_function вҖ” 23-round stale carry resolved.
changes_bullets: wz-runtime-tokio/Cargo.toml: sce-rust-runtime + sce-rust-lua + log + sce-build (build-dep) added; SCE statechart-kind codegen + Lua script engine pulled into the workspace's dep graph.

wz-runtime-tokio/build.rs: shells out to `vendor/sce/target/release/sce-codegen generate --language rust` with `--workspace-root` plus a post-emit strip pass that removes both `#![...]` attr-style inner attributes and `//!` inner doc comments (extends the R40 carry strip to statechart emit; lib.rs's wrapping module restores the lint allows as OUTER attributes).
wz-runtime-tokio/src/lib.rs: `pub mod session_fsm_unicast` exposes the generated emit; `pub mod session_glue` carries the new SessionLinkActions + 17-fn install surface.

wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs (~300 lines): SessionLinkActions struct owns BoxedLinkDriver + ActionTrace; install_session_actions registers 17 native Rust closures with the process-wide Lua engine (sce_rust_lua::lua_engine_singleton + register_global_function) вҖ” 7 outbound link calls (link_driver_open/send_init_syn/send_open_syn/send_init_ack_with_cookie/send_open_ack/send_close_frame_with_reason/release_link), 6 state-internal actions (enable_rx_tx_regions, start/stop_lease_monitor, start/stop_keepalive_worker, free_pool_slots), 4 close-reason discriminator setters, and 3 boolean guards (half_open_cap_available/accept_rate_token/cookie_valid).

TokioLinkDriverAdapter<D: LinkDriver> implements BoxedLinkDriver by holding a tokio::runtime::Handle and `block_on`-ing the async LinkDriver methods inside the sync NativeMethod closure shape (Lua's register_global_function takes a `Fn(&[ScriptValue]) -> ScriptValue + Send + Sync`).

wz-runtime-tokio/tests/session_glue_dispatch.rs (~140 lines): single integration test walking the full happy-path + listener + close-reason script-action sequence through `dispatch_script`, asserting both the trace counters on SessionLinkActions and the RecordingDriver-side open/close/sends list. Single test on purpose вҖ” the Lua engine singleton + register_global_function are process-global, so cargo test's thread-parallel runner races concurrent installs.
verification_bullets: cargo build --workspace clean; cargo test --workspace 39 вҶ’ 40 (one new R54 integration test).
cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings clean (one doc_lazy_continuation cleanup in lib.rs module-level doc).
validate_workspace post-mutation: T1=0, RT 12/12, GENERATED.md=sync, atomic ledger entries 54 вҶ’ 55 (after this Round 54 append), sections 214 unchanged.

Recording driver verified 1 open + 1 close + 5 outbound sends (INIT_SYN / OPEN_SYN / CLOSE / INIT_ACK_COOKIE / OPEN_ACK), each marked Reliability::Reliable, in source-order matching the SCXML's onentry script ordering across the initiator + listener handshake paths.
carry_forward_bullets: R55 = codec-encoded wire bytes for the 7 outbound link calls. Current placeholders (b'INIT_SYN', b'OPEN_SYN', etc.) sit where the proper init_body/open_body/close codec emit + transport-message header + cookie payload bytes will land. Splits cleanly from R54 because the wire-format choices need session-layer transport-header decisions that belong in their own round.

R55b = wire the Engine-driving path. R54 verified the script-action dispatch in isolation (`dispatch_script(name)` direct call); the next step is to construct `Engine::new(SessionFsmUnicastPolicy::new())`, `raise_external_by_name("outbound.start", "")`, `step()` quiescence, and assert the Engine's emitted execute_script sequence matches the action-ordering R54 pinned by hand.

Lua-engine process-globality is a production concern for multi-peer FSM instances on one process. Two FSM instances would race on `register_global_function` name space (link_driver_open() means whose LinkDriver). Either (a) push per-session bindings via `bind_native_object(session_id, ...)` and use session-scoped function tables, or (b) accept the single-FSM-per-process constraint and document it. Decide before multi-peer integration tests land.

R29 SCXML script-name single-source-of-truth audit. R54 wired 17 native fns by string name match; if `session_fsm_unicast.scxml` adds a new <script>foo()</script> without a matching install_session_actions registration, the Lua engine silently returns Nil + the SCE FSM continues. Consider a SCE codegen pass that emits the action-name set as a const `&[&str]` and a build-time check that `register_global_function` covers every name in that set.

R40 carry strip widened to `#![...]` + `//!` lines вҖ” the R40 SCE PR option (`--no-inner-attr` carve-out) would now also need to suppress module-level `//!` doc; rethink the SCE PR shape or accept the wz-side strip as the durable answer.
53 + R53 + R54 commits unpushed; pre-push hook re-runs validate_workspace at push time. Push remains explicit-consent only.

Round 57.decision_summary: Wire-format reality + production safety guards вҖ” placeholder bytes swapped to real codec encoding; single-install + multi-thread + script-name SSoT guards land in one pass.
changes_bullets: wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs (~280 lines rewrite): SessionInitParams struct (version/whatami/zid/seq_num_res/req_id_res/batch_size/lease/lease_in_seconds/initial_sn/cookie); encode_init / encode_open / encode_close compose wz-codecs::{init_body,open_body,close}::encode (Layer 3 byte-equiv verified vs zenoh-pico _z_*_encode) + transport-message header byte (T_MID + parent flags from vendor/zenoh-pico/include/zenoh-pico/protocol/definitions/transport.h).

Placeholder bytes b"INIT_SYN" / b"OPEN_SYN" / b"CLOSE" / b"INIT_ACK_COOKIE" / b"OPEN_ACK" replaced with real codec output. CloseReason discriminator now flows from the trace into encode_close (reason byte sourced from the session.close transition that set the reason).

INSTALLED: OnceLock<Arc<SessionLinkActions>> single-install guard; install_session_actions returns Err(SessionActionsAlreadyInstalled) on the second call per process (closes R54+R55b carry on multi-FSM Lua global namespace race).
TokioLinkDriverAdapter::new asserts runtime_flavor == MultiThread at construction site (closes the R54 carry on block_on deadlock risk; converts a silent runtime hazard into a fail-fast panic).

wz-runtime-tokio/build.rs (audit_script_names + find_script_names + find_cond_names): build-time audit confirms every <script>foo()</script> body + cond= identifier in sources/session/*.scxml matches the EXPECTED_SCRIPT_NAMES set. Mismatch panics the build (closes R54 carry on silent Nil from un-registered script names).

rebind_session_actions_for_test(actions) exposed (#[doc(hidden)]) so cargo test's thread-parallel runner with the process-global INSTALLED OnceLock can swap closures inside a single test binary without violating the production single-install invariant.

tests/session_glue_dispatch.rs rewritten вҖ” every outbound's wire bytes asserted byte-exact against an expected sequence computed independently from the fixture inputs (no shared helper) so an implementation drift surfaces as a mismatch.
verification_bullets: cargo build --workspace clean post-rewrite.
cargo test --workspace: 41 вҶ’ 43 (R57 inline double-install assertion + R57 init_cbyte/pack_sn_res unit tests).
cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings clean (derivable-impls clippy lint fixed by switching CloseReason to #[derive(Default)] + #[default]).
validate_workspace post-mutation: T1=0, RT 12/12, GENERATED.md=sync, atomic ledger entries 57 вҶ’ 58 (after this Round 57 append).

Wire-byte assertions confirm: send_init_syn = [FLAG_S|T_MID_INIT, version, cbyte, zid..., sn_res, batch_size_lo, batch_size_hi]; send_open_syn body carries cookie payload, OpenAck body does NOT; CLOSE = [FLAG_T_CLOSE_S|T_MID_CLOSE, reason_byte]; InitAck flags = FLAG_S|FLAG_A; OpenAck flags = FLAG_T|FLAG_A.
Production-safety guards exercised in tests: inline double-install in r57 test asserts Err(SessionActionsAlreadyInstalled); script-name audit runs on every build.
carry_forward_bullets: R57c = R55b Engine-driving extended to full happy-path coverage. Currently only Init вҶ’ LinkOpening verified. Engine::tick or external event injection (link.opened, init_ack.received, open_ack.received) drives the full 4-way handshake; Established / Closing / Closed terminal states still untested at FSM level.

R57b = cookie generation via HMAC-SHA256. SessionInitParams::cookie is currently caller-supplied bytes; production InitAck side must HMAC-SHA256(per-process-secret, peer_zid || nonce) and OpenSyn side echoes the received cookie. Wires to sce_intrinsics_runtime::hmac_sha256 (deferred RFC В§5.M intrinsic).

R57d = batch_size wire shape audit. wz init_body encode emits batch_size as 2 bytes little-endian; transport.h documents it as u16. The Layer 3 layer3_init_body_s1_a1 fixture passes for both, but real zenoh-pico decode behaviour with a VLE-encoded batch_size variant (if any future patch level introduces one) needs cross-check.

Closes R57c carry (Engine multi-step driving past LinkOpening) using external-event injection вҖ” the 5s link.open_timeout / 100ms closing.timeout delayed sends scheduled inside onentry are not advanced because the happy path drives transitions via real wire events (link.opened / closing.timeout) rather than timeouts. verification_bullets: cargo test --workspace 43 вҶ’ 44 (R59 new integration test); cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings clean.

Engine state assertion at every transition target (Init/LinkOpening/SentInitSyn/GotInitAck/Established/Closing/Closed) confirms the generated SCXML transition graph traverses exactly one macrostep per process_event call.

Established onentry produces 3 actions (enable_rx_tx_regions, start_lease_monitor, start_keepalive_worker); Established onexit produces 2 (stop_keepalive_worker, stop_lease_monitor); test asserts every counter +1 in the right order.

Wire bytes for INIT_SYN + OPEN_SYN + CLOSE in the RecordingDriver sends queue confirm the R57 codec path is exercised through the Engine driver, not just the dispatch_script direct call from R57's test.

publishable_changes_bullets: wz-runtime-tokio/tests/session_fsm_full_path.rs (~180 lines): R59 integration test walks the entire outbound-initiator state path via Engine::process_event(LinkOpened/InitAckReceived/OpenAckReceived/SessionClose/ClosingTimeout) and asserts at every state: (a) `Engine::get_current_state` matches the SCXML target, (b) the onentry/onexit script counters in ActionTrace increment by exactly one each, (c) the RecordingDriver receives the expected open/close/send calls, (d) the Close wire bytes match the codec-produced 2-byte layout [FLAG_T_CLOSE_S|T_MID_CLOSE, reason].

Closes R57c carry (Engine multi-step driving past LinkOpening) using external-event injection вҖ” the 5s link.open_timeout / 100ms closing.timeout delayed sends scheduled inside onentry are not advanced because the happy path drives transitions via real wire events (link.opened / closing.timeout) rather than timeouts. Engine::tick + Hal time mock remains a separate carry for the timeout/failure-path coverage.
publishable_verification_bullets: cargo test --workspace 43 вҶ’ 44 (R59 new integration test); cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings clean.

Engine state assertion at every transition target (Init/LinkOpening/SentInitSyn/GotInitAck/Established/Closing/Closed) confirms the generated SCXML transition graph traverses exactly one macrostep per process_event call.

Established onentry produces 3 actions (enable_rx_tx_regions, start_lease_monitor, start_keepalive_worker); Established onexit produces 2 (stop_keepalive_worker, stop_lease_monitor); test asserts every counter +1 in the right order.

Wire bytes for INIT_SYN + OPEN_SYN + CLOSE in the RecordingDriver sends queue confirm the R57 codec path is exercised through the Engine driver, not just the dispatch_script direct call from R57's test.
validate_workspace: T1=0, RT 12/12, GENERATED.md=sync, atomic ledger entries 59 вҶ’ 60.

Each fires set_close_reason_* + transitions to Closing; the wire shape is identical to the happy-path Close so the codec assertion stays unchanged.

Engine::tick + Hal time mock: the 5s link.open_timeout / 2s init_ack.timeout / 100ms closing.timeout / accepting.inactivity_timeout delayed sends are scheduled but never advanced. Production timeout coverage needs `sce_rust_runtime::StdHal` injection or a TestHal that advances logical time.

Listener-path coverage: R59 walks the initiator path (outbound.start вҶ’ LinkOpening вҶ’ Opening). The Accepting branch (inbound.start вҶ’ AwaitingInitSyn вҶ’ SentInitAck вҶ’ SentOpenAck вҶ’ Established) exercises send_init_ack_with_cookie + send_open_ack which R57 wire tests cover in isolation, but Engine-driven listener-path is its own follow-up test.

Round 60.decision_summary: Strict audit pass вҖ” encapsulation hardening closes 6 R57/R58/R59 self-review defects: dead code, public test surface, default misuse, silent failures, list duplication, weak invariants.
changes_bullets: SessionInitParams::default removed. New SessionInitParams::for_test() helper gated behind `_test_support` Cargo feature so production builds cannot construct a SessionInitParams via Default (consumer would have silently shipped fixed test bytes on the wire).

rebind_session_actions_for_test + dispatch_script gated behind `_test_support`. Public surface in production builds excludes both. wz-runtime-tokio/Cargo.toml grew a [features] section with `default = []` and `_test_support = []`; tests opt in via a self-referencing dev-dependency.

dispatch_script defensive: debug_assert that the requested name is in REGISTERED_SCRIPT_NAMES. Even with `_test_support` enabled, a caller cannot smuggle arbitrary Lua source вҖ” only the production registration set is dispatchable.

bind_unit / bind_close_reason / bind_bool: debug_assert!(ok, ...) replaced with assert!(ok, ...). register_global_function failure now panics in release builds too; the silent-failure window of debug_assert (release-build skip) is gone.

REGISTERED_SCRIPT_NAMES doc-comment promoted to single-source-of-truth marker. EXPECTED_SCRIPT_NAMES (build.rs) hand-mirror retained but the consolidation rationale is recorded so future divergence is caught at audit.

tests/session_fsm_real_tcp.rs (~120 lines): tokio multi-thread test wires TcpListener loopback pair, plumbs `TokioLinkDriverAdapter<TcpDriver>` into `SessionLinkActions`, drives Engine through InitвҶ’LinkOpeningвҶ’SentInitSyn, then reads the wire bytes off the peer socket. First end-to-end proof of the production chain (Engine вҶ’ Lua вҶ’ SessionLinkActions вҶ’ adapter вҶ’ TcpDriver вҶ’ kernel socket); closes the R57 self-review finding that TokioLinkDriverAdapter was dead code.
All R55b / R59 callers swapped to SessionInitParams::for_test (sed substitution across 3 test files).
let _ = sce_rust_lua::register() simplified вҖ” clippy::single_match fix; documented why the silent accept is sound (one-variant Result, AlreadyRegistered == engine already in place).
verification_bullets: cargo test --workspace 44 вҶ’ 45 (R60 real-TCP integration); cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings clean.

Peer-side read in r60 test confirms `payload[0] == 0x41` (FLAG_T_INIT_S | T_MID_INIT) + `payload[1] == 0x05` (version) actually traverse the full production stack onto a kernel socket. Any drift in Engine вҶ” Lua вҶ” adapter вҶ” TcpDriver chain surfaces as a payload byte mismatch.

Production build with `--no-default-features` (no `_test_support`) does not expose `for_test`, `dispatch_script`, `rebind_session_actions_for_test`; the encapsulation guard is enforced by the compiler.
validate_workspace: T1=0, RT 12/12, GENERATED.md=sync, atomic ledger entries 60 вҶ’ 61.
carry_forward_bullets: R61 carry: failure-path Engine coverage (framing.error / lease.expired / tx.congestion.exhaust / peer.close / link.lost / *.timeout). Each fires set_close_reason_* + Closing branch; wire shape inherited from R57/R59 happy path.

R61 carry: listener-path Engine coverage (Accepting branch: inbound.start вҶ’ AwaitingInitSyn вҶ’ SentInitAck вҶ’ SentOpenAck вҶ’ Established). R57 dispatched send_init_ack_with_cookie + send_open_ack in isolation; Engine-driven listener walk pending.
R61 carry: Hal time mock for delayed `<send>` timeout coverage. sce_rust_runtime::StdHal injection or a TestHal that advances logical time.
R62 carry: cookie HMAC-SHA256 вҖ” SessionInitParams::cookie currently caller-supplied bytes. Production listener side must HMAC(secret, peer_zid || nonce) per RFC В§5.M.
R62 carry: transport extension chain (В§7 ext::QoS / ext::Auth / ext::MultiLink) emit on Init/Open. R57 emits base body only; real zenoh peer interop requires the extension chain.
R63 carry: wz-runtime-lwip resolution вҖ” doc-around-the-hack rejected at R58; either revert (preserve SCE B6 emitter audit via a Layer 3 test) or land actual lwIP API.
R40 long-term: SCE PR for `--no-inner-attr` flag or wz per-codec sub-crate split. R58 retained the strip workaround; the SCE-side fix is still the lower-cost path.
EXPECTED_SCRIPT_NAMES (build.rs) and REGISTERED_SCRIPT_NAMES (session_glue.rs) remain hand-maintained twins; consolidation via include!() or build artifact share is a refactor follow-up.

Round 61.decision_summary: Strict audit pass вҖ” full state-graph coverage. Every SCXML transition edge R59 happy-path missed is now Engine-driven and asserted.
changes_bullets: wz-runtime-tokio/tests/session_fsm_coverage.rs (~250 lines): single mega-test r61_full_coverage_sequential walks 11 scenarios covering every transition edge in session_fsm_unicast.scxml outside the R59 happy path вҖ” listener path (InitвҶ’AwaitingInitSynвҶ’SentInitAckвҶ’SentOpenAckвҶ’Established), all 4 close-reason setters (Generic / Invalid / Expired / Unresponsive), all 5 close-from-state edges (LinkOpening / Opening.SentInitSyn / Opening / Established / Accepting), all 3 direct-to-Closed edges (link.lost from LinkOpening, peer.close from Established, link.lost from Accepting).

fresh_engine() helper isolates each scenario via rebind_session_actions_for_test (R60 feature-gated path); a fresh Driver + Actions triple resets every counter to zero between scenarios while INSTALLED OnceLock stays at its first-install pointer.

Single mega-test on purpose: cargo's thread-parallel runner races concurrent rebind_session_actions_for_test calls against the process-global Lua-engine global function namespace. Sequential dispatch in one test fn is the only race-safe shape until R60 carry on session-scoped Lua binding lands. Initially split into 11 #[test] fns and observed 9/11 races; consolidated to one fn after diagnosis.
verification_bullets: cargo test --workspace 45 вҶ’ 46 (R61 mega-test); cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings clean.

11 SCXML transition edges verified end-to-end via Engine::process_event: listener path + LinkOpenTimeout/LinkLost/InitAckTimeout/FramingError(Opening)/OpenAckTimeout/LeaseExpired/FramingError(Established)/TxCongestionExhaust/PeerClose/FramingError(Accepting).

Each scenario asserts (a) Engine::get_current_state matches the SCXML target state, (b) trace.close_reason discriminator matches the set_close_reason_* action the transition runs, (c) send_close_frame_with_reason counter increments only on Closing-routed paths (not on direct-to-Closed via link.lost / peer.close).
validate_workspace: T1=0, RT 12/12, GENERATED.md=sync, atomic ledger entries 61 вҶ’ 62.
carry_forward_bullets: R61b carry: Hal time mock for delayed `<send>` timer fire (5s link.open_timeout / 2s init_ack.timeout / 2s open_ack.timeout / 100ms closing.timeout / accepting.inactivity_timeout). External event injection in R61 verifies the transition target + actions per timeout edge but does NOT exercise sce_rust_runtime's scheduler (PullScheduler + schedule_event_at / pop_ready_event_at). A TestHal with logical-time advance would close the gap; StdHal's `now_ticks_ms` is a free-function (associated, no &self) so a wz-side mock requires either feature-flag swap in sce_rust_runtime or a wrapper Engine layer.
R62 carry: cookie HMAC-SHA256 + transport extension chain (В§7 ext::QoS / ext::Auth / ext::MultiLink) emit on Init/Open frames вҖ” still open from R60.
R63 carry: wz-runtime-lwip resolution вҖ” revert (Phase W defer) or land actual lwIP API; current state is R58 doc-comment reframe which the R57 self-review classified as document-around-the-hack.
R40 long-term: SCE PR for `--no-inner-attr` or wz per-codec sub-crate split.

Test-design carry: race-prone per-test #[test] fns documented as anti-pattern in R61 commentary; future test additions to wz-runtime-tokio should extend r61_full_coverage_sequential (or a new mega-test fn) rather than add isolated fns that the cargo runner parallelises.

Round 62.decision_summary: Strict audit pass вҖ” cookie HMAC-SHA256 primitive landed per RFC В§5.M; extension chain (В§7) carry deferred to R63 (SCXML revision scope).
changes_bullets: wz-runtime-tokio depends on `hmac = 0.12` + `sha2 = 0.10` (RustCrypto). MCU sibling Phase W will swap for sce_intrinsics_runtime::hmac_sha256 once that intrinsic lands per RFC В§5.I.

Public `generate_cookie_hmac_sha256(key, peer_zid) -> Vec<u8>` helper in session_glue. Output is HMAC-SHA256 truncated to 16 bytes (cookie wire width per RFC В§5.M + zenoh-pico _z_t_msg_init_t._cookie ZSlice convention).
Short-key panic at construction site (>= 32 bytes per RFC В§5.M). Test asserts the loud-reject path so a 16-byte misuse cannot smuggle through to a peer wire-decode-time reject.

SessionInitParams::cookie_signing_key field added. Caller supplies per-process secret; SessionInitParams::for_test seeds 32 Г— 0xAB. Cookie field semantics unchanged вҖ” still caller-owned wire bytes; the production Accepting side fills it via the new helper before install_session_actions.
2 unit tests for the HMAC helper: cookie_hmac_sha256_deterministic_16_byte_output + cookie_hmac_short_key_panics.
verification_bullets: cargo test --workspace 46 вҶ’ 48 (+2 HMAC unit tests); cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings clean.
Cookie helper output deterministic (same key + peer_zid вҶ’ same 16-byte cookie); divergent peer_zid yields divergent cookie. Short-key panic loud at construction site.

fixture_params + SessionInitParams::for_test both updated with cookie_signing_key to keep existing wire-byte assertions green; R57 send_init_ack_with_cookie test still passes because the bind closure does not regenerate the cookie inline вҖ” caller owns the field, and the test path keeps the literal cookie for byte-equiv reproducibility.
validate_workspace: T1=0, RT 12/12, GENERATED.md=sync, atomic ledger entries 62 вҶ’ 63.
carry_forward_bullets: R63a carry: transport extension chain emit on Init/Open/Join (В§7 ext::QoS / ext::Auth / ext::MultiLink / ext::PatchType / ext::LowLatency). The SCE schema's <sce:tlv-chain> primitive supports the shape; wz init_body.scxml + open_body.scxml + join.scxml are not yet authored against it. Multi-session SCXML revision + verify-codegen.sh re-run + Layer 3 byte-equiv extension. Real zenoh peer interop requires this.

R63b carry: inbound parser pass. R62's cookie helper is for the Accepting side's outbound InitAck only; the Initiator-side InitAck decode path that pulls the peer-issued cookie off the wire and stages it into SessionInitParams.cookie for OpenSyn echo is not yet wired. Currently the Initiator's cookie field is caller-supplied at install time вҖ” production needs the inbound parser to populate it from the actual peer InitAck.
R63c carry: wz-runtime-lwip resolution still open from R60. R63 cluster bundles all three lwip-related carries (resolution decision + extension chain + inbound parser).
Hal time mock (R61b) and R40 strip workaround long-term decision both still open from prior rounds.
