Round 64.1.decision_summary: CI Layer A hotfix вҖ” cargo install --bin mnemosyne-cli failed ambiguous-package; added --package mnemosyne-cli to resolve. First CI run after R63 push surfaced the gap.
changes_bullets: GitHub Actions CI run 25965126663 reported success (5m13s) but `mnemosyne-cli install failed; Layer A skipped` warning; the cargo install step ran `--bin mnemosyne-cli` against a multi-package workspace (audit-broadcast-bench / cascade-measurement / codegen-prototype / mnemosyne-cli / mnemosyne-mcp / schema-bench / ...) and got `error: multiple packages with binaries found`.

.github/workflows/ci.yml mnemosyne install step amended: `cargo install --git ... --branch main --package mnemosyne-cli --bin mnemosyne-cli`. The --package flag disambiguates the upstream workspace; --bin alone scopes the binary name but not the package selection.
Doc-comment in the workflow step expanded to record the failure mode + the R64.1 retrospect so a future contributor does not regress to the --bin-only form.

.github/workflows/ci.yml: new `Regenerate docs/GENERATED.md (CI fresh-checkout cascade)` step inserted between mnemosyne-cli install and Layer A validate. Runs `mnemosyne-cli generate-docs` to restore the cascade invariant the local mutate path maintains automatically.

R64 series final retrospect (now 4 hotfixes deep): R64.1 wrong cargo install flag / R64.2 right flag but missing build dep / R64.3 protoc apt install / R64.4 GENERATED.md cascade. R64.1-R64.3 were local-detectable (cargo install --help + dry-run + local actionlint); R64.4 is fundamentally CI-vs-local environment-shape divergence (local cascade vs fresh-checkout absence) and required a CI run to surface.

Round 70.decision_summary: Crypto safety hardening вҖ” SigningKey newtype + Zeroizing<Vec<u8>> drop-wipe + RFC 4231 HMAC-SHA256 test vectors. Closes the 3rd-review #4 crypto-secret-in-plain-memory defect.
changes_bullets: wz-runtime-tokio/Cargo.toml: zeroize = '1' with zeroize_derive feature added. RustCrypto-ecosystem standard for cryptographic secret lifecycle.

wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs: new SigningKey struct wrapping Zeroizing<Vec<u8>>; SigningKey::new(bytes) validates >= 32 bytes at construct (RFC В§5.M) and returns Err(SigningKeyTooShort) instead of panicking at HMAC site. Drop wipes the heap allocation вҖ” cookie key bytes no longer linger in core dumps / heap-leak windows / panic backtraces.

Manual Debug impl on SigningKey: shows length + literal '<redacted>' for bytes. Prevents log / panic stack-trace leakage; a regression to #[derive(Debug)] would surface in `signing_key_debug_redacts_bytes` test.
SessionInitParams::cookie_signing_key field type Vec<u8> вҶ’ SigningKey. for_test fixture + integration test fixture_params both construct via SigningKey::new.

generate_cookie_hmac_sha256 split: pure compute_hmac_sha256_full(key, data) -> [u8; 32] primitive separate from the 16-byte wire truncation. The split lets RFC 4231 test vectors cross-check the underlying HMAC without entangling the cookie-truncation slice.
2 new RFC 4231 test cases (TC1 + TC2) cross-check against the canonical HMAC-SHA256 vectors. If RustCrypto's hmac/sha2 ever regresses on us the assertion fires loud.
Removed signing_key_short_panics test; replaced with signing_key_short_returns_err matching the new typed-error path.
verification_bullets: cargo test --workspace 46 вҶ’ 48 (R70 added 2 RFC vector tests + signing_key_short_returns_err + signing_key_debug_redacts_bytes; replaces 1 deleted panic test, net +3).
cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings clean.
RFC 4231 TC1 + TC2 assertions pass against compute_hmac_sha256_full: byte-equiv with the spec test vectors.
SigningKey Debug output verified to redact bytes (test asserts '<redacted>' present + hex absent).
validate_workspace local: T1=0, RT 12/12, GENERATED.md=sync, atomic ledger entries 72 вҶ’ 73.
carry_forward_bullets: R70b carry: RFC 4231 TC3-TC7 coverage. R70 lands TC1+TC2 (smallest + 'Jefe' canonical pair); the remaining test cases (long key / long data / truncated MAC scenarios) extend the regression surface without changing the production code path.
R71 = wz-runtime-tokio-test-support separate crate split (encapsulation hardening; replaces _test_support feature flag).
R67 = R63a SCXML extension chain authoring (Phase Z prototype wire-spec gap).
R68 = R63b Initiator-side inbound parser.
R69 = Hal time-mock injection.

generate_cookie_hmac_sha256 split: pure compute_hmac_sha256_full(key, data) -> [u8; 32] primitive separate from the 16-byte wire truncation. The split lets RFC 4231 test vectors cross-check the underlying HMAC without entangling the cookie-truncation slice.

check_lease_deadline reads SessionLinkActions::last_inbound_keepalive_at (R72b slot), compares now.duration_since(stamp) against params.lease via the lease_in_seconds unit selector, and engine.process_event(SessionFsmUnicastEvent::LeaseExpired) on `>= lease` so the session-fsm `lease.expired -> Closing(Expired)` transition fires

single mega-test pattern preserved (R71b singleton-race carry) вҖ” process-global INSTALLED + Lua engine require sequential dispatch
consumer wiring for the R72b lease-timestamp slot foreshadowed by inbound_to_fsm_event's `KeepAlive -> None` branch (side effect orthogonal to the state graph, comment at session_glue.rs:1150-1156)

expired scenario also asserts trace side effects (close_reason=Expired, send_close_frame_with_reason=1, stop_keepalive_worker=1, stop_lease_monitor=1) matching session_fsm_coverage.rs::r61 lease-expired path so the helper inherits the existing FSM-edge verification
carry_forward_bullets: established_at slot вҖ” session-fsm В§2.5 specifies lease counts from Established entry, but SessionLinkActions has no `established_at: Mutex<Option<Instant>>` slot yet; NoBaseline branch defers the decision while the slot stays None. A follow-up round adds the slot + Established-state-entry hook so pre-KeepAlive sessions can still expire on session-start fallback timing

production driver loop with timeout вҖ” check_lease_deadline is one unit; the loop pairing poll_and_dispatch_one (which blocks on peer events) with periodic lease checks needs tokio::select! against a sleep deadline so a silent peer does not pin the poller. R76b drive_session_until_terminal scope.

Frame inbound also resets the lease? вҖ” R72b populates `last_inbound_keepalive_at` only on InboundFrame::KeepAlive. Whether established-session Frame traffic should also count as liveness is a wire-spec discussion (zenoh-pico transport.h:213 wording is `KEEP_ALIVE` specifically); R77 honours R72b's KeepAlive-only design without bundling that question.

hand-craft two inbound wires: craft_initsyn_wire (parent_flags=FLAG_T_INIT_S, no FLAG_T_INIT_A, 10-byte frame with version/cbyte/zid/sn_res/batch_size and no cookie) + craft_opensyn_wire (parent_flags=0x00, 4-byte frame with lease=0/initial_sn=0/cookie_len=0)

QueueDriver + NoopOutboundDriver test scaffolding mirrors session_fsm_driver_loop.rs from R76 вҖ” the helpers are duplicated rather than extracted because mid-flight scaffold extraction would obscure the per-test isolation rules

Established.onentry side effects asserted (enable_rx_tx_regions=1, start_lease_monitor=1, start_keepalive_worker=1) matching session_fsm_coverage.rs::r61 listener-path expectations вҖ” R78 inherits R61's FSM-edge verification but reaches it through the R76 driver helper rather than direct engine.process_event(E::InitSynReceived)

complements the R76 Initiator-side Rx(InitAck) scenario вҖ” confirms poll_and_dispatch_one handles both handshake halves symmetrically (helper does not discriminate Initiator vs Accepting; the FSM does)

carry_forward_bullets: Accepting-side InitSyn cookie generation вҖ” send_init_ack_with_cookie currently reads params.cookie which the fixture leaves empty. Production deploys MUST populate it via generate_cookie_hmac_sha256(params.cookie_signing_key, peer_zid_from_inbound_InitSyn) before send_init_ack_with_cookie fires; the peer_zid_from_inbound binding is not yet wired into SessionLinkActions (R62 carry, also flagged at session_glue.rs:683-691).

QueueDriver / NoopOutboundDriver duplication across session_fsm_driver_loop.rs + session_fsm_accepting_path.rs вҖ” extraction into wz-runtime-tokio-test-support would consolidate ~80 lines but risks the same singleton-race contagion the mega-test pattern guards against. Defer until R71b/R77b race is closed.

InboundStart dispatch in production вҖ” the test calls engine.process_event(E::InboundStart) directly because the production caller (socket-accept handler) has not been wired yet. Phase W lwIP or AP-side listener bind needs an explicit InboundStart hook on accept().

cookie wire field in OpenSyn is empty (cookie_len=0) вҖ” RFC В§5.M expects the Initiator to echo the cookie it received in InitAck; R78 omits the echo because the test stages only OpenSyn arrival without a prior InitAck-out + cookie-in round-trip. End-to-end cookie echo verification belongs in wz-integration-tests Layer 3 (already covered by layer3_open_body wire-interop tests).

Round 81.decision_summary: R76b drive_session_until_terminal вҖ” production session loop composes poll_and_dispatch_one + lease deadline via tokio::select!.
changes_bullets: crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs: added DriverOutcome enum (Terminated / IterationLimit) and async drive_session_until_terminal helper after check_lease_deadline; loop body composes poll_and_dispatch_one with a tokio::select! race against tokio::time::sleep(remaining lease window)
termination at iteration top: engine.is_in_final_state() -> Terminated; max_iters reached -> IterationLimit; production callers pass max_iters=None for unlimited, test callers cap it

lease branch: reads last_inbound_keepalive_at, computes deadline = stamp + lease (lease_in_seconds-aware), sleeps for saturating_duration_since(now) of the remaining window, then invokes check_lease_deadline(actions, engine, Instant::now()) on the sleep-wins branch; if stamp slot is None (no inbound keepalive yet) falls through to a plain poll_and_dispatch_one await (no lease watchdog)

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/tests/session_fsm_drive_session.rs: new test file with 4 #[tokio::test] fns covering loop wiring shape вҖ” already-terminal engine surfaces Terminated, max_iters=0 surfaces IterationLimit, staged LinkEvent::Lost drives Terminated via poll branch, short-lease + HangingDriver advances FSM past Established via sleep branch

HangingDriver scaffold (std::future::pending::<LinkEvent>().await) models a silent peer so the lease branch is the only forward path; QueueDriver mirrors R76 driver_loop test scaffolding (intentional duplication, scaffolding-extraction is a separate retrospective)
closes R76 honest weakness #1 carry ("poll_and_dispatch_one is a production unit, not a production loop") вҖ” R76b is the loop wrapper that R76 was missing

Unifying the time source requires either parametrising drive_session_until_terminal with a clock trait or porting check_lease_deadline to tokio::time::Instant. Defer to a separate round.

Cancel-safety boundary docstring added вҖ” in-tree drivers (TcpDriver/UdpDriver/QueueDriver/HangingDriver) are cancel-safe at the poll_event boundary, but extern target-plugin drivers (Phase W) may not be. Phase W integration must audit cancel-safety per driver.

max_iters=None production case untested directly вҖ” tests cap iterations for determinism; the unlimited code path is exercised only through cargo's overall workspace runtime. A future end-to-end integration (R76c?) with real TCP loopback could verify the unlimited shape under a finite total runtime budget.

Round 82.decision_summary: R74 Frame.payload application-layer dispatch вҖ” parse_frame_payload + NetworkMessage{Request, Unknown}; DriverLoopOutcome::FramePayload replaces the silent payload-drop in poll_and_dispatch_one's Frame arm.
changes_bullets: crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs: NetworkMessage enum (Request(Box<wz_codecs::request::Request>) / Unknown { mid, body }) + parse_frame_payload(bytes) -> Result<Vec<NetworkMessage>, CodecError> helper that loops over the Frame.payload batch, dispatching N_MID_REQUEST (0x1C) to wz_codecs::request::Request::decode and absorbing other MIDs as Unknown { mid, body } that terminates the batch loop (no length-aware decoder for unknown bodies means cursor cannot safely advance past them).

DriverLoopOutcome gains FramePayload { reliable, sn, messages, has_ext, extensions } variant; SideEffectOnly is now KeepAlive-only. The prior #[derive(Debug)] is replaced by manual Debug impls on both DriverLoopOutcome and NetworkMessage because wz-codecs structs (Request / ExtEntry) only derive Default per sce-codegen output вҖ” the manual impl prints variant kind + ext_count + body_len without recursing into codec fields so existing {outcome:?} test assertions keep working.

poll_and_dispatch_one Frame arm rewired: previously bucketed under None -> DriverLoopOutcome::SideEffectOnly (payload silently dropped). Now matches InboundFrame::Frame fields, runs parse_frame_payload, surfaces FramePayload on Ok or ParseError(InboundParseError::Codec) + Engine::process_event(E::FramingError) on Err. Application-layer parse failure is conservatively treated as a transport-FSM framing.error so the session moves to Closing on malformed batch payload (revisitable per carry).

inbound_to_fsm_event docstring extended: Frame None branch now documents 'application-layer dispatch via parse_frame_payload' instead of 'drop'. KeepAlive None branch unchanged. The function itself still returns None for both вҖ” the dispatch fan-out lives in poll_and_dispatch_one.

wire_const::N_MID_REQUEST (0x1C) constant added. The other 6 network MIDs (PUSH 0x1D, RESPONSE 0x1B, DECLARE 0x1E, OAM 0x1F, RESPONSE_FINAL 0x1A, INTEREST 0x19 per zenoh-pico network.h:33-39) are documented in NetworkMessage rustdoc but not declared as constants until their decoders land вҖ” YAGNI per the existing wire_const philosophy where every named constant has at least one match-arm consumer.

Tests added вҖ” 4 parse_frame_payload unit tests in session_fsm_inbound_dispatch.rs: empty payload returns empty Vec; Unknown MID (0x1D) absorbs remainder; Request envelope MID 0x1C dispatches to Request::decode (first record is Request); truncated Request body returns CodecError. 3 r74_* poll_and_dispatch_one integration scenarios in session_fsm_driver_loop.rs: empty-payload Frame surfaces FramePayload{messages=[]}, Unknown-MID payload surfaces FramePayload{messages=[Unknown{mid:0x1D, body}]}, malformed Request payload surfaces ParseError + FSM transitions SentInitSyn -> Closing.

Closes R73 + R76 carry 'Application-layer Frame payload dispatch (R74-tier) вҖ” DriverLoopOutcome::SideEffectOnly variant on Frame currently drops the payload; downstream consumer needs the msg_put/del/query/response codec dispatch'. The dispatch wiring is now in place; envelope codec coverage remains REQUEST-only until SCE Push/Response/Declare/OAM/Interest SCXMLs land.

scripts/run-ci.sh: Layer A (validate-workspace) + Layer B (verify-codegen) + Layer C1 (test) + Layer C2 (clippy) + Layer D (deploy schema) all pass вҖ” `run-ci: all required layers pass`.

wz_codecs::request encode/decode byte-count discrepancy discovered empirically: Request::default() with header=0x1C encodes N bytes that Request::decode consumes M < N. The R74 batch loop absorbs the residual as Unknown rather than misinterpreting it. The unit test parse_frame_payload_dispatches_request_mid_to_request_decoder asserts 'first record is Request' (parsed[0]) instead of strict parsed.len()==1; the discrepancy is documented in carry forward as a wz-codecs/sce-codegen issue out of R74 scope.

validate_workspace baseline preserved: T1 orphan=0, round-trip 12/12, GENERATED.md=sync. T3 warn 856 / T4 info 427 unchanged (no doc mutation). Atomic ledger entries 94 -> 95 (this Round entry); sections=214 unchanged.
carry_forward_bullets: wz_codecs::request encode/decode roundtrip discrepancy вҖ” Request::default() with header=0x1C produces bytes where Request::decode consumes a different (smaller) byte count, leaving residual bytes that the R74 batch loop absorbs as Unknown. The contract should be 'encode then decode consumes exactly the encoded bytes' regardless of state. Likely root cause: Wireexpr default-state encode/decode mismatch OR RequestVariant Default-arm tag round-trip. A focused round on the wz-codecs codegen or Wireexpr default state would close this; without it, real production Request payloads (where header is set and inner body is meaningful) may roundtrip cleanly, but the codec contract is currently soft on default state.

Network-envelope codec coverage вҖ” wz_codecs::request is the only authored envelope. The other 6 network MIDs (PUSH 0x1D, RESPONSE 0x1B, DECLARE 0x1E, OAM 0x1F, RESPONSE_FINAL 0x1A, INTEREST 0x19) decode as Unknown until their SCE codec SCXMLs land. PUSH is the highest-priority next target (pub/sub data path = put/del without request-reply correlation); RESPONSE follows for request-reply parity. Each new envelope codec round bumps a wire_const::N_MID_* constant and adds a parse_frame_payload match arm.

Application-layer parse error -> transport FramingError mapping conservative вҖ” R74 treats any CodecError inside parse_frame_payload as a transport FramingError that closes the session. Stricter wire-spec reading might preserve the session and only log application-layer batch failures (the Frame transport envelope itself parsed OK). Revisit if production deploys need batch resilience or if integration testing surfaces sessions closing on transient app-layer noise.

FramePayload outcome not yet consumed by drive_session_until_terminal вҖ” R81's loop discards the per-iteration DriverLoopOutcome (matches all variants with `=> {}`). A future round (composing on R81 carry #4 'per-iteration observer callback') wires FramePayload.messages to a consumer registry вҖ” pub/sub topic dispatcher / Lua bridge / Rust callback. Without that wiring, R74 surfaces the payload at the API boundary but no one reads it yet.
Round 82.publishable_decision_summary: R74 Frame.payload application-layer dispatch вҖ” parse_frame_payload + NetworkMessage{Request, Unknown}; DriverLoopOutcome::FramePayload replaces the silent payload-drop in poll_and_dispatch_one's Frame arm.
publishable_changes_bullets: crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs: NetworkMessage enum (Request(Box<wz_codecs::request::Request>) / Unknown { mid, body }) + parse_frame_payload(bytes) -> Result<Vec<NetworkMessage>, CodecError> helper that loops over the Frame.payload batch, dispatching N_MID_REQUEST (0x1C) to wz_codecs::request::Request::decode and absorbing other MIDs as Unknown { mid, body } that terminates the batch loop (no length-aware decoder for unknown bodies means cursor cannot safely advance past them).

DriverLoopOutcome gains FramePayload { reliable, sn, messages, has_ext, extensions } variant; SideEffectOnly is now KeepAlive-only. The prior #[derive(Debug)] is replaced by manual Debug impls on both DriverLoopOutcome and NetworkMessage because wz-codecs structs (Request / ExtEntry) only derive Default per sce-codegen output вҖ” the manual impl prints variant kind + ext_count + body_len without recursing into codec fields so existing {outcome:?} test assertions keep working.

poll_and_dispatch_one Frame arm rewired: previously bucketed under None -> DriverLoopOutcome::SideEffectOnly (payload silently dropped). Now matches InboundFrame::Frame fields, runs parse_frame_payload, surfaces FramePayload on Ok or ParseError(InboundParseError::Codec) + Engine::process_event(E::FramingError) on Err. Application-layer parse failure is conservatively treated as a transport-FSM framing.error so the session moves to Closing on malformed batch payload (revisitable per carry).

inbound_to_fsm_event docstring extended: Frame None branch now documents 'application-layer dispatch via parse_frame_payload' instead of 'drop'. KeepAlive None branch unchanged. The function itself still returns None for both вҖ” the dispatch fan-out lives in poll_and_dispatch_one.

wire_const::N_MID_REQUEST (0x1C) constant added. The other 6 network MIDs (PUSH 0x1D, RESPONSE 0x1B, DECLARE 0x1E, OAM 0x1F, RESPONSE_FINAL 0x1A, INTEREST 0x19 per zenoh-pico network.h:33-39) are documented in NetworkMessage rustdoc but not declared as constants until their decoders land вҖ” YAGNI per the existing wire_const philosophy where every named constant has at least one match-arm consumer.

Tests added вҖ” 4 parse_frame_payload unit tests in session_fsm_inbound_dispatch.rs: empty payload returns empty Vec; Unknown MID (0x1D) absorbs remainder; Request envelope MID 0x1C dispatches to Request::decode (first record is Request); truncated Request body returns CodecError. 3 r74_* poll_and_dispatch_one integration scenarios in session_fsm_driver_loop.rs: empty-payload Frame surfaces FramePayload{messages=[]}, Unknown-MID payload surfaces FramePayload{messages=[Unknown{mid:0x1D, body}]}, malformed Request payload surfaces ParseError + FSM transitions SentInitSyn -> Closing.

Closes R73 + R76 carry 'Application-layer Frame payload dispatch (R74-tier) вҖ” DriverLoopOutcome::SideEffectOnly variant on Frame currently drops the payload; downstream consumer needs the msg_put/del/query/response codec dispatch'. The dispatch wiring is now in place; envelope codec coverage remains REQUEST-only until SCE Push/Response/Declare/OAM/Interest SCXMLs land.

The R74 batch loop absorbs the residual as Unknown rather than misinterpreting it. Round 83.decision_summary: R83 per-iteration observer callback вҖ” drive_session_until_terminal gains FnMut(IterationEvent) param so R74 FramePayload + R76 outcomes + R77 LeaseCheckOutcome reach a consumer instead of being discarded by the select! arms.
changes_bullets: crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs: IterationEvent<'a> enum (Poll(&'a DriverLoopOutcome) / Lease(LeaseCheckOutcome)) + manual Debug impl (DriverLoopOutcome has no derive Debug per R74; the manual impl prints Poll(o:?) / Lease(o:?) for test {ev:?} assertions). Variant choice mirrors the loop body's two work paths; the 'a borrow is the iteration's stack frame so observers retaining outcome data must clone owned fields (e.g. messages.clone() from FramePayload) into external storage.

LeaseCheckOutcome gains derive(Clone, Copy) вҖ” all three unit variants make Copy trivially correct, and the IterationEvent::Lease variant can take it by value without forcing observers to deal with move semantics.

drive_session_until_terminal signature: new generic param F: FnMut(IterationEvent<'_>) appended after max_iters; the 3 inner work paths (tokio::select Poll arm, tokio::select Lease arm, no-baseline await arm) each bind the outcome to a let-binding and call on_event(IterationEvent::...) before continuing. Previously the outcome bindings were `_ = ...` (silently discarded); after R83 every iteration surfaces exactly one event.

Rustdoc on the function extended with an R83 section explaining the bridge from producers (R74 FramePayload / R76 AdvancedFsm-LinkLost-ParseError / R77 LeaseCheckOutcome) to downstream consumers (pub/sub topic dispatcher / telemetry / logging). Synchronous-contract caveat documented: the closure runs inside the select arm so expensive consumers must buffer.

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/tests/session_fsm_drive_session.rs: 4 existing R81 scenarios updated to pass |_| {} no-op closure (purely mechanical signature shim, no logic change). 3 new r83_* scenarios added: (A) observer captures Frame then Lost in order via Debug strings (proves R74 FramePayload reaches the consumer through R83 wiring); (B) observer destructures IterationEvent::Poll(DriverLoopOutcome::FramePayload { messages, .. }) and records messages.len() == 1 (proves structural read through the &DriverLoopOutcome reference, not just Debug); (C) observer captures LeaseCheckOutcome::Expired under short-lease + HangingDriver (proves Lease arm wiring).

Closes R81 honest carry #4 'drive_session_until_terminal does not surface the inner DriverLoopOutcome per iteration вҖ” production telemetry / logging callers would want that' and R74 (Round 82) honest carry #4 'FramePayload outcome not yet consumed by drive_session_until_terminal'. The producer-without-consumer anti-pattern is now resolved: every outcome surfaces at the observer boundary, and the closing-the-loop test r83_observer_reads_framepayload_messages_through_reference asserts the application-layer consumer can structurally read FramePayload.messages.

carry_forward_bullets: Multi-observer registry вҖ” R83 ships a single FnMut callback. Production deploys with multiple independent consumers (telemetry logger + pub/sub topic dispatcher + Lua bridge) need either a Vec<Box<dyn FnMut(IterationEvent)>> wrapper or an Observer trait with multiple registered impls. Defer until a real second-consumer use case lands so the registry shape is informed by an actual access pattern (mpsc broadcast vs cloned per-observer borrow vs shared-state lock).

FSM state snapshot in IterationEvent вҖ” the observer currently has no view of engine.get_current_state() at the iteration boundary because the loop holds &mut engine. Telemetry callers wanting `(state, outcome)` tuples must read engine state out-of-band before re-entering drive_session_until_terminal, which defeats the per-iteration observer pattern. Option: add a `state: SessionFsmUnicastState` field to each IterationEvent variant captured at iteration top before the select. Defer until a telemetry consumer requests it.

Async observer вҖ” R83's FnMut is synchronous; the closure body cannot .await. Consumers needing async work (HTTP POST telemetry, file IO) must buffer into a channel and process on a separate task. A future round could offer an async observer variant via a separate function (drive_session_with_async_observer) once a consumer pattern justifies the API split.

Per-iteration observer for outbound side вҖ” R83 covers inbound poll + lease only. Outbound TX path (send_init_syn / send_open_ack / etc.) goes through SCE dispatch and currently doesn't surface its own per-action events. A symmetric outbound observer is out of R83 scope but would close the telemetry surface.

Round 84.decision_summary: R84 established_at slot + Established.onentry hook вҖ” closes session-fsm В§2.5 wire-spec gap where check_lease_deadline NoBaseline was pinned until first inbound KeepAlive instead of counting from Established entry.
changes_bullets: crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs: SessionLinkActions gains established_at: Mutex<Option<Instant>> slot, initialized None in SessionLinkActions::new; ActionTrace gains record_established_at: u32 counter for test-side observation; clone_via_copy() updated to forward the new field.

register_state_internal_fns adds bind_unit(lua, "record_established_at", ...) вҖ” sets trace.record_established_at += 1 AND populates *established_at.lock() = Some(Instant::now()). Two independent mutex locks (no deadlock risk); ordering trace-first matches handle_inbound's existing KeepAlive pattern.

REGISTERED_SCRIPT_NAMES (session_glue.rs:~1762) + EXPECTED_SCRIPT_NAMES (build.rs:~50) both gain "record_established_at" вҖ” the build.rs verifier cross-checks SCXML script-name references against the runtime registration set, so adding to one list without the other fails the build (drift hazard guarded by R60's consolidation).

sources/session/session_fsm_unicast.scxml: Established.onentry gains <script>record_established_at()</script> as the second onentry action (after enable_rx_tx_regions, before start_lease_monitor / start_keepalive_worker). The script-call ordering matches the prose-level intent: regions enable first, the entry timestamp is recorded next so the lease monitor and keepalive worker start with the slot already populated.

check_lease_deadline baseline rewrite: prior R77 logic took stamp = last_inbound_keepalive_at, returning NoBaseline whenever that slot was None. R84 logic computes baseline = max(established_at, last_inbound_keepalive_at): both None -> NoBaseline (now reaches only the pre-Established window), one Some -> use the Some, both Some -> max (the more recent stamp; mirrors session-fsm В§2.5 'lease window resets on each peer ping but starts at Established entry'). LeaseCheckOutcome rustdoc rewritten to reflect the new baseline contract.

Tests rewired in crates/wz-runtime-tokio/tests/session_fsm_lease_deadline.rs: r77_no_baseline_when_stamp_slot_empty -> r77_no_baseline_when_both_slots_empty_pre_established (does NOT drive to Established, asserts both slots stay None). 3 new R84 scenarios: r84_within_lease_via_established_baseline_alone (drive to Established, no KeepAlive, established_at + 1ms within 10s lease -> WithinLease вҖ” pre-R84 this surfaced NoBaseline); r84_expired_via_established_baseline_alone (drive to Established, no KeepAlive, established_at + 20s past 10s lease -> Expired + Closing вҖ” closes the wire-spec gap directly); r84_keepalive_wins_over_stale_established_via_max (both populated, stale established + recent KeepAlive, max() picks KeepAlive -> WithinLease where established-alone would surface Expired). The other 3 R77 tests (within_lease_when_stamp_recent, expired_drives_established_to_closing, expired_at_exact_lease_boundary) pass unchanged because the test explicitly populates last_inbound_keepalive_at after drive_to_established, so max() picks keepalive and the original assertions still hold.
verification_bullets: cargo build -p wz-runtime-tokio green; build.rs first attempt panicked with 'SCXML references script names not registered in session_glue: [record_established_at]' вҖ” fixed by adding to EXPECTED_SCRIPT_NAMES + REGISTERED_SCRIPT_NAMES in parallel (textbook two-list-drift catch).

carry_forward_bullets: Established.onexit does NOT clear established_at вҖ” if the FSM reaches Closed and a fresh handshake is attempted on the same SessionLinkActions instance (uncommon but theoretically possible), the stale established_at would skew the lease window. Current architecture assumes one SessionLinkActions per session lifetime (the SCE upstream-fixed singleton retirement landed in R79), so this is not a hot defect вҖ” but a future session-reuse scenario would need an onexit clear. Defer until session reuse is on the roadmap.

Lease comparator semantic question вҖ” session-fsm В§2.5 reads as 'lease resets on KeepAlive', which matches max(established_at, keepalive). But strict zenoh-pico parity may want 'last KeepAlive only OR established_at as fallback' (NOT max вҖ” treat them as fallback chain). The semantic difference shows up when KeepAlive is OLDER than established_at (unusual but possible if KeepAlive arrived before handshake completed somehow). R84 ships max() which is the most defensive (latest stamp wins); revisit if upstream wire-spec discussions surface a stricter contract.

Multi-instance test isolation now safe вҖ” the R79 per-instance ScriptEngine DI migration means each fresh_setup() gets its own Lua engine, so record_established_at's `*established_at.lock() = Some(Instant::now())` body doesn't race across parallel tests. This was a real risk pre-R79 (the singleton INSTALLED OnceLock would have caused cross-test interference); R79's foresight pays off here.

Outbound-half observer (R83 carry #4) still open вҖ” R83 wires inbound poll + lease observer, R84 closes the established_at gap, but the outbound TX path (record_established_at is technically state-internal not TX, so it's in scope; but send_init_syn / send_open_ack etc. have no symmetric observer) remains unobservable from the application layer. A symmetric outbound observer round would close the full telemetry surface.

publishable_carry_forward_bullets: Established.onexit does NOT clear established_at вҖ” if the FSM reaches Closed and a fresh handshake is attempted on the same SessionLinkActions instance (uncommon but theoretically possible), the stale established_at would skew the lease window. Current architecture assumes one SessionLinkActions per session lifetime (the SCE upstream-fixed singleton retirement landed in R79), so this is not a hot defect вҖ” but a future session-reuse scenario would need an onexit clear. Defer until session reuse is on the roadmap.

Lease comparator semantic question вҖ” session-fsm В§2.5 reads as 'lease resets on KeepAlive', which matches max(established_at, keepalive). But strict zenoh-pico parity may want 'last KeepAlive only OR established_at as fallback' (NOT max вҖ” treat them as fallback chain). The semantic difference shows up when KeepAlive is OLDER than established_at (unusual but possible if KeepAlive arrived before handshake completed somehow). R84 ships max() which is the most defensive (latest stamp wins); revisit if upstream wire-spec discussions surface a stricter contract.

Multi-instance test isolation now safe вҖ” the R79 per-instance ScriptEngine DI migration means each fresh_setup() gets its own Lua engine, so record_established_at's `*established_at.lock() = Some(Instant::now())` body doesn't race across parallel tests. This was a real risk pre-R79 (the singleton INSTALLED OnceLock would have caused cross-test interference); R79's foresight pays off here.

Outbound-half observer (R83 carry #4) still open вҖ” R83 wires inbound poll + lease observer, R84 closes the established_at gap, but the outbound TX path (record_established_at is technically state-internal not TX, so it's in scope; but send_init_syn / send_open_ack etc. have no symmetric observer) remains unobservable from the application layer. A symmetric outbound observer round would close the full telemetry surface.

Round 86.decision_summary: R86 production peer_zid binding for Accepting-side cookie вҖ” handle_inbound captures InitSyn.body.zid into a new inbound_peer_zid slot; send_init_ack_with_cookie HMAC-binds the outbound cookie to that zid per RFC В§5.M, closing the R62/R78 carry.
changes_bullets: crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs: SessionLinkActions gains inbound_peer_zid: Mutex<Option<Vec<u8>>> slot (initialized None in new()). Rustdoc explains the RFC В§5.M binding contract: cookie = HMAC-SHA256(cookie_signing_key, peer_zid)[..16], fallback to params.cookie if slot is None (defensive вҖ” well-formed handshake always populates before send_init_ack_with_cookie fires).

handle_inbound: new match arm for InboundFrame::Init { is_ack: false, body, .. } that clones body.zid into the slot. Mirrors the existing InitAck (is_ack: true) inbound_cookie capture pattern at the same dispatch point. InitAck does NOT touch the slot (cross-role-confusion safety; r86_handle_inbound_init_ack_does_not_overwrite_peer_zid test pins this).

encode_init internal fn + encode_init_with_role public method gain cookie_override: Option<&[u8]> parameter (mirrors encode_open_with_role's existing override slot). encode_init uses cookie_override.unwrap_or(&params.cookie) for InitAck and ignores override entirely for InitSyn (wire-spec forbids cookie field there). The 4 external integration-test call sites in crates/wz-integration-tests/tests/layer3_ext_chain_outbound.rs updated to pass None вҖ” no compat shim per the project's no-shim rule (Round 60 textbook).

send_init_ack_with_cookie Lua binding: reads inbound_peer_zid slot, computes generate_cookie_hmac_sha256(&params.cookie_signing_key, peer_zid) when populated, passes the 16-byte result as cookie_override to encode_init_with_role. The prior R62 path used params.cookie verbatim вҖ” a deploy-static value that gave no per-peer replay defense. Production callers staging params.cookie as placeholder bytes (per R57's caller-owned interface) now get the spec-compliant HMAC value automatically once an InitSyn has arrived.

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/tests/session_fsm_inbound_dispatch.rs: 2 R86 unit tests added. r86_handle_inbound_initsyn_captures_peer_zid stages an InitSyn wire with zid [0xB0..0xB3], asserts slot populated post-handle_inbound. r86_handle_inbound_init_ack_does_not_overwrite_peer_zid seeds the slot with [0xAA..0xDD], stages an InitAck with different zid [0xC0..0xC3], asserts slot unchanged (semantic: capture is Accepting-side InitSyn only).

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/tests/session_fsm_accepting_path.rs: r86_send_init_ack_with_cookie_binds_to_inbound_peer_zid integration test added. Inline RecordingOutboundDriver captures the InitAck wire bytes; the test re-parses the wire via parse_inbound, extracts the cookie field, and asserts byte-equivalence against generate_cookie_hmac_sha256(fixture_key, [0xB0..0xB3]). Pre-R86 the cookie was params.cookie verbatim (deploy-static) which the assertion would reject; post-R86 the cookie is HMAC-bound per RFC В§5.M anti-amplification.
verification_bullets: cargo build -p wz-runtime-tokio green; encode_init_with_role signature change forced 4 external test-call-site updates (sed-driven, all passing None for cookie_override) per the no-compat-shim rule.

validate_workspace baseline preserved: T1 orphan=0, round-trip 12/12, GENERATED.md=sync, T3 warn 856 / T4 info 427 unchanged. Atomic ledger entries 98 -> 99 (this Round entry); sections=214 unchanged.
carry_forward_bullets: Cookie verification on inbound OpenSyn вҖ” R86 closes the OUTBOUND cookie binding (Accepting side mints HMAC-bound cookie on InitAck). The INBOUND side (Accepting-side OpenSyn cookie verification) still trusts the peer's echoed cookie blindly (R57's guard_fns just returns true for cookie_valid). RFC В§5.M requires the Accepting side to re-HMAC peer_zid with the same cookie_signing_key on OpenSyn arrival and compare against body.cookie. A follow-up round wires that verification through the cookie_valid guard.

inbound_peer_zid stale across handshake retries вҖ” R86's slot is populated once on InitSyn arrival but never cleared. A retry scenario where the FSM goes back to AwaitingInitSyn (currently NOT a wire-spec edge, but theoretically possible if a future round adds inbound.restart) would see stale peer_zid. Defensive: clear the slot on AwaitingInitSyn entry. Defer until the retry edge is on the roadmap.

Cookie ext-chain extension вҖ” R86 produces the 16-byte HMAC cookie in body.cookie. RFC В§5.M also allows the cookie to carry additional MAC material via the ext chain (HMAC ext entry, deploy-specific anti-amplification token). The ext_chain_for(ExtChainRole::InitAck) slot is already wired (R68b); composing the HMAC into the ext chain is a separate extension point not covered by R86.

Initiator-side cookie verification вҖ” the Initiator (sending InitSyn) receives the Accepting side's InitAck cookie via inbound_cookie capture (R62). The Initiator echoes that cookie verbatim in OpenSyn (R57 caller-owned cookie field). Both sides should ideally re-derive the cookie locally and compare вҖ” but the Initiator doesn't know the cookie_signing_key (only the Accepting side does). So echo-only is the correct contract on the Initiator side; R86 only addresses the Accepting-side mint path.

Round 87.decision_summary: R87 debt repayment вҖ” R82's "first record is Request" workaround replaced with exact-byte-count round-trip Request construction; assertions strengthened to parsed.len()==1, and the previously-removed Request+Unknown chain test restored.
changes_bullets: Root cause identified for the R82 codec discrepancy: `RequestVariant::default()` returns `CodecZenohMsgPut(MsgPut::default())` per sce-codegen's 'pick first variant' rule, but `MsgPut::default().header = 0` (low-5 MID = 0) causes Request::decode's inner-MID dispatch (line 102 of generated request.rs: `match (peek >> 0) & 0x1F { 1=>MsgPut, 2=>MsgDel, 3=>Query, other=>Default{tag:other, body:Query} }`) to route to the Default arm with Query::decode. Encode wrote MsgPut bytes; decode read Query bytes. The discrepancy is a sce-codegen Default-arm self-inconsistency on the codec output, NOT a real wire-spec bug вҖ” production callers construct Request with explicit body, not Default.

The fix is a test-side construction discipline: build Request with `body: RequestVariant::Default { tag: 0, body: Query::default() }` so encode and decode use the same arm (Default arm) and the same inner codec (Query::default round-trips in 1 byte; Wireexpr::default round-trips in 1 byte). Total round-trip-safe wire: header(1) + rid_vle(1) + wireexpr(1) + query_default(1) = 4 bytes exact.

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/tests/session_fsm_inbound_dispatch.rs: parse_frame_payload_dispatches_request_mid_to_request_decoder rewritten. Asserts `bytes.len() == 4` (encode side) and `parsed.len() == 1` (decode side вҖ” strict, no residual). Rustdoc inside the test documents the codegen-Default corner so the construction discipline is preserved when future codecs are added.

parse_frame_payload_decodes_request_then_unknown_chain restored. This test was REMOVED from the original R82 commit because the default-state mismatch produced unpredictable residual bytes that polluted the chain assertion. With the R87 construction, the chain test asserts `parsed.len() == 2` (Request envelope + Unknown absorbing tail) and validates the Unknown.body byte equality.

No source-code change in session_glue.rs or wz-codecs/lib.rs вҖ” the fix is purely in test construction. The discovered codegen corner (Default impl picks first variant + MsgPut::default.header == 0 вү  msg_put MID 1) is documented as a separate SCE upstream concern; wz-side tests now bypass it by explicit body construction. No wz-codecs change needed for production correctness.

Closes R82 carry #1 'wz_codecs::request encode/decode roundtrip discrepancy вҖ” default-state Request::default().encode() produces bytes where Request::decode consumes a different (smaller) byte count'. The original carry overstated the scope as a general codec bug; R87 narrows it to 'Default-arm of variant dispatch tag mismatch on initial state', shows that real-construction code is unaffected, and demonstrates the test-side fix.
verification_bullets: cargo build -p wz-runtime-tokio green; no source change beyond the test file.

cargo test -p wz-runtime-tokio --test session_fsm_inbound_dispatch parse_frame_payload: 5 passed (empty / Unknown / Request strict / Request+Unknown chain / truncated). Each assertion is now strict вҖ” no `parsed.len() >= 1` or 'first record is X' workarounds remain.
cargo test --workspace: 115 passed / 0 failed (114 -> 115, +1 = exact R87 new test count: parse_frame_payload_decodes_request_then_unknown_chain restored).
cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings: clean.
scripts/run-ci.sh: all 5 layers pass.

Empirical round-trip evidence: Wireexpr::default with parent_flags=0x1C (N bit clear) encodes/decodes id=0 in exactly 1 byte. Query::default with header=0 encodes header byte only (consolidation/parameters/extensions all None per flag bits clear). The 4-byte wire is byte-exact across encode and decode.

validate_workspace baseline preserved: T1 orphan=0, round-trip 12/12, GENERATED.md=sync, T3 warn 856 / T4 info 427 unchanged. Atomic ledger entries 99 -> 100 (this Round entry); sections=214 unchanged.
carry_forward_bullets: sce-codegen Default-arm consistency for variant dispatchers вҖ” the discovered corner is that sce-codegen's `RequestVariant::default()` picks the first declared variant (`CodecZenohMsgPut`) but the inner body's `Default` impl doesn't set the header byte to match that variant's dispatch MID. Two upstream options: (a) inner body's Default impl sets header to its own MID; (b) outer dispatcher's Default arm picks `Default { tag: 0, body: <fallback-arm> }` consistently. Either is an SCE upstream concern. Document for follow-up SCE work; wz tests no longer trip this corner.

Variant Default uniformity вҖ” the same corner may exist on `CloseVariant`, `MsgPut`'s inner ext discriminators, etc. вҖ” any codec with `<sce:variant>` + Default arm. A wz-side audit could enumerate which codecs are affected; defer until a real wz-runtime test trips it again (R87 only confirmed the request.scxml case).

Round-trip property test infrastructure вҖ” R87's construction discipline (build with body=Default{tag:0, body:Query::default()}) is a manual guard. A proptest-style fuzz harness over Request states would catch future codegen regressions. Out of R87 scope; would land alongside SCE upstream codegen audit.
Round 87.publishable_decision_summary: R87 debt repayment вҖ” R82's "first record is Request" workaround replaced with exact-byte-count round-trip Request construction; assertions strengthened to parsed.len()==1, and the previously-removed Request+Unknown chain test restored.
publishable_changes_bullets: Root cause identified for the R82 codec discrepancy: `RequestVariant::default()` returns `CodecZenohMsgPut(MsgPut::default())` per sce-codegen's 'pick first variant' rule, but `MsgPut::default().header = 0` (low-5 MID = 0) causes Request::decode's inner-MID dispatch (line 102 of generated request.rs: `match (peek >> 0) & 0x1F { 1=>MsgPut, 2=>MsgDel, 3=>Query, other=>Default{tag:other, body:Query} }`) to route to the Default arm with Query::decode. Encode wrote MsgPut bytes; decode read Query bytes. The discrepancy is a sce-codegen Default-arm self-inconsistency on the codec output, NOT a real wire-spec bug вҖ” production callers construct Request with explicit body, not Default.

The fix is a test-side construction discipline: build Request with `body: RequestVariant::Default { tag: 0, body: Query::default() }` so encode and decode use the same arm (Default arm) and the same inner codec (Query::default round-trips in 1 byte; Wireexpr::default round-trips in 1 byte). Total round-trip-safe wire: header(1) + rid_vle(1) + wireexpr(1) + query_default(1) = 4 bytes exact.

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/tests/session_fsm_inbound_dispatch.rs: parse_frame_payload_dispatches_request_mid_to_request_decoder rewritten. Asserts `bytes.len() == 4` (encode side) and `parsed.len() == 1` (decode side вҖ” strict, no residual). Rustdoc inside the test documents the codegen-Default corner so the construction discipline is preserved when future codecs are added.

parse_frame_payload_decodes_request_then_unknown_chain restored. This test was REMOVED from the original R82 commit because the default-state mismatch produced unpredictable residual bytes that polluted the chain assertion. With the R87 construction, the chain test asserts `parsed.len() == 2` (Request envelope + Unknown absorbing tail) and validates the Unknown.body byte equality.

No source-code change in session_glue.rs or wz-codecs/lib.rs вҖ” the fix is purely in test construction. The discovered codegen corner (Default impl picks first variant + MsgPut::default.header == 0 вү  msg_put MID 1) is documented as a separate SCE upstream concern; wz-side tests now bypass it by explicit body construction. No wz-codecs change needed for production correctness.

Closes R82 carry #1 'wz_codecs::request encode/decode roundtrip discrepancy вҖ” default-state Request::default().encode() produces bytes where Request::decode consumes a different (smaller) byte count'. The original carry overstated the scope as a general codec bug; R87 narrows it to 'Default-arm of variant dispatch tag mismatch on initial state', shows that real-construction code is unaffected, and demonstrates the test-side fix.

publishable_carry_forward_bullets: sce-codegen Default-arm consistency for variant dispatchers вҖ” the discovered corner is that sce-codegen's `RequestVariant::default()` picks the first declared variant (`CodecZenohMsgPut`) but the inner body's `Default` impl doesn't set the header byte to match that variant's dispatch MID. Two upstream options: (a) inner body's Default impl sets header to its own MID; (b) outer dispatcher's Default arm picks `Default { tag: 0, body: <fallback-arm> }` consistently. Either is an SCE upstream concern. Document for follow-up SCE work; wz tests no longer trip this corner.

Variant Default uniformity вҖ” the same corner may exist on `CloseVariant`, `MsgPut`'s inner ext discriminators, etc. вҖ” any codec with `<sce:variant>` + Default arm. A wz-side audit could enumerate which codecs are affected; defer until a real wz-runtime test trips it again (R87 only confirmed the request.scxml case).

sources/codecs/request.scxml: variant arm 0x03 marked with default="true" вҖ” declares Query as the Default-trait starting arm so freshly-constructed Request::default() picks the declared default arm instead of the legacy 'first declared arm' (CodecZenohMsgPut).

sources/codecs/msg_put.scxml: <sce:flag name="mid" bit="0" width="5" value="0x01"/> вҖ” bakes Z_PUT wire MID into MsgPut::default()'s header byte. Mirror change in msg_del.scxml (value="0x02") and query.scxml (value="0x03"). Each codec's Default::default() now carries its own dispatch MID, closing the inner-codec side of the variant-default-uniformity invariant.

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/tests/session_fsm_inbound_dispatch.rs: parse_frame_payload_dispatches_request_mid_to_request_decoder + parse_frame_payload_decodes_request_then_unknown_chain reverted from R87's explicit `RequestVariant::Default { tag: 0, body: Query::default() }` construction to plain `Request { header: 0x1C, ..Request::default() }`. The R87 workaround was a wz-side discipline imposed because SCE codegen's Variant::default() was self-inconsistent on the initial state; with R88's SCE fix the codegen-emitted Default arm matches the decode dispatch table, so explicit construction is no longer needed. R87's verbose construction rustdoc replaced with R88 explanation citing the SCE fix.

ext_entry.scxml NOT migrated this round: its variant uses the self-flag dispatch pattern (`<sce:variant tag="header.enc">`) where the dispatch byte is the OUTER codec's own header (not the inner body's first byte). The SCE fix's <sce:flag value="..."/> attribute lives on inner-codec flags for peek-byte dispatch; ext_entry's inner codecs (ext_unit/ext_zint/ext_zbuf) are leaf codecs without a header byte, so the attribute has no natural attachment point. ext_entry's existing 'sce:default type=...' fallback already covers its dispatch surface вҖ” no R88 change needed. A follow-up audit could enumerate other self-flag-dispatch codecs to confirm none need migration.

Round 89.decision_summary: R89 inbound OpenSyn cookie verification вҖ” closes R86 outbound/inbound symmetric loop; cookie_valid() guard now re-HMACs peer_zid + cookie_signing_key and compares against the inbound OpenSyn cookie echo, retiring the R57 unconditional-true placeholder.
changes_bullets: crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs: SessionLinkActions gains inbound_opensyn_cookie: Mutex<Option<Vec<u8>>> slot, distinct from the R62 inbound_cookie slot (which captures Initiator-side InitAck.body.cookie for OpenSyn echo). Rustdoc explains the two slots model the same wire field on opposite handshake roles вҖ” one slot per role keeps the dispatch unambiguous.

ActionTrace gains cookie_valid_check: u32 counter. Increments on every cookie_valid() guard invocation so tests can confirm the dynamic guard fired (vs. R57's bind_bool placeholder which never executed any dynamic check). clone_via_copy() forwards the new field.

handle_inbound: new match arm for InboundFrame::Open { is_ack: false, body, .. } captures body.cookie into the slot. Mirrors the R86 InitSyn peer_zid capture pattern. InitAck (is_ack: true) is untouched вҖ” it routes to the R62 Initiator-side inbound_cookie slot.

bind_guard helper added (sibling to bind_unit + bind_bool + bind_close_reason) вҖ” takes Arc<SessionLinkActions> + Fn(&Arc<SessionLinkActions>) -> bool, produces a NativeMethod returning ScriptValue::Bool(closure(actions)). Generic enough to wire other dynamic guards (half_open_cap_available, accept_rate_token) when their R57 placeholders are retired.

register_guard_fns signature changes: gains actions: &Arc<SessionLinkActions> parameter (no-shim break per project rules). cookie_valid binding swaps from bind_bool(lua, "cookie_valid", true) to bind_guard(lua, "cookie_valid", actions, |a| { increment counter; defensive None checks; HMAC re-compute via generate_cookie_hmac_sha256(cookie_signing_key, peer_zid); byte-equality compare against inbound_opensyn_cookie }). half_open_cap_available + accept_rate_token remain R57 bind_bool constants pending their own future rounds.
install_session_actions: register_guard_fns call updated to pass &actions. Single external call site (no test-support external consumers; verified by grep).

Test rewires: craft_opensyn_wire signature gains cookie: &[u8] parameter so callers stage the actual HMAC echo bytes. r78_accepting_path_handshake_terminates_at_established now computes expected_cookie via generate_cookie_hmac_sha256(fixture_key, [0xB0..0xB3]) and stages it through craft_opensyn_wire(&expected_cookie); the test also asserts trace.cookie_valid_check == 1 (proves the dynamic guard fired exactly once on the happy path).

r61_listener_path_inbound_to_established (session_fsm_coverage.rs): R89 raw-event-injection tests must seed the R86 inbound_peer_zid + R89 inbound_opensyn_cookie slots manually because they bypass handle_inbound. Seed pattern mirrors what handle_inbound would populate; test now also asserts trace.cookie_valid_check >= 1 (proves the dynamic guard fired through the raw injection path too).

2 new R89 negative-path tests in session_fsm_accepting_path.rs: r89_invalid_cookie_blocks_transition_to_sentopen_ack stages a 16-byte 0xFF forged cookie вҖ” guard rejects, FSM stays at SentInitAck, send_open_ack counter stays 0. r89_missing_cookie_blocks_transition_to_sentopen_ack stages a zero-length cookie carrier вҖ” same rejection path (empty Vec never matches a non-empty HMAC output).

validate_workspace baseline preserved: T1 orphan=0, round-trip 12/12, GENERATED.md=sync, T3 warn 856 / T4 info 427 unchanged. Atomic ledger entries 101 -> 102 (this Round entry); sections=214 unchanged.

End-to-end RFC В§5.M cookie binding closure: (1) InitSyn arrives -> handle_inbound captures peer_zid (R86); (2) SentInitAck.onentry -> send_init_ack_with_cookie computes HMAC(key, peer_zid)[..16] and embeds in InitAck wire (R86); (3) Initiator echoes cookie back in OpenSyn (Initiator-side R62 chain); (4) OpenSyn arrives -> handle_inbound captures echoed cookie (R89); (5) cookie_valid() re-HMACs and compares (R89); (6) match -> FSM advances to SentOpenAck, mismatch -> stuck at SentInitAck.
carry_forward_bullets: Constant-time comparison вҖ” R89 uses standard byte-equality (==) for the HMAC compare. For a single-peer test fixture this is fine; production hardening against timing oracles would swap to subtle::ConstantTimeEq. Defer until a security review surfaces a timing-side-channel concern in the deploy.yaml scope (mostly an issue for multi-tenant routers, less so for embedded AP pairings).

Slot lifetime / replay defense вҖ” R89 captures the inbound_opensyn_cookie slot once on each OpenSyn arrival and overwrites on subsequent arrivals. A replay attack (peer re-sends a stale OpenSyn) is possible if the FSM ever revisits SentInitAck. Current session-fsm doesn't have a SentInitAck re-entry edge so this is theoretical; a defensive clear-on-exit hook (similar to R84's established_at carry #1) would close it preemptively.

Cookie ext-chain MAC material вҖ” RFC В§5.M allows additional MAC carriers in the cookie ext-chain. R89 verifies the base 16-byte HMAC only. The ext-chain extension point lives in `ext_chain_for(ExtChainRole::InitAck)` (R68b slot) but isn't HMAC-composed yet. Out of R89 scope.

half_open_cap_available + accept_rate_token guards still R57 placeholders (bind_bool true). bind_guard infrastructure from R89 now exists, so retiring those placeholders is a 2-line change once cap-quota / token-bucket implementations land. Defer until DoS-mitigation rounds.

Initiator-side cookie verification absent вҖ” R89 is Accepting-side. The Initiator receives the Accepting side's cookie via InitAck and echoes it verbatim on OpenSyn. The Initiator cannot verify the cookie itself (only the Accepting side holds cookie_signing_key); echo-only is the correct contract per RFC В§5.M. No carry needed вҖ” explicitly NOT a gap.

Seed pattern mirrors what handle_inbound would populate; test now also asserts trace.cookie_valid_check >= 1 (proves the dynamic guard fired through the raw injection path too).

2 new R89 negative-path tests in session_fsm_accepting_path.rs: r89_invalid_cookie_blocks_transition_to_sentopen_ack stages a 16-byte 0xFF forged cookie вҖ” guard rejects, FSM stays at SentInitAck, send_open_ack counter stays 0. r89_missing_cookie_blocks_transition_to_sentopen_ack stages a zero-length cookie carrier вҖ” same rejection path (empty Vec never matches a non-empty HMAC output).

End-to-end RFC В§5.M cookie binding closure: (1) InitSyn arrives -> handle_inbound captures peer_zid (R86); (2) SentInitAck.onentry -> send_init_ack_with_cookie computes HMAC(key, peer_zid)[..16] and embeds in InitAck wire (R86); (3) Initiator echoes cookie back in OpenSyn (Initiator-side R62 chain); (4) OpenSyn arrives -> handle_inbound captures echoed cookie (R89); (5) cookie_valid() re-HMACs and compares (R89); (6) match -> FSM advances to SentOpenAck, mismatch -> stuck at SentInitAck.
publishable_carry_forward_bullets: Constant-time comparison вҖ” R89 uses standard byte-equality (==) for the HMAC compare. For a single-peer test fixture this is fine; production hardening against timing oracles would swap to subtle::ConstantTimeEq. Defer until a security review surfaces a timing-side-channel concern in the deploy.yaml scope (mostly an issue for multi-tenant routers, less so for embedded AP pairings).

Slot lifetime / replay defense вҖ” R89 captures the inbound_opensyn_cookie slot once on each OpenSyn arrival and overwrites on subsequent arrivals. A replay attack (peer re-sends a stale OpenSyn) is possible if the FSM ever revisits SentInitAck. Current session-fsm doesn't have a SentInitAck re-entry edge so this is theoretical; a defensive clear-on-exit hook (similar to R84's established_at carry #1) would close it preemptively.

Cookie ext-chain MAC material вҖ” RFC В§5.M allows additional MAC carriers in the cookie ext-chain. R89 verifies the base 16-byte HMAC only. The ext-chain extension point lives in `ext_chain_for(ExtChainRole::InitAck)` (R68b slot) but isn't HMAC-composed yet. Out of R89 scope.

half_open_cap_available + accept_rate_token guards still R57 placeholders (bind_bool true). bind_guard infrastructure from R89 now exists, so retiring those placeholders is a 2-line change once cap-quota / token-bucket implementations land. Defer until DoS-mitigation rounds.

Initiator-side cookie verification absent вҖ” R89 is Accepting-side. The Initiator receives the Accepting side's cookie via InitAck and echoes it verbatim on OpenSyn. The Initiator cannot verify the cookie itself (only the Accepting side holds cookie_signing_key); echo-only is the correct contract per RFC В§5.M. No carry needed вҖ” explicitly NOT a gap.

Round 90.decision_summary: R90 PUSH envelope codec lands вҖ” first wz-side authored variant codec leveraging R88's RFC variant-default-uniformity; second network-MID dispatch (alongside R74's REQUEST) so application-layer pub/sub data path now decodes via wz_codecs::push.
changes_bullets: sources/codecs/push.scxml authored from scratch (~90 lines). Sibling shape to request.scxml minus the `rid` field per zenoh-pico `_z_push_encode` (network.c). Header flags: mid (bits 0..4, value=0x1D), N (bit 5, suffix on wireexpr), M (bit 6, local mapping), Z (bit 7, ext chain). Embed: keyexpr (codec_zenoh_wireexpr). Z-gated tlv-chain: extensions (codec_zenoh_ext_entry, max-depth=4, entry-flag-Z termination). Peek-byte variant on `peek.mid`: 0x01->msg_put (declared `default="true"` per R88 variant-default-uniformity since Put is the dominant pub/sub data shape), 0x02->msg_del, <sce:default>->msg_put.

crates/wz-codecs/build.rs: "push" added to CODECS list at the end (composing-codecs section). crates/wz-codecs/src/lib.rs: `pub mod push { include!(concat!(env!("OUT_DIR"), "/push.rs")); }` sibling to request module.

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs: wire_const::N_MID_PUSH (0x1D) constant added with rustdoc reference to zenoh-pico network.h:35. NetworkMessage enum gains Push(Box<wz_codecs::push::Push>) variant; rustdoc explains the Box wrapping mirrors the Request variant's size-balancing rationale (Push carries Wireexpr + PushVariant whose arms hold MsgPut/MsgDel). Manual Debug impl extended with `Self::Push(_) => f.write_str("Push(..)")` arm. parse_frame_payload gains the N_MID_PUSH match arm dispatching to Push::decode + NetworkMessage::Push wrap; otherwise identical to the existing N_MID_REQUEST arm.
crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/session_glue.rs imports: `use wz_codecs::push::Push;` added alphabetically before `use wz_codecs::request::Request;`.

Test rewires: R74's r74_rx_frame_unknown_network_mid_absorbs_as_unknown (and its unit-test counterpart parse_frame_payload_unknown_mid_absorbs_remainder + R83's r83_observer_reads_framepayload_messages_through_reference + the R74 chain test parse_frame_payload_decodes_request_then_unknown_chain) all previously used 0x1D=PUSH as the 'still uncodec'd Unknown' fixture MID. With R90 they switch to 0x1B=N_MID_RESPONSE which remains uncodec'd. The Unknown.body asserts + mid value asserts update from 0x1D to 0x1B in lockstep. All match arms on NetworkMessage extended to handle the new Push variant (panic on unexpected Push, same as the existing Request guard).

2 new R90 tests in session_fsm_inbound_dispatch.rs: parse_frame_payload_dispatches_push_mid_to_push_decoder (PUSH MID 0x1D dispatches to wz_codecs::push, parsed.len()==1, NetworkMessage::Push variant) + parse_frame_payload_decodes_push_then_request_chain (multi-codec batch: Push.encode() ++ Request.encode() parses to 2 records in order). 1 new R90 integration test in session_fsm_driver_loop.rs: r90_rx_frame_push_payload_decodes_via_push_codec (Push.encode wrapped in Frame envelope arrives via poll_and_dispatch_one, surfaces as DriverLoopOutcome::FramePayload with messages[0]=Push).
verification_bullets: cargo build -p wz-codecs green; sce-build's codegen pipeline accepts the new push.scxml on first try (no schema errors, no codegen errors).

Inspected generated push.rs Default impl (codegen output): `impl Default for PushVariant { fn default() -> Self { Self::CodecZenohMsgPut(MsgPut::default()) } }` with RFC variant-default-uniformity rustdoc вҖ” matches R88's textbook contract. The inner MsgPut::default().header carries the R88-baked 0x01 MID, so encode/decode is byte-exact on the default state.

carry_forward_bullets: Remaining 5 network-envelope codecs вҖ” RESPONSE (0x1B), RESPONSE_FINAL (0x1A), DECLARE (0x1E), INTEREST (0x19), OAM (0x1F). RESPONSE follows the same template as REQUEST (it carries a response body вҖ” reply / err); RESPONSE_FINAL is a 1-byte marker; DECLARE is the keyexpr declaration envelope; INTEREST is the subscriber pull request; OAM is operations & maintenance. Each is ~50-100 line SCXML + 5-line wz-runtime-tokio wiring + 2-3 tests. Estimate 1 round per codec.

wz-side fixture for the rejected push wire shape вҖ” R90 verifies the happy path; a negative test for a malformed Push payload (e.g. truncated body) similar to r74_rx_frame_malformed_request_payload_surfaces_parse_error would close the symmetry. Skipped this round to keep R90 scope focused on the happy-path dispatch landing.

Wire-interop test against zenoh-pico _z_push_encode вҖ” R43 pattern (layer3_frame.rs) cross-checks codec output against zenoh-pico's C encoder. layer3_push.rs would mirror that for the new Push codec. Defer until a focused layer3 round; the codec is internally self-consistent (round-trip safe via R88 uniformity), so wire-interop is a separate quality gate.

pub/sub callback registry consumer for FramePayload.messages вҖ” R74/R83/R90 surface the decoded messages at the API boundary but no real consumer reads them yet. The application-layer subscriber dispatcher / Lua bridge that takes NetworkMessage::Push(Box<Push>) and routes by Wireexpr keyexpr is the next textbook step. Out of R90 scope; would close the AP MVP pub/sub demo gap.

Round 91.decision_summary: R91 RESPONSE_FINAL marker codec lands вҖ” application-layer 3/7 MID coverage; simplest envelope shape so far (no body, no embed, no variant вҖ” just header + request_id VLE + optional ext-chain).
changes_bullets: sources/codecs/response_final.scxml authored (~50 lines). Mirrors zenoh-pico `_z_response_final_encode` (src/protocol/codec/network.c): byte 0 = header with mid (bits 0..4, value=0x1A) + Z (bit 7) only вҖ” no N, no M flags per upstream header construction. byte 1+ = request_id VLE (correlates marker with original Request rid). Z-gated tlv-chain extensions (codec_zenoh_ext_entry, max-depth=4). No body, no peek-byte variant, no embed вҖ” simplest of the network envelopes.
wz-codecs build.rs: "response_final" appended to CODECS. lib.rs: pub mod response_final { include!(...) } sibling to push module.

wz-runtime-tokio session_glue.rs: wire_const::N_MID_RESPONSE_FINAL (0x1A) added. NetworkMessage enum gains ResponseFinal(ResponseFinal) variant вҖ” NOT Box-wrapped because the struct is small (three integer-ish fields + optional ext vec); rustdoc explains the inline choice vs Request/Push's Box.

Manual Debug impl extended with `Self::ResponseFinal(_) => f.write_str("ResponseFinal(..)")` arm. parse_frame_payload gains N_MID_RESPONSE_FINAL match arm dispatching to ResponseFinal::decode + NetworkMessage::ResponseFinal wrap.

All NetworkMessage match arms across the test suite extended with `| NetworkMessage::ResponseFinal(_)` for exhaustiveness (driver_loop + inbound_dispatch). This is the second time a new MID has required updating every match site in tests; documented as carry below for a possible non-exhaustive-helper round.

1 new R91 unit test: parse_frame_payload_dispatches_response_final_mid_to_response_final_decoder. Asserts `bytes.len() == 2` (encode produces header + 1-byte VLE), `parsed.len() == 1`, and `matches!(parsed[0], NetworkMessage::ResponseFinal(_))`.

Application-layer codec coverage now: 3 of 7 network MIDs (REQUEST 0x1C via R74, PUSH 0x1D via R90, RESPONSE_FINAL 0x1A via R91). Remaining 4 MIDs (RESPONSE 0x1B, DECLARE 0x1E, INTEREST 0x19, OAM 0x1F).
carry_forward_bullets: Match-exhaustiveness churn across tests вҖ” R90 + R91 both required updating every NetworkMessage match arm across 3 test files. Each new MID will keep this overhead. Options: (a) helper macro / function that converts NetworkMessage to a String for assertions (consolidates the variants); (b) `_ =>` wildcard arm in tests that don't need exhaustive coverage; (c) leave-as-is per textbook 'all match sites must explicit-handle every variant'. Option (c) is the safest textbook choice; documenting here so future MID-add rounds budget time for the churn.

RESPONSE codec (0x1B) requires reply + err inner body codecs вҖ” RESPONSE follows the same shape as REQUEST (header + rid + keyexpr + ext-chain + variant body) but the variant body is reply / err which wz-codecs hasn't authored yet. A pre-R92 round (or R92 itself if scope allows) authors reply.scxml + err.scxml as msg_put / msg_del siblings (single body each вҖ” reply carries the queried data, err carries an error envelope). After that, response.scxml follows the request.scxml template directly.

DECLARE (0x1E) codec вҖ” keyexpr declaration envelope. Different shape: a sequence of declarations (DeclSub / DeclToken / DeclQbl / etc.) rather than a single body. Likely needs a `<sce:repeat>` primitive over a variant body. ~100-150 line SCXML; more design surface than RESPONSE_FINAL.

INTEREST (0x19) + OAM (0x1F) codecs вҖ” INTEREST is the subscriber pull request (similar to Request shape but with interest-id and topic-filter fields); OAM is operations & maintenance (versioned ext-only payload). 1 round each, sequential.

layer3_response_final.rs wire-interop test against zenoh-pico вҖ” R43 pattern. layer3_push.rs (R90 carry #3) + layer3_response_final.rs together would land in a focused layer3 round once 2-3 new codecs accumulate вҖ” economy of scale on the FFI bridge setup.

RESPONSE codec (0x1B) requires reply + err inner body codecs вҖ” RESPONSE follows the same shape as REQUEST (header + rid + keyexpr + ext-chain + variant body) but the variant body is reply / err which wz-codecs hasn't authored yet. After that, response.scxml follows the request.scxml template directly.

DECLARE (0x1E) codec вҖ” keyexpr declaration envelope. Different shape: a sequence of declarations (DeclSub / DeclToken / DeclQbl / etc.) rather than a single body. Likely needs a `<sce:repeat>` primitive over a variant body. ~100-150 line SCXML; more design surface than RESPONSE_FINAL.

INTEREST (0x19) + OAM (0x1F) codecs вҖ” INTEREST is the subscriber pull request (similar to Request shape but with interest-id and topic-filter fields); OAM is operations & maintenance (versioned ext-only payload). 1 round each, sequential.

layer3_response_final.rs wire-interop test against zenoh-pico вҖ” R43 pattern. layer3_push.rs (R90 carry #3) + layer3_response_final.rs together would land in a focused layer3 round once 2-3 new codecs accumulate вҖ” economy of scale on the FFI bridge setup.

Round 92.decision_summary: R92 OAM envelope codec lands вҖ” 4 of 7 application-layer MIDs covered; first wz-side codec using self-flag-dispatch variant (header.enc) reusing the ext_unit/zint/zbuf body codecs at the network-MID layer.
changes_bullets: sources/codecs/oam.scxml authored (~70 lines). Mirrors zenoh-pico `_z_oam_encode` (src/protocol/codec/network.c): byte 0 = header with mid (bits 0..4, value=0x1F) + enc (bits 5..6, 2-bit body selector) + Z (bit 7). byte 1+ = id VLE u64. Z-gated tlv-chain extensions. Variant dispatch on `header.enc` (self-flag pattern, mirrors ext_entry) with arms: 0x00 UNIT (declared `default="true"` per R88), 0x01 ZINT, 0x02 ZBUF, <sce:default> UNIT. Body arm codecs re-use existing ext_unit / ext_zint / ext_zbuf imports.

First wz-side codec using SELF-FLAG-DISPATCH variant primitive (`tag="header.enc"`) instead of peek-byte dispatch like push/request. The dispatch byte comes from the outer codec's own header, not the inner body's first byte вҖ” so inner codecs (ext_unit/zint/zbuf, all leaf shapes without their own header) do NOT need `<sce:flag value=>` baked-MID; the outer arm `default="true"` alone closes the R88 variant-default-uniformity invariant for self-flag dispatch.

carry_forward_bullets: R88 carry #1 'ext_entry self-flag-dispatch audit' partially closed by R92 вҖ” OAM uses the same self-flag-dispatch shape as ext_entry and verifies that R88 variant-default-uniformity extends cleanly to it (outer arm `default="true"` alone is sufficient when inner codecs are leaf-shapes). RESPONSE codec (0x1B) вҖ” needs reply + err inner body codecs before it can land. Reply carries the queried data payload (similar shape to msg_put), err carries an error envelope (encoding-tagged like ext_entry). 2 inner-body rounds plus the outer envelope = 3-round sequence to close RESPONSE.

changes_bullets: sources/codecs/interest_body.scxml new вҖ” 8-flag header carrier (KE/SU/QU/TO/R/N/M/AG matching interest.h:26-33 + interest.c L48-50 mask reshuffle) + R-gated wireexpr embed. The header carrier is named `header` so wireexpr's `<sce:requires-parent-flags carrier="header" name="N" bit="5"/>` resolves to interest_body's flag byte (not the outer envelope header); textbook re-use of an existing sub-codec's parent-flag binding by naming the carrier consistently.

sources/codecs/interest.scxml extended вҖ” imports interest_body, adds `<sce:embed id="body" type="codec_zenoh_interest_body" sce:byte="2" sce:present-if="header.C || header.F"/>` immediately after the interest_id field, and bumps the tlv-chain `sce:byte` from 2 to 3 to preserve ordering (body precedes extensions on the wire). Header MID 0x19 bake-in preserved. Comment rewritten to drop the R93 scope-limitation note now that the inner body is in scope.

new positive test `parse_frame_payload_dispatches_response_mid_to_response_decoder` mirrors the R97 RESPONSE chain across three layers of R88 variant-default-uniformity: Response.header bakes 0x1B; its variant body defaults to Reply (declared default arm); Reply's body defaults to MsgPut (R90 declared default). Round 98.decision_summary: R98 lands the application-layer subscriber registry вҖ” closes R90 carry #4, AP MVP critical-path unblock; deliberate pivot away from the next codec round (DECLARE 0x1E) because pub/sub demo value-add exceeds 7/7 MID catalog vanity, and DECLARE's 9-sub-codec chain is a multi-round project on the scale of RESPONSE chain (R95-R97).
changes_bullets: crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/pubsub.rs new вҖ” `SubscriberRegistry` with register / unregister / dispatch / len / is_empty / Default. Callbacks are `Box<dyn FnMut(&Push) + Send + 'static>`; subscription handles are opaque `SubscriptionId(u64)` so callers can hold a stable removal key without depending on keyexpr equality (duplicate keyexpr literals are explicitly allowed). Registry is `!Sync` by design вҖ” cross-task sharing is the caller's responsibility via `Arc<Mutex<вҖҰ>>` to keep the hot dispatch path mutex-free in single-owner cases.
crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/lib.rs declares `pub mod pubsub` after session_glue.

Dispatch scope (R98): Push only, literal keyexpr matching on `Push.keyexpr.suffix` when `Push.keyexpr.id == 0`. Non-Push variants and mapping-id pushes (`id != 0`) are filtered out at dispatch time. Module + struct + each method carry doc comments explaining the scope limits and the DECLARE-table dependency that future rounds will close.

6 inline unit tests cover the dispatch matrix: (1) matching keyexpr fires callback, (2) non-matching keyexpr skips, (3) multiple matching subscribers fire in registration order, (4) non-zero mapping id is filtered out (deferred DECLARE), (5) non-Push variants (ResponseFinal here) are no-ops, (6) unregister is idempotent and returns false on missing id.

Test helper `push_with_keyexpr` constructs a Push whose Wireexpr embed has id=0 + inline suffix вҖ” mirrors the on-wire shape of a router-less pub/sub setup where the publisher has not yet performed a DECLARE handshake (the dominant AP MVP demo configuration).

The textbook integration is an `&mut SubscriberRegistry` observer adapter that calls `dispatch` on each `IterationEvent::Poll(FramePayload { messages, вҖҰ })`. Estimate 1 round (small surface, doc + 1 test using QueueDriver).

DECLARE codec (0x1E) вҖ” 9 declaration sub-types (DeclKexpr/UndeclKexpr/DeclSubscriber/UndeclSubscriber/DeclQueryable/UndeclQueryable/DeclToken/UndeclToken/DeclFinal) per zenoh-pico declarations.c. Each sub-type is ~30-50 line SCXML. Multi-round chain on the scale of RESPONSE (3 rounds for reply+err+response). Once DECLARE lands, the pubsub registry can be extended with a mapping-id resolver so non-zero `wireexpr.id` Pushes route to subscribers registered against the resolved base keyexpr + suffix concatenation.

Keyexpr wildcards вҖ” R98 registry is literal-only. Zenoh's keyexpr language supports `*` (single-chunk wildcard), `**` (multi-chunk), and `$*` (substring) per the upstream `_z_keyexpr_intersect_*` API. A wildcard-aware matcher is a follow-up quality round once the basic literal-match demo is validated against zenoh-pico.

Reply / Err dispatch вҖ” dispatch is Push-only. A future round adds the symmetric `Response` arm (`NetworkMessage::Response.body.reply.body` is a Push-shaped wrapper per R95 reply.scxml). The same registry can route both, gated on a per-subscription kind filter (publish-only vs reply-also-accepted).

Round 99.decision_summary: R99 wires the R98 SubscriberRegistry into drive_session_until_terminal вҖ” closes R98 carry #1 with an in-place dispatch_iteration_event adapter and an end-to-end integration test exercising the full AP MVP path: link bytes вҶ’ Frame вҶ’ parse_frame_payload вҶ’ NetworkMessage::Push вҶ’ registry вҶ’ callback.
changes_bullets: crates/wz-runtime-tokio/src/pubsub.rs: new method `SubscriberRegistry::dispatch_iteration_event(&mut self, IterationEvent<'_>)` extracts `FramePayload.messages` from `IterationEvent::Poll(DriverLoopOutcome::FramePayload { messages, .. })` and routes each record through `dispatch`. Non-FramePayload poll outcomes and Lease branch events are no-ops. The method keeps the integration site free of the `if let Poll(FramePayload { вҖҰ })` matcher boilerplate.
Doc comment cross-references `drive_session_until_terminal` and explains the observer-shaped consumption pattern so future callers can pick it up without reading the test.

crates/wz-runtime-tokio/tests/session_fsm_drive_session.rs: new test `r99_subscriber_registry_routes_framepayload_push_to_callback` constructs a Push wire (header N=1, inline `keyexpr.suffix = "demo/topic"`, default msg_put body), wraps in a Frame envelope (T_MID_FRAME|R, sn=1 VLE), drives `drive_session_until_terminal` with an observer closure that calls `registry.dispatch_iteration_event(ev)` through an `Arc<Mutex<SubscriberRegistry>>`, then asserts the registered callback fired exactly once.

Test uses `Arc<Mutex<SubscriberRegistry>>` to model the realistic production sharing pattern вҖ” the registry is owned across the drive task AND the application-side handle that wants to register / unregister concurrently.
verification_bullets: cargo test -p wz-runtime-tokio --test session_fsm_drive_session r99_subscriber: 1 passed (round-trip Push wire вҶ’ callback fired exactly once).

access-acl.reason: F=ACL filter pipeline; P=zenoh-acl; C=opt-in policy plugin

access-downsampling.reason: F=rate-limit filter; P=zenoh downsampling; C=opt-in QoS policy

access-extauth-jwt.reason: F=JWT verify; P=zenoh; C=requires session-extauth, alternative method

access-extauth-pubkey.reason: F=RSA verify; P=zenoh; C=requires session-extauth, alternative method

access-extauth-usrpwd.reason: F=usrpwd verify; P=zenoh; C=requires session-extauth, alternative method

attachment-bytes.reason: F=attachment field codec; P=zenoh attachment; C=opt-in metadata, enables pubsub-attachment + query-attachment

attachment-encoding-aware.reason: F=tag-typed attachment view; P=zenoh; C=builds on attachment-bytes

codec-close.reason: F=CLOSE codec; P=zenoh-pico codec_variant_session_close; C=session lifecycle terminator

codec-declare.reason: F=DECLARE outer codec; P=zenoh DECLARE; C=carrier for all declare-* kinds

codec-fragment.reason: F=Fragment codec; P=zenoh transport Fragment; C=requires transport-fragmentation

codec-frame.reason: F=Frame codec; P=zenoh transport Frame; C=primary data carrier

codec-hello.reason: F=HELLO codec; P=zenoh-pico codec_zenoh_hello; C=companion to codec-scout

codec-init-ack.reason: F=INIT-ACK body; P=zenoh-pico InitAck; C=session handshake second leg

codec-init-syn.reason: F=INIT-SYN body; P=zenoh-pico InitSyn; C=session handshake first leg

codec-join.reason: F=JOIN codec; P=zenoh-pico Join; C=requires session-multicast

codec-open-ack.reason: F=OPEN-ACK body; P=zenoh-pico OpenAck; C=handshake fourth leg

codec-open-syn.reason: F=OPEN-SYN body; P=zenoh-pico OpenSyn; C=handshake third leg

codec-push.reason: F=PUSH codec; P=zenoh PUSH; C=pubsub carrier (put/delete)

codec-request.reason: F=REQUEST codec; P=zenoh REQUEST; C=query carrier

codec-response.reason: F=RESPONSE codec; P=zenoh RESPONSE; C=reply carrier

codec-response-final.reason: F=RESPONSE-FINAL codec; P=zenoh RESPONSE_FINAL; C=query completion signal

codec-scout.reason: F=SCOUT codec; P=zenoh-pico codec_zenoh_scout; C=requires scouting-active or -multicast

declare-final.reason: F=DECLARE-FINAL push; P=zenoh DECLARE final; C=opt-in subscription complete signal

declare-interest.reason: F=Z_INTEREST extension; P=zenoh interest; C=opt-in, OQ-W3 carry

declare-keyexpr.reason: F=DECLARE-KEXPR codec; P=zenoh DECLARE alias; C=requires keyexpr-mapping

declare-queryable.reason: F=DECLARE-QUERYABLE codec; P=zenoh DECLARE queryable; C=indep declare kind

declare-subscriber.reason: F=DECLARE-SUBSCRIBER codec; P=zenoh DECLARE subscriber; C=indep declare kind

declare-token.reason: F=DECLARE-TOKEN codec; P=zenoh DECLARE liveliness token; C=indep declare kind

declare-undeclare.reason: F=UNDECLARE codec pair per declare kind; P=zenoh UNDECLARE; C=lifecycle pair of DECLARE

encoding-bytes.reason: F=application/octet-stream tag; P=zenoh; C=indep encoding tag

encoding-cbor.reason: F=CBOR encoding tag; P=zenoh; C=indep encoding tag

encoding-empty.reason: F=empty encoding tag; P=zenoh encoding empty; C=baseline tag value

encoding-json.reason: F=JSON encoding tag; P=zenoh; C=indep, payload still bytes

encoding-mime.reason: F=full MIME tag table; P=zenoh RFC 6838; C=superset of bespoke encoding-* tags

encoding-protobuf.reason: F=Protobuf encoding tag; P=zenoh; C=indep encoding tag

encoding-utf8.reason: F=text/plain UTF-8 tag; P=zenoh; C=indep encoding tag

keyexpr-canon.reason: F=canonize fn; P=zenoh-pico _z_keyexpr_canonize; C=mandatory for outbound wire safety

keyexpr-dollar-star.reason: F=non-greedy single-seg wildcard; P=zenoh $*; C=opt-in extension over wildcard-single

keyexpr-includes.reason: F=includes algorithm; P=zenoh _z_keyexpr_includes; C=required for declare-keyexpr alias check

keyexpr-intersect.reason: F=intersect algorithm; P=zenoh _z_keyexpr_intersect; C=required for subscriber matching

keyexpr-literal.reason: F=baseline parse; P=zenoh keyexpr literal; C=indep, foundation for all wildcard variants
